content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates inclusion of student legal rights, housing contract cautions, academic integrity differences, campus safety, healthcare/insurance expectations, and data/privacy norms in legal research—topics aligned to her risk profile and legal mindset.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structure and navigability for multi-country comparison
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report uses a consistent per-country template (e.g., curriculum, credits, culture, costs, visa, scholarships), clear H1/H2/H3 headings, a clickable TOC, and an upfront executive summary plus a concluding decision flow to enable rapid scanning and back-and-forth comparison.

presentation_fit.criterion: Comparative tables and visual consistency
presentation_fit.explanation: Check if key dimensions (curriculum model, credit transfer, visa/language requirements, cost structure, scholarships) are presented in side-by-side tables or aligned lists with consistent labels/units so the reader can instantly contrast countries without rereading prose.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology clarity and bilingual key terms
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether academic, legal, and immigration terms (e.g., ECTS, I-20/DS-2019, GPA, notarization) are precise, expanded at first mention, and paired with Chinese-English equivalents or brief glossaries to support form-filling and document reading.

presentation_fit.criterion: Quantitative normalization and unit presentation
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether costs are normalized (e.g., per month/semester) with CNY conversions and ranges, credit systems are mapped (US credits vs. ECTS) with simple ratios, and assumptions/timeframes are clearly noted in-line, minimizing cognitive load when comparing numbers.

presentation_fit.criterion: Timeline and checklist formatting for preparation steps
presentation_fit.explanation: Check whether the application/preparation process is formatted as a clear timeline (ordered steps with date placeholders) and compact checklists (documents, tasks), making the process easy to follow without hunting through paragraphs.

presentation_fit.criterion: Professional, neutral, and evidence-oriented tone
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether the language reflects legal-writing discipline (concise, claim–evidence–implication structure), avoids promotional wording, and uses cautious qualifiers, aligning with a law student’s expectations for professional discourse.

presentation_fit.criterion: Scannability and cognitive load management
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess use of bullets, numbered lists, short paragraphs, call-out key takeaways, and consistent icons/tags or labels (e.g., [Law], [Psych]) to segment information, enabling quick grasp during short reading windows.

presentation_fit.criterion: Source citation and reference formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Check whether policies, costs, and requirements have clearly formatted references or links (consistent citation style, link titles, and dates accessed) so the reader can verify or update information efficiently.

presentation_fit.criterion: Risk communication formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether risks are presented in a structured format (e.g., categories like academic, legal/visa, health/safety; with severity/likelihood notes and mitigation checklists) to match a risk-averse reader’s need for quick situational awareness.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Program-selection framework and prioritized shortlist tailored to law/psych goals
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a clear decision matrix (e.g., academic fit in civil/criminal law, common-law vs civil-law exposure, clinics/moots, psychology cross-enrollment, costs, credit transfer risk, visa/language ease, semester timing) and produces a ranked shortlist with explicit trade-offs per region (US/Europe/Japan/Singapore). This enables an immediate, defensible choice instead of generic descriptions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Application timeline and process checklist aligned to next semester
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for a dated, step-by-step plan covering internal nomination, departmental approvals, transcript translation, course pre-selection, credit pre-approval, LoR strategy, housing selection, and insurance enrollment—sequenced backward from host start dates and aligned with her current semester workload. Without this, she cannot execute on time.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget and scholarship/aid plan with itemized costs and application steps
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report calculates full cost-of-attendance per destination (tuition/waivers, fees, housing, food, transport, insurance, visa, flights, contingencies) and maps concrete funding sources (university exchange grants, regional scholarships, host waivers), with eligibility, deadlines, required documents, and success tips. Cost feasibility is critical for a cost-conscious student.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Visa and language feasibility with contingencies
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for visa type per region (e.g., J-1/F-1, Student Pass), realistic processing times, document lists, and language requirements (IELTS/TOEFL/JLPT or waiver routes). Must include score targets, test booking windows, and Plan B options (programs with waivers or later intakes) if timelines slip. These are common blockers; actionable guidance must de-risk them.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Credit transfer mapping and graduation impact control
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines whether the report shows how to pre-map host courses to her civil/criminal law requirements and psychology minor, distinguishes core vs elective transferability, provides sample syllabi mapping, and outlines the approval workflow. Prevents graduation delays and preserves internship timing—key to practical feasibility.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Academic and career leverage plan with measurable outputs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report offers a concrete plan to translate the exchange into outcomes: target clinics/moots/RA roles, research deliverables (e.g., a comparative-law paper draft), reference letters, and internship leads, with timelines and KPIs (e.g., 2 faculty meetings/month, 1 competition entry, 1 polished writing sample). Ensures benefits are realized, not just intended.

actionability_practicality.criterion: On-the-ground classroom and cultural integration playbook
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for practical tactics specific to law classrooms (Socratic method participation, case brief templates, citation norms), plus a 2-week acclimation plan, micro-habits for mental health, and daily routines to manage workload. Concrete techniques increase day-one readiness and reduce adaptation friction.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Networking and mentorship action plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report includes a stepwise outreach schedule, email/LinkedIn templates, target groups (law societies, arbitration centers, moot clubs, alumni), and a cadence for informational interviews and events. Actionable networking is essential for building references and internship pipelines within a short visit.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk, safety, and compliance management
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses presence of a practical checklist covering health insurance/claims, emergency contacts, housing safety, data/cyber hygiene, student-visa work limits, and an escalation protocol for disruptions (visa delay, illness, flight cancellations), plus a small emergency budget. Aligns with her risk-averse profile and safeguards execution.

depth_insight.criterion: Comparative Granularity and Differentiation Across Countries and Program Types
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report moves beyond listings to contrast the US, Europe, Japan, Singapore, etc. on curriculum/teaching models, credit transfer norms, academic culture, living cost profiles, visa/language requirements, and funding, highlighting nuanced trade-offs, intra-region variation, and conditions under which each option excels or underperforms.

depth_insight.criterion: Evidence-backed Cost-Benefit and Funding Realism
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report quantifies total cost of attendance (tuition/fees, housing, insurance, visa, transport, hidden costs) and realistically analyzes scholarships/aid (eligibility, competitiveness, typical award sizes, timelines), enabling credible value judgments rather than generic affordability claims.

depth_insight.criterion: Credit Transfer and Academic Alignment Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Examines depth in explaining credit systems and conversions (e.g., ECTS-to-US), grade conversion impacts, course pre-approval/assurance strategies, calendar (quarter/semester) mismatches, and lab access constraints, plus risk mitigation steps to secure academic outcomes from a short-term visit.

depth_insight.criterion: Causal Linkage from Program Features to Academic and Career Outcomes
depth_insight.explanation: Judges the rigor with which the report connects specific features (interdisciplinary/practice-oriented curricula, supervision models, lab/industry access) to outcomes (research skills, publications/outputs, network strength, cross-cultural competence), including discipline/level nuances and short-term ROI considerations.

depth_insight.criterion: Personalized Decision Framework and Trade-off Reasoning
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a structured way to match student profiles (major, language, budget, risk tolerance, career goals) to program choices, including explicit trade-offs, threshold conditions, and scenario-based recommendations that enable high-quality decisions.

depth_insight.criterion: Practical Playbook for Rapid Integration and Risk Management
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the specificity and actionability of tactics for fast classroom/cultural integration, mental health and resilience, compliance (visa/work rules), safety/healthcare/insurance, and contingency planning, showing foresight into common failure modes and high-leverage interventions.

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and Non-obvious, High-value Insights
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards unique, counterintuitive, or expert-level insights (e.g., academic calendar pitfalls, exam/assessment styles, IRB/ethics onboarding, data/export controls, lab onboarding lead times, GPA conversion risks) that materially improve outcomes and are not commonly known.

logical_coherence.criterion: Coherent Macro-Structure Aligned to Task (Description → Analysis → Prescription)
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report first compares countries on specified dimensions, then analyzes academic/career value, and finally derives preparation and adaptation strategies—so later sections build explicitly on earlier findings rather than introducing disconnected advice.

Assesses whether each country is evaluated using the same set of dimensions (curriculum model, credit transfer, culture/academia, living costs, visa/language, cost structure, scholarships), enabling valid cross-country reasoning and avoiding apples-to-oranges comparisons.

logical_coherence.criterion: Decision Logic from Learner Goals/Constraints to Program Selection
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the report explicitly links user objectives (e.g., research focus, budget, language level, credit needs, time window) and constraints to selection criteria, shows prioritization/trade-offs, and traces a clear path from criteria to recommended countries/program types.

logical_coherence.criterion: Causal Reasoning from Program Features to Academic/Career Outcomes
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether claims about value (research capability, networking, cross-cultural skills) follow from program features via clear mechanisms (e.g., lab access → methods training; industry ties → internships/networking), avoiding unsupported leaps or post hoc assertions.

Assesses whether factual statements (e.g., credit recognition rules, visa timelines, typical costs, scholarship availability) are supported by appropriate sources or data, and whether cited evidence is directly relevant to the claims it supports.

logical_coherence.criterion: Smooth Transitions and Logical Bridges Between Sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether section/paragraph transitions summarize prior points and preview next steps (e.g., from country comparisons to value analysis, then to adaptation tactics), preventing logical jumps and guiding readers through the reasoning flow.

Checks for consistency in definitions (e.g., what counts as ‘practice-oriented’), units and timeframes (semester lengths, currencies), and alignment between earlier constraints (credit transfer limits, visa rules) and later recommendations; key assumptions are stated where uncertainty exists.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structured Layout and Navigational Aids for Cross-Country Comparison
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a consistent heading schema across countries (e.g., Curriculum/Teaching, Credit Transfer, Culture, Cost of Living, Visa/Language, Funding, Practical Value, Adaptation Tips), provides a table of contents, section numbering, and cross-references/anchors so readers can quickly locate a country or facet.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity, Conciseness, and Professional Tone of Language
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether sentences are clear, concise, and free from ambiguity, using active voice and concrete verbs; avoids unnecessary jargon and long, nested sentences; maintains a neutral, professional tone suitable for guidance, improving comprehension and reducing cognitive load.

clarity_readability.criterion: Terminology Definition and Consistent Use (Acronyms, Units, and Conversions)
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether specialized terms are defined on first use and/or in a glossary (e.g., ECTS vs. US credits, modular credits, J-1/Schengen, JLPT/IELTS/TOEFL), whether abbreviations are used consistently, and whether units/currencies/dates are standardized with clear conversions (e.g., USD/EUR/JPY/SGD and local per-month costs).

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness and Readability of Visuals (Tables, Timelines, Flowcharts, Budgets)
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether comparative tables, application timelines, process flowcharts, and budget breakdowns are legible and self-explanatory (clear titles, labeled axes/columns, units, legends, footnotes for assumptions), use readable colors/contrast, and actually simplify complex comparisons rather than cluttering the page.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Key Actions and Takeaways
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether the report surfaces critical points with summaries, callouts, do/don’t boxes, and checklists (e.g., pre-departure checklist, visa document list, first-week actions), enabling quick scanning and immediate application without reading every paragraph.

clarity_readability.criterion: Accessibility and Formatting Hygiene
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses font size/line spacing, adequate white space, consistent styles, mobile-friendly layout or PDF bookmarks, descriptive and working hyperlinks, color-blind-safe palettes, and alt text for key figures, ensuring the document is readable across devices and accessible to diverse readers.

task: I plan to participate in an overseas exchange or short-term visiting program in the coming semester to broaden my international perspective and enhance my academic capabilities. However, I currently lack a systematic understanding of how to select a suitable program and prepare comprehensively. Please help me outline the characteristics of exchange or visiting programs in different countries (such as the United States, Europe, Japan, Singapore, etc.), including curriculum design and teaching models (e.g., interdisciplinary courses, practice-oriented courses), credit transfer mechanisms, cultural and academic atmosphere, living costs, visa and language requirements, cost structure, and available scholarships or financial aid. In addition, please analyze the practical value of these programs for future academic development and career competitiveness, including their impact on research capability enhancement, networking opportunities, and cross-cultural communication skills. Finally, provide practical suggestions for adapting to studying and living abroad, including techniques for quickly integrating into local classrooms and culture, psychological adjustment, and risk management strategies, so that I can make the most of the exchange opportunity within a short time and maximize both academic and professional gains.

goal_alignment.criterion: Discipline-specific alignment to intelligent manufacturing and robotics (programs, labs, and project access)
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the article maps each country to concrete robotics/intelligent manufacturing options (e.g., host labs, project-based courses, industry testbeds, maker spaces), and shows how Han Lei can access equipment/industry collaborations relevant to his research. This directly serves his field and maximizes academic relevance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Credit transfer safety and semester alignment for a Chinese graduate program
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates if it provides operable steps for pre-approval, syllabus matching, ECTS-to-credit mapping, thesis timeline fit, and calendars (e.g., US/Europe vs China semesters), plus differences between exchange vs visiting (credit vs non-credit). Critical for a risk-averse student to avoid graduation delays.

goal_alignment.criterion: Cost and funding plan with China-relevant scholarships and cost-optimization
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether it gives city-level living cost ranges, tuition/fee structures for exchange vs fee-paying visiting, and specific funding routes (e.g., CSC, university mobility grants, Sino–foreign joint programs), plus practical savings (shared housing, cooking, transit). Matches his frugality and limited income.

goal_alignment.criterion: Visa and language requirements with timelines, buffers, and checklists
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks clarity on visa types (US J-1/F-1, EU student/residence permit, Japan Student, Singapore Student Pass), processing times, document lists, insurance, and language benchmarks (TOEFL/IELTS/JLPT), presented as a schedule/checklist to suit his Trello-style planning and low risk tolerance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Research and career ROI design for a one-semester visit
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether it translates destinations into tangible outputs (paper/poster at ICRA/IROS/CASE, open-source code/datasets, recommender relationships, industry exposure) with milestones and deliverables. Aligns with his ambition to enhance research capability and employability in robotics.

goal_alignment.criterion: Supervisor targeting and networking strategy tailored to a reserved communicator
goal_alignment.explanation: Looks for stepwise guidance to shortlist PIs, craft a one-page pitch, pre-arrival contact emails, meeting scripts, lab etiquette across cultures (US open debate; Japan hierarchy; Europe formality), and maintaining ties (WeChat/LinkedIn). Essential for building high-quality references and collaborations.

goal_alignment.criterion: Classroom and cultural integration tactics plus living adaptation aligned to habits
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for practical methods to participate in discussions, group work norms, academic integrity, and daily living tips matching his habits (night study/day experiments, cooking to save costs, cycling commute with local rules). Supports fast integration and well-being.

goal_alignment.criterion: Time-boxed action plan to maximize a short-term exchange
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses presence of a pre-departure → first 2 weeks → midterm → final deliverables timeline, leveraging his time preferences and planning tools (Trello), to ensure rapid onboarding, midterm checkpoints, and end-of-visit outputs.

goal_alignment.criterion: Comprehensive risk management (academic, legal, health/safety, financial) with Plan B
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates coverage of lab safety, IP/authorship, export control (e.g., US EAR/ITAR for robotics), visa work limits, insurance, cycling/road safety, and contingency plans for visa delays or credit issues. Matches his low risk appetite and need for reliability.

goal_alignment.criterion: Analytical communication style: structured comparisons, data-backed guidance, and checklists
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks whether information is organized in comparative, quantitative terms with clear decision frameworks, minimal fluff, and China-specific references/resources, aligning with his detail-oriented, analytical reading habits.

content_alignment.criterion: Discipline-Specific Fit to Intelligent Manufacturing and Robotics
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the content prioritizes programs, labs, courses, and project formats directly relevant to robotics, mechatronics, controls, machine vision, and Industry 4.0, including lab/testbed access and industry-collaborative projects, matching the user’s specialization and research orientation.

content_alignment.criterion: Graduate-Level Depth and Technical Specificity
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the report goes beyond generalities to provide graduate-appropriate details (e.g., typical course structures and codes, lab joining mechanisms, seminar/reading group norms, research credit vs. taught credit distinctions, IP/NDAs), aligning with the user’s strong academic background and IEEE/Scholar habits.

content_alignment.criterion: Credit Transfer Specificity for Chinese Home Institutions
content_alignment.explanation: Check for precise, China-relevant credit mapping guidance (US credit hours to domestic credits, ECTS conversion, Japan/Singapore unit systems, research credit recognition, transcript/grade scale nuances), reflecting the user’s need to port credits back efficiently.

content_alignment.criterion: Cost-Effectiveness and Funding Tailored to a Frugal, Low-Risk Student
content_alignment.explanation: Determine if the content provides detailed, location-specific cost breakdowns (tuition/fees, housing, transport, food) and practical funding avenues (tuition waivers, exchange fee reciprocity, lab stipends, travel grants, country-specific scholarships), matching the user’s budget-conscious profile.

content_alignment.criterion: Visa/Language and Compliance Focus for Risk-Sensitive Planning
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether visa categories, processing timelines, documentation, language requirements (TOEFL/IELTS, Japanese proficiency realities in labs, English medium in Europe/Singapore), and compliance topics (health insurance, safety training) are covered at a level that reduces uncertainty for a risk-averse user.

content_alignment.criterion: Country/Ecosystem Comparison Anchored in Robotics/Manufacturing
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the US/Europe/Japan/Singapore comparison emphasizes robotics/manufacturing ecosystems (research institutes, industry clusters, tech transfer culture, internship pathways), enabling a field-driven country choice rather than generic comparisons.

content_alignment.criterion: Cultural and Lab Integration Tailored to a Reserved Chinese Engineering Student
content_alignment.explanation: Check if integration advice addresses engineering-lab realities (PI/postdoc expectations, safety and documentation culture, meeting participation norms, email/Slack etiquette) and helps a diligent but reserved student engage effectively in Western/Japanese/Singaporean classrooms.

content_alignment.criterion: Networking and Community Alignment with IEEE/Robotics Career Paths
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the content highlights discipline-relevant venues and communities (e.g., ICRA/IROS, CASE, IEEE RAS chapters, industry partnerships in automotive/robotics) and how short visits can translate to ongoing collaborations, matching the user’s research-centric goals.

content_alignment.criterion: Program Format and Timeline Suitability for a Short-Term Next-Semester Plan
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the report prioritizes formats feasible within one semester (exchange modules, research visiting student status, intensive blocks/summer schools) and clarifies their typical duration/intake cycles to fit the user’s near-term schedule.

presentation_fit.criterion: Consistent comparative structure across countries
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether each country section follows identical subheadings (curriculum model, credit transfer, culture, living costs, visa/language, cost breakdown, scholarships) and provides side‑by‑side comparability. This fits Han Lei’s analytical style and enables quick, fair comparisons.

presentation_fit.criterion: Professional, concise, and neutral tone
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate if the report uses an academic, evidence-oriented style, avoids promotional language, and remains concise. This matches his IEEE/Scholar reading habits and risk-sensitive preference for sober, reliable communication.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology and acronym handling
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that administrative/academic acronyms (e.g., ECTS, J‑1, CoE) are expanded on first use with brief, plain-language definitions. This reduces cognitive load for non-engineering terms while respecting his technical competence.

presentation_fit.criterion: Quantitative clarity and localization of units
presentation_fit.explanation: Verify costs and metrics are presented with clear units, ranges, currency conversion to CNY alongside local currency, and an ‘as‑of’ note for exchange rates. This supports his cost-effectiveness mindset and precise decision-making.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use and quality of comparative visuals
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess presence and clarity of tables/matrices for country comparisons and simple diagrams (decision trees/flowcharts) for processes like credit transfer or visa steps. Well-labeled visuals boost comprehension for a detail-oriented engineer.

presentation_fit.criterion: Navigability and layered information
presentation_fit.explanation: Check for an executive summary/TL;DR, numbered headings, consistent hierarchy, and (if digital) TOC/anchors for quick jumps. Layered structure supports his night-time study efficiency and scanning behavior.

presentation_fit.criterion: Phase-based checklists and timeline formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether action steps are presented as phase-grouped checklists with due-time markers (e.g., T‑12/T‑8 weeks), making it easy to copy into Trello. This is a presentation choice that aids his planning workflow without judging feasibility.

presentation_fit.criterion: Source and citation presentation
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess if links/footnotes are cleanly formatted, distinguish official policy sources from blogs, and are placed without interrupting flow. This aligns with his academic habits and need to verify information quickly.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Decision framework with weighted scoring and go/no-go rules
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a concrete scoring matrix (e.g., weights for lab fit, credit transfer risk, deadlines feasibility, total cost, language requirements) plus tie-breakers and explicit go/no-go thresholds so Han Lei can convert analysis into a single executable choice.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Lab/course fit verification workflow for robotics/intelligent manufacturing
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for step-by-step methods to verify academic fit (how to find relevant labs/courses, confirm equipment/project access, assess supervision bandwidth, and contact hosts), including checklists/questions and email templates—so he can actively validate fit rather than passively read descriptions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Credit transfer and degree-progress assurance
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report gives a concrete process to map courses to home requirements, obtain pre-approvals, understand grade conversion, and secure advisor/department sign-offs with timelines and templates—preventing delays or wasted credits.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Total-cost and funding feasibility plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for itemized cost breakdowns by destination (tuition/fees, housing, meals, insurance, visa, flights, transport), scholarship/aid options with deadlines, proof-of-funds requirements, and cost-saving tactics, enabling a realistic budget and cash-flow plan consistent with his frugal, low-risk profile.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Backward timeline and milestone plan for the coming semester
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report lays out a milestone schedule with dependencies and buffers (application, nomination, tests, scholarship deadlines, visa appointment, housing, insurance) that he can execute with his Trello workflow to ensure feasibility under time constraints.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Visa and compliance readiness
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report specifies visa category expectations by region, required documents, processing times, financial statements, insurance mandates, and contingency steps if appointments or approvals are delayed—critical for avoiding last-minute failure.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Language and academic readiness plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines whether the report sets explicit test benchmarks (e.g., TOEFL/IELTS thresholds), prep timelines, test booking guidance, and academic communication skills prep (presentation/writing resources) so he can clear language gates and perform well academically.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Rapid classroom and culture integration playbook
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies that the report offers a first-2-weeks action plan (office hours, joining lab meetings, forming study groups, participation norms, teamwork etiquette) and concrete tactics that accelerate adaptation and learning in a short-term stay.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Networking and career leverage plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for actionable steps to build relationships and convert the visit into outcomes (target faculty/industry lists, alumni outreach scripts, event calendars, one-page research pitch, follow-up cadence, paths to internships/research collaborations).

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk management for health, safety, and research/IP
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses inclusion of practical risk controls (health insurance coverage, lab safety training, data/IP policy compliance, emergency contacts, housing vetting, cyber/fraud precautions) and contingency plans for visa/course changes—aligned with his low risk tolerance.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Execution supports and resources
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report provides ready-to-use links and templates (official program/visa pages, scholarship portals, budget spreadsheets, checklists, contact points at home/host international offices) to reduce friction from planning to action.

depth_insight.criterion: Cross-country comparative depth and decision-relevant trade-offs
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report goes beyond listing features to contrast the US, Europe, Japan, Singapore, etc., on curriculum models, credit norms, costs, visa/language hurdles, and scholarships, highlighting who benefits where and why, and what is sacrificed—enabling an informed program choice.

depth_insight.criterion: Academic fit and credit transfer rigor
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the depth of analysis on course/lab mapping to home requirements (e.g., ECTS vs. US credits, grade translation, pass/fail policies, accreditation constraints), and identification of non-obvious pitfalls (caps on transfer credits, lab/IP policies) that determine whether learning actually counts.

depth_insight.criterion: Causal mapping from program features to academic and career ROI
depth_insight.explanation: Judges whether the report explicitly links program characteristics to outcomes (research skills, publications, mentorship/recommendations, internships, employability, cross-cultural competencies), with mechanisms and conditions under which benefits materialize.

depth_insight.criterion: Interdependency analysis across academic, administrative, cultural, and financial factors
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for insight into how factors interact (e.g., visa timelines constrain course add/drop; language policies shift assessment methods; cost-of-living pressures limit time for labs; culture affects classroom participation), altering feasibility and expected value.

depth_insight.criterion: Actionable, short-term optimization strategies
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether recommendations are specific, prioritized, and time-bound for a short visit (e.g., pre-contacting advisors/PIs, course shopping tactics, early lab onboarding, network-building playbook, targeted use of office hours), maximizing gains quickly.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk identification and mitigation depth
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates coverage of key risks (visa delays, course cancellation, housing scams, health/culture shock, safety/compliance in labs, insurance gaps) with concrete mitigations (buffers, contingency course lists, verification steps, support resources, emergency protocols).

depth_insight.criterion: Use of realistic benchmarks and evidence
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether the report grounds judgments with comparative ranges and norms (living costs by city, visa processing times, typical scholarship amounts/eligibility, workload expectations, credit conversion examples), avoiding generic or anecdotal claims.

depth_insight.criterion: Original, context-aware insights beyond common advice
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards nuanced, high-value ideas tailored to this context (e.g., scholarship stacking and taxation rules, choosing smaller labs for higher mentorship density, currency-hedging living costs, leveraging bilingual advantage in Japan/Singapore, GPA management via grading options).

logical_coherence.criterion: Structured Comparative Framework and Scope Control
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear, consistent framework (e.g., a fixed set of dimensions: curriculum, credit transfer, culture, costs, visa/language, scholarships) to profile each country, followed by cross-cutting comparison, then mapping to user needs, and finally preparation/risk sections. Proper scope control ensures the analysis stays focused on short-term exchanges/visits and does not drift to unrelated program types.

logical_coherence.criterion: Consistency of Dimensions and Definitions Across Countries
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether comparable categories, definitions, and units are applied consistently across regions (e.g., same cost components, time bases, and definitions of 'credit transfer' or 'practice-oriented courses'), preventing apples-to-oranges comparisons or internal contradictions.

Evaluates whether factual statements (e.g., visa timelines, language thresholds, typical living costs, scholarship availability) are explicitly tied to support (sources, institutional policies, or typical ranges) so that each assertion is anchored and the argumentative chain is verifiable.

logical_coherence.criterion: Causal Linkage from Program Features to Outcomes
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that the report explicitly explains how specific features (interdisciplinary design, research labs, internships, teaching models) lead to academic and career outcomes (research skill gains, networking breadth, cross-cultural competence), including mechanisms and boundary conditions, rather than relying on generic claims.

logical_coherence.criterion: Decision Logic from Goals/Constraints to Selection and Preparation Plan
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the report builds a transparent decision framework (e.g., criteria/weights, flow or eligibility gates for visa/language/GPA, budget constraints) that connects the user’s objectives to program choices and yields a coherent preparation timeline (applications, credit checks, risk mitigations).

logical_coherence.criterion: Smooth Transitions and Logical Bridges between Sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether section transitions (country profiles → cross-country synthesis → value analysis → adaptation/risk strategies) are explicitly bridged, referencing what has been established and why the next step follows, avoiding logical jumps.

Checks for explicit statements of assumptions (discipline, exchange length, academic calendar), handling of uncertainty/variability across institutions, and structured discussion of trade-offs (cost vs. value, research intensity vs. workload), including any sensitivity or scenario logic used.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Layout and Navigability for Cross-Country Comparison
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear heading hierarchy, consistent section templates per country (e.g., curriculum, credits, visas, costs), a table of contents, numbered subsections, adequate white space, and bullet lists so readers can locate and compare topics quickly.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity and Precision of Language
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether sentences are concise, grammar and punctuation are correct, word choice is unambiguous, and tone is accessible to non-experts; avoids long, dense paragraphs and jargon-heavy phrasing that impede understanding.

clarity_readability.criterion: Technical Term and Acronym Handling
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether technical terms and acronyms (e.g., ECTS, SEVIS, CPT/OPT, CEFR, JASSO) are defined at first use, used consistently thereafter, optionally consolidated in a glossary, and not overused without explanation.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Comparative Tables and Visualizations
clarity_readability.explanation: Rates whether tables/matrices (e.g., country × dimension), timelines (visa steps), and cost breakdown charts are readable and self-explanatory with clear titles, labels, legends, notes, and sufficient contrast; visuals should complement text and reduce scanning time.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency of Units, Scales, and Notation
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks standardized currency presentation (e.g., one base currency with conversion date), unified credit notation (e.g., mapping ECTS to US credits), consistent date formats and time horizons, and uniform labeling across countries to prevent misreading.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Key Actions, Deadlines, and Executive Summaries
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether key takeaways, checklists, timelines, and risk cautions are prominently formatted (e.g., callouts, bullet points, bolded deadlines) and whether concise executive summaries allow quick grasp of what to do and by when.

task: I plan to participate in an overseas exchange or short-term visiting program in the coming semester to broaden my international perspective and enhance my academic capabilities. However, I currently lack a systematic understanding of how to select a suitable program and prepare comprehensively. Please help me outline the characteristics of exchange or visiting programs in different countries (such as the United States, Europe, Japan, Singapore, etc.), including curriculum design and teaching models (e.g., interdisciplinary courses, practice-oriented courses), credit transfer mechanisms, cultural and academic atmosphere, living costs, visa and language requirements, cost structure, and available scholarships or financial aid. In addition, please analyze the practical value of these programs for future academic development and career competitiveness, including their impact on research capability enhancement, networking opportunities, and cross-cultural communication skills. Finally, provide practical suggestions for adapting to studying and living abroad, including techniques for quickly integrating into local classrooms and culture, psychological adjustment, and risk management strategies, so that I can make the most of the exchange opportunity within a short time and maximize both academic and professional gains.

goal_alignment.criterion: Environmental Studies–centric program matching with sustainable urban focus
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the article prioritizes and substantiates program options in the US/Europe/Japan/Singapore with strong environmental science, sustainability, urban planning, and circular economy tracks, including concrete course/studio/lab and fieldwork examples (e.g., urban sustainability studios, applied ecology, circular systems). This matches Gao Yi’s academic strengths and interests and ensures the exchange directly advances her domain expertise.

goal_alignment.criterion: China-specific credit transfer planning and graduation security
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the report explains ECTS–Chinese credit, US credit hour, and Japan/Singapore credit conversions; differentiates core vs elective mappings; details pre-approval with department/international office; and aligns host calendars with her home university to prevent graduation delays. This targets her need for a systematic path that preserves academic progress at a prestigious Zhejiang university.

goal_alignment.criterion: Next-semester application and visa/language roadmap for Chinese passport holders
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for a backward-plan timeline (tests like TOEFL/IELTS, nomination/acceptance windows, DS-2019/I-20 or Schengen/Japan/Singapore visa appointments, document prep), highlighting typical lead times and bottlenecks for outbound Chinese students. Timeliness is critical given her near-term plan.

goal_alignment.criterion: Cost structure realism and scholarship/aid pathways tailored to exchange
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether living cost breakdowns by region (housing, transport, insurance, food with vegetarian options) are realistic, whether tuition-waiver reciprocity is explained, and whether scholarships suitable for incoming exchange (e.g., JASSO in Japan, DAAD offerings in Germany, host-university bursaries, home-university/provincial funds) and application steps are provided. Matches her stable-yet-finite budget and conservative risk profile.

goal_alignment.criterion: Research capability and career competitiveness plan with concrete outputs
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the article translates exchange into actionable research steps: targeting labs/PIs in environmental fields, email outreach templates, joining projects/field campaigns, presenting posters, short paper opportunities, building faculty references, and joining sustainability networks (student clubs, NGOs, conferences). This aligns with her award record and graduate school aspirations.

goal_alignment.criterion: Classroom/cultural integration tailored to proactive, eco-conscious lifestyle
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for strategies fitting her extroversion and routines: norms for participation and office hours (US/Europe), group work etiquette (Japan/SG), fast onboarding to local classrooms, leveraging MOOCs for prerequisite gaps, morning routines and campus resources, vegetarian dining and eco-friendly living options. This ensures day-one usability and value resonance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk management and wellbeing aligned to conservative risk appetite
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess inclusion of health insurance and care pathways, campus safety practices, document/security backups, travel and housing safety, mental health strategies leveraging her exercise/meditation habits, and contingency plans (visa delays, course mismatch). Matches her conservative risk stance and structured lifestyle.

goal_alignment.criterion: Decision framework and tools for personalized program selection
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the report provides a scoring matrix weighted by her priorities (sustainability strength, fieldwork access, credit fit, cost, visa complexity, language), checklists, and a week-by-week prep plan, with suggestions to use spreadsheets/Notion and to consult mentors/advisors at key gates. Fits her independent-but-advised decision style and app/tool habits.

goal_alignment.criterion: Evidence density and China-relevant sourcing/clarity
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for concrete examples (named labs/courses, city-specific cost ranges), clear definitions of credit/visa terms, links or pointers to official pages, and guidance that reflects Chinese outbound student context rather than generic global advice. Supports her preference for scientific accuracy and mentor consultation.

goal_alignment.criterion: Sustainability ethics and low-carbon practice integration
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the article embeds low-carbon travel options (e.g., European rail), addresses greenwashing in programs/volunteering, and suggests measurable sustainability practices abroad. This aligns with her environmental values and strengthens personal-professional coherence.

content_alignment.criterion: Environmental-Studies and Sustainable Urban Development Focus by Country
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the report prioritizes environmental curricula and teaching models (e.g., urban ecology, water management, climate policy, circular economy) and differentiates country strengths (US project-based interdisciplinarity; Europe’s ECTS, field schools; Japan’s lab-seminar culture; Singapore’s applied sustainability) instead of generic program overviews.

content_alignment.criterion: Environmental Research, Fieldwork, and Networking Opportunities Mapping
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the content identifies discipline-specific labs, institutes, city agencies/NGOs, living labs, and relevant conferences that a short-term visitor can realistically join, and how these build research capability and professional networks in environmental fields.

content_alignment.criterion: China-to-Host Credit Transfer and Semester/Calendar Compatibility
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates depth on credit systems (ECTS vs US credits vs Japanese units), assessment modes affecting transfer, and alignment with a Chinese university calendar for the coming semester, going beyond generic ‘credits can transfer’ statements.

content_alignment.criterion: Funding, Cost Structure, and Scholarship Options Tailored to Chinese Undergraduates
content_alignment.explanation: Checks if the report distinguishes exchange (tuition waiver) vs visiting (fee-paying) models, provides city-level cost ranges in target regions, and highlights scholarship/aid channels commonly accessible to Chinese undergrads (e.g., university bilateral awards, host-university short-term grants, environmental-focused funds), rather than vague funding advice.

content_alignment.criterion: Cultural and Sustainability Lifestyle Fit (Vegetarian, Low-Waste, Green Campus, Outdoor Access)
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the content covers lifestyle alignment: availability of vegetarian options, waste sorting/low-waste infrastructure, green campus initiatives, safe running routes/parks, and eco-commuting, reflecting the user’s eco-conscious habits and routines.

content_alignment.criterion: Language and Visa Requirements for Short-Term Programs with English-Taught Access
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether country sections specify English-taught course availability in environmental topics, typical TOEFL/IELTS expectations (or Japanese proficiency where relevant), and short-term visa categories/timelines suitable for a semester visit.

content_alignment.criterion: Country Comparisons Anchored in Environmental Innovation Hubs and Proximity/Carbon Considerations
content_alignment.explanation: Checks that comparisons emphasize cities/regions with environmental strengths (e.g., climate tech hubs, water management exemplars) and acknowledge proximity from Hangzhou and travel carbon trade-offs in line with the user’s sustainability values.

content_alignment.criterion: Adaptation and Risk Management Content Personalized to a Young Female Environmental Student
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether adaptation tips address integration into lab/classroom norms in environmental disciplines, fieldwork safety, outdoor exercise safety, and mental well-being practices (running/meditation), aligning with her gendered safety needs and routines.

content_alignment.criterion: Decision Criteria Framed Around Her Values and Consultative Decision Style
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the report proposes selection factors reflecting her priorities (practice-oriented learning, sustainability impact, mentorship access, credit certainty, funding) and encourages expert consultation, focusing on what to consider rather than how to write or format decisions.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structural consistency and modular organization across country sections
presentation_fit.explanation: Check whether each country profile follows the same ordered subheadings (e.g., Curriculum/Teaching Model → Credit Transfer → Culture/Academic Atmosphere → Living Costs → Visa/Language → Cost Structure → Scholarships/Financial Aid) so the reader can scan and compare efficiently without re-learning the layout.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tone and readability fit for a high-achieving Chinese undergraduate
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate if the report uses a professional, evidence-based, concise style in Chinese as the primary language, avoids marketing language, uses active voice and short sentences/paragraphs appropriate for an academically capable but time-pressed reader.

presentation_fit.criterion: Jargon handling and bilingual clarification of key terms
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether administrative and academic acronyms/terms (e.g., ECTS, GPA, J-1/F-1, Schengen, CoE, STP, credit transfer) are expanded once and briefly defined, with bilingual labels where helpful, minimizing cognitive friction during reading.

presentation_fit.criterion: Comparative visuals and decision aids that reduce cognitive load
presentation_fit.explanation: Check for clean comparison tables/matrices across countries, checklists for preparation items, flowcharts for application processes, and semester timelines. These visuals should be legible, labeled, and consistent, enabling quick grasp without digging into long text.

presentation_fit.criterion: Localization of numbers, units, and formats
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether costs are shown with CNY conversions alongside local currencies, units/ranges are standardized, and dates/deadlines are presented with clear formats/time zones. This improves immediate comprehension without separate calculations.

presentation_fit.criterion: Navigation and information layering for scanability and mobile reading
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess presence of an executive summary/TL;DR, a clickable table of contents, descriptive headings, bullet lists, and chunked sections suitable for mobile viewing. Layering should allow a quick overview first, then deeper details.

presentation_fit.criterion: Emphasis and alerting of critical deadlines and constraints
presentation_fit.explanation: Check whether key risks, deadlines, and lead times are visually emphasized (e.g., callout boxes, icons, bold dates) in a calm, non-alarming style, helping the reader prioritize attention and plan effectively.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Program-selection decision framework and tailored shortlist
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report offers a clear decision matrix (e.g., environmental field strength, semester timing, English-taught availability, exchange agreements, budget range) and produces a ranked shortlist of concrete programs with links and contacts so the user can choose and act immediately.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Credit transfer feasibility and step-by-step process clarity
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report maps ECTS/US credit hours/Singapore modular credits to the home university, specifies required pre-approval forms, signatories, grade conversion, and provides a timeline and sample course-to-course mapping to secure graduation progress.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Cost and funding plan realism
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report provides itemized total cost-of-attendance per option (tuition/fees, housing, food, transport, insurance, visa, flights), identifies scholarships/fee waivers available to exchange/visiting undergrads from China with deadlines and eligibility, and includes a plan B if not awarded.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Visa, language, and compliance readiness
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines if the report lists the exact visa category per destination, required documents (financial proof amounts, insurance, biometrics), typical processing times/appointment backlogs, language score thresholds, and recommends buffer timelines aligned to next semester start.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Time-bound application and pre-departure timeline
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a backward, month-by-month (or week-by-week) checklist for applications, credit approvals, funding, visa, housing, and flights, including internal and external deadlines to fit the user’s next-semester schedule.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Research/practicum execution plan during exchange
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for concrete steps to secure lab placement or practice-oriented projects (professor outreach templates, when to contact, lab/fieldwork eligibility for undergrads, required trainings/ethics) and defines measurable outputs (poster, dataset, letter of recommendation).

actionability_practicality.criterion: Networking and career leverage plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report lists specific events/clubs/centers (e.g., sustainability labs, student societies, seminars) and provides tactics to build relationships (informational interviews, mentor mapping, LinkedIn/WeChat follow-ups) and convert them into resume-ready outcomes.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Cultural/classroom integration playbook
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines if the report offers a first-2-weeks integration checklist, classroom participation norms by region, group work conflict-resolution tactics, and practical tips for vegetarian/eco-friendly living so the user can function effectively from day one.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk management and contingency planning
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report provides concrete mitigations for visa delays, housing scams, health/safety issues, academic fallback (alternate courses/online options), and emergency contacts/insurance guidance consistent with a conservative risk profile.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Actionable resources, links, and templates
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses the inclusion of directly usable resources—official program/visa links, scholarship portals, budget calculators, pre-approval forms, email templates—to reduce friction and enable immediate execution.

depth_insight.criterion: Comparative, decision-oriented synthesis of country archetypes and fit-to-profile trade-offs
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report goes beyond country-by-country listing to synthesize program archetypes (e.g., US discussion/case-heavy, Europe ECTS/independent study, Japan zemi culture, Singapore industry-linked) and explicitly matches them to different student goals, constraints, and majors, with clear trade-offs (cost, language, academic rigor, internship access). This is core to producing actionable choice guidance.

depth_insight.criterion: Mechanism-level mapping from curriculum/teaching models to learning and research outcomes
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report explains how course formats and assessment norms (interdisciplinary studios, labs, case/participation grades, seminars/zemi, project-based learning) affect skill acquisition, research readiness, and workload strategies, rather than merely naming formats. Insight lies in linking pedagogy to outcomes and advising how to prepare and select courses accordingly.

depth_insight.criterion: Depth of credit transfer analysis and academic risk mitigation
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether the report details equivalency mechanisms (e.g., ECTS mapping, quarter/semester conversions), grade conversion impacts (pass/fail, GPA), major/minor requirement alignment, and timing pitfalls, and offers concrete mitigation (syllabus pre-approval, contingency course lists, accreditation checks). This protects degree progress and ensures academic value.

depth_insight.criterion: Visa, language, and compliance constraints insight with practical mitigation
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report surfaces non-obvious constraints (full-time load definitions, work authorization limits, English-taught course availability by country/field, proof-of-funds, ethics/IRB/GDPR/export-control issues) and provides timeline-aware mitigation strategies. Insight here reduces legal/administrative risk that can derail plans.

depth_insight.criterion: Financial depth: total cost structure, hidden costs, and funding strategy
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report goes beyond tuition/living to analyze hidden and location-sensitive costs (insurance, fees, deposits, transport, visas, housing market frictions), compares city/region differentials, and maps scholarship/aid landscapes (e.g., Erasmus+ credit mobility, JASSO, host fee waivers) with eligibility, competitiveness, timelines, and tactics to improve odds.

depth_insight.criterion: Value-realization pathway for academic and career outcomes with measurable outputs
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether the report explicitly links program features to research capability growth (lab access, co-authorship, methods training), networking (faculty, peers, industry), and cross-cultural competence, and proposes concrete KPIs and capture plans (letters of recommendation, conference abstracts, portfolios, GitHub repos, informational interviews). This demonstrates deep, outcome-focused insight.

depth_insight.criterion: High-yield adaptation and risk playbooks tailored to regional academic cultures
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report offers nuanced, region-specific tactics to integrate quickly (e.g., US office hours/participation norms; Europe independent study planning/ECTS workload; Japan senpai–kohai and zemi etiquette; Singapore industry collaborations), plus psychological adjustment and scenario-based risk responses (visa delays, course cancellations, health/security).

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and non-obvious, actionable heuristics
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards unique insights that materially improve outcomes, such as decision matrices by profile, pre-arranged micro-projects with host PIs, strategic use of pass/fail, leveraging short-term grants, pre-departure networking scripts, or compliance checklists. Originality indicates genuine expertise beyond generic travel/study tips.

logical_coherence.criterion: Macro-structure aligned to task stages (describe → compare/analyze → conclude/recommend)
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report is organized to first profile countries, then synthesize cross-country insights, then derive selection advice and preparation plans—avoiding jumps from description to advice without intermediate analysis.

logical_coherence.criterion: Consistent comparative schema across country sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether each country profile uses the same subheadings (e.g., curriculum/teaching model, credit transfer, culture/academia, living costs, visa/language, cost structure, scholarships) so comparisons are logically valid and not confounded by uneven coverage.

logical_coherence.criterion: Argument–evidence linkage and causal pathways for value claims
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether claims about research skill gains, networking, or cross-cultural competence are tied to specific program features (e.g., lab rotations, industry practicums) and supported by data/examples, making the reasoning from features to outcomes explicit.

logical_coherence.criterion: Premise-to-conclusion traceability for selection and preparation recommendations
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that selection criteria and preparation steps (e.g., language prep, course mapping) are explicitly derived from earlier analysis and stated assumptions (discipline, budget, time horizon), with clear if–then logic rather than unsupported prescriptions.

logical_coherence.criterion: Transitional logic and signposting between sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether transitions explain why the discussion moves from country profiles to comparative insights, then to value analysis and practical guidance, preventing logical gaps or topic jumps.

logical_coherence.criterion: Internal consistency of constructs and variables
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks for stable definitions and non-contradictory use of key terms (e.g., distinguishing tuition vs. living costs, credit transfer vs. recognition), ensuring arguments are built on consistent concepts.

logical_coherence.criterion: Risk–strategy alignment in the prescriptive section
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether risk management actions (e.g., health insurance, visa timing, contingency for credit non-transfer) correspond directly to risks identified from country/program characteristics, avoiding orphaned or generic advice.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structure and Navigability of Layout
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for a clear TOC, consistent heading hierarchy (e.g., by country and by dimension such as curriculum, credit, cost, visa), adequate white space, and consistent typography so readers can quickly find relevant sections without confusion.

clarity_readability.criterion: Cross-country Comparability and Consistency of Dimensions
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether each country uses the same subheadings, ordering, and labels (e.g., curriculum, credit transfer, culture, cost-of-living, visa/language, scholarships, tips) and whether comparison tables/matrices use uniform terms, enabling effortless side-by-side scanning.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity, Plainness, and Correctness
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether the text uses plain language, short sentences, active voice, and correct grammar/punctuation; avoids ambiguity and idioms that hinder non-native readers; and maintains a consistent, professional tone.

clarity_readability.criterion: Technical Terms and Acronyms: Definition and Consistent Use
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that specialized terms and acronyms (e.g., ECTS, J-1/DS-2019, Schengen, CEFR, GPA, TOEFL/IELTS) are defined at first use or listed in a glossary, used consistently, and, where needed, mapped to equivalents across regions to reduce comprehension barriers.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Visualizations (Tables, Charts, Timelines, Flowcharts)
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether visuals are well-labeled, have clear titles that state the takeaway, use readable axes/units/legends, are color-blind-friendly, and include notes on sources/assumptions; timelines for visas and cost breakdowns should be immediately understandable.

clarity_readability.criterion: Emphasis of Key Actions and Deadlines
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates use of callouts, checklists, and highlighted dates to surface application windows, visa lead times, required documents, and next steps, ensuring critical items are easy to spot and hard to miss.

clarity_readability.criterion: Information Chunking and Scannability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for short paragraphs, bullet lists for subpoints, and micro-summaries/TL;DRs at section starts or ends so readers can skim and grasp essentials before diving deeper.

clarity_readability.criterion: Normalization and Localization of Numbers, Units, and Dates
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses consistent currency display with conversion rate and date, clear units (e.g., monthly costs), and unambiguous date formats; such normalization prevents misreading when comparing countries.

task: I plan to participate in an overseas exchange or short-term visiting program in the coming semester to broaden my international perspective and enhance my academic capabilities. However, I currently lack a systematic understanding of how to select a suitable program and prepare comprehensively. Please help me outline the characteristics of exchange or visiting programs in different countries (such as the United States, Europe, Japan, Singapore, etc.), including curriculum design and teaching models (e.g., interdisciplinary courses, practice-oriented courses), credit transfer mechanisms, cultural and academic atmosphere, living costs, visa and language requirements, cost structure, and available scholarships or financial aid. In addition, please analyze the practical value of these programs for future academic development and career competitiveness, including their impact on research capability enhancement, networking opportunities, and cross-cultural communication skills. Finally, provide practical suggestions for adapting to studying and living abroad, including techniques for quickly integrating into local classrooms and culture, psychological adjustment, and risk management strategies, so that I can make the most of the exchange opportunity within a short time and maximize both academic and professional gains.

goal_alignment.criterion: Field-specific program and mentor matching (new media/public opinion)
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the content maps US/Europe/Japan/Singapore options specifically to communication/media schools, computational social science labs, and public opinion/analytics courses; identifies potential mentors/groups, typical methods (NLP, network analysis, experiments), and data-access/regulatory contexts. This directly serves Xu Yang’s specialization and his goal of research capability enhancement.

goal_alignment.criterion: Data-driven cross-country comparison and adjustable decision rubric
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for quantified matrices comparing countries/programs on curriculum model (interdisciplinary/practice), credit transfer ease, city-level living cost, visa lead time, language burden, scholarship likelihood, networking density; plus a scoring rubric with adjustable weights and instructions. This aligns with his data-oriented, tool-using decision style.

goal_alignment.criterion: Total cost-of-attendance and scholarship path tailored to Chinese exchange students
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the report provides per-country, city-specific budgets (tuition/fees under exchange vs visiting, housing, food, transport, insurance, visa, flights) and realistic funding sources (e.g., host exchange tuition waivers, JASSO/MEXT for Japan, DAAD-type short-term options, school-level travel grants, department awards), with success tips and ROI framing. This matches his frugality and low risk appetite.

goal_alignment.criterion: Next-semester operational feasibility plan (timeline, checklists, buffers)
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess presence of a T−12 to T−0 week plan covering test booking, document preparation, advisor approvals, host nomination forms, DS-2019/CoE/Student Pass/SCHENGEN steps as applicable from Beijing, housing search milestones, and time buffers. This meets the urgent ‘coming semester’ constraint and his efficiency mindset.

goal_alignment.criterion: Credit transfer mechanics and documentation to protect graduation
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether the content shows how to pre-approve credits (syllabus mapping), convert ECTS/US credits to home-university credits, handle grading conversions/pass–fail options, and archive evidence (syllabi, transcripts, assessments) to avoid graduation delays. This fits his rational risk control and need for certainty.

goal_alignment.criterion: Classroom and cultural integration tactics tailored to media/public opinion
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether it offers first-2-weeks integration playbooks for US/EU/JP/SG classrooms (participation norms, office hours, group work), scripts/templates for introducing China-related cases sensitively, and strategies that use his calm, analytical strengths to build presence. This targets his field’s discourse nuances and quick adaptation need.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk and ethics management aligned with low-risk appetite and research domain
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess coverage of health insurance selection, housing safety/scam avoidance, visa/status compliance, digital security, research ethics/IRB alignment across institutions, and data privacy (e.g., GDPR implications for public opinion/social media data). This reflects his conservative risk profile and the compliance demands of his research.

goal_alignment.criterion: Networking and output plan for academic and career leverage
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for a concrete plan to identify target labs/scholars/seminars, outreach email templates, conference/event calendars, and deliverables (working paper, dataset, portfolio case) within one semester; plus how to sustain ties post-visit. This converts the exchange into measurable gains in reputation and opportunity.

goal_alignment.criterion: Country-specific visa and language requirement granularity
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate clarity and accuracy of per-country visa pathways for short-term exchange (e.g., typical categories and steps) and language expectations (English tracks vs JLPT where relevant), including pointers to authoritative, up-to-date sources. This supports feasibility and reduces uncertainty.

goal_alignment.criterion: Concise, evidence-backed style with actionable tools
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report uses clear logic, citations to authoritative sources, and provides ready-to-use tools (checklists, timelines, budget sheets, decision matrices), matching Xu Yang’s tool proficiency and efficiency-driven habits.

content_alignment.criterion: Field-Specific Fit to New Media/Public Opinion and Computational Methods
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the report prioritizes programs, courses, and labs in computational social science, media analytics, digital journalism, platform governance, and policy-tech intersections, with concrete examples (e.g., social media analytics, NLP for public opinion, network analysis). This aligns content with Xu Yang’s specialization and avoids generic exchange advice.

content_alignment.criterion: Evidence- and Data-Backed Comparisons Across Regions
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether key comparisons (costs, research outputs, acceptance/selectivity, class sizes, dataset availability) are quantified and sourced from reputable references (e.g., cost-of-living indices, subject rankings, official visa timelines). This matches his data-driven decision style and desire for authoritative information.

content_alignment.criterion: Budget and Funding Specificity for a Frugal, Low-Risk Student
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the content breaks down total costs by city (rent, transport, food, insurance), clarifies exchange tuition waivers, and lists realistic scholarships/aid accessible to China-based exchange students (e.g., CSC/Chinese funding, host university bursaries), including visa work limits. This aligns with his conservative budget management.

content_alignment.criterion: Credit Transfer Mapping and One-Semester Timeline Fit
content_alignment.explanation: Checks for explicit credit conversion (US credits↔Chinese credits; ECTS mapping), grade requirements, caps, and term calendars aligned to the next semester, highlighting risks to graduation progress. This reflects his need to optimize within a short exchange period.

content_alignment.criterion: Regional Differentiation of Platform Ecosystems, Data Access, and Regulation
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the report distinguishes the US, Europe, Japan, and Singapore by their dominant platforms (e.g., X/Reddit/YouTube, LINE), legal context (GDPR/DSA, POFMA), and API/data access feasibility for public opinion research. This directly affects the research value of the exchange for his field.

content_alignment.criterion: Visa, Language, and Compliance Realism for China-Based Applicants
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the content covers visa categories and processing times from Beijing, realistic TOEFL/IELTS thresholds for exchange, Japanese daily-use expectations, and compliance constraints (work hours, insurance mandates) relevant to a Chinese student. This aligns with his risk-averse, rule-following context.

content_alignment.criterion: Career and Network Value Mapping to Media/Tech/Policy Ecosystems
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the analysis links programs to specific conferences (e.g., ICA, AoIR), think tanks, university labs, media organizations, and alumni networks that matter for communication/public opinion careers, including relevance to the China job market and PhD pathways.

content_alignment.criterion: Tech Tool and Lab Infrastructure Alignment (Python/NLP/Data Pipelines)
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether suggested courses/projects assume and build on Python/data analysis skills and whether host labs provide relevant infrastructure (APIs, compute clusters, datasets, secure data environments). This meets his technical profile and research workflow.

content_alignment.criterion: Cultural and Classroom Integration Tailored to a Chinese Graduate Student
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether integration advice addresses seminar participation norms, discussion etiquette, group project expectations, and high-/low-context differences specifically for US/Europe/Japan/Singapore, reflecting Xu Yang’s Beijing academic background and communication style.

content_alignment.criterion: Cross-Jurisdiction Ethics and Research Risk Considerations
content_alignment.explanation: Checks if the content covers IRB/ethics processes, data privacy, scraping/ToS risks, and topic-sensitivity differences across regions, allowing a conservative, compliant approach to public opinion research topics.

presentation_fit.criterion: Whether the overall structure and navigability align with the user's decision flow
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess if the report uses a clear hierarchy (e.g., by country→academic model→credits→costs→visa/scholarships→impact→adaptation), descriptive headings, numbered sections, and signposting/TOC so Xu Yang can jump directly to needed parts and trace steps logically.

presentation_fit.criterion: Whether cross-country comparisons are presented with scannable matrices and standardized fields
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate use of side-by-side tables with consistent row labels (curriculum, credits, costs, visa, language, scholarships) to let him quickly contrast options and copy to Excel for further analysis.

presentation_fit.criterion: Whether terminology usage is calibrated (Chinese core text with precise English acronyms and first-mention expansions)
presentation_fit.explanation: Check if key terms (e.g., ECTS, GPA, MOI, CPT/OPT, JASSO) are introduced with full names on first use and concise definitions, ensuring accessibility while preserving professional accuracy.

presentation_fit.criterion: Whether numeric information is normalized and clearly formatted
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess currency conversion and unit consistency (e.g., CNY equivalents in parentheses, per-month vs per-semester costs), date formats, ranges, and category breakdowns to support his budget-conscious, data-oriented reading.

presentation_fit.criterion: Whether the tone is analytical and evidence-signaling with transparent citations
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate if the language avoids marketing hype, uses neutral, precise phrasing, and provides source links/footnotes to official pages and recent policies, matching his trust in authoritative, verifiable information.

presentation_fit.criterion: Whether visual aids (tables, timelines, flowcharts) are appropriate and mobile-readable
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that visuals are chosen to clarify processes (e.g., credit transfer or visa steps), have legible fonts, adequate contrast, and responsive-friendly widths so he can read comfortably on phone or laptop.

presentation_fit.criterion: Whether layered presentation (executive summary, TL;DR, checklists) supports quick entry and deep dive
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess presence of a succinct overview with key comparisons, plus end-of-section checklists for preparation steps; this matches his time management habit and need for fast grasp plus detailed reference.

presentation_fit.criterion: Whether cognitive load is managed through chunking and consistent formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate short paragraphs, bullet lists, consistent styles for callouts (e.g., Tip/Risk/Policy), whitespace, and stable heading/bullet conventions to reduce fatigue and improve scanability.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Program fit decision framework tailored to new media/public opinion
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a weighted scoring rubric and shortlist method that operationalizes mentor/lab alignment, relevant course types (e.g., computational social science, data journalism, digital methods), calendar fit (one-semester/short-term), and city/country constraints, enabling Xu to rank options and choose quickly.

actionability_practicality.criterion: End-to-end timeline and critical path for next semester
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report gives a backward-planned schedule with specific milestones and time buffers for language tests, application materials, faculty approvals, credit pre-approval, scholarships, visa appointment/issuance, housing, and flight booking, so Xu can execute without missing deadlines.
