Include specific recommendations for platform development in terms of functional architecture, technology stack selection, and iterative processes. Additionally, propose effective marketing and user engagement strategies tailored to the behavioral characteristics of university students, and help me assess the feasibility of different business models (such as subscription-based, value-added services, or campus partnerships) to ensure both short-term launch readiness and long-term scalability. Please also provide forward-looking advice on resource allocation, potential risks, and financing channels.

goal_alignment.criterion: Founder-strength leverage into product differentiation and roadmap
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan explicitly turns Li’s AI/recommendation expertise into core value (e.g., adaptive curricula, AI TA, personalized practice scheduling, intelligent recommendation of learning paths) and sequences roadmap items to exploit his competitive edge first for fast, defensible MVP traction.

goal_alignment.criterion: China-context technical architecture and stack feasibility with cost control
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the platform architecture and stack choices align with Li’s skills (Python/PyTorch, MLOps), local providers (AliCloud, Qwen/GLM/Baichuan), latency/cost trade-offs (token costs, caching, batching), and compliance constraints (ICP filing, PIPL data governance), ensuring scalable yet budget-conscious execution.

goal_alignment.criterion: Student-centric market research design using real campus channels
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if the research plan targets Chinese university students via effective channels (WeChat/QQ groups, Bilibili, campus associations, class reps), segments use cases (STEM vs non-STEM, exam prep, competitions, internships), and defines hypothesis-driven instruments with measurable decision criteria (sample sizes, screeners, JTBD, willingness-to-pay).

goal_alignment.criterion: Marketing and engagement strategies tailored to student behavior and founder’s media assets
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the go-to-market leverages Bilibili/Zhihu content, student ambassador programs, WeChat mini-programs, campus KOLs, and exam-calendar cadences; and whether it integrates Li’s existing creator footprint to lower CAC and build trust among students.

goal_alignment.criterion: Business model feasibility with China-specific campus partnerships and unit economics
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the report compares subscription, freemium/value-added, and campus/department partnerships with concrete assumptions (pricing bands for students, conversion funnels, CAC/LTV payback, pilot-to-contract pathways) and addresses procurement realities of Chinese universities.

goal_alignment.criterion: Operationalization of 'learning efficiency' and experimental iteration plan
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if the plan defines precise metrics (time-to-mastery, quiz accuracy, spaced-repetition retention, task completion time), instrumentation, A/B testing, and OKRs to rapidly validate value; and proposes sprint-based iteration that a small team can execute.

goal_alignment.criterion: Resource and timeline planning aligned with founder constraints and build-vs-buy
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan proposes a lean team composition, prioritizes build-vs-buy for non-core components (e.g., LMS, payment, analytics), and maps a realistic timeline that fits Li’s night productivity and budget, ensuring short-term launch readiness.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk, compliance, and academic integrity safeguards specific to AI EdTech in China
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan anticipates and mitigates key risks: AI hallucinations, model cost volatility, academic misconduct policies, content moderation, and regulatory compliance (PIPL, data residency, ICP), with concrete controls (guardrails, proctoring features, honor codes, audit logs).

goal_alignment.criterion: Financing and ecosystem fit for Hangzhou/Zhejiang AI-EdTech
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for tailored financing routes (local government grants, university incubators at ZJU/HUST, Alibaba Cloud/industry accelerators, AI/EdTech angels), stage-appropriate milestones, and a pitch narrative connecting measurable learning efficiency gains with social impact and scalability.

goal_alignment.criterion: Structured, data-driven deliverables and ready-to-use templates
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the content is organized with clear frameworks (Lean Canvas, PRD, RICE prioritization, KPI dashboards), checklists, and templates that match Li’s analytical style and enable immediate execution without reformatting.

content_alignment.criterion: Engineer-grade Technical Specificity in Platform and AI Architecture
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the content dives into architecture choices and trade-offs that match an algorithm engineer’s expectations: e.g., LLM orchestration (RAG, tool-use), recommendation system design for personalized learning (embedding choice, ranking, cold-start strategies), data schemas/pipelines, evaluation metrics (MRR/NDCG for recs, task success/latency for LLMs), inference cost/latency optimization (quantization, caching), vector stores, and MLOps (CI/CD for models, offline/online evaluation).

content_alignment.criterion: Rigor of Market Research Design and Quantitative Depth
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan uses multi-method, statistically grounded research aligned to a data-driven decision style: segmentation (major/grade/regions), JTBD/interviews, survey design with power analysis, conjoint or pricing sensitivity tests, diary studies for learning workflows, funnel analytics, A/B testing protocols, and instrumentation plans to quantify learning efficiency and product-market fit.

content_alignment.criterion: Localization to Chinese University Student Behaviors and Calendars
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether student insights and go-to-market are tailored to China: channels (Bilibili, WeChat Official Accounts, Xiaohongshu, QQ campus groups, Douyin), campus orgs/CS clubs, academic cycles (CET-4/6, postgraduate entrance exams, internship recruitment seasons), device usage (mobile-first), study pain points, and student price sensitivity and purchasing paths.

content_alignment.criterion: Leverage of Founder’s Technical Authority and Existing Channels
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the strategy explicitly exploits Li Zhuo’s strengths and networks: founder-led technical content on Bilibili/Zhihu, open-source toolkits on GitHub to drive credibility and inbound leads, collaborations with university labs/tech clubs, and showcasing recommender/AI expertise as a differentiator.

content_alignment.criterion: Integration of Evidence-based Learning Science for Efficiency
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content grounds ‘learning efficiency’ features in proven pedagogy: retrieval practice, spaced repetition, interleaving, formative assessment, metacognitive prompts, and learning analytics; and maps these to product features and measurable outcomes (e.g., time-to-mastery, retention uplift). This matches his science/logic bias and the core goal.

content_alignment.criterion: Cost- and Resource-fit Technology Stack and Iteration Plan
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether stack and tools reflect a cost-effective, early-stage setup aligned with his background: Python/PyTorch or TensorFlow, FastAPI, lightweight microservices, PostHog/Mixpanel analytics, Supabase/PlanetScale, vector DB selection, cloud credits (e.g., Alibaba Cloud), GPU rental strategies, and phased MVP iterations with prioritization frameworks (RICE/ICE) and clear trade-offs.

content_alignment.criterion: China-specific Regulatory, Data, and Academic Integrity Considerations
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the content includes local compliance/risks: ICP filing, PIPL data residency/consent, security reviews, content moderation, anti-cheating safeguards for AI tools in academic contexts, and governance for model outputs (hallucination mitigation, logs, audits).

content_alignment.criterion: Business Model Fit to Student Market and Founder’s Risk Profile
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the business model analysis reflects Chinese university affordability and campus realities, and offers staged, low-risk pathways: freemium + student-tier subscriptions, value-added services (certificates, mentoring), campus/B2B2U partnerships, enterprise sponsorships, and clear unit economics (CAC/LTV/payback) suited to a conservative risk appetite.

content_alignment.criterion: Localization of Funding and Support Ecosystem Mapping
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether financing/resource channels are tailored to Hangzhou/Zhejiang and a CS founder: local government grants, university incubators, Alibaba Cloud credits/entrepreneur programs, angel networks, and China-focused accelerators, with criteria and timelines relevant to the next 12 months.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structural clarity and navigability (executive summary, TOC, numbered hierarchy, clear section signposting, appendices)
presentation_fit.explanation: For a cross-domain startup plan, a logical, easily navigable structure lets a technical reader jump between market research, architecture, and business model sections quickly. Executive summary/TL;DR, numbered headings, and appendices reduce cognitive load and fit his scanning habits.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology calibration across domains (assume AI/product fluency; succinctly define business/finance/legal terms on first use)
presentation_fit.explanation: As an AI engineer, he’s comfortable with technical jargon but may need concise definitions for BD/finance terms (e.g., CAC/LTV, cohort, SAFEs). First-use expansions and brief parenthetical notes prevent interruptions to reading flow.

presentation_fit.criterion: Objective, concise, evidence-signaling tone (no hype; data-oriented language with inline citations/links)
presentation_fit.explanation: A rational, data-driven reader prefers precise, non-promotional prose and quick access to sources. Clear citation formatting supports trust and efficient verification without drifting into narrative fluff.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visualization and schematics fit (architecture diagrams, timelines/roadmaps, funnels, and comparison tables; text-based/ASCII alternatives with clear labels when images aren’t available)
presentation_fit.explanation: Engineering-friendly visuals accelerate understanding of platform architecture, iteration plans, and model comparisons. When rich media is constrained, well-structured text diagrams and tables keep the presentation effective.

presentation_fit.criterion: Decision-oriented presentation devices (section-end key takeaways, decision prompts, and checklists)
presentation_fit.explanation: Section summaries with bullet points and decision prompts support fast scanning at night and help him identify what to act on next without wading through dense paragraphs.

presentation_fit.criterion: Localization and context labeling (RMB currency, China campus calendar cues, local platform names; bilingual labels where helpful)
presentation_fit.explanation: Consistent use of RMB, China-specific academic cycles, and accurate naming of local channels (e.g., Bilibili, Xiaohongshu) reduces ambiguity. Brief bilingual labels prevent confusion between Chinese and English technical/business terms.

presentation_fit.criterion: Readability for expert audience (sentence economy, bullet lists, minimal over-explanation of AI basics; code/pseudocode formatted when included)
presentation_fit.explanation: Crisp, dense information delivery respects his expertise and time. Bullets and properly formatted snippets improve scan-ability without diluting technical rigor.

presentation_fit.criterion: Consistency and formatting hygiene (acronyms expanded on first use, consistent units/metrics, versioning/date stamps, coherent typography)
presentation_fit.explanation: Clean, consistent formatting reduces friction and errors when referencing sections over time (e.g., iteration versions) and aligns with expectations from technical documentation standards.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Phased 12-month execution roadmap with milestones and dependencies
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report provides a realistic, month-by-month plan from research to MVP, beta, and launch, with deliverables, task owners (even if solo), dependencies, and clear ‘definition of done’ for each phase so Li can act week by week.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Market research design with concrete methods, channels, and decision thresholds
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report specifies target segments, sampling frames (e.g., ZJU/HUST faculties, QQ/WeChat groups), sample sizes, survey/interview guides, testing tasks, analysis methods (JTBD), timelines, and go/no-go thresholds to validate needs before building.

actionability_practicality.criterion: MVP scope and functional architecture feasibility for a solo/lean engineer
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the proposed MVP focuses on the minimum features that improve learning efficiency, includes a clear module-level architecture, and can be built in 8–12 weeks by Li (with explicit buy-vs-build choices and data/privacy constraints).

actionability_practicality.criterion: Technology stack and infrastructure plan with cost-performance and scalability path (China context)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for concrete stack choices (e.g., Python/FastAPI, React, vector DB, LLM provider), deployment on Aliyun/Tencent, CI/CD/MLOps setup, estimated monthly costs under expected user loads, and a plan to scale or switch providers without major rewrites.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Iteration and measurement system (post-MVP) with instrumentation and experiment cadence
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for a defined North Star metric (e.g., weekly active learners or learning time saved), OMTMs (activation, retention), analytics tooling, A/B testing process, and weekly/biweekly review rituals so Li can learn and adapt quickly.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Student-targeted GTM playbooks with executable channel tactics and budgets
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report details channel-specific actions (campus ambassadors, WeChat/QQ groups, Bilibili content plan, Xiaohongshu seeding), messaging, cadences, referral mechanics, event scripts, expected CAC ranges, and a 90-day launch plan.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Business model feasibility with unit economics and scenario analysis
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies pricing hypotheses (subscription, value-added, campus partnerships), conversion funnel assumptions, cohort retention targets, take rates, LTV/CAC, breakeven volume, and sensitivity scenarios to guide monetization choices and pivots.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Resource and budget allocation aligned to founder time and outsourcing
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the plan specifies founder time blocks (nights/weekends), what to outsource/contract (e.g., UI/UX), a monthly burn and cash runway, and concrete trade-offs to stay within a realistic budget before external funding.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk and compliance mitigation specific to China edtech and AI
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report identifies key risks (model cost/latency, hallucinations, competition, seasonality) and compliance items (PIPL, ICP, content moderation, academic integrity) with practical mitigations, costs, and contingency triggers.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Financing pathway tied to traction milestones and required artifacts
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report outlines bootstrapping, grants/incubators (university/local), angels/seed, with timing, target milestones (users/revenue), and deliverables (deck, demo, metrics) so Li can raise at the right time with the right proof.

depth_insight.criterion: User Needs Segmentation & JTBD Insightfulness
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report goes beyond generic 'students' to develop evidence-backed personas and jobs-to-be-done (by major, year, course load, study style, budget, language/international status), identifies high-intensity pain points, constraints (academic integrity policies, privacy), and willingness-to-pay tiers that actually inform product and pricing decisions.

depth_insight.criterion: Market Research Design Quality & Actionability
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the rigor and practicality of the multi-method research plan (e.g., stratified sampling across campuses/majors, diary studies, cohort shadowing, conjoint/Gabor-Granger/Van Westendorp for WTP, baseline learning-efficiency measurement), including bias control and privacy/consent considerations, to produce decision-grade insights.

depth_insight.criterion: Insight-to-Product Mapping & Prioritized Feature Rationale
depth_insight.explanation: Judges how well the report translates research findings into a prioritized feature set and learning experience design (e.g., adaptive practice, AI note synthesis, citation-safe writing support) with explicit rationale (e.g., Kano/RICE/impact vs. effort) tied to expected learning-efficiency outcomes.

depth_insight.criterion: Technical Architecture Tradeoff Insight (AI-specific)
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses depth in selecting and justifying the AI/tech stack (foundation model choice, RAG vs fine-tuning, prompt caching, cost/latency/privacy tradeoffs, LTI/SSO with LMS, model fallback, data governance and academic-integrity safeguards), showing awareness of constraints unique to AI in education.

depth_insight.criterion: Experimentation Strategy for Learning Efficiency Uplift
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report proposes credible methods to measure and causally attribute learning-efficiency gains (e.g., time-to-mastery, retention, GPA proxies, pre/post or A/B tests by course cohort, power analysis, ethics), ensuring insights go beyond vanity usage metrics.

depth_insight.criterion: Business Model Viability & Unit Economics Depth
depth_insight.explanation: Judges the realism and analytical depth of revenue model assessment (subscription vs freemium, value-added services, campus partnerships/B2B2C), including LTV/CAC, LLM cost sensitivity, payment frictions for students, campus procurement cycles, and breakeven scenarios.

depth_insight.criterion: Student-Behavior-Tailored GTM & Retention Mechanics
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether marketing and engagement strategies reflect student behavioral insights (peer influence, semester cycles, ambassadors, study communities, referrals, micro-influencers, gamified streaks), with hypotheses about activation, habit formation, and network effects grounded in student life.

depth_insight.criterion: Competitive Landscape Analysis & Moat Hypothesis
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report maps incumbents and substitutes (e.g., LMS, note/quiz tools, general-purpose LLM apps) and articulates defensible differentiation (e.g., proprietary outcomes data, LTI integrations, content partnerships, pedagogy, data network effects), not just a feature list.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Surface Coverage & Mitigation Originality
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses the identification of high-impact risks (academic integrity bans, hallucinations, model/provider lock-in, content/IP, regulatory/privacy, cost volatility) and the creativity/practicality of mitigations (guardrails, citation enforcement, model abstraction layers, policy alignment).

depth_insight.criterion: Resource Allocation & Financing Strategy Foresight
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates forward-looking insight on phased resourcing (team composition, content vs model spend, compute forecasting), and financing fit (grants, university funds, edu accelerators, cloud credits, revenue-based financing) aligned to milestones and risk reduction.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report builds a hierarchical logic from vision and measurable objectives to hypotheses, research plan, product roadmap, GTM, business model, KPIs, and stage-gated timeline; ensures each layer logically derives from the prior and defines feedback loops for iteration.

logical_coherence.criterion: Hypothesis-Driven Market Research and Decision Rules
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether target-need hypotheses are explicit, operationalized (metrics, segments, willingness-to-pay), and mapped to specific methods/samples; includes predefined decision rules (e.g., thresholds to pivot features/pricing) so findings logically drive choices.

Evaluates whether each major recommendation (feature set, tech stack, pricing, campus partnerships, engagement tactics) explicitly references supporting findings, benchmarks, or experiments, avoiding assertions unlinked to evidence.

Checks that outputs of earlier sections explicitly inform later ones (e.g., segment prioritization feeding feature prioritization and marketing channels; WTP informing pricing; research insights shaping MVP scope), with dependencies and rationale clearly stated.

Verifies that calculations and numeric logic are consistent across sections (e.g., TAM/SAM/SOM align with target segments; CAC/LTV coherent with pricing and channels; hiring/budget match roadmap scope and timeline; scenario logic is consistent).

logical_coherence.criterion: Business Model Comparison and Selection Logic
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether alternative models (subscription, value-added, campus partnerships) are evaluated using comparable criteria and assumptions, with transparent trade-offs and a reasoned selection tied to objectives and constraints.

Evaluates whether identified risks are logically mapped to specific mitigations and contingencies with clear triggers/thresholds and ownership, showing a coherent path from risk recognition to action.

logical_coherence.criterion: Section Transitions and Signposting of Logical Links
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that transitions explicitly state how the preceding section informs the next, include brief recaps or decision summaries, and avoid logical jumps between market research, product, GTM, financials, and risks.

clarity_readability.criterion: Sectioning and Navigability Aligned to Task Requirements
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report’s structure mirrors the requested deliverables (market research, business plan framework, platform development, marketing/user engagement, business models, resources/risks/financing) with a clear TOC, consistent H1–H3 headings, numbered steps, page numbers, and cross-references/links so readers can locate information quickly.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity and Precision of Instructional Language
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether sentences are concise and unambiguous, use active voice and directive verbs, and specify measurable parameters (e.g., sample sizes, timelines, KPIs). Clear, prescriptive phrasing minimizes misinterpretation and makes execution straightforward.

clarity_readability.criterion: Correct and Accessible Use of Technical Terms and Acronyms
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether AI and business terms (e.g., RAG, embeddings, orchestration, CAC, LTV, churn) are spelled out on first use, defined succinctly, used consistently, and compiled in a short glossary. This reduces cognitive load for mixed technical/nontechnical readers.

clarity_readability.criterion: Design and Integration Quality of Diagrams, Tables, and Visuals
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that visuals (architecture diagrams, user journey maps, funnels, Gantt timelines, unit economics tables, risk matrices) are legible, properly labeled (titles, legends, units), minimally cluttered, and explicitly referenced/explained in the text with short captions that state the takeaway.

clarity_readability.criterion: Emphasis of Key Points and Scannability
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses the presence and quality of an executive summary, per-section key takeaways, bullet lists, callouts, and adequate white space/paragraphing. Good emphasis patterns enable quick skimming and retention of core instructions and priorities.

clarity_readability.criterion: Practicality and Usability of Templates and Checklists
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether provided artifacts (survey/interview guides, business plan outline, platform spec templates, iteration checklists, launch runbook) are clearly formatted with fields, examples, and instructions so they can be used immediately without reinterpretation.

task: I plan to launch an online AI education startup targeting university students within the next year, with the core goal of significantly improving their learning efficiency through innovative courses and AI tools. However, I still need a systematic plan to ensure the project can progress from concept to execution. Please provide me with an actionable overall strategy that covers how to design and conduct multi-dimensional market research to accurately identify target needs, along with a ready-to-use business plan framework. Include specific recommendations for platform development in terms of functional architecture, technology stack selection, and iterative processes. Additionally, propose effective marketing and user engagement strategies tailored to the behavioral characteristics of university students, and help me assess the feasibility of different business models (such as subscription-based, value-added services, or campus partnerships) to ensure both short-term launch readiness and long-term scalability. Please also provide forward-looking advice on resource allocation, potential risks, and financing channels.

goal_alignment.criterion: 12-month, milestone-based execution roadmap for a lean, technical founding team
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the article converts strategy into a quarter-by-quarter plan (MVP scope, pilot universities, metric gates, hiring sequence) that respects Zhang Yu’s weekday focus and morning decision windows, enabling disciplined progress from concept to launch within one year.

goal_alignment.criterion: China-adapted technical stack and architecture specificity
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether platform guidance specifies concrete functional modules (RAG, LLM orchestration, content pipeline, analytics), tech stack options (e.g., React/Vue, Node/FastAPI, Postgres, Milvus), domestic LLM providers (Qwen/Baichuan/GLM), cloud choices (Alibaba/Tencent), and build-vs-buy trade-offs with cost/compliance in China.

goal_alignment.criterion: Student-behavior-tailored product and GTM design
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan aligns with Chinese university behaviors and seasonality: Bilibili/WeChat/QQ channels, campus clubs/ambassador programs, internship/exam cycles, pricing sensitivity, after-class usage windows, gamification/streaks, and mini-program/mobile-first delivery.

goal_alignment.criterion: Operational definition and measurement of 'learning efficiency'
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for a rigorous metric framework (e.g., time-to-mastery, quiz uplift, task-time reduction, retention, cohort L28) with instrumentation, A/B testing, and dashboards that match Zhang Yu’s data-driven style and prove value for students and partners.

goal_alignment.criterion: Multi-dimensional market research plan leveraging founder networks
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the research design covers qualitative (interviews, campus ethnography), quantitative (surveys, conjoint), and in-product telemetry; segmentation (major/year/tier of school); and practical recruitment via Tsinghua/alumni, incubators, and campus organizations.

goal_alignment.criterion: Business model feasibility with China-specific unit economics
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the article compares subscription, freemium + value-added (certificates, mentoring), and campus partnerships/B2B2C using realistic student price points, conversion funnels, CAC/LTV, and institutional revenue-sharing typical in China’s higher-ed context.

goal_alignment.criterion: Compliance, data privacy, and academic integrity safeguards
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for concrete guidance on ICP filing, PIPL, 等保2.0, payments (WeChat/Alipay), content moderation, logging/governance for LLMs, and policies/features that deter cheating/plagiarism and enable instructor trust—crucial for university adoption.

goal_alignment.criterion: Resource allocation and build-vs-buy decision logic
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the plan proposes a realistic budget and team plan (core hires, contractors), GPU vs cloud credits, third-party APIs vs self-hosted models, and prioritization that fits Zhang Yu’s moderate risk appetite and need for short-term launch with long-term scalability.

goal_alignment.criterion: Founder-friendly deliverables and templates
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for ready-to-use artifacts (business plan outline, PRD, architecture diagrams, market research protocols, experiment plans, campus ambassador playbook) that can be dropped into Notion/GitHub to accelerate team execution.

goal_alignment.criterion: Use of China-local cases, benchmarks, and channels
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether examples and benchmarks reference local players (e.g., XuetangX, CSDN Academy, Youdao) and channels (Rain Classroom/Chaoxing integrations, Bilibili) to enhance contextual resonance and decision relevance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Structured, data-first communication and decision frameworks
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the write-up uses clear frameworks (decision trees, scorecards, trade-off matrices), avoids fluff, and matches a Tsinghua-trained engineer’s expectation for rigor and reproducibility.

goal_alignment.criterion: Financing and partnership roadmap aligned to founder networks
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for actionable channels Zhang Yu can access (alumni funds, Zhongguancun/Haidian grants, cloud credits, corporate sponsors) and a BD plan leveraging his travel to Shenzhen/Hangzhou and incubator ties for pilots and partnerships.

content_alignment.criterion: China university student behavior and channel specificity
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content selects student insights and marketing/user engagement tactics that reflect Chinese campus realities (e.g., segmentation by 985/211 vs. non-key universities, major/grade cohorts; channels like Bilibili, WeChat Official Accounts and Mini Programs, QQ groups, Xiaohongshu, Douyin; campus ambassadors, student unions). This matches the target audience and Zhang Yu’s Beijing context, avoiding generic Western-centric advice.

content_alignment.criterion: China-specific regulatory and data-compliance embedding
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether platform design and GTM content incorporate Chinese compliance requirements (PIPL, Data Security Law, CAC algorithm filing, ICP/备案, education content regulation) and model/provider compliance considerations. For an AI founder operating in China, including this depth is essential for realistic planning and strongly signals contextual alignment.

content_alignment.criterion: Advanced AI architecture and stack depth aligned to the China ecosystem
content_alignment.explanation: Check that the report proposes technically credible, implementation-aware architecture choices (e.g., RAG tuned for Chinese, tokenization/segmentation issues, Milvus/FAISS, Qwen/GLM/ERNIE availability, on-prem vs. Aliyun/Tencent Cloud, serving with vLLM/Triton, observability/MLOps) with trade-off reasoning. This matches Zhang Yu’s expert background and need for depth beyond surface-level tool lists.

content_alignment.criterion: Metrics rigor for learning efficacy and product growth
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content selects quantitative frameworks that Zhang Yu cares about (learning efficacy measures like pre/post tests, time-on-task reduction, mastery curves; product metrics like activation, DAU/WAU/MAU, retention cohorts; growth economics like CAC/LTV, payback; A/B testing design). This aligns with his data-driven decision style and research orientation.

content_alignment.criterion: Business model feasibility tailored to China higher-ed context
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the business model analysis reflects local realities (student pricing bands, WeChat/Alipay payments, B2B2C campus licensing cycles, partnerships with faculties/IT centers, value-added services such as verified certificates with local platforms, corporate sponsorship/internship pipelines). This ensures the content matches Zhang Yu’s market and investor mindset.

content_alignment.criterion: Competitive landscape relevance to Chinese AI-edtech
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether competitors and benchmarks include current China-focused players (e.g., Youdao/NetEase offerings, Tencent Classroom, Chinese LLM study assistants like Kimi/Doubao/GLM-based tools, MOOC platforms such as icourse163/中国大学MOOC, Bilibili learning channels), rather than generic Western examples. This validates market research content selection for his context.

content_alignment.criterion: Alignment with founder’s operating toolchain and workflow
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan references GitHub/Notion/Slack for internal execution (e.g., monorepo structure, CI/CD, issue templates, Notion OKR/BP templates, Slack + GitHub automation) and bridges to student-facing integrations common in China (e.g., WeChat Mini Programs). This respects Zhang Yu’s daily stack and increases content resonance.

content_alignment.criterion: Research-grounded and evidence-based content selection
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether recommendations reference learning science/AIED findings (retrieval practice, spaced repetition, mastery learning, RAG/LLM tutoring efficacy) and relevant case studies or benchmarks. As a Tsinghua-trained, publication-experienced founder, Zhang Yu expects research-backed content rather than opinion.

content_alignment.criterion: Local resource and financing channel alignment
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether resource allocation and financing advice includes Beijing/Zhongguancun incubators, municipal/MIIT/MOE grants, university-industry programs, and startup credits from Alibaba/Tencent Cloud; and leverages networks in Shenzhen/Hangzhou. This matches his geography and partnership habits, improving contextual relevance.

presentation_fit.criterion: Hierarchical structure and modular organization aligned to the task scope
presentation_fit.explanation: Check for a clear TOC, executive summary, and numbered sections that map 1:1 to the user's requested modules (market research, business plan, platform dev, marketing/engagement, business models, resource/risk/financing), with consistent subheadings and cross-references for easy navigation.

presentation_fit.criterion: Analytical, concise, non-hype language tone
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether the report uses precise, objective language, strong verbs, and avoids vague buzzwords or salesy tone—matching a technical founder's preference for clarity and efficiency.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology accuracy and depth calibration
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess correct usage of AI, product, and business terms (e.g., LLM, RICE, LTV/CAC), with acronyms expanded on first use and brief parenthetical definitions for less common terms to support rapid understanding without oversimplification.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use of visual frameworks and text-friendly representations
presentation_fit.explanation: Check inclusion of architecture sketches, comparison tables, funnels, scoring matrices, and roadmap timelines rendered in tables/ASCII/monospace so they are copyable into Notion/Slack and legible without images.

presentation_fit.criterion: Scannability and navigation aids
presentation_fit.explanation: Verify presence of TL;DR/key takeaways, numbered steps, bullet lists, section headers with consistent numbering, and brief section intros/outros that summarize 'So what' and 'Next steps' to support fast reading and team briefings.

presentation_fit.criterion: Evidence transparency and reference formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether data points include inline citations/links, date stamps, markets/regions, and clearly labeled assumptions or uncertainty—presented in a consistent citation/link style for credibility and quick source checks.

presentation_fit.criterion: Collaboration-readiness for Notion/Slack/GitHub
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report offers copy-paste-ready templates (checklists, issue templates, OKR/KPI tables), code/command blocks in monospace, and clear section anchors to facilitate sharing and collaboration in the user's daily tools.

presentation_fit.criterion: Localization and bilingual term mapping
presentation_fit.explanation: Check whether key terms and metrics include EN/zh mappings where helpful, RMB currency notation, and consistent date/number formats to minimize ambiguity for China-based stakeholders while preserving technical precision.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Phased 12-month execution roadmap with milestones, deliverables, and owners
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report lays out a realistic quarter-by-quarter (or sprint-level) plan with critical path, dependencies, stage gates (e.g., MVP readiness, PMF signal, monetization pilot), and named accountable roles so work can start immediately and progress can be tracked.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Operable market research plan linked to decisions
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if research is fully executable: clear hypotheses, target segments, sampling frames on campus, channel access plans, instrument drafts (surveys/interview guides/usability scripts), sample sizes, incentives, timelines, and predefined decision thresholds that map to product features, pricing, and GTM choices.

actionability_practicality.criterion: MVP scope, prioritization, and acceptance criteria
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report defines a minimal, testable product that delivers measurable learning-efficiency value (must-haves vs. nice-to-haves), includes acceptance criteria, de-scoping rules, and a first sprint backlog so engineering can begin without ambiguity.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Technical feasibility and stack justification
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for a concrete functional architecture and stack choice (build vs. buy for LLMs, backend/frontend, data, infra) justified by latency, cost, scalability, security/privacy, maintainability, and local compliance considerations, aligned with the team’s skills and vendor availability.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Iteration and experimentation operating cadence
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether there is a defined build–measure–learn loop with weekly/biweekly sprint rituals, experiment design templates, hypothesis backlogs, and release/feedback cadences to systematically reduce uncertainty and accelerate PMF discovery.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Resource allocation and budget realism (incl. runway & financing milestones)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report provides a headcount plan (roles, hire vs. outsource timing), costed budgets (engineering, model tokens/GPU, cloud, content, marketing), cash-flow/runway projections, and fundraising milestones tied to traction targets, enabling credible execution within constraints.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Business model feasibility and unit economics
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether subscription, value-added, and campus partnership models are analyzed with concrete pricing, LTV/CAC, payback, gross margin structure, capacity constraints, and sensitivity/scenario analyses to support go/no-go and sequencing decisions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: GTM execution plan for university students
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines whether the report specifies channel playbooks tailored to students (e.g., campus ambassadors, societies, WeChat groups, Bilibili/Xiaohongshu content, campus events), funnel metrics (CTR, CVR, retention), test budgets, calendar, and partner outreach steps.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk, compliance, and contingency planning
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for a risk register covering technical (model reliability/cost), academic integrity policies, data privacy (e.g., PIPL), campus access/approvals, content moderation, and vendor lock-in, each with early-warning indicators, mitigations, and fallback options.

actionability_practicality.criterion: KPI tree and analytics/instrumentation plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report defines leading/lagging metrics tied to the core goal (e.g., learning efficiency proxies like active learning minutes, quiz gains; plus DAU/WAU, activation, retention, ARPU), specifies analytics stack and event schema, and sets experiment success thresholds.

depth_insight.criterion: Depth of Problem–Solution Fit and Student Behavioral Insights
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report segments university students meaningfully (e.g., majors, study workflows, academic year), articulates jobs-to-be-done (e.g., note synthesis, exam prep, coding/debugging), quantifies pains/benefits (hours saved, grade lift, cognitive load), and integrates behavioral drivers (price sensitivity, procrastination, social proof) to craft a differentiated value proposition.

depth_insight.criterion: Rigor and Actionability of Multi-dimensional Market Research Design
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the research plan goes beyond surface surveys to include mixed methods (diary studies, campus intercepts, telemetry, cohort interviews), clear hypotheses, sampling frames (by departments/clubs), instrument design, bottom-up TAM/SAM/SOM sizing, and explicit decision linkages (how findings will drive product scope, pricing, GTM).

depth_insight.criterion: Validity in Measuring Learning Efficiency Outcomes
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the strategy proposes rigorous outcome validation (e.g., A/B tests, quasi-experiments/RCTs, pre-post designs) with metrics such as time-to-mastery, grade improvement, retention of knowledge, and addresses confounders, ethics, and instrumentation—demonstrating insight into proving the core promise.

depth_insight.criterion: Competitive Landscape and White-space/Differentiation Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report maps direct and indirect competitors (LMS/LTI tools, LLM apps, study platforms), identifies substitution threats, adoption constraints in academia, and articulates a wedge strategy and defensibility (data moats, partnerships, proprietary content/tool synergy) rather than generic positioning.

depth_insight.criterion: Product/Platform Functional Architecture Grounded in User Workflows and AI Affordances
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the proposed feature set and architecture align tightly with target workflows (e.g., syllabus ingestion, automated quiz generation, code feedback, citation-aware assistance), distinguishes must-haves vs differentiators, anticipates content–tool interplay, and enables outcome measurement and LMS/LTI integrations.

depth_insight.criterion: Technology Stack Justification and Data/Privacy/MLOps Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether stack choices (LLM provider vs open-source, retrieval/vector DB, orchestration, observability) are justified via constraints (cost, latency, safety, FERPA/GDPR), with thoughtful plans for prompt/retrieval, safety/citations, model monitoring, inference cost control, and data governance.

depth_insight.criterion: Depth of Iteration and Experimentation Strategy
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether MVP scope and backlog prioritization are tied to falsifiable hypotheses and expected learning value, with a disciplined experimentation cadence (A/Bs, usability tests), clear success metrics (activation, cohort retention tied to learning outcomes), and feedback loops specific to student users.

depth_insight.criterion: Business Model Feasibility, Unit Economics, and Scalability Analysis
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates analytical comparison across subscription, freemium/value-added, and campus partnerships (B2B2C), including LTV/CAC drivers, seasonality sensitivity, pricing/packaging experiments, channel-specific CAC, cash flow timelines, and scalability paths (departmental licensing, SDK/LTI distribution).

depth_insight.criterion: Student-tailored Go-to-Market and Engagement Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether GTM leverages student-specific channels and behaviors (campus ambassadors, clubs, Discord/Reddit, TikTok/IG), includes credible referral and community mechanics, aligns to academic calendars, and defines evidence plans for growth (k-factor, activation/retention metrics) beyond generic tactics.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Landscape Comprehensiveness and Mitigation Creativity
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates anticipation of academic integrity policies, hallucinations, copyright/content liability, dependency on LLM APIs, seasonality/churn, and proposes concrete mitigations (citation/attribution, guardrails, compliance modes, offline/backup models, policy-aligned use cases) with triggers and contingencies.

depth_insight.criterion: Resource Allocation and Financing Pathway Foresight
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether resource phasing (team composition, content vs engineering), budget allocation (cloud/inference costs, content production, GTM), runway math tied to milestones and risks, and financing channels (grants/accelerators/strategic partners) are proposed with insight into de-risking the journey.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a coherent sequence from problem definition → market research plan → validated insights → product/tech decisions → marketing/partnership strategy → business model → resource/risk/financing → roadmap, with each stage specifying inputs and outputs so there are no logical jumps.

logical_coherence.criterion: Needs-to-Solution Traceability (Market Insights → Features → Tech Stack)
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether student needs discovered (e.g., study patterns, course pain points) are explicitly mapped to prioritized features and then to technology choices and architecture, including stated trade-offs that justify why the chosen stack best satisfies the validated requirements.

Evaluates whether each recommendation (e.g., pricing, acquisition channels, partnership approach) cites relevant findings from the proposed research or credible benchmarks, avoiding unsupported claims and ensuring evidence directly supports the claim it is used for.

Checks the logical derivation from user acquisition assumptions (funnel conversion, CAC) to revenue (pricing, ARPU), unit economics (LTV/CAC), capacity and cost structure (compute for AI features, support), and scalability conclusions, with clear intermediate steps and formulas.

Assesses whether product, tech, marketing, partnerships, operations, compliance, and finance plans are mutually consistent and identify dependencies (e.g., campus partnerships require FERPA/GDPR compliance; marketing campaigns align with feature readiness and semester cycles).

logical_coherence.criterion: Uncertainty Management, Scenarios, and Decision Gates
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether key uncertainties (e.g., adoption rates, model accuracy, campus procurement timelines) are linked to scenario/sensitivity analysis and explicit decision gates with criteria for pivot/continue, maintaining logical robustness under varying assumptions.

Checks whether transitions clearly connect why the next section follows from the previous one (e.g., how research findings inform MVP scope; how MVP metrics drive GTM choices), preventing logical breaks between major sections.

Assesses whether critical assumptions are explicitly listed, prioritized by impact/uncertainty, and tied to specific validation experiments (e.g., preorders, campus pilot MOUs), with pass/fail thresholds that logically determine subsequent actions.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Organization and Navigability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report has a clear section hierarchy and wayfinding: a table of contents, numbered headings, consistent section templates (e.g., Objective → Steps → Deliverables → Owners → Timeline), and cross-references so readers can quickly locate parts like market research design, platform architecture, marketing, business models, risks, and financing.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity and Action-Oriented Tone
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether sentences are concise and unambiguous, using active voice and imperative verbs for instructions (e.g., 'Run 20 student interviews'), with parallel structure in lists and consistent terminology, avoiding filler jargon and overly long sentences that obscure action steps.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Visuals and Diagrams
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether charts/diagrams (e.g., functional architecture, research workflow, Gantt timeline, marketing funnel, pricing comparison tables) are readable on-screen, with clear titles, labeled axes/units/legends, accessible color contrast, and a one-sentence takeaway caption so visuals stand alone and clarify text.

clarity_readability.criterion: Terminology and Acronym Clarity
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that technical and business terms (e.g., LLM, RAG, MLOps, MVP, CAC, LTV, ARPU) are defined at first use or in a glossary, acronyms are expanded on first mention, similar terms are distinguished, and labels for features/modules are used consistently to prevent confusion.

clarity_readability.criterion: Scannability and Highlighting of Key Points
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether key information is easy to scan via bullet lists, numbered steps, checklists, callout boxes, and emphasis for deadlines, KPIs, and next actions; information is chunked with adequate white space so readers can quickly extract what to do and when.

clarity_readability.criterion: Formatting Consistency and Typography Hygiene
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses consistent fonts, heading sizes, spacing, bullet/numbering styles, table formatting, and uniform treatment of dates, currencies, and units; prevents visual noise that distracts from content and improves long-form reading comfort.

clarity_readability.criterion: Quality of Executive Summary and Section Summaries
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for a succinct executive summary and per-section TL;DRs that surface objectives, key recommendations, and next steps in 3–5 bullets, enabling busy readers to grasp the essentials rapidly without reading the full text.

task: I plan to launch an online AI education startup targeting university students within the next year, with the core goal of significantly improving their learning efficiency through innovative courses and AI tools. However, I still need a systematic plan to ensure the project can progress from concept to execution. Please provide me with an actionable overall strategy that covers how to design and conduct multi-dimensional market research to accurately identify target needs, along with a ready-to-use business plan framework. Include specific recommendations for platform development in terms of functional architecture, technology stack selection, and iterative processes. Additionally, propose effective marketing and user engagement strategies tailored to the behavioral characteristics of university students, and help me assess the feasibility of different business models (such as subscription-based, value-added services, or campus partnerships) to ensure both short-term launch readiness and long-term scalability. Please also provide forward-looking advice on resource allocation, potential risks, and financing channels.

goal_alignment.criterion: Data-driven, PM-oriented framing with evidence and metrics
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report adopts a structured product-thinking approach (JTBD, PRD outline, OKRs) and specifies measurable learning-efficiency metrics (e.g., time-on-task reduction, spaced-repetition recall, pass-rate uplift) plus analytics plans (A/B testing, funnels, cohorts), matching Liu Lu’s analytical skill set and desire to transition into PM.

goal_alignment.criterion: Depth of China university student insight and behavioral tailoring
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content reflects localized, nuanced understanding of Chinese student segments (985/211 vs non-211, STEM vs non-STEM, year-in-school), academic calendar peaks (semester start, midterms/finals, CET-4/6, postgraduate entrance exams), and native channels (WeChat/QQ groups, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, Douyin, campus societies), ensuring strategies resonate with the actual target users.

goal_alignment.criterion: Rigor and personalization of multi-dimensional market research design
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for a concrete research program using Liu Lu’s strengths: sampling via campus channels, quant surveys with statistically sound sample sizing, logs-based telemetry, diary studies/usability tests, scraping/qual coding of UGC comments, and an analysis plan (hypothesis matrices, segmentation, regression/cluster methods) that directly maps insights to product and pricing decisions.

goal_alignment.criterion: Platform feature prioritization anchored to learning efficiency and integrity
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the feature set prioritizes mechanisms that measurably improve efficiency (LLM study copilot, adaptive practice, spaced repetition, schedule optimizer) while addressing academic integrity (anti-cheat guardrails, citation/source links, hallucination mitigation) to avoid institutional pushback—critical for campus adoption.

goal_alignment.criterion: Tech stack localization and skill compatibility with China constraints
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if the stack proposes China-accessible LLMs/cloud (e.g., Qwen/ERNIE/GLM, Alibaba/Tencent Cloud), a Python-friendly backend (FastAPI), analytics (PostgreSQL, dbt, Superset/Metabase), and messaging infra (WeChat mini program/SDK), with cost, latency, and compliance trade-offs articulated so Liu Lu can implement with her existing skills.

goal_alignment.criterion: Iterative roadmap with realistic time-boxing and instrumentation
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan sets a 12-month milestone map (discovery → MVP → pilot → GA) sized for an evenings/weekends founder, integrates Notion/Trello workflows, defines sprint cadences, and embeds instrumentation (events schema, KPIs, dashboards) and decision gates to curb perfectionism and support staged risk reduction.

goal_alignment.criterion: Student-tailored marketing and engagement system
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for tactics tuned to student behaviors and budgets: campus ambassador programs, society/teacher partnerships, WeChat/QQ community playbooks, Bilibili/XHS micro-influencers, exam-season campaigns, referral loops, study challenges/gamification, and messaging that emphasizes ethical use and GPA/test improvements.

goal_alignment.criterion: Business model feasibility with unit economics and scenarios
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report compares subscription (semester passes), freemium/value-added services (1:1 tutoring, certification), and campus licenses/partnerships, with LTV/CAC, payback, price elasticity, and scenario analyses (base/upside/downside) appropriate for student price sensitivity and institutional sales cycles.

goal_alignment.criterion: Resource allocation and financing plan aligned to conservative risk and Shanghai ecosystem
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether it proposes lean team composition (founder + part-time engineer/design), outsource vs in-house choices, budget phasing (compute/content/marketing), and financing channels (Shanghai incubators, university innovation funds, grants, angels) with clear runway and contingency planning that respect Liu Lu’s risk profile.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk, compliance, and delivery reliability specific to China edtech/AI
goal_alignment.explanation: Check identification and mitigation of PIPL/data privacy, content moderation, model hallucination, academic misconduct, API accessibility/firewall risks, and SLAs/incident response—key to campus trust and partnership viability.

goal_alignment.criterion: Deliverable usability: templates, checklists, and ready-to-use artifacts
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine whether the report provides plug-and-play assets (business plan outline, PRD template, research questionnaires, consent forms, analytics event schema, KPI dashboard spec, RICE/OKR sheets, sprint/Gantt skeleton) that Liu Lu can drop into Notion/Trello to accelerate execution.

content_alignment.criterion: Data-Driven Rigor and Quantitative Depth Tailored to a Data Analyst
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the content proposes concrete, statistically sound methods (e.g., survey design with sampling frames, stratification; experiment/A-B testing plans; cohort/retention analysis; TAM/SAM/SOM with assumptions; metric trees and event schemas) at a depth appropriate for someone proficient in Python/R/SQL. This matches her evidence-first decision style and avoids superficial advice.

content_alignment.criterion: Tech Stack and Architecture Recommendations Matching Her Skills and China-available AI Options
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether platform architecture and stack suggestions align with her competencies (e.g., Python/FastAPI, SQL warehouse, dbt, Airflow, Tableau/Metabase, SDK-based event tracking) and reference pragmatic LLM choices in China (e.g., ERNIE, Tongyi, Hunyuan, iFlytek; on-prem/LLM proxy), so she can assess feasibility and extend the solution herself.

content_alignment.criterion: China University Market, Channels, and Compliance Specificity
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the strategy reflects China-specific student behavior and acquisition channels (WeChat mini-programs, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, Zhihu, campus ambassador programs), university partnership mechanisms, and regulatory constraints (PIPL/data residency, ICP/filings, campus IT/security review). This ensures the content aligns with her Shanghai context and target market reality.

content_alignment.criterion: Product-Management-Oriented Framing Aligned with Her Career Transition Goals
content_alignment.explanation: Assess inclusion of PM frameworks and artifacts (Lean Canvas/business plan mapping, PRD structure, RICE prioritization, OKRs/North Star, AARRR funnel KPIs) so the content doubles as a PM learning scaffold, matching her aspiration to move into product/management.

content_alignment.criterion: Workflow and Tooling Integration with Notion/Trello and Analytics Habits
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content provides structures that plug into her existing tools (e.g., Notion templates for research repository/PRDs/OKRs, Trello/Jira board schemas, analytics dashboard outlines), aligning with her nightly planning routine and maximizing fit with her working style.

content_alignment.criterion: Risk-Conscious Business Model and Financing Path Consistent with Conservative Profile
content_alignment.explanation: Check if business model and financing recommendations emphasize staged, low-burn, revenue-first options (e.g., pilot subscriptions with universities, value-added services, non-dilutive grants/incubators, milestone-based fundraising) and include unit economics sensitivity, reflecting her low-risk investment preference.

content_alignment.criterion: Use of Credible Benchmarks and Comparative Evidence
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the content cites credible data (China EdTech/AI adoption reports, competitor metrics, student price sensitivity studies) and uses grounded benchmarks for KPIs (e.g., conversion, CAC/LTV) to validate proposals, matching her habit of saving data-backed articles and screenshots.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structural clarity and navigability (TOC, numbered headings, cross-references)
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear hierarchy (e.g., 1, 1.1, 1.1.1), a table of contents, and cross-references between related sections (e.g., market research → personas used in marketing) to minimize search time across many components.

presentation_fit.criterion: Professional, concise, evidence-based tone (non-hype, precise wording)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether the language is crisp, objective, and free of marketing fluff, using precise verbs and quantified claims to match a data analyst’s preference for rigor.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology and acronym management (definitions, first-mention expansion)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether technical/business acronyms and AI terms (e.g., PMF, LTV/CAC, RAG, CI/CD) are expanded and briefly defined at first use or in a glossary to reduce cognitive friction across domains.

presentation_fit.criterion: Scannability and hierarchy cues (bullets, checklists, callouts)
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks use of bullet lists, numbered steps, checklists, and callout boxes for key points so the reader can quickly grasp and capture items into tools like Notion/Trello.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visualization fit and clarity (architecture, flow, roadmap, funnel, comparison matrices)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether appropriate visuals are used and well-labeled: system architecture/flowcharts for the platform, Gantt/roadmaps for iterations, funnels for marketing, and matrix tables for business model comparisons.

presentation_fit.criterion: Quantitative presentation quality (formulas, units, assumptions, number formatting)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether metrics are presented with clear formulas (e.g., LTV, CAC), units, assumption boxes, and consistent number formats, enabling accurate interpretation and reuse.

presentation_fit.criterion: Template- and artifact-readiness (copyable frameworks and outlines)
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks whether key deliverables (business plan outline, market research questionnaire guide, PRD skeleton, experiment log template) are formatted as ready-to-copy sections or appendices.

presentation_fit.criterion: Layered depth and progressive disclosure (executive summary, section summaries, appendices)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether there is a TL;DR/executive summary, per-section key takeaways, and deeper detail in appendices to suit time-constrained evening reading while preserving depth.

presentation_fit.criterion: Prioritization and sequencing cues (visual phase markers, labels like RICE/MoSCoW)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether the presentation visually communicates what comes first and why (phase labels on roadmaps, priority tags on backlogs) to guide attention without altering content.

presentation_fit.criterion: Source transparency and traceability (citations, links, chart attributions)
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks that claims and visuals include properly formatted references, live links, and attributions on charts, supporting the user’s evidence-based decision style.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Executable 12-month roadmap with phase gates and critical path
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report breaks the next year into clear phases (e.g., research → MVP → beta → launch), defines deliverables, dependencies, and dates so the user knows exactly what to do in weeks 1–4, 5–12, etc., enabling steady progress despite limited evening/weekend time.

actionability_practicality.criterion: MVP scope definition and prioritization with acceptance criteria
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report specifies a tight, must-have feature set for the first release (e.g., core AI study tools and minimal course delivery), uses a prioritization method (must/should/could), and includes acceptance criteria to prevent scope creep and ensure the MVP is shippable.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Resource, team, and budget plan aligned to founder constraints
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report provides a realistic headcount/role plan (solo plus contractors vs. small team), time allocation, outsource vs. in-house choices, and monthly budget ranges (OPEX/CAPEX) that match a cautious risk appetite and enable disciplined spending.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Technical feasibility and stack recommendations with build/buy decisions
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for a concrete architecture and stack (e.g., cloud provider, backend/frontend frameworks, LLM API vs. self-host, database, analytics, CI/CD) with rationale, integration steps, and security considerations so a small team can implement without dead ends.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Actionable market research design and execution plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report defines research questions, sampling frames for university students, recruitment channels, instrument drafts (surveys/interviews/usability tests), sample sizes, timelines, and analysis plans that directly inform product, pricing, and GTM decisions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Go-to-market and student engagement experiments with channel playbooks
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for step-by-step campaigns tailored to university behaviors (e.g., campus ambassadors, WeChat groups, Bilibili/Douyin content, referral loops), with messaging, incentive structures, experiment calendars, and small test budgets to quickly validate acquisition.

actionability_practicality.criterion: KPI and measurement framework with instrumentation plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report defines success metrics (activation, retention, learning efficiency proxies, CAC), target thresholds, analytics tooling and event schemas, and dashboards so the user can make data-driven go/kill/iterate decisions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Business model feasibility with unit economics and pricing scenarios
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses presence of CAC/LTV models, churn assumptions, pricing tiers for students, model API cost impacts, campus partnership economics, scenario and sensitivity analyses, and break-even timelines to ensure revenue viability and scalability.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk register and compliance checklist with mitigations and triggers
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report lists key risks (e.g., model costs, content quality, data privacy), provides mitigations and contingency triggers, and includes China-specific compliance steps (ICP filing, PIPL, on-campus promotion approvals, payment rails) to avoid delays.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Financing strategy and runway plan tied to milestones
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report outlines staged financing options (bootstrapping, Shanghai grants/incubators, angels), expected amounts, use of funds, documentation, and timing keyed to phase gates, ensuring adequate runway and risk-managed capital planning.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Agile iterative process and execution rituals
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report specifies sprint cadence, backlog setup, standups, demos, and retros, plus artifact expectations (PRDs, tech specs), enabling disciplined iteration and velocity tracking for a small or part-time team.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Ready-to-use templates and operational checklists
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report supplies plug-and-play assets (business plan outline, survey/interview scripts, PRD template, experiment log, partnership outreach email) that reduce friction and let the user act immediately.

depth_insight.criterion: Depth of Market Need Discovery and Segmentation Insight (University AI Learning)
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report designs multi-method research (e.g., surveys, interviews, diary studies, analytics), appropriate sampling (major, year, institution type), and triangulation to surface non-obvious personas and jobs-to-be-done (e.g., exam cram vs project workflows). Looks for prioritized pain points with magnitude and constraints (e.g., academic integrity policies) rather than generic needs.

depth_insight.criterion: Rigor of Demand Sizing and Willingness-to-Pay Quantification
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether TAM/SAM/SOM for university AI education is quantified with defensible assumptions (enrollment, adoption rates, seasonality) and WTP measured via appropriate methods (e.g., Van Westendorp, Gabor-Granger) across segments, including price fences and student affordability considerations.

depth_insight.criterion: Insightfulness of Product Architecture and Tech Stack Trade-off Decisions
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses the depth of reasoning behind architecture choices (LLM provider vs open-source, RAG vs fine-tuning, vector DB, monitoring) with explicit cost, latency, accuracy, safety, privacy (e.g., FERPA/GDPR) and vendor lock-in trade-offs, plus build-vs-buy and a scalable path (caching, distillation, offline inference).

depth_insight.criterion: Causal and Practical Measurement of Learning Efficiency Gains
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan defines meaningful learning outcomes (e.g., time-to-mastery, grade improvement, reduction in time-on-task) and proposes credible validation (A/B tests, quasi-experiments, proctored assessments), including effect-size targets, privacy-preserving data collection, and controls for academic integrity.

depth_insight.criterion: Experimentation and Iterative Learning Strategy Specificity
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses the use of hypothesis-driven sprints, instrumentation, decision thresholds, and feedback loops (e.g., human-in-the-loop review, safety guardrails) to iterate quickly and learn efficiently, distinct from outcome validation of learning efficacy.

depth_insight.criterion: Behavior-anchored GTM and Engagement Insights for University Students
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether marketing and engagement strategies reflect real student behavior (semester/exam cycles, student orgs, social channels like TikTok/Discord), propose community/ambassador/referral loops, and include testable channel economics (expected CAC, conversion, retention drivers).

depth_insight.criterion: Business Model Feasibility and Unit Economics Depth
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses analysis of subscription vs value-added services vs campus partnerships, with concrete unit economics (compute costs per query/session, gross margin, LTV/CAC, cohort retention), bundling/discounting, and sensitivity to model pricing and adoption rates.

depth_insight.criterion: Anticipation of AI/EdTech-specific Risks and Mitigation Depth
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates identification of high-salience risks (cheating enablement, hallucinations, bias, IP/copyright, privacy/security, institutional bans, compute cost volatility) and concrete mitigations (guardrails, usage policies, logging/auditability, content provenance, consent management, compliance program).

depth_insight.criterion: Resource Allocation Priorities and Milestone-based Leverage
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report identifies the highest-leverage allocations (engineering vs content vs GTM), critical roles, build-vs-outsourcing choices, and milestone gates that maximize learning per dollar and de-risk the next stage.

depth_insight.criterion: Scalability and Defensibility Strategy (Moats, Partnerships, Data Network Effects)
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates forward-looking insight on how to scale across campuses (LMS/LTI integrations, institutional partnerships), build moats (proprietary datasets, fine-tuned models, community effects), and avoid vendor lock-in through modular design.

depth_insight.criterion: Financing Strategy Fit, Optionality, and Milestone Alignment
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether proposed financing channels (grants, university funds, cloud credits, angels/pre-seed, revenue-based, strategic partners) match risk stage and milestones, articulate trade-offs (dilution, speed, constraints), and preserve optionality for future rounds.

logical_coherence.criterion: Coherent End-to-End Framework and Dependency Mapping
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a logically ordered lifecycle (goals -> market research -> product/platform -> marketing/user engagement -> business model -> resources/risks/financing -> roadmap) and explicitly maps dependencies (e.g., research outputs informing product scope, model assumptions informing finance). This ensures the argument is built on a structured progression rather than disjointed sections.

Evaluates whether key assumptions and constraints (e.g., student behavior, budget, regulatory constraints, timeline) are explicitly stated and each major recommendation (methods, features, tech stack, pricing) cites which assumptions it relies on. Traceability prevents hidden leaps and enables consistent reasoning from premises.

logical_coherence.criterion: Evidence–Argument Alignment for Market and Strategy Claims
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether conclusions about target needs, feature priorities, marketing channels, and business model choices are explicitly supported by appropriate data or method outputs (e.g., survey/diary studies -> need statements -> feature requirements; CAC estimates -> pricing). This checks linkage and sufficiency of support for claims without judging writing quality.

logical_coherence.criterion: Cross-Section Consistency of Segmentation, Value Proposition, and KPIs
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that the defined student segments, value propositions, and success metrics remain consistent across market research, product design, marketing strategy, and financial modeling. Consistency avoids contradictions (e.g., focusing on STEM majors in research but marketing to all students).

Evaluates whether each section ends with explicit outputs that become inputs to the next (e.g., research insights -> prioritized JTBD and feature backlog; backlog -> MVP scope and roadmap; marketing hypotheses -> experiments). Clear hand-offs prevent logical gaps between sections.

Assesses whether the report uses explicit frameworks to reason from premises to conclusions for key choices (e.g., RICE/MoSCoW for features, build-vs-buy for AI components, tech trade-off matrices, channel selection criteria) and articulates trade-offs. This tests the rigor of the reasoning chain behind recommendations.

Checks that hypotheses are stated, experiments are designed to test them, metrics/decision rules are defined, and iteration plans follow logically from outcomes (e.g., if retention < X, change onboarding). This ensures the execution logic translates findings into next steps coherently.

logical_coherence.criterion: Risk–Mitigation Causality and Financial Derivation Coherence
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether risks are linked via cause->effect->mitigation->trigger, and whether financials are derived logically from assumptions (unit economics, CAC/LTV, runway, milestone-based financing). This criterion checks internal causal logic rather than numeric precision.

clarity_readability.criterion: Sectioning and Navigability Aligned to Task Scope
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear hierarchy and TOC that maps 1:1 to the requested components (market research design, business plan framework, platform architecture/stack/iteration, student-focused marketing & engagement, business model options, resources/risks/financing). Includes signposting, numbered headings, and breadcrumbs so readers can jump to the exact operational part they need.

clarity_readability.criterion: Formatting and Visual Hierarchy for Scannability
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses consistent use of headings, bullet lists, spacing, readable fonts, and white space; uses tables and checklists for frameworks and timelines; avoids dense blocks of text. The goal is to make procedures (e.g., research steps, rollout phases) quickly scannable and easy to follow.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity, Conciseness, and Actionable Language
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether instructions use plain language, active voice, and unambiguous phrasing (e.g., numbered steps, role-owner tags, due dates). Limits sentence length and avoids verbosity so cross-functional readers can execute without misinterpretation.

clarity_readability.criterion: Terminology and Acronym Management
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that AI, product, and startup terms (e.g., JTBD, A/B testing, CAC/LTV, AARRR, cohort, MLOps, microservices, RICE, OKRs) are defined at first use, used consistently, and optionally consolidated in a mini-glossary. Reduces comprehension barriers for readers from different backgrounds.

clarity_readability.criterion: Quality and Labeling of Visualizations
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether diagrams (architecture, data flow, iteration cycles), funnels (acquisition → retention), roadmaps/Gantt, and unit economics tables are legible, properly titled, labeled with units/timeframes, use legends, and avoid color-only encoding. Visuals should complement text and allow quick extraction of the key message.

clarity_readability.criterion: Emphasis of Key Takeaways and Action Items
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates presence and clarity of an executive summary, section-level TL;DRs, callouts for risks/assumptions/dependencies, and end-of-section action checklists. Helps readers grasp priorities and next steps without reading every detail.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency and Cross-Referencing
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks consistent labels, metric definitions, and terminology across sections (e.g., ‘subscription’ vs. ‘membership’), consistent units/currency and date formats, figure/table numbering, and clear internal references to appendices/templates. Prevents reader confusion during cross-section navigation.

task: I plan to launch an online AI education startup targeting university students within the next year, with the core goal of significantly improving their learning efficiency through innovative courses and AI tools.
