clarity_readability.criterion: Terminology and Acronym Management (Visa/HR/Language)
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether technical terms and acronyms are defined on first use and listed in a glossary (e.g., H-1B, LMIA, CoS, EU Blue Card, PR, CEFR B2/C1, ATS, STAR). Checks for consistent usage across countries and clear disambiguation of similar terms, lowering the comprehension barrier for non-experts.

clarity_readability.criterion: Visualization Effectiveness for Comparisons, Timelines, and Flows
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether charts/tables/flowcharts (e.g., country–industry opportunity matrices, salary vs. cost-of-living tables, hiring season timelines, visa process flowcharts) are appropriately chosen, clearly labeled (titles, axes, legends, units), readable, and accessible (colorblind-safe palettes), enabling rapid understanding without over-reliance on dense text.

clarity_readability.criterion: Actionability and Scannability of Steps, Checklists, and Templates
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the action plan is time-phased (e.g., 0–3, 3–6, 6–12, 12–24 months), with numbered tasks, milestones, and deadlines visually highlighted; whether checklists, résumé/interview templates, and sample scripts are formatted for quick use (e.g., bullets, callouts, checkboxes). Focuses on presentation of steps, not their substantive content.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency and Localization of Units, Formats, and Data Notation
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether currencies are labeled with codes (USD/EUR/GBP) and conversion date noted, dates use an unambiguous format (e.g., ISO 8601), time zones are specified, and measurement/qualification labels are consistent across countries. Clear, consistent notation prevents misreading in an international context.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Key Takeaways, Risks, and Calls to Action
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses the presence and clarity of an executive summary, TL;DR boxes, section-end key points, and callouts that emphasize deadlines (e.g., visa quota windows) and must-do items. Effective highlighting helps readers capture essentials quickly and revisit critical points efficiently.

clarity_readability.criterion: Reference, Linking, and Cross-referencing Hygiene
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether internal cross-references (e.g., to appendices, templates) and external links (e.g., official visa pages) are descriptive, current, and placed near relevant text; whether figures cite data sources. Clean references improve credibility and navigation without requiring deep content knowledge.

task: I plan to seek international career development opportunities within the next 1–2 years, ideally securing a position in an overseas company. Please provide me with an international career development roadmap, including an analysis of overseas job markets, target role requirements, language and cultural adaptation training, international résumé and interview preparation, visa and legal considerations, and strategies for improving cross-cultural communication and workplace skills. Please evaluate the employment prospects and adaptation challenges of different countries and industries, and provide a practical, actionable action plan.

goal_alignment.criterion: Realistic role-path alignment for a Chinese copywriter
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the report correctly identifies viable international roles given native-language constraints—e.g., bilingual/transcreation, localization, content strategy, social media/community, China/APAC market marketing, or internal transfer/remote-first setups—rather than generic local-language copywriting that requires native fluency.

goal_alignment.criterion: Country and industry targeting matched to creative profile and feasibility
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether the report compares employment prospects and adaptation challenges across specific markets (e.g., Japan, Singapore, Hong Kong, UK/EU) for creative/marketing roles, explicitly covering language requirements, sponsorship likelihood, creative industry density, and cultural fit (his interest in Japanese aesthetics and art-house culture).

goal_alignment.criterion: Phased, anxiety-aware 1–2 year roadmap with milestones and contingencies
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess if there is a month-by-month or quarter-by-quarter plan aligned with his evening productivity and weekend habits, including measurable milestones (tests, portfolio pieces, outreach targets), contingency paths (remote gigs, internal transfer), and stress-reduction mechanisms (buffers, decision checkpoints with family) to honor his stability needs and anxiety profile.

goal_alignment.criterion: International portfolio and personal brand leverage
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine whether the plan uses his existing social presence and aesthetic strengths to build a bilingual portfolio: case studies with metrics, English subtitles on Bilibili videos, LinkedIn/Behance/Medium presence, participation in D&AD/One Show/Clio, and storytelling tailored to international recruiters’ expectations.

goal_alignment.criterion: Visa/legal/logistics feasibility customized (including pet relocation)
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the report outlines accurate visa pathways for marketing/humanities roles (e.g., Japan Humanities/International Services, Singapore EP constraints, UK Skilled Worker thresholds), sponsorship realities, timelines, document prep, and pet relocation regulations (e.g., Japan’s rabies titer 180-day wait), integrating them into the 1–2 year timeline.

goal_alignment.criterion: Language and cultural training with ROI-driven choices
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for a pragmatic plan balancing English test targets (IELTS/TOEFL) and, if aiming at Japan, JLPT goals (N2/N1) with realistic payoffs; includes high/low-context communication training, feedback styles in agencies (creative reviews), and uses film/literature/media immersion methods aligned to his interests.

goal_alignment.criterion: Resume/interview preparation tailored to global creative hiring
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether guidance covers bilingual CV/portfolio walkthroughs, STAR stories for campaign impact, transcreation tests, creative critique simulations, asynchronous/video interview etiquette across time zones, and accent/clarity strategies relevant to marketing roles.

goal_alignment.criterion: Financial planning and ROI for a risk-averse creative
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify inclusion of salary benchmarks by city/role, cost-of-living comparisons (Tokyo/Singapore/UK/EU), relocation cost estimates, a 6–9 month cash runway plan, and decision gates that prioritize stability to match his conservative risk profile.

goal_alignment.criterion: Cross-cultural workplace practices for creative teams
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine whether the report translates cultural insights into concrete behaviors in agencies/brands (handling direct vs. indirect feedback, creative review rituals, conflict styles, presentation cadence), plus micro-habits for mental health and creative block management under pressure.

goal_alignment.criterion: Stylistic resonance and motivational fit
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether the narrative tone and examples speak to his art/film/literature sensibilities (e.g., references to Japanese/indie cinema aesthetics), maintain clarity without generic advice, and provide inspiring but pragmatic guidance that fits his decision style (intuition plus family consultation).

content_alignment.criterion: Precision Role/Industry Mapping to a Creative Copywriter Background
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report translates Xu Ke’s Literature + Marketing training and copywriting role into specific overseas titles (e.g., content strategist, brand storyteller, localization writer/translator, UX writer/content designer, social media copy, entertainment/media copy) and niches (luxury, gaming, film/TV, tourism, art/culture) with the right skill gaps and portfolio depth, avoiding generic job families unrelated to creative writing.

content_alignment.criterion: Country/City Targeting that Balances Cultural Affinity with Realistic Demand and Sponsorship
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether target markets reflect his cultural preferences (Japan/Italy, indie/arts cities) while realistically prioritizing hubs where Mandarin + creative skills are valued and sponsorship is plausible (e.g., Tokyo/Osaka, Singapore, London, Amsterdam, Toronto/Vancouver), including industry-specific prospects and adaptation risks (e.g., JP overtime culture, EU language barriers), rather than broad, non-specific market overviews.

content_alignment.criterion: Portfolio and Personal Branding Leveraging Existing Literary Achievements and Social Presence
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the content recommends a bilingual portfolio strategy that repurposes his Douban/Zhihu/Bilibili assets, literary awards, and campaign work into case studies suitable for global employers (Notion/Behance/Contently/Medium), with creative-writing samples tailored to target roles (UX microcopy tests, localization samples), instead of generic résumé advice.

content_alignment.criterion: Visa/Legal Pathways Tailored to Chinese Passport and Creative (Non-STEM) Roles
content_alignment.explanation: Determine whether visa guidance spotlights relevant routes (Japan Specialist in Humanities/International Services; Singapore EP for marketing; UK Skilled Worker with recognized sponsors; arts/global talent where feasible; study-to-work; intra-company transfer; EOR/remote) and avoids STEM-centric or inapplicable paths (e.g., misusing working-holiday assumptions for PRC passport), with nuance on sponsorship likelihood by role.

content_alignment.criterion: Language and Cultural Learning Plan Integrated with Artistic Media Habits
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the language/culture section uses his preferred media (Murakami texts, Wong Kar-wai films, indie cinema, creative ads) to build domain-specific English/Japanese proficiency (e.g., N2 ad vocabulary, portfolio writing in English, critique language) and fits his evening study rhythm, instead of generic language-learning tips.

content_alignment.criterion: Risk-Managed Pathways and Financial Planning Aligned with Stability Preference
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan includes lower-risk routes suited to his risk aversion and family stability concerns (intra-company transfer via global agencies in Shanghai, remote project trials, short-term exchanges, one-year master’s as a bridge) and city-specific budget considerations for creative salaries and cost of living, rather than high-risk leaps.

content_alignment.criterion: Cross-Cultural Creative-Team Scenarios and Feedback Norms
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the content covers creative-specific cultural frictions (brief interpretation, pitch etiquette, direct vs. indirect critique, JP hierarchy/senpai–kohai, client-facing storytelling) and proposes scenario-based adjustments relevant to copy/brand roles, instead of generic workplace culture notes.

content_alignment.criterion: Networking and Community Entry via Familiar and Industry-Specific Platforms
content_alignment.explanation: Determine if networking advice connects his current platforms and habits (WeChat, Weibo, Bilibili) to global channels (LinkedIn, CreativeMornings, Cannes Young Lions communities, Slack groups for UX writing/content design, Behance/Dribbble for collaborations), enabling credible bridges into international creative circles.

content_alignment.criterion: Time-Phased Roadmap Matching 1–2 Year Horizon and Daily Rhythm
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether milestones are structured around a realistic 1–2 year arc with evening-focused learning and weekend portfolio sprints that align with his routine (morning efficiency, creative evenings), ensuring depth and pacing that match his habits rather than a one-size-fits-all timeline.

content_alignment.criterion: Pet Relocation and Lifestyle Fit Considerations
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the content acknowledges his cat and lifestyle preferences (pet entry/quarantine rules in target countries, pet-friendly rentals, nearby indie cinemas/art neighborhoods), reflecting a unique personal constraint that can materially impact destination choices and settlement success.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tone and voice alignment with a creative professional transitioning internationally
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report uses a professional, calm, and encouraging tone (second-person where appropriate), avoiding dense corporate/legalese and alarmist wording. This matches a creative copywriter’s preference for clear yet human language and reduces anxiety during complex planning.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology and acronym handling with bilingual cues
presentation_fit.explanation: Check if key international job-search and immigration terms (e.g., ATS, STAR, sponsorship, skilled worker routes) are introduced with concise definitions and English originals, and, where helpful, brief Chinese equivalents or explanations. This ensures clarity for a China-based reader navigating global jargon.

presentation_fit.criterion: Section architecture mirrors the requested roadmap components
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether top-level headings map 1:1 to the task (market analysis, target roles, language/culture training, résumé/interview, visa/legal, cross-cultural skills, country/industry comparisons, and the action plan), enabling quick navigation and mental indexing.

presentation_fit.criterion: Scannability and mobile-first formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Judge the use of short paragraphs, bullet/numbered lists, descriptive subheadings, and a brief executive summary or TL;DR. This fits the user’s mobile reading habits (WeChat/Weibo/Bilibili) and reduces cognitive load.

presentation_fit.criterion: Phased timeline and milestone visualization for the 1–2 year plan
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the roadmap is presented in phases (e.g., 0–3, 4–6, 7–12, 13–24 months) with milestones and checklists. A clear timeline structure aligns with the user’s planning horizon and aids progress tracking.

presentation_fit.criterion: Cross-country/industry comparison visualization clarity
presentation_fit.explanation: Check for matrices/tables or structured comparison lists that make differences across countries and industries instantly graspable (e.g., visa difficulty, language barrier, creative industry demand). This addresses the comparative evaluation request with low cognitive effort.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use of illustrative examples/templates/snippets
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether the report includes formatted examples (e.g., résumé bullet patterns with action–impact, STAR answer snippets, cross-cultural scenarios). For a copywriter, examples clarify expectations and elevate understanding through presentation, not just description.

presentation_fit.criterion: Resource listing and link-label presentation
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether job boards, visa portals, language platforms, and tools are grouped by category with clean labels and one-line descriptors. Clear packaging improves usability without overwhelming the reader.

presentation_fit.criterion: Anxiety-aware microcopy and callouts
presentation_fit.explanation: Check for supportive notes, tips, and non-intimidating disclaimers in callouts that pace the reader (e.g., ‘If short on time, start here’). This presentation choice aligns with the user’s tendency to feel pressure under complex tasks.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Target role mapping and skill-gap closure plan tailored to a non-native creative professional
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report maps Xu Ke’s current copywriting skills to viable overseas roles (e.g., localization/content strategy/China-market comms) per country, identifies specific gaps, and provides concrete upskilling steps with resources, outputs, and timelines.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Visa and sponsorship pathway specificity for target countries
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report recommends feasible work visa routes for a Chinese citizen (sponsorship likelihood in marketing/creative fields, required documents, lead times, employer types, and step-by-step actions), reducing legal/immigration uncertainty.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Market-country feasibility for non-native talent in creative/marketing roles
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report provides realistic job market analysis by country/industry, including language requirements, job volume, sponsorship rates, and viable niches, enabling Xu Ke to prioritize countries where he has a competitive edge.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Time-bound roadmap with milestones and workload fit
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines whether the plan translates into a quarter-by-quarter schedule with monthly/weekly tasks, estimated hours aligned to his routine (morning admin, evening creative work), and clear dependencies to avoid overload.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Financial feasibility and relocation budget planning
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report quantifies costs (courses/tests, credential verification, visa fees, relocation, initial living expenses) and defines savings targets, runway length, and contingency options consistent with a risk-averse profile.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Portfolio and personal brand deliverables with quality standards
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report specifies concrete deliverables (English portfolio site, case studies, spec work tailored to target markets), quality controls (native/editor review), and publication channels with deadlines.

actionability_practicality.criterion: International résumé and interview preparation specificity
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report includes ATS-compatible résumé guidance, keyword strategies per role, STAR-based story banks, mock interview schedule, and resources/tools to practice and receive feedback.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Language proficiency and cultural adaptation training plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the plan sets measurable language goals (e.g., CEFR/IELTS or JLPT targets if relevant), defines weekly practice routines, cross-cultural communication modules, and applied practice with international teams.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Networking and application pipeline with measurable activity targets
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report gives a concrete outreach plan (platforms, communities, target lists), weekly quotas for informational interviews/applications, and templates that convert to interviews.

depth_insight.criterion: Global Job Market Synthesis and Prioritized Targeting
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report synthesizes multi-country, multi-industry signals (talent shortages, hiring velocity, COL-adjusted compensation, openness to foreign hires, remote share, language requirements) into a prioritized shortlist with clear rationale, rather than merely listing markets.

depth_insight.criterion: Target Role Requirement Mapping and Skill-Gap Bridging Plan
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates depth in extracting competency models from target roles across countries, auditing transferability of existing skills, identifying gaps (including credential recognition or regulated-profession constraints), and proposing ROI-ranked bridging steps (courses, certifications, projects) with timelines and proof-of-skill.

depth_insight.criterion: Visa and Legal Feasibility Integrated with Job Strategy
depth_insight.explanation: Rates how well the report analyzes immigration pathways (sponsorship likelihood, quotas, timelines, eligibility points), right-to-work, and compliance factors, and then integrates these constraints into targeting choices (countries, company sizes, roles) to maximize feasibility within 1–2 years.

depth_insight.criterion: Prospects vs Adaptation Challenge Trade-off by Country-Industry
depth_insight.explanation: Measures the sophistication of comparing employment prospects with adaptation burdens (language acquisition effort, cultural distance, workplace norms, credential transfer, regulation), articulating mitigations and path dependencies to support informed choices.

depth_insight.criterion: Evidence-based Reasoning and Use of Relevant Indicators
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether claims are grounded in credible, relevant metrics (e.g., vacancy-to-applicant ratios, time-to-fill, shortage lists, salary distributions net of COL/taxes, sponsorship rates) with explicit assumptions, sensitivity checks, and triangulation rather than anecdote.

depth_insight.criterion: Original, High-Value Entry Pathways and Risk Mitigation
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the presence of non-obvious, high-leverage strategies (remote-first to relocate, regional hub stepping stones, internal transfer, short-term study/work visas, volunteer/contract-to-hire) including prerequisites, risks, and small-scale experiments to de-risk them.

depth_insight.criterion: Targeted Language and Cross-cultural Competence Development Design
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report sets role- and country-specific proficiency targets (e.g., CEFR/IELTS/TOEFL/Versant), selects effective modalities (immersion, tutoring, task-based practice), operationalizes cultural skills (feedback, meetings, negotiation) into drills and assessments (shadowing, 360 feedback).

depth_insight.criterion: International Résumé/Portfolio and Interview Localization with Evidence of Impact
depth_insight.explanation: Judges the depth of adapting application materials to country norms (ATS optimization, photo/age conventions, achievement quantification), building competency-aligned STAR stories, and including verifiable proof (portfolio, GitHub, case artifacts) tailored to target markets.

depth_insight.criterion: Actionable Sequenced Roadmap with Milestones, Dependencies, KPIs, and Fallbacks
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan sequences initiatives over 1–2 years with critical-path dependencies (visa lead times, exam dates), resource/budget assumptions, measurable KPIs (applications/week, networking targets, proficiency gains), and scenario-based fallbacks (Plan B/C) to handle uncertainty.

logical_coherence.criterion: Goal-Aligned and Modular Overall Report Framework
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report is organized around the 1–2 year overseas employment goal, with modules ordered logically (market/country landscape → target roles/requirements → capability gaps → training and application toolkit → visa/legal → cross-cultural integration → action plan). A coherent framework prevents fragmentation and sets a sound basis for subsequent reasoning.

logical_coherence.criterion: End-to-End Reasoning Integrity from Analysis to Roadmap
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether conclusions and recommendations follow a clear chain: market and country insights lead to role selection, which leads to gap analysis, which drives training and preparation steps, culminating in a feasible roadmap. Penalizes logical leaps where actions are proposed without being derived from prior analysis.

Evaluates whether each identified requirement (skills, certifications, language levels, cultural competencies, visa eligibility) is explicitly mapped to concrete actions (courses, practice regimes, certifications, document preparation), ensuring no orphan recommendations or unaddressed requirements.

Checks that claims about employment prospects and adaptation challenges by country/industry are supported by relevant data or facts and that the report explains how the evidence justifies the claim, avoiding overgeneralization or unsupported assertions.

Looks for explicit baseline assumptions (current skills, experience, language level, budget, mobility constraints, citizenship/work authorization, timeline) and verifies they are applied consistently across sections without contradictions or shifting premises.

logical_coherence.criterion: Transition Coherence and Logical Handoffs Between Sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether outputs of one section are used as inputs for the next (e.g., market insights feeding role requirements; visa constraints shaping target countries), with explicit linkages that avoid abrupt topic shifts and ensure a continuous argumentative flow.

logical_coherence.criterion: Prioritization and Sequencing Logic of Actions and Milestones
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the roadmap orders tasks by dependency and lead time (e.g., language training before interviews, credential evaluation before applications, visa steps post-offer), includes decision gates, and fits within the 1–2 year horizon.

logical_coherence.criterion: Decision Framework for Country/Industry Selection and Scenario Branching
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks for an explicit, criteria-based method to select countries/industries (e.g., demand, visa feasibility, language requirements) and for if–then branches that adapt the path to different conditions (e.g., insufficient language level, visa denial), ensuring logical completeness.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Organization and Navigability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear table of contents, consistent heading hierarchy, modular sections mapped to the task (market analysis, role requirements, language/culture, résumé/interview, visa/legal, cross-cultural skills, action plan), and numbered steps and timelines so readers can quickly locate and follow the parts relevant to them.

clarity_readability.criterion: Actionable, Plain, and Concise Language
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether the writing uses plain English, active voice, short sentences, and imperative phrasing for steps (e.g., 'Schedule IELTS by Month 2'), avoiding hedging and ambiguity so non-expert readers can immediately understand what to do.

clarity_readability.criterion: Terminology Definition and Consistency
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether domain terms and acronyms (e.g., CEFR, JLPT, IELTS/TOEFL scores, visa categories like H‑1B/Skilled Worker, NOC/ANZSCO, PPP) are defined on first use, used consistently thereafter, and accompanied by standardized units (currency codes, date formats), reducing confusion across countries and industries.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Visual Aids for Complex Information
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether timelines (e.g., 1–2 year Gantt), visa process flowcharts, country–industry heatmaps, and comparison tables are clear, labeled with titles/legends/units/sources, and designed to highlight differences and steps without clutter, enabling fast comprehension of procedures and trade-offs.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Key Actions, Deadlines, and Risks
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses the use of visual emphasis (callouts, checklists, icons, bolding) to surface critical actions, deadlines (e.g., application windows), compliance notes, and country-specific cautions, helping readers not miss important operational details.

clarity_readability.criterion: Internationalization and Accessibility
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates adherence to globally readable conventions (ISO date formats, ISO currency codes with units, metric/imperial where relevant), color-blind–safe palettes, readable font sizes, adequate contrast/whitespace, and avoidance of culture-specific idioms so diverse readers can interpret content reliably.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency and Microcopy Standards
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks consistency in style (capitalization, punctuation, list grammar), naming (countries, visa categories, job titles), figure/table numbering, and cross-references, minimizing cognitive load caused by small but distracting inconsistencies.

clarity_readability.criterion: Executive Summary and Section Summaries for Quick Scanning
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether the report offers an upfront executive summary and per-section takeaway boxes (e.g., per-country prospects/challenges, per-role requirements) so readers can grasp the main points and navigate to details efficiently.

task: Over the next six months, I plan to focus on improving my self-awareness and emotional management, as I often experience emotional fluctuations in daily life that lead to poor decision-making and strained interpersonal relationships. Please recommend self-study books, online courses, and practical strategies that can be integrated into daily habits to help me systematically identify emotional triggers, enhance self-reflection, and build effective emotional regulation mechanisms.

goal_alignment.criterion: Graduate-level, evidence-based framing of emotion regulation
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report anchors recommendations in contemporary models and interventions (e.g., Gross’s process model, CBT/DBT/ACT/mindfulness, metacognition/interoception), cites or clearly references reputable sources, and avoids pop-psych oversimplification—matching a clinical psychology graduate student’s expectation for rigor.

goal_alignment.criterion: Resource curation aligned with platforms, language, and budget
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if the books/courses include reputable Chinese translations and bilingual options, leverage platforms she uses (Coursera audit, Chinese MOOC/公开课, Zhihu columns), flag student discounts/free audits, and balance depth with cost-effectiveness to fit her frugal, practical spending style.

goal_alignment.criterion: Structured 6-month roadmap integrated with her daily rhythm
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether there is a phased plan with monthly milestones and weekly routines mapped to her schedule (morning study for theory/skill drills, afternoon social windows for in-vivo practice, evening reflection/journaling), and incorporating her 3x/week exercise—ensuring realistic, sustainable implementation.

goal_alignment.criterion: Validated measurement and feedback loops
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess inclusion of baseline and monthly reassessments using validated tools (e.g., DERS, ERQ, PANAS, MAAS), trigger/mood logs, and review rituals with clear criteria for adjusting the plan. Templates compatible with cloud note tools reflect her digital workflow and desire for systematic progress tracking.

goal_alignment.criterion: Direct targeting of decision-making and interpersonal strain
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for concrete protocols that address her stated pain points: DBT interpersonal effectiveness (DEAR MAN/GIVE/FAST), assertive communication scripts, emotion-in-the-moment regulation options (paced breathing/urge surfing), and decision safeguards (if-then plans, 10/10/10, pre-mortem, hot–cold gap bridging).

goal_alignment.criterion: Feasibility and habit design within time/energy constraints
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate the specificity of micro-habits and implementation intentions (e.g., 2-minute check-ins post-lunch, Pomodoro emotion labeling, mindful commute), energy management (sleep/nap alignment), and practical considerations (e.g., monitoring caffeine’s impact on arousal) to ensure adoption and sustainability.

goal_alignment.criterion: Cultural and contextual resonance with Beijing graduate life
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether examples and scenarios fit her campus/counseling center context, Chinese academic norms, and daily environments (library, coffee shops), and include locally relevant resources/authors—enhancing relevance and emotional resonance for a Beijing-based student.

goal_alignment.criterion: Professional boundaries and ethical safeguards
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for guidance on limits of self-help (e.g., trauma triggers, severe impairment), when to seek supervision/therapy, privacy of self-tracking data, and safe practice principles—aligning with her clinician-in-training role and ethical awareness.

goal_alignment.criterion: Tone and communication style fit for a peer-level clinician
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the writing is respectful, non-patronizing, concise yet nuanced, with structured summaries and optional deep dives—matching her communication skills and preference for balanced logic/emotion in decisions.

goal_alignment.criterion: Integration with digital toolchain and templates
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess provision of ready-to-use Notion/Obsidian/Excel templates for trigger logs, scale dashboards, and habit trackers; calendar integrations and reminders; and suggestions for WeChat mini-programs—leveraging her cloud notes habit and time management tools.

content_alignment.criterion: Evidence-Based, Graduate-Level Theoretical Anchoring
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether recommended books/courses/strategies are grounded in established emotion science and clinical frameworks (e.g., Gross’s Process Model, DBT skills, ACT, MBCT/MBSR), with references or primary sources suitable for a clinical psychology master’s student—avoiding pop-psych oversimplifications.

content_alignment.criterion: Localization and Access Fit (Mandarin/China-Friendly and Cost-Effective)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether resources are available in Mandarin or with reliable Chinese translations, accessible via Mainland-friendly platforms (e.g., MOOCs she already uses) or university library, and favor free/low-cost options to match her Beijing locale and frugal budget.

content_alignment.criterion: Targeted Subskill Coverage: Triggers, Self-Reflection, Regulation Mechanisms
content_alignment.explanation: Check if content precisely maps to her stated aims—systematic trigger identification (e.g., EMA, mood tracking), structured self-reflection (e.g., reflective practice models, therapist self-reflection diaries), and evidence-based regulation strategies (e.g., DBT modules, cognitive reappraisal), including appropriate assessments (e.g., DERS, ERQ).

content_alignment.criterion: Habit-Integration Suitability for a Six-Month Cadence
content_alignment.explanation: Determine whether chosen resources naturally support daily/weekly practice (e.g., books with end-of-chapter exercises, courses with weekly skill modules, protocols designed for daily micro-practices) and can be realistically paced across six months given her morning study and night reading rhythm.

content_alignment.criterion: Digital Workflow Alignment (Platforms, Apps, and Note Ecosystem)
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether resources align with her existing tools and habits—Coursera/Chinese MOOCs, Zhihu for discussion, cloud note-taking, and data-friendly mood-tracking/EMA apps with exportable templates—so she can capture and iterate on learning effectively.

content_alignment.criterion: Interpersonal and Academic-Context Relevance
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content includes interpersonal effectiveness and communication skills relevant to lab/supervisor/peer dynamics and Chinese family communication patterns, directly reflecting her relationship strain and decision-making contexts.

content_alignment.criterion: Mind–Body Congruence with Yoga/Jogging Preferences
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the selection includes mindfulness or MBCT/MBSR-informed practices and breath/body-based regulation that pair well with her yoga/light jogging routine, leveraging existing habits to deepen emotional regulation.

content_alignment.criterion: Inclusion of Companion-Animal–Informed Soothing Practices
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether content thoughtfully incorporates pet-facilitated emotion regulation (e.g., mindful pet interaction, grounding with sensory focus), acknowledging her bond with her cat as a personalized lever for affect regulation.

presentation_fit.criterion: Phased roadmap and sectional structure aligned to a six-month plan (clear headings, numbered steps, time-of-day tags)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether the report organizes content into a logical six-month progression with consistent H2/H3 headings, week/month phases, and labels like 'Morning practice'/'Evening reflection'. This matches the user's time-block habit and reduces cognitive load for planning.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tone calibration: evidence-based, collaborative, non-patronizing academic style
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks whether the language respects a graduate-level reader: neutral, precise, and supportive, signaling evidence (brief citations or source mentions) without hype. This fosters credibility and aligns with her academic communication norm.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology accuracy with concise glosses and consistency (include brief parenthetical definitions/Chinese equivalents when helpful)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses correct use of psychological terms (e.g., cognitive reappraisal, DBT skills) with short in-line glosses and occasional Chinese terms in parentheses to aid recall across platforms. Consistent naming reduces ambiguity and aids expert comprehension.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visual scaffolding quality (emotion-cycle diagrams, comparison tables, trackers/checklists with captions)
presentation_fit.explanation: Looks for clear, captioned visuals that map trigger–appraisal–emotion–response loops, and tables to compare resources. Visuals increase scan-ability, support screenshotting, and match the user’s preference for structured, visual content.

presentation_fit.criterion: Standardized resource cards with key metadata
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether each book/course/practice is presented with a consistent field set (level, duration/time commitment, platform, language, prerequisites, cost). Uniform metadata improves quick filtering and alignment with her structured note-taking.

presentation_fit.criterion: Skimmability and navigation aids (TL;DR, key takeaways, internal anchors, consistent bullets)
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks for an executive summary/TL;DR, section key points, and internal anchors for quick jumping. Consistent bulleting and short paragraphs match her online reading and forum-sharing habits, aiding rapid scanning.

presentation_fit.criterion: Digital capture and note-taking friendliness
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses copy/paste-friendly formatting (clean bullets, minimal nested styling), stable descriptive link labels, and chunked sections that paste cleanly into cloud notes. This aligns with her habit of saving notes/screenshots.

presentation_fit.criterion: Bilingual labeling/localization for key constructs and platform names where relevant
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks whether key psychological constructs and platform names include Chinese/English labels to ease cross-referencing (e.g., '认知重评 cognitive reappraisal', 'Coursera/课程') without clutter. This supports her mixed Chinese/English learning environment.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Structured six-month implementation roadmap with phases and milestones
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a month-by-month (or phase-based) plan that sequences skills (e.g., awareness and tracking → regulation skills → generalization to interpersonal contexts), sets weekly goals, and defines milestones/deliverables so the user can execute consistently and avoid drift.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Step-by-step daily/weekly practice protocols with time budgets and cues
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report translates methods (e.g., emotion labeling, CBT ABC, DBT skills, mindfulness) into precise routines (frequency, duration, context), uses habit stacking (e.g., after morning coffee → 10-min mindfulness), and fits 30–60 minutes/day to match her time preferences and improve adherence.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Resource accessibility and cost feasibility (Mainland-friendly)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether recommended books and courses are available via Chinese platforms/libraries or have local alternatives, include price ranges and time commitments, and avoid VPN-only access where possible—critical for a frugal student aiming for low-friction acquisition.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Monitoring and feedback system with clear metrics and adaptation rules
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for a measurable progress plan (e.g., daily mood/trigger log, weekly PANAS, monthly DERS-SF), review cadences, and explicit decision rules for adjusting the plan if KPIs plateau or worsen—ensuring continuous improvement rather than passive consumption.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Integration with the user’s environment and routine
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether practices are mapped to her daily pattern (morning study, afternoon social, evening reading), leverage campus/library/counseling resources, and optionally pair with existing exercise habits (yoga/jogging) to support regulation—improving real-world feasibility.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk management and scope boundaries for self-guided interventions
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for safety guardrails (e.g., signs to pause/seek supervision, crisis resources, avoiding trauma exposure without guidance), ensuring that self-application remains within safe limits and prevents adverse effects.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Choice architecture and decision rules for selecting books/courses
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report provides shortlists (2–3 options per category) with time, difficulty, and language notes, plus selection heuristics and fallback options—reducing decision fatigue and enabling quick commitment.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Interpersonal practice design using peer role-plays and supervision
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report includes concrete interpersonal skill drills (e.g., NVC/DBT IE scripts), schedules for role-plays, feedback rubrics, and pointers to peer/supervision opportunities at the counseling center—vital for addressing relationship strain.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Provision of operational assets (templates, checklists, scripts)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report supplies ready-to-use materials (mood/trigger log templates, ABC worksheets, if-then scripts, Notion/Obsidian pages) compatible with cloud note-taking to reduce setup friction and speed implementation.

depth_insight.criterion: Mechanism-grounded Diagnostic Depth and Trigger Mapping
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report goes beyond surface symptoms to identify emotional triggers, patterns, and mechanisms (e.g., appraisal styles, emotional granularity, interoceptive cues) that concretely explain poor decisions and strained relationships, producing a usable trigger taxonomy.

depth_insight.criterion: Evidence-based Skill Framework and Resource Fit
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether recommended books/courses are justified by empirical foundations (e.g., CBT/DBT/ACT, mindfulness, interpersonal effectiveness) and explicitly mapped to target skills the user needs (e.g., reappraisal, distress tolerance), rather than generic or popularity-based lists.

depth_insight.criterion: Habit Integration Strategy Grounded in Behavior-Change Science
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses the depth of converting techniques into daily, feasible routines (e.g., habit stacking, implementation intentions, cue design, friction reduction, dosage), showing practical insight into sustaining practice over six months.

depth_insight.criterion: Context-Specific Decision Rules and Tool Selection
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report provides clear, mechanism-informed rules for when to use which tool (e.g., physiological downregulation for high arousal, reappraisal for cognitive distortions, interpersonal skills for conflict), avoiding one-size-fits-all strategies.

depth_insight.criterion: Measurement, Experimentation, and Iterative Feedback Design
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether there are meaningful metrics and feedback loops (e.g., trigger frequency, DERS/ERQ use, conflict counts, decision quality logs) and small experiments to refine strategies, demonstrating learning and adaptation over time.

depth_insight.criterion: Prioritization and Sequencing Rationale
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the rationale for focusing on high-leverage skills/resources first and sequencing phases logically (foundational regulation before advanced interpersonal work), including explicit trade-offs and what to defer or discard.

depth_insight.criterion: Personalization to Constraints and Risk/Ethical Guardrails
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses tailoring to time, motivation, and context (work vs home triggers), and inclusion of boundaries for self-study (e.g., when to seek professional help, crisis protocols), ensuring realistic and safe application.

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and Added Value of Insights
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report introduces novel, actionable frameworks or non-obvious integrations (e.g., trigger maps, if-then scripts, decision pre-mortems, emotional inoculation) that materially enhance effectiveness beyond standard advice.

logical_coherence.criterion: Phased, Goal-Driven Overall Framework and Dependency Structure
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report is organized from baseline (current issues and triggers) to objectives, conceptual framework (trigger→mechanism→practice), resource selection, phased schedule (e.g., months 1–6), and evaluation plan, with prerequisites sequenced logically (e.g., awareness before advanced regulation).

logical_coherence.criterion: Justified Mapping from Needs to Interventions and Resources
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether each recommended book/course/practice is explicitly linked to a specific need (identify triggers, self-reflection, regulation) and justified by a mechanism or empirical rationale (e.g., mindfulness for attention training, CBT for reappraisal), avoiding unsupported or generic recommendations.

logical_coherence.criterion: Causal Reasoning from Premises to Conclusions
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the report states assumptions (e.g., certain contexts trigger affective surges), derives mechanisms addressing them (e.g., affect labeling to reduce amygdala reactivity), and arrives at conclusions (selected routines and schedule) without logical gaps or leaps.

logical_coherence.criterion: Smoothness of Section and Timeline Transitions
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether transitions between sections and across months are coherent (e.g., clear rationale for moving from trigger logging to reappraisal practice), with bridging statements that connect prior findings to next steps and avoid abrupt topic or phase changes.

Examines whether goals map to concrete activities and measurable indicators, and whether terms and constructs are used consistently across sections; each activity should trace back to a goal, and each goal should have at least one aligned activity and metric.

Assesses whether the plan specifies data collection (e.g., mood logs, trigger counts), review cadence, and decision rules for adjusting interventions, ensuring that updates logically feed back into goals and subsequent phases rather than being ad hoc.

clarity_readability.criterion: Macro-Structure and Timeline Navigation
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report’s sections and headings clearly map to the six-month plan (e.g., month-by-month or phase-based headings), with consistent hierarchy and navigational aids (TOC, anchors). A clear macro-structure reduces cognitive load and helps readers quickly locate time-specific guidance.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity and Plain Style
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether sentences are concise, active, and unambiguous; jargon is minimized; and instructions are written in straightforward, reader-friendly language. Clear prose prevents misinterpretation and makes implementation easier for non-experts.

clarity_readability.criterion: Scannability and Emphasis of Key Points
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates the use of bullet points, callouts, bolding, and short summaries to surface key recommendations, daily habits, and cautions. Good scannability enables readers to grasp the essentials quickly and revisit important items efficiently.

clarity_readability.criterion: Technical Term Introduction and Consistent Usage
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that psychological terms and acronyms (e.g., CBT, DBT, cognitive reappraisal, affect labeling) are introduced at first use with brief, plain explanations and used consistently thereafter. Proper handling of terms lowers entry barriers and prevents confusion.

clarity_readability.criterion: Visual Aids and Templates Legibility
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether tables, trackers (emotion logs, habit trackers), matrices, and flow diagrams (trigger → thought → feeling → behavior) are well-labeled, readable on screen, color/legend accessible, and aligned to the text. Effective visuals make procedures and patterns easier to grasp and apply.

clarity_readability.criterion: Procedural Step Formatting for Practices
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether techniques (e.g., mindfulness, cognitive reappraisal) are presented with numbered steps, estimated time, frequency, and prerequisites clearly separated and easy to follow. Clear procedural formatting supports accurate, repeatable practice.

clarity_readability.criterion: Resource Listing Clarity and Metadata Completeness
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether books and courses are presented in a standardized format (title, author/instructor, level, duration, time commitment, link) with consistent styling. Uniform metadata helps readers compare options and select appropriate resources quickly.

task: Over the next six months, I plan to focus on improving my self-awareness and emotional management, as I often experience emotional fluctuations in daily life that lead to poor decision-making and strained interpersonal relationships. Please recommend self-study books, online courses, and practical strategies that can be integrated into daily habits to help me systematically identify emotional triggers, enhance self-reflection, and build effective emotional regulation mechanisms.

goal_alignment.criterion: Context-specific trigger modeling for a Chinese STEM grad student
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the article accurately identifies and addresses typical triggers in his environment—advisor expectations, failed experiments, paper deadlines, lab hierarchy, peer competition, and family hopes—while acknowledging cultural norms (restraint, humility, saving face) that shape emotional expression. Personalized examples and scenarios should reflect lab life and family interactions.

goal_alignment.criterion: Six-month phased roadmap with measurable milestones
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate if there is a clear 6‑month plan broken into phases (e.g., baseline tracking → trigger mapping → cognitive reappraisal/skills → interpersonal practice → stress/load management → consolidation/relapse prevention), with weekly tasks, check-ins, and concrete metrics (mood intensity/frequency, decision logs, conflict frequency). Milestones should fit his academic calendar and provide review loops.

goal_alignment.criterion: Daily habit integration leveraging his routines and tools
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether recommended practices are embedded into his actual day (evening reflection blocks, noon rest, daytime experiments) and existing tools (Trello checklists, simple templates), using habit stacking (e.g., with coffee brewing, cycling warm-ups/cool-downs), 5‑minute fallback versions for busy weeks, and portability for travel/rural homestays.

goal_alignment.criterion: Evidence-based mechanisms with systems-engineering framing
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if methods are grounded in CBT/REBT (ABC model, cognitive reappraisal), DBT (STOP, TIPP), ACT (values/defusion), and mindfulness with empirical backing, and whether explanations connect to concepts he relates to (control loops/PID, root-cause analysis/5 Whys, failure mode analysis) to enhance comprehension and buy-in.

goal_alignment.criterion: Resource curation aligned to platform, language, and budget
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether books and courses are bilingual/Chinese-accessible, from platforms he already uses (Coursera/edX/Bilibili/WeChat), low-cost or free, with clear time requirements and difficulty levels. Include options suited for a weekly book club (discussion prompts) and succinct summaries for efficient learning.

goal_alignment.criterion: Interpersonal and decision-making protocols for lab and family contexts
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan provides culturally appropriate communication scripts and frameworks (NVC, DESC, BIFF), meeting/email templates for advisor interactions, and decision aids (checklists, pre-mortem, if‑then rules) to reduce emotion-driven errors and improve relationships with mentors, labmates, and parents.

goal_alignment.criterion: Low-friction tracking and N-of-1 experimentation with privacy safeguards
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for simple mood/trigger logging (e.g., 1–3 minute daily entries), ABC records, weekly pattern analysis, and lightweight A/B tests of regulation strategies. Tools should respect privacy (local files/locked notes) and minimize cognitive load to ensure sustained use.

goal_alignment.criterion: Expression style and cultural resonance
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the writing is concise, analytical, and respectful, with examples from robotics/lab scenarios, sci‑fi analogies when helpful, and culturally sensitive framing (values, family expectations) that reduces stigma. Bilingual terminology where useful improves clarity and searchability.

goal_alignment.criterion: Safety boundaries and escalation guidance
goal_alignment.explanation: Confirm the inclusion of clear criteria for seeking professional help (persistent functional impairment, suicidal ideation, sleep/appetite collapse), references to campus counseling resources, and prudent notes on lifestyle changes (e.g., pet adoption responsibilities) without medical overreach.

content_alignment.criterion: Contextual Relevance to Chinese Graduate Engineering Life and Stressors
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the selected books/courses/strategies explicitly address lab/advisor dynamics, paper deadlines, perfectionism, competition, and family expectations common to Chinese STEM graduate students, making examples and case scenarios feel personally salient.

content_alignment.criterion: Evidence-Based Psychological Frameworks Suited to an Analytical Learner
content_alignment.explanation: Check that content relies on empirically supported models (e.g., CBT/DBT/ACT/mindfulness, habit loops, feedback systems) with sufficient conceptual rigor, references, and measurement tools, avoiding pop-psych or pseudoscience.

content_alignment.criterion: Language and Mainland China Platform Accessibility
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether resources are available in Chinese or with Chinese subtitles/translations and hosted on accessible platforms (e.g., Coursera/edX with CN access, iCourse/MOOC, Bilibili, WeChat), ensuring frictionless access from Jinan.

content_alignment.criterion: Cost-Effectiveness and Free/Audit Availability
content_alignment.explanation: Determine if selections favor free/audit MOOCs, library-available books, and low-cost apps/tools with transparent pricing, matching the user’s frugal, value-focused spending habits.

content_alignment.criterion: Fit to Time Routines and Learning Rhythm
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the content’s format and duration align with his schedule (evening deep-study blocks, micro-sessions between lab tasks), including chunked modules and readings that can be paced over six months.

content_alignment.criterion: Toolchain and Data-Driven Self-Tracking Alignment
content_alignment.explanation: Check that resources include or support structured tracking (mood logs, trigger matrices, thought records, HRV tracking) and can be integrated with Trello/Notion/Excel, resonating with his systems-oriented mindset.

content_alignment.criterion: Cultural Sensitivity to Chinese Values and Male Emotional Norms
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether content acknowledges filial piety, face consciousness, humility, and gendered norms around emotional expression in Chinese contexts, improving relevance to interpersonal dilemmas.

content_alignment.criterion: Integration with Physical Activity and Somatic Regulation
content_alignment.explanation: Consider whether selections connect emotional regulation with his cycling habit (e.g., mindful movement, breathing, HRV biofeedback), leveraging existing routines to enhance resonance.

content_alignment.criterion: Community and Discussion Compatibility
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether recommended books/courses are discussable in local book clubs or Douban groups and whether there are credible Bilibili/WeChat channels that align with his media habits.

content_alignment.criterion: Alignment with Emerging Interest in Pet-Assisted Stress Relief
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the content thoughtfully includes evidence-based perspectives on pets (e.g., adopting a cat) for stress reduction, reflecting his current consideration without overemphasis.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structural clarity and navigability (clear hierarchy, TOC, modular sections)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether the report uses an explicit heading hierarchy, a brief table of contents, and modular sections (e.g., summary, roadmap, routines, resource comparisons, troubleshooting) so a technically trained reader can quickly locate and digest parts.

presentation_fit.criterion: Scannability and cognitive load management
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks use of bullet points, numbered steps, short paragraphs, callouts/key takeaways, and white space to support quick on-screen reading (e.g., WeChat/desktop) and reduce fatigue.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology and concept clarity (plain language with brief definitions)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether psychology terms (e.g., trigger, cognitive reappraisal, mindfulness, ABC model) are introduced with one‑line explanations and acronyms defined once, avoiding dense clinical jargon.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use of comparative tables/matrices for resources
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether books/courses are presented in tables with consistent columns (e.g., language, duration, difficulty, cost in RMB, platform, prerequisites) and clear link labels to match an engineer’s preference for structured comparison.

presentation_fit.criterion: Process visuals and frameworks for workflows
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks for diagrams/flowcharts (e.g., trigger → thought → emotion → behavior), ABC model visuals, and decision flows for choosing regulation strategies, with captions and labels that make procedures immediately understandable.

presentation_fit.criterion: Time-structured six-month roadmap presentation
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether the six‑month plan is visualized in phases with milestones and review checkpoints (e.g., timeline or Gantt-like bars), making progression and iteration points explicit.

presentation_fit.criterion: Template and checklist formatting quality
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses presence and clarity of ready‑to‑use mood logs, trigger trackers, weekly review checklists, and habit trackers (printable/mobile-friendly), with example entries and consistent formatting.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tone and language style fit (analytical, respectful, non-hyped)
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks that the tone is objective, humble, and non‑judgmental, avoiding marketing or pathologizing language while maintaining an evidence‑informed, engineering‑compatible style.

presentation_fit.criterion: Bilingual labeling and localization of resource names/links
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether English resources include Chinese titles or parenthetical translations when available, link/platform labels are explicit, and units/formats are familiar, reducing access friction.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Presence of a concrete 6-month execution roadmap with phases and weekly routines
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report provides a phased plan (e.g., months 1–2 focus on trigger mapping, months 3–4 on regulation skills, months 5–6 on consolidation), with weekly tasks and session durations so the user can start immediately and sustain progress.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Time feasibility aligned to the user’s schedule and energy patterns
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether daily and weekly time commitments (e.g., 5–10 minute micro-practices, 20–40 minute evening blocks, 60-minute weekly review) fit his evening deep-work preference, lab daytime constraints, and cycling routine to ensure consistent execution.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Resource accessibility and cost practicality in mainland China
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates if recommended books and courses are available in Mandarin or bilingual editions, purchasable via common platforms (e.g., JD/Tmall/Kindle/Coursera/edX/Bilibili) with clear pricing and free/low-cost options or accessible alternatives, ensuring no high barriers to access.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Systematic trigger identification toolkit with templates and tools
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for concrete methods (e.g., ABC model, emotion intensity scales, situation–thought–feeling logs), ready-to-use templates (spreadsheet/app), and clear instructions on frequency and context of use so the user can reliably capture emotional triggers.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Specific emotional regulation protocols and if–then decision rules
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for step-by-step instructions for techniques (e.g., paced breathing, mindfulness, cognitive reappraisal, behavioral activation), with situation-based selection rules (e.g., if arousal >7/10 then do 4-7-8 breathing; if ruminating then use thought record) and time estimates.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Progress metrics and feedback loops for self-monitoring and adjustment
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses inclusion of measurable indicators (e.g., weekly average mood volatility, count of trigger incidents, decision quality rating, relationship strain rating), scheduled reviews (weekly and monthly), and criteria to iterate the plan based on data.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Adherence supports and habit design integrated with existing tools
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report provides habit-stacking cues, environmental design (e.g., cue cards at desk), Trello/WeChat reminder setups, relapse plans, and accountability options (e.g., book club check-ins) to reduce friction and sustain practice.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Applicability to the user’s academic and cultural interpersonal contexts
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for lab-specific and culturally appropriate interpersonal strategies (e.g., de-escalation scripts with mentors/classmates, reflective pauses before responding, conflict checklists) that the user can directly apply in daily interactions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Safety boundaries and escalation guidance
actionability_practicality.explanation: Ensures the report sets clear thresholds for seeking professional help (e.g., persistent functional impairment, sleep/appetite disruptions, self-harm ideation) and provides local or online counseling options, enabling safe self-study.

depth_insight.criterion: Mechanistic Alignment of Recommendations to Emotional Dysregulation
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report explicitly maps books, courses, and practices to underlying mechanisms (e.g., cognitive reappraisal for hot cognitions, mindfulness for attentional control, DBT distress tolerance for acute arousal, ACT for experiential avoidance) and explains why these mechanisms address trigger identification, self-reflection, and regulation.

depth_insight.criterion: Rigor of Trigger Identification Framework
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report provides a systematic approach to uncovering triggers and patterns (e.g., ABC functional analysis, emotion episode logs with context-bodily-cognitive cues, emotion granularity/labeling, weekly pattern synthesis) rather than superficial self-observation.

depth_insight.criterion: Depth of Six-Month Integration and Progressive Skill-Building
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether resources and practices are staged and integrated (e.g., month-by-month skill progression, habit stacking, spaced retrieval, deliberate practice), showing how competencies build on each other and how books/courses/practices reinforce the same skills.

depth_insight.criterion: Personalization Logic and Risk Safeguards
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report offers decision rules to tailor the plan to individual factors (time, learning style, severity, comorbidities), includes alternatives, and flags thresholds for professional help (e.g., trauma cues, suicidality, impairment), reflecting nuanced judgment.

depth_insight.criterion: Measurement, Feedback, and Iterative Refinement
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report defines meaningful metrics (e.g., emotion intensity/frequency, recovery time, decision error rate, relationship conflict frequency), baseline setting, review cadence, and A/B testing of practices to adapt the plan based on data.

depth_insight.criterion: Translation to Decisions and Interpersonal Behaviors
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether insights are operationalized into concrete protocols that reduce poor decisions and strained relationships (e.g., cooling-off rules, pre-commitment checklists, DEAR MAN/NVC scripts, repair routines), demonstrating practical impact.

depth_insight.criterion: Evidence Base and Critical Appraisal
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether recommendations are grounded in established frameworks or empirical findings (e.g., CBT/DBT/ACT/mindfulness research), with brief recognition of limitations, boundary conditions, or contraindications.

depth_insight.criterion: Anticipation of Adherence Barriers and Relapse Prevention
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report identifies likely obstacles (ego depletion, emotional avoidance, schedule friction) and proposes specific countermeasures (if-then plans, environment design, social accountability, relapse normalization and recovery plans).

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and Value-Adding Synthesis
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report contributes unique frameworks or integrations (e.g., novel trigger taxonomy, cross-mapping resources to skills, creative habit design) that go beyond generic lists and enhance actionable insight.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report explicitly links the stated problems (emotional fluctuations affecting decisions/relationships) to goals (trigger identification, self-reflection, regulation), and organizes sections into a coherent framework (diagnosis → resource mapping → practice plan → feedback/iteration) with a six-month phased roadmap.

logical_coherence.criterion: Justified Mapping from Needs to Resources (Books/Courses/Strategies)
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether each recommended resource is explicitly tied to a specific sub-skill (e.g., mindfulness for awareness, CBT for cognitive reappraisal), with clear reasoning for selection, usage method, and placement in the schedule, avoiding unsupported or generic recommendations.

logical_coherence.criterion: Causal Reasoning from Practices to Expected Outcomes with Explicit Assumptions
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the report articulates how daily practices lead to outcomes via clear mechanisms (e.g., mindfulness → earlier trigger detection → timely regulation → fewer impulsive decisions) and states assumptions/conditions (frequency, duration, context) that make the reasoning testable.

Checks that the transitions between phases (e.g., baseline tracking → trigger analysis → technique selection → practice → review) are explicitly justified, prerequisites are identified, and criteria to move to the next phase are defined, avoiding logical jumps.

logical_coherence.criterion: Internal Consistency of Constructs, Criteria, and Tracking Logic
logical_coherence.explanation: Verifies consistent definitions of key terms (trigger, emotion, regulation), alignment between metrics/KPIs and goals across sections, and absence of contradictions (e.g., recommending incompatible techniques simultaneously without conditions).

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Organization and Navigability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report is organized into clear sections (e.g., Executive Summary, Books, Courses, Daily Practices, 6‑Month Roadmap, Troubleshooting), with a table of contents, descriptive headings, numbering, and internal links so readers can quickly find the month-by-month plan and specific resources.

clarity_readability.criterion: Formatting and Layout Cleanliness
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses visual hierarchy and legibility: consistent heading levels, adequate white space, readable font size/line spacing, concise paragraphs, bullet/numbered lists for steps, and well-aligned tables for resources (e.g., time commitment, level, focus) to reduce eye strain and scanning effort.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity, Brevity, and Supportive Tone
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates plain-language writing with short, active sentences and a nonjudgmental, encouraging tone; eliminates ambiguity and hedging so readers understand instructions and feel motivated to act without needing prior psychological expertise.

clarity_readability.criterion: Definition and Onboarding of Psychological Terms
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that terms like ‘cognitive reappraisal,’ ‘ABC model,’ ‘RAIN,’ ‘affect labeling,’ and ‘interoception’ are defined at first use with one‑sentence explanations and simple examples, preventing jargon from becoming a barrier to comprehension.

clarity_readability.criterion: Step-by-Step Instructional Clarity for Practices and Habits
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether routines (e.g., trigger mapping, journaling, breathing drills) are broken into numbered steps with time estimates, frequency, materials/templates, and ‘what to do next’ so the reader can execute them immediately without guesswork.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Visual Aids (Data, Charts, Frameworks)
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether visuals such as an emotion wheel, ABC/RAIN flowcharts, mood/trigger trackers, weekly habit calendars, and decision trees are clear, properly labeled, referenced in the text, and genuinely simplify understanding and implementation.

clarity_readability.criterion: Scannability and Emphasis of Key Information
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for clear emphasis on critical points via callouts, ‘This week’s focus,’ key takeaways, checklists, and bolded keywords; ensures no walls of text, enabling quick review and recall for busy readers.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency and Cross-Referencing
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses consistent naming (e.g., ‘Month 1/2/3’ vs. ‘Phase 1/2/3’), uniform labels for techniques, consistent table fields, working hyperlinks to courses/resources, and accurate references to sections and figures to avoid confusion.

clarity_readability.criterion: Accessibility and Device Friendliness
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks color contrast, color-blind–safe palettes (not relying solely on color), alt-text for key visuals, readable layouts on mobile, and availability of downloadable templates so all readers can comfortably use the report.

clarity_readability.criterion: Grammar, Spelling, and Terminology Accuracy
clarity_readability.explanation: Ensures error-free writing and consistent terminology/punctuation to prevent misunderstandings and maintain professional polish that supports reader trust and smooth comprehension.

task: Over the next six months, I plan to focus on improving my self-awareness and emotional management, as I often experience emotional fluctuations in daily life that lead to poor decision-making and strained interpersonal relationships. Please recommend self-study books, online courses, and practical strategies that can be integrated into daily habits to help me systematically identify emotional triggers, enhance self-reflection, and build effective emotional regulation mechanisms.

goal_alignment.criterion: Six-month phased roadmap integrated with morning routine and operations mindset
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan is organized into clear phases/sprints (e.g., monthly themes), with weekly routines that explicitly use her 7–9 AM window and stack onto yoga/gym habits. Look for OKR-like goals, checklists/templates, and time-boxed practices that fit her commute and 5-km life radius—matching her product ops style for adherence.

goal_alignment.criterion: Deep, context-specific trigger mapping using behavioral frameworks
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate if trigger identification goes beyond generic lists and leverages ABC/cognitive distortions/ACT concepts tailored to her literacy in psychology. It should include templates/logs and examples from product ops (release crunch, stakeholder conflict, PRD review, social media feedback) and family expectations—so insights feel personally diagnostic.

goal_alignment.criterion: Practical emotional regulation toolkit embedded in existing habits and environments
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for 2–10 minute tools that can be slotted into mornings, pre-meeting buffers, and post-work cooldowns: paced breathing, mindfulness on walks, yoga-based downregulation, body scan, HRV/biofeedback using her fitness tracker, art journaling, pet-assisted soothing with Meimei, and green exposure in community gardens.

goal_alignment.criterion: Decision hygiene protocols to reduce emotion-driven choices
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine whether the plan includes decision journaling, cooling-off rules, pre-mortem/checklists, if-then plans, and a consult-flow that preserves autonomy (given her tendency to seek advice for big decisions). Tie-ins to her rational consumer style (e.g., purchase delays) strengthen relevance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Interpersonal communication playbooks for workplace and family
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess the inclusion of scenario-specific scripts and drills: NVC, DBT (DEAR MAN, GIVE) for boundary-setting, feedback and conflict navigation with designers/engineers/PMs, and respectful conversations about career expectations at home. Scripts should reflect a young woman in China’s tech context.

goal_alignment.criterion: Resource curation aligned to language, platforms, and budget
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if books/courses/podcasts are evidence-based, Chinese-language or bilingual, and available on platforms she uses (Bilibili, NetEase/Coursera, 喜马拉雅/小宇宙 podcasts, Xiaohongshu creators). Prioritize free/low-cost options and long-video/podcast formats she prefers.

goal_alignment.criterion: Measurement and iteration with data-informed tracking
goal_alignment.explanation: Look for a concrete tracking system: daily mood ratings or PANAS, sleep/HRV from her wearable, trigger frequency, decision outcomes, and weekly retrospectives with monthly adjustments—implemented in Notion/Excel or a simple template—matching her comfort with metrics.

goal_alignment.criterion: Tone, motivation, and creative resonance
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content is compassionate and non-pathologizing (suited to low stress tolerance), includes self-compassion practices, and leverages her creative identity via art journaling/visual trackers. The pacing should prevent overwhelm and sustain motivation.

goal_alignment.criterion: Cultural and environmental tailoring to Beijing lifestyle and travel
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for tailoring to her 15-minute walk to work, community gardens/yoga studios, weekend hikes, quarterly trips to Nanjing and planned SE Asia travel—offering portable routines and contingency plans for disruptions, plus diet-related cues (vegetarian/healthy snacks) affecting mood.

goal_alignment.criterion: Safety and escalation guidance
goal_alignment.explanation: Ensure the plan names red flags (worsening anxiety, impaired functioning) and provides culturally relevant counseling/EAP directories and crisis steps, while clarifying scope (self-study vs. therapy). This protects her well-being without undermining autonomy.

content_alignment.criterion: Evidence-based psychological frameworks at an intermediate depth
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the recommended books/courses/practices are grounded in established frameworks (e.g., CBT cognitive restructuring, DBT emotion regulation and distress tolerance, ACT acceptance/values, mindfulness, emotional granularity, NVC) and presented at a depth suitable for a psychology minor—beyond pop-psych tips but not purely academic—ensuring conceptual fit with her background.

content_alignment.criterion: Morning routine and exercise/yoga integration
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether practices and content are naturally attachable to her 7–9 a.m. reading/meditation/exercise window and to yoga/jogging/Pilates (e.g., brief breathwork, pre/post-workout emotion labeling, short reflective prompts), aligning selection with her actual daily time blocks.

content_alignment.criterion: Mandarin-language and China-accessible resource localization
content_alignment.explanation: Check if books have Chinese editions/translations and if courses/podcasts/apps are available on platforms she uses or can access in Mainland China (Bilibili, NetEase Cloud Classroom, Coursera with CN subtitles, Ximalaya, Xiaohongshu/Weibo creators), ensuring platform and language fit.

content_alignment.criterion: Creative/visual modalities for self-reflection
