presentation_fit.explanation: Check for a clear executive summary, meaningful headings/subheadings, short paragraphs, numbered/bulleted lists, and an at-a-glance overview, enabling quick grasp and efficient navigation on mobile and desktop.

presentation_fit.criterion: Role- and age-segment tagging within each section
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether activities, facilities, and suggestions are visibly labeled (e.g., [孩子适合]/[老人友好]/[全家]) or equivalent cues so that suitability for children, elders, and whole family is instantly recognizable without re-reading.

presentation_fit.criterion: Time-blocked itinerary formatting aligned with routine and rest needs
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether days are organized into clear time blocks (morning/afternoon/evening) with explicit time stamps and buffer/rest windows, and whether alternative options (e.g., Plan A/B) are visually separated to reduce scheduling friction.

presentation_fit.criterion: Quantitative clarity: units, prices, and distance/time formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that distances are in km and walking/driving minutes, costs in CNY with per-night/per-person/per-family clearly stated, and any currency conversions or assumptions are labeled consistently to suit an engineering mindset.

presentation_fit.criterion: Comparison and decision visuals for hotels, beaches, and dining
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether key options are presented in structured comparison tables or bullet matrices with consistent fields (e.g., distance to beach, breakfast quality, accessibility), aiding quick trade-off assessment.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-first shareability and checklist formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether sections are chunked into screenshot-friendly blocks and essential lists (packing, medications, photography gear) are formatted as concise checklists that copy well into WeChat without losing structure.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Destination and beach suitability for multi-generational needs (July-specific)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report chooses and justifies a beach with gentle slope/small waves, soft sand, shade/umbrella availability, clean facilities, lifeguard presence and hours, jellyfish nets or alerts, UV/crowd conditions in July, and nearby rest areas—ensuring elders can rest comfortably while the child can play safely.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Door-to-door travel logistics feasibility from Harbin (peak-season ready)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan presents concrete transport options (HSR/flight/self-drive) with travel times, transfers, frequency, costs, recommended departure times, ticket/seat booking windows for July, baggage considerations, and buffers—so the family can actually secure seats and arrive with minimal fatigue.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Accommodation practicality and proximity
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses if hotels/apartments are within a short, flat walk to the beach (e.g., ≤5–10 min), have elevators/quiet rooms, family or connecting rooms, elder-friendly features (handrails, minimal stairs), quality breakfast, clear pet policy (pet-friendly or boarding alternatives), nearby clinic/pharmacy access, price/night ranges, cancellation terms, and booking channels.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Executable daily itinerary with rest windows, split-group options, and contingencies
actionability_practicality.explanation: Judges whether the report offers a realistic day-by-day schedule with morning beach time (heat avoidance), midday rest/nap, light late-afternoon activities, options for elders to rest while the child engages in safe activities, time estimates, walking distances, and rainy-day/heatwave backups within close range.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget transparency and cost-control levers
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for an itemized cost estimate (transport, lodging, meals, beach rentals, activities, tickets), total for the week and per person, July price premiums, and concrete savings tactics (advance purchase windows, weekday check-in, bundle passes, off-peak dining), enabling confident booking decisions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Dining feasibility and diversity near the base
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines whether the plan lists varied, nearby dining (walkable seafood, barbecue/Northeastern-style, light/healthy options), average spend, reservation/queue tips in July, kid-friendly dishes, elder-friendly seating/low-salt choices, breakfast arrangements, and nearby supermarkets/takeaway for simple family meals.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Safety and emergency preparedness in operation
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies that the plan provides practical safety steps: beach flag system, lifeguard hours, jellyfish/sea conditions, sun/heatstroke prevention, hydration, first-aid/meds checklist, child supervision rules and depth limits, storm/typhoon monitoring sources, and nearby hospital/clinic addresses and emergency numbers.

actionability_practicality.criterion: On-site mobility and accessibility for elders
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether on-the-ground movement is practical: flat routes, shaded rest spots/benches, shuttle or short taxi options, wheelchair/stroller access at key points, elevator/escalator availability, and realistic walking time caps—reducing strain and accommodating limited stamina.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Activity booking feasibility and age-appropriateness
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if age-suitable activities include concrete providers, ages/height limits, session times, prices, required reservations, and gear rental (e.g., umbrella/chair, beach toys, beginner water sports), plus low-effort options aligned with interests (sunrise/sunset photo spots, light fishing) and indoor backups, with clear booking steps.

depth_insight.criterion: Granular Needs Segmentation and Constraints Modeling
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report explicitly models the distinct needs of active children and rest-seeking elders (mobility, shade/cooling, safety, medical proximity), articulates assumptions if data are missing, and maps these needs to planning constraints (trip length, July heat/UV, travel time limits). This is foundational to producing insightful, personalized recommendations rather than generic lists.

depth_insight.criterion: Evidence-based Destination Suitability and Tradeoff Justification
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the chosen destination is justified via criteria that matter in July for all ages (calmness/swimmability, lifeguards/flags, beach slope and entry, ADA access/mats, shade availability, crowding, medical access, travel time, regional weather risks), including comparison of viable alternatives and transparent tradeoffs. Insight is shown by defending why this destination best fits the family profile.

depth_insight.criterion: Seasonality-aware Risk Analysis and Mitigation (July-specific)
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether the report anticipates July-specific risks (heat index/UV peaks, thunderstorms/hurricanes by region, jellyfish/sargassum, rip currents) and offers concrete mitigations (time-of-day scheduling, shaded setups, gear like rash guards/cooling towels, region selection to avoid sargassum, monitoring resources). This demonstrates depth through proactive, evidence-based planning.

depth_insight.criterion: Accommodation Fit Analysis and Location Strategy
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether accommodation recommendations are evaluated for multi-gen comfort and convenience (beachfront vs near-beach tradeoff, elevator access, quiet orientation, connecting rooms/bedroom layout, kitchen/laundry, kid amenities, grab bars, distance to beach by steps/grade, walkability to dining/groceries, parking). Insight is shown by aligning features to specific family needs.

depth_insight.criterion: Activity Portfolio Depth with Age-Appropriate Pacing and Parallel Tracks
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the activity plan balances energy levels and recovery (cooler morning water time, midday rest, evening low-impact options), includes safe and engaging options for kids and restful/low-impact options for elders, and enables parallel activities when interests diverge. Depth is reflected in realistic pacing and fit to local conditions.

depth_insight.criterion: Dining Strategy Depth: Diversity, Dietary Needs, and Peak-Season Tactics
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether dining suggestions go beyond listing restaurants to address diversity (seafood/local, familiar options), kid-friendly menus, soft/low-sodium or allergy-aware choices, and peak-season tactics (reservations, off-peak dining, proximity to lodging). Insight is evidenced by practicality and inclusiveness for all ages.

depth_insight.criterion: Budget and Value Optimization under Peak-Season Conditions
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report provides value judgments and strategies appropriate for July (booking windows, midweek stays, fee awareness like resort/parking, rental vs hotel tradeoffs, passes/bundles), maintaining comfort and safety while optimizing cost. This demonstrates analytical value beyond simple pricing.

depth_insight.criterion: Contingency and Scenario Planning (Weather, Health, Crowds)
depth_insight.explanation: Checks for thoughtful Plan B options (indoor/shaded activities, museum/aquarium days, trolley/boardwalk alternatives), guidance on cancellation/flex policies, and responses to health or beach closure scenarios. Insight is shown by anticipating disruptions and preserving enjoyment.

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and High-Value Micro-Insights
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses the presence of unique, actionable tips beyond common knowledge (e.g., local tide/UV/heat index planning, lifeguard flag system usage, sargassum or jellyfish forecast resources, ADA beach mat locations, gear rental hacks, early-bird beach timing). These elevate practical utility and reflect real expertise.

logical_coherence.criterion: Explicit Needs-and-Constraints Framing that Organizes the Plan
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report explicitly extracts user needs (all-ages suitability, one-week duration, July seasonality, beach comfort, convenience) and uses them to structure subsequent sections, ensuring all recommendations trace back to stated requirements.

logical_coherence.criterion: Transparent Decision Framework for Destination and Lodging Selection
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the report lays out clear selection criteria (e.g., surf calmness, shade/lifeguards, accessibility for elderly, proximity to healthcare, family amenities) and shows how alternatives are compared to arrive at the chosen destination and accommodations.

Evaluates whether claims about suitability (e.g., beach crowd levels in July, water conditions, distances from lodging to beach, dining variety within walking distance) are supported by relevant facts or sources, creating a coherent link between assertions and support.

Checks that the weekly itinerary follows a plausible sequence with realistic travel times, rest-activity balance for children and elderly, time-of-day logic for heat/UV, and no conflicting commitments, ensuring conclusions follow from practical constraints.

logical_coherence.criterion: Coherent Transitions and Cross-Referencing Between Sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether each section naturally builds on the previous (needs → criteria → selections → itinerary → dining/activity mapping), with explicit references that prevent logical jumps or isolated recommendations.

logical_coherence.criterion: Consistency, Trade-off Handling, and Contingency Logic
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates internal consistency across sections (no contradictions in times, locations, or constraints), explicit handling of trade-offs (e.g., calmer waters vs. dining variety), and logically tied contingency plans for July-specific risks (heat, storms, crowds).

clarity_readability.criterion: Structured day-by-day and sectioned layout for scannability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for a clear heading hierarchy and logical sections (e.g., Overview, Destination, Accommodations, Dining, Activities by age group, Transportation, Safety, Packing) plus day-by-day itinerary headings and a TOC. This enables readers to quickly find what they need without wading through dense text.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language clarity, brevity, and error-free writing
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses concise, unambiguous sentences, consistent tense and person, appropriate reading level, and absence of typos/grammar errors. Clear, plain language reduces misinterpretation when making on-the-spot travel decisions.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of tables, maps, and visual aids
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether itinerary tables, age–activity matrices, accommodation comparison tables, distance/time maps, and simple budget/weather visuals are well-labeled, have legible legends/units, and are readable on mobile/print. Good visuals compress logistics into an at-a-glance format.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of critical operational information
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks use of callouts/icons/bolding for must-know items (addresses, check-in/out times, reservation windows, cancellation deadlines, emergency contacts, lifeguard/shade availability, UV index). Emphasis helps users avoid missing actionable details.

clarity_readability.criterion: Summaries and quick-reference aids
clarity_readability.explanation: Looks for an executive summary, at-a-glance daily snapshots, packing checklists (with kids/seniors notes), dining shortlists by proximity, and simple Plan B (rainy day) boxes. These aids speed up understanding and decision-making.

clarity_readability.criterion: Accessibility and usability for mixed ages
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates presentation features that support all ages: clear labels for step-free/stroller/wheelchair access, quiet rest spots, readable font sizes and contrast, colorblind-safe palettes, icons paired with text, and a printable version. This ensures inclusive readability.

clarity_readability.criterion: Handling and explanation of terms and symbols
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that travel/safety/accessibility terms (e.g., Blue Flag, rip current, ADA-accessible, lifeguarded), icons, and age bands are defined or accompanied by a legend. Explaining jargon prevents confusion for non-expert readers.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency of formats, units, and labels
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses consistent date/time format (12h/24h), currency, distance/temperature units, age group labels, and venue naming across sections. Consistency avoids cognitive load and misreading.

clarity_readability.criterion: Navigation and cross-reference clarity
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates TOC/anchors, descriptive link text (e.g., 'Map to Beach A' rather than 'click here'), cross-references from summaries to detailed sections, and visible page/section IDs. Good navigation speeds retrieval of details.

clarity_readability.criterion: Information density, spacing, and list use
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for adequate white space, sensible paragraph length, bullet/numbered lists for steps, captions for visuals, and avoidance of cluttered tables. Comfortable spacing prevents fatigue during prolonged use.

task: I plan to spend one week at the beach with my family in July. The destination should be suitable for all ages—active children and elderly family members who need rest. Please create a personalized travel plan recommending comfortable beaches, convenient accommodations, diverse dining options, and age-appropriate activities so that everyone can enjoy the trip.

goal_alignment.criterion: July-safe, multigenerational destination fit from Beijing
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the chosen destination(s) explicitly match July conditions (UV/heat, regional typhoons), have gentle-slope, lifeguarded beaches, shaded promenades, barrier-free access, quiet zones for elderly rest, and named nearby hospitals/pharmacies—plus practical access from Beijing (direct HSR/flight) to reduce travel strain.

goal_alignment.criterion: Age-segmented, rhythm-balanced 7-day plan with contingencies
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate if the itinerary structures mornings/evenings for active kids, protects a fixed midday rest window for elderly, times beach use to avoid peak UV, limits daily walking, and includes indoor/rain-day alternatives—reflecting her disciplined routine and caregiving mindset.

goal_alignment.criterion: Accommodation selection aligned to cleanliness, proximity, and accessibility
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether lodging is within a short, flat walk to the beach (≈10 minutes), offers elevators/barrier-free rooms, quiet floors/rooms for naps, connecting/suite options for family cohesion, high hygiene ratings and service quality, and flexible cancellation—matching her standards and elderly needs.

goal_alignment.criterion: Nursing-informed health and safety measures
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if the plan provides an evidence-based first-aid checklist (including sun/heatstroke, minor cuts, jellyfish stings), SPF/UPF guidance, hydration/electrolyte plan, lifeguard hours/flags, water quality advisories, emergency routes and hospital names; includes neck-care tips given her cervical strain.

goal_alignment.criterion: Beijing-origin transport and local mobility convenience
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether options compare direct HSR vs flights vs self-drive with times/transfers, luggage handling, last-mile to hotel, and on-site mobility (shuttles, taxis, golf carts), plus rental info for strollers/wheelchairs—minimizing transfers and physical burden for elderly.

goal_alignment.criterion: Dining plan matching low-oil/low-salt and family diversity
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for seafood venues and menus emphasizing steamed/boiled/grilled-light options, low-salt preorder instructions, elder-friendly soft dishes, kid-friendly choices, breakfast variety, hygiene ratings/clean kitchens, and peak-season reservation/queue strategies—aligned with her dietary and quality priorities.

goal_alignment.criterion: Budget-value transparency and peak-season booking strategy
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan provides a realistic July cost breakdown (transport/hotel/food/activities), early-bird tips, weekday vs weekend price differences, cancellation/refund policies, and travel insurance guidance—suiting her rational, risk-averse spending style without sacrificing comfort.

goal_alignment.criterion: Decision support via persona-relevant option comparison
goal_alignment.explanation: Look for 2–3 candidate beaches with pros/cons tailored to her (crowd levels, sea conditions, medical access, travel time, costs), a clear recommendation with rationale, and prompts to consult family (e.g., elders’ walking tolerance, kids’ activity preferences).

goal_alignment.criterion: Usability and communication style fit
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether content is structured into scannable sections, checklists, time blocks, and action steps (what to book and when), suitable for WeChat saving/screenshots; tone should be calm, professional, empathetic—matching her nurse identity and planning habits.

goal_alignment.criterion: Cultural and light-wellness enrichment for all ages
goal_alignment.explanation: Check inclusion of nearby museums/old towns/markets, seaside squares suitable for evening square dance, sunrise gentle yoga/stretching, easy boardwalks with benches—balancing her cultural interests and the family’s mixed activity levels.

goal_alignment.criterion: Pet-care planning consideration
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify that the plan acknowledges her cat by suggesting reputable boarding/sitter options in Beijing with vaccination requirements, or clarifies hotel pet policies if considering travel with pets—reducing pre-trip friction and aligning with recent pet-focused behavior.

content_alignment.criterion: Beijing-origin and July-season-aware beach shortlisting
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether destinations are curated with Beijing departure convenience and July conditions (heat, crowds, typhoon risk) in mind, prioritizing family-friendly beaches with gentle slopes, lifeguards, good water quality, shade, and nearby medical access—core to a safe, comfortable multigenerational trip.

content_alignment.criterion: Age-spectrum activity matching with time-of-day sensitivity
content_alignment.explanation: Check if activities are distinctly tailored for teens/young adults (e.g., water sports, cycling) and elders (e.g., shaded boardwalks, tea houses, scenic rides), plus shared family bonding options, scheduled around UV/heat windows (early morning/evening) to suit all ages' comfort.

content_alignment.criterion: Health- and safety-informed content aligned to head nurse profile
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan integrates evidence-based health considerations (UV index windows, hydration/heatstroke precautions, water quality cues, first-aid readiness, proximity to clinics/hospitals) and ergonomic rest tips (e.g., pillow options), matching her professional mindset and risk aversion.

content_alignment.criterion: Accommodation cleanliness, service quality, and accessibility fit
content_alignment.explanation: Determine if lodging options emphasize verified cleanliness, attentive service, quiet floors, elevators/barrier-free paths, family suites/interconnecting rooms, and healthy breakfast choices—aligned with her high standards and elders’ mobility/rest needs.

content_alignment.criterion: Dining aligned with healthy, low-oil/low-salt habits and Jiangnan/seafood preferences
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether dining content highlights steamed/boiled/low-salt preparations, fresh seafood, vegetable-forward menus, and eateries willing to adjust seasoning, with nods to Jiangnan flavors—matching her dietary focus and cultural palate.

content_alignment.criterion: Decision-support depth for a rational, self-guided planner
content_alignment.explanation: Check for 2–3 well-matched destination options presented with pros/cons tied to her family’s needs (climate, beach profile, crowd levels, accessibility, medical proximity), offering comparison frameworks rather than a one-size-fits-all pick.

content_alignment.criterion: Routine-aware pacing (early start, midday rest, evening activities)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the itinerary accommodates her habit of early mornings, an afternoon nap/rest period for elders, and evening options (e.g., waterfront strolls, square dance-friendly plazas), reflecting her daily rhythm and recovery needs.

content_alignment.criterion: Pet-related alignment (bring-the-cat vs. reliable boarding)
content_alignment.explanation: Assess if the plan acknowledges her recent pet focus by offering either pet-friendly travel logistics and beach/hotel policies or reputable boarding options in Beijing, enabling an informed choice without forcing pet travel.

content_alignment.criterion: Cultural enrichment near the beach
content_alignment.explanation: Check for inclusion of nearby cultural experiences (historic districts, museums, tea culture) that are low-exertion and elder-friendly, aligning with her interest in natural plus cultural sites and Jiangnan-influenced tastes.

content_alignment.criterion: Value-conscious, quality-first positioning
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether recommendations reflect mid to upper-midrange, high-quality options with clear value logic (cleanliness/service reputation), matching her rational spending and quality preference without pushing ultra-luxury or bargain-basement choices.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-first structure and scannability (clear sections, bullet points, day-by-day itinerary)
presentation_fit.explanation: Ensures the report is easy to read on a phone in WeChat: clear headings, short paragraphs, bulleted lists, and a structured 7-day plan help a busy head nurse quickly scan and act.

presentation_fit.criterion: Decision-first executive summary at the top
presentation_fit.explanation: A concise TL;DR with destination shortlist and key rationale supports her decisive style and allows quick family alignment before deep reading.

presentation_fit.criterion: Language tone and register fit (plain Mandarin, warm yet professional, no hype/slang)
presentation_fit.explanation: As a healthcare professional and family decision-maker, she needs calm, respectful, and concise language that feels trustworthy and family-oriented.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology simplicity and localization of units
presentation_fit.explanation: Uses everyday travel terms with brief clarifications; formats time in 24-hour, currency in CNY, distances in km, and weather/UV in familiar terms to reduce cognitive effort.

presentation_fit.criterion: Family suitability labels (age/intensity/accessibility tags on activities and hotels)
presentation_fit.explanation: Clear labels such as ‘Kids-friendly’, ‘Elder-friendly’, ‘Low walking’, ‘Shade available’ let her quickly match options to different family members’ needs.

presentation_fit.criterion: Screenshot- and share-friendly modularization
presentation_fit.explanation: Self-contained cards (daily plan, hotel shortlist, dining map notes) and checklists that stand alone make it easy to screenshot and share in WeChat family groups.

presentation_fit.criterion: Text-based visual aids (comparison tables, timelines, simple icons)
presentation_fit.explanation: Lightweight tables for Option A/B and timeline-style day schedules improve clarity on mobile without requiring images, aiding quick comparison.

presentation_fit.criterion: Health and safety callout formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Dedicated callouts for July heat/UV hydration/rest timing and accessibility considerations match her nurse-level risk communication expectations without cluttering the main text.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Door-to-door transport feasibility and time–cost transparency
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides clear, low-friction routes from Beijing (e.g., direct flight vs high-speed rail), total door-to-door time, transfers, seasonal schedules, and cost ranges for all travelers. This enables the family—especially elderly members—to choose comfortable, efficient travel and secure tickets in peak July.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Accommodation suitability for multi-generational needs and beach proximity
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if recommended hotels/resorts are within easy walking distance to the beach, have elevators or ground-floor options, quiet rooms, family suites/connected rooms, reliable cleanliness/service, laundry, shaded pool/relaxation areas, and clear cancellation policies. This directly impacts daily comfort and rest quality for elderly and children.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Itinerary pacing and rest design for all ages
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the 7-day plan sets realistic daily timeboxes, includes midday rest/nap and shade breaks, avoids over-scheduling in summer heat, and sequences nearby activities to reduce transit fatigue. Practical pacing is essential for elderly comfort and sustained enjoyment for kids.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Age-appropriate activities with logistics and safety details
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for concrete activity options for children, adults, and elderly with age/height limits, duration, difficulty, required gear, pricing, distance from lodging, lifeguard presence, and reservation steps. This ensures activities can be safely booked and executed by each age group.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Dining practicality and health suitability near lodging
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for family-friendly restaurants within short distance of the hotel (or delivery options), price per person, hygiene ratings, reservation advice, kid menus, and low-oil/low-salt choices suited to the user’s diet habits. Practical dining avoids long waits and supports healthy eating.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget transparency with totals and alternatives
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a clear total trip estimate and per-category breakdown (transport, lodging, food, activities), plus two budget tiers (e.g., midrange vs higher-comfort) and refund/change policies. This enables rational, quality-focused decision-making without budget surprises.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Peak-season operability: reservations, crowd avoidance, and weather contingencies
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan specifies when to book lodging/transport/activities, strategies for avoiding crowds (early/late beach times), and Plan B options for rain/typhoons/jellyfish/algae, including indoor alternatives. July success hinges on these operational precautions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Health, safety, and accessibility provisions
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for beach flag systems and lifeguard hours, sun/heat protection guidance, hydration points, jellyfish season notes, nearby clinic/hospital contacts, and basic accessibility info (ramps, gentle gradients). This reduces risk for elderly and aligns with the user’s health-conscious profile.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Within-destination mobility and convenience
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses clarity of airport/rail transfers, hotel shuttle or ride-hailing pickup points, local transit options, walking times, and parking guidance if needed. Clear last-mile logistics prevent delays and fatigue across the week.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Booking readiness and pre-trip checklist
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for direct booking links or reputable platforms, recommended booking timeline, required documents, travel insurance suggestions, and a packing list (sun protection, swim gear, simple first-aid). Optional note on pet care arrangements improves real-world readiness.

depth_insight.criterion: Destination Suitability Analysis and Selection Rationale (July + Multi-generational fit)
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report evaluates and justifies the chosen beach destination(s) using July-specific evidence (water temperature, UV/heat, crowding, sea conditions, lifeguard presence, accessibility features, medical proximity) and explicitly balances children’s activity needs with seniors’ comfort and access.

depth_insight.criterion: Seasonality and Risk Assessment with Mitigation and Contingencies
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates depth in identifying July risks (e.g., hurricanes, sargassum, jellyfish, rip currents, heat waves) and proposing concrete mitigation (timing, location selection, equipment) plus backup plans for adverse weather/sea conditions.

depth_insight.criterion: Personalization via Explicit Assumptions/Elicitation and Trade-off Justification
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether the plan surfaces key unknowns (ages, mobility, nap schedules, dietary needs, budget, travel time) and either elicits or transparently assumes them, then makes and justifies trade-offs (e.g., calm bays vs surf, proximity vs price) to tailor recommendations.

depth_insight.criterion: Accommodation Solution Depth for Convenience and Comfort
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether accommodation choices are justified beyond brand names: walk-to-beach distance, elevator/ADA access, ground-floor/quiet rooms, suite layout for naps, kitchen/washer, shaded pool, blackout curtains, and booking strategies relevant to July peak season.

depth_insight.criterion: Age-Appropriate Activity Programming with Pacing and Time-of-day Strategy
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the thoughtfulness of activity design across ages, including energy management (alternating high/low-exertion days), co-located options, supervision needs, intergenerational activities, and timing to avoid heat/UV and crowds (e.g., early/late beach sessions).

depth_insight.criterion: On-the-ground Logistics and Comfort Optimization
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses practical, high-impact insights: airport transfers vs car, car seats/strollers, beach wheelchair access and reservations, ADA beach mats, gear rentals, shade solutions, lifeguard hours, pharmacy/urgent care access, and micro-tactics that minimize friction for all ages.

depth_insight.criterion: Dining Strategy Tailored to Family Needs
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the depth of dining recommendations beyond listings: proximity to lodging, early seating to avoid crowds, kids menus, allergen-aware options, healthy choices, local cuisine exposure, reservations strategy, and breakfast-in-room setups to reduce morning friction.

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and Decision Value of Insights
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards unique, evidence-based insights that materially improve the trip (e.g., sargassum/UV forecasts, tide tables, microclimate choices, shaded walking routes, nap-friendly room configurations), going beyond generic advice to create clear decision advantages.

logical_coherence.criterion: Explicit translation of user requirements into planning premises and structure
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report explicitly extracts the July timing, one-week duration, multigenerational needs (active kids, rest for elderly), comfortable beaches, convenient accommodations, diverse dining, and age-appropriate activities, and uses these as organizing premises that drive the plan’s structure.

logical_coherence.criterion: Option filtering and destination selection rationale
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether candidate beach destinations are screened against the stated premises using explicit criteria (e.g., surf calmness, shade/lifeguards, medical access, heat/crowd levels, travel time), with clear trade-offs and a justified final choice that follows from the screening.

logical_coherence.criterion: Itinerary temporal–spatial feasibility and internal consistency
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the day-by-day plan sequences activities logically with realistic durations and travel times, includes rest windows for elders, active blocks for children, and avoids contradictions (e.g., overlapping times, excessive transit on rest days).

logical_coherence.criterion: Justified linkage between recommendations and relevant evidence/constraints
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that each recommendation (beach, hotel, dining, activities) is backed by succinct, relevant reasons tied to the premises (e.g., gentle slope beach for kids, elevators and shade for elders, proximity to diverse eateries), avoiding unsubstantiated assertions.

logical_coherence.criterion: Coherent transitions and dependency handoffs between sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether outputs of one section inform the next (e.g., lodging location constrains dining and activity choices; transport timing informs daily schedule), with explicit references that prevent logical jumps between sections.

logical_coherence.criterion: Explicit assumptions, constraints, and contingency logic
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks for stated assumptions (typical July weather, crowd patterns), constraints (mobility limits, heat tolerance), and clear fallback plans (indoor or low-heat alternatives, rainy-day activities) with triggers, ensuring reasoning accounts for uncertainty.

logical_coherence.criterion: Synthesis and decision traceability in the final plan
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the final summarized plan and key decisions (destination, lodging zone, daily cadence) are traceable to earlier premises and analyses, without introducing new criteria at the end.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Organization and Scannability (sections and day-by-day layout)
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses clear section headers (e.g., Beaches, Lodging, Dining, Activities, Transport, Safety) and a day-by-day itinerary with bullet points, time blocks, and adequate white space so readers can quickly locate relevant parts and skim efficiently.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity, Fluency, and Conciseness of Language
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses use of plain, concise sentences and unambiguous wording; avoids run-ons and overly flowery prose so non-expert readers (including older adults) can understand instructions and options without rereading.

clarity_readability.criterion: Use and Explanation of Travel-Specific Terms and Labels
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether necessary terms (e.g., Blue Flag beach, rip currents, all-inclusive, resort fee, ADA-accessible) are used consistently and briefly defined at first mention or in a mini-glossary to reduce comprehension barriers.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Visual Aids (tables, maps, matrices, checklists)
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether comparison tables (lodging, dining), activity matrices (by age/mobility), simple maps (walk times), and concise checklists have clear titles, legends, and captions, and whether they present key facts at a glance without clutter.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Key Information and Quick-Reference Elements
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether the report surfaces must-know items (safety notes, booking windows, peak-hour tips) via callouts, summaries, or bolding, and provides at-a-glance boxes for essentials (daily overview, packing list) to reduce cognitive load.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency of Units, Formats, and Notation
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether times, dates, currencies, distances, and temperatures are presented consistently (e.g., 12h vs 24h time; miles/km, USD/local currency, °C/°F) and converted when helpful to avoid confusion during planning and on-trip use.

clarity_readability.criterion: Audience Tagging for Age and Mobility Suitability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether activities, beaches, and lodging are labeled with clear, consistent tags (e.g., Kids, Teens, Adults, Seniors, Mobility-friendly) and a brief legend, enabling each family member to find suitable options quickly.

task: I plan to spend one week at the beach with my family in July. The destination should be suitable for all ages—active children and elderly family members who need rest. Please create a personalized travel plan recommending comfortable beaches, convenient accommodations, diverse dining options, and age-appropriate activities so that everyone can enjoy the trip.

goal_alignment.criterion: Multi-generational suitability and safety by design
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the plan deliberately selects calm, gentle-slope beaches with lifeguards, shade and benches, barrier-free access for elders/strollers, short walking distances, and nearby clinics; includes age-appropriate pacing (midday rest), child-safe shallow play/creative experiences, and low-impact relaxation for elders. This matches Zhou Jun’s family composition (active child, elders needing rest) and his risk-controlled, pragmatic style.

goal_alignment.criterion: Chengdu-origin logistics efficiency and low-friction travel
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses if routes prioritize direct flights from Chengdu, minimal transfers, airport-to-hotel transfer times (e.g., ≤45–60 min), limited hotel changes, and time-blocked arrivals/departures that suit a family with elders/child. This aligns with his efficiency mindset and preference for self-planned, low-hassle itineraries.

goal_alignment.criterion: July-season adaptation and weather/UV contingency planning
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan accounts for high UV/heat, thunderstorms/typhoons (e.g., in Hainan), crowd peaks, jellyfish risks, and proposes morning/evening beach windows, shaded indoor alternatives, and clear plan-B triggers (weather thresholds, refundable bookings). This fits his risk management orientation and ensures family safety/comfort.

goal_alignment.criterion: Accommodation fit for family and elder accessibility
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks if recommended stays balance his preference for quiet, scenic homestays/family suites with practical features for elders (elevators, few stairs, accessible bathrooms), proximity to the beach (≤10–15 min walk), kids’ club/play area, shaded pool, noise control, and nearby medical facilities.

goal_alignment.criterion: Decision-ready structure and comparative rationale
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the article compares 2–3 suitable destinations (e.g., Sanya Yalong Bay vs. Dalian vs. Xiamen/Qingdao) with concrete metrics (flight time from Chengdu, wave conditions, UV/crowd levels, hospital proximity, estimated budget), then gives a clear, justified final choice and a buffered day-by-day outline. Mirrors his analytical, efficiency-driven decision style.

goal_alignment.criterion: Budget transparency and high-value strategy for peak season
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates clarity of price bands for flights/hotels/activities/dining in July, total budget estimates, cost-saving levers (early booking, weekday stays, family packages), and flexible cancellation policies. Aligns with his rational spending, planning habits, and risk aversion.

goal_alignment.criterion: Dining diversity aligned to family tastes and health
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for a mix of local seafood and readily available Sichuan options, plus low-oil/low-salt dishes for elders and kid-friendly menus. Includes hygiene standards, reservation advice, and convenient locations near lodging. Matches his food preferences and elder/kid needs.

goal_alignment.criterion: Sustainability and light-travel integration
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses suggestions for direct flights/train where viable, eco-certified hotels, reef-safe sunscreen, reusable bottles/utensils, minimal disposable beach toys, and sustainable seafood choices. Reflects his recent eco-focus and preference for light travel.

goal_alignment.criterion: Pet travel decision support (bring or board)
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan clarifies pet policy considerations for a large-breed dog (hotel/beach rules, airline rules, heat risks) and offers either pet-friendly options or vetted boarding services in Chengdu with vaccination/booking guidance. Acknowledges the family’s attachment to Dundan and prevents last-minute friction.

content_alignment.criterion: Age-tailored activity alignment (child creative/educational, elderly restorative, parents’ fitness)
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether activities explicitly reflect the six-year-old’s interests (e.g., art/creative, gentle exploration), provide low-exertion, shaded rest options for elderly, and include light fitness options (jogging/yoga) for the user—matching the family’s composition and preferences.

content_alignment.criterion: Beach selection for July comfort and accessibility (gentle surf, shade, lifeguards, medical proximity)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether chosen beaches are comfortable and safe for kids and elderly in peak-summer conditions, prioritizing calm waters, clean facilities, shaded areas, lifeguard coverage, and nearby clinics—directly aligned with ‘suitable for all ages’ and July realities.

content_alignment.criterion: Accommodation fit to quiet, scenic family stays with accessibility and kid amenities
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether lodging matches the user’s preference for quiet scenic homestays/mid-to-high comfort, offers barrier-free/elevator access for elderly, is within short walking distance to the beach, and provides child-friendly amenities (e.g., kids’ club, family rooms).

content_alignment.criterion: Dining alignment to Sichuan palate, healthy eating, and diverse cuisines with child-friendly choices
content_alignment.explanation: Checks that dining content accounts for spicy preferences and healthy options (low-oil/low-salt), safe and fresh seafood in summer, kid menus, and opportunities to try other cuisines (e.g., Thai/Italian)—matching the family’s tastes and health focus.

content_alignment.criterion: Evidence-backed, value-focused curation (ratings, distances, July price bands)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether recommendations are grounded in relevant metrics—user ratings/reviews, beach water quality or cleanliness indicators, walking distances/transfer times, and peak-season price ranges—fitting the user’s rational, review-oriented, value mindset.

content_alignment.criterion: Sustainability and low-carbon alignment in choices
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses inclusion of eco-certified accommodations, public transit/shuttle options, low-impact activities, and waste/plastic reduction tips—reflecting the user’s growing eco-conscious consumption and light-travel inclination.

content_alignment.criterion: Immersive local culture and educational elements
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the plan incorporates local culture/learning experiences (e.g., markets, museums, marine ecology/ aquariums, hands-on workshops) that align with the family’s immersive travel preference and support the child’s educational growth.

content_alignment.criterion: Origin-based feasibility from Chengdu (access and transfer convenience)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether destination choices acknowledge Chengdu-origin travel, prioritizing direct flights/HSR and short airport-to-hotel transfers to reduce fatigue for elderly and a child—matching the user’s efficiency preference.

content_alignment.criterion: Pet-related consideration (pet-friendly options or reliable boarding alternatives)
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the plan acknowledges the family’s attachment to a large dog by either identifying pet-friendly stays (if self-driving) or suggesting reputable boarding near home—addressing a unique household factor.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structural clarity and navigability of the report
presentation_fit.explanation: Check if the report opens with an executive summary, uses clear section headings (e.g., Destination Rationale, Day-by-Day Itinerary, Accommodation, Dining, Activities), and provides a logical flow with numbered subsections so Zhou Jun can scan and coordinate quickly.

presentation_fit.criterion: Time-structured itinerary with intensity/rest cues
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether daily plans are presented with time stamps (24-hour format), clear activity duration, and explicit labels indicating activity intensity and rest windows (e.g., ‘Low walking load,’ ‘Nap/Shade time’) suitable for children and elderly.

presentation_fit.criterion: Language tone and conciseness fit
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the tone is pragmatic, objective, and free of hype; sentences are concise and action-oriented, appropriate for a manager accustomed to analytical content and quick decision-making.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology, units, and bilingual place naming
presentation_fit.explanation: Check if technical terms (e.g., UV index, rip current, accessibility) are briefly clarified, units are standardized (°C, km, minutes, RMB), time is in 24-hour format, and place names include Chinese/English (and/or pinyin) to ease search and booking.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-friendly formatting for WeChat/DingTalk reading
presentation_fit.explanation: Verify use of short paragraphs, bullet lists, and minimal heavy tables; ensure key information fits into screen-friendly blocks for easy skimming and screenshotting on mobile devices.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visual summaries and tagging system
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate presence of at-a-glance boxes (e.g., ‘Daily Overview’, ‘Logistics Snapshot’), light comparison templates, and consistent tags/icons for ‘Kids-friendly’, ‘Accessibility’, ‘Shade’, and ‘Quiet’ to speed comprehension without overloading text.

presentation_fit.criterion: Safety and accessibility callouts formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether safety notes (heat/UV, tide times) and accessibility cues (elevators, ramps, seating availability) are clearly highlighted with distinct callouts or labels so elderly-friendly considerations are unmistakable.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Decision-ready destination selection for July (final choice + viable backups)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report narrows to one recommended beach destination (plus 1–2 backups) with explicit July suitability: door-to-door time from Chengdu, climate/typhoon risk, crowd levels, and a clear rationale so the user can quickly choose and proceed.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Door-to-door transportation plan feasibility from Chengdu
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for specific flight/rail options with typical July durations and prices, minimal transfers, child-friendly departure times, and airport–hotel transfers (e.g., shuttle/ride-hailing with child seat), reducing friction for elders and a 6-year-old.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Accommodation practicality for multi-generational needs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether concrete hotels/homestays are named with room types (family suite/connecting rooms), elevator access, noise level, walking minutes to beach, on-site kids’ club/pool/quiet areas, breakfast, cancellation policy, and July price ranges.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Beach comfort and safety specifics
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for named beaches with gentle waves/slope, lifeguard hours, shaded areas/umbrella rentals, clean toilets/showers, accessibility features, water quality notes, and recommended visiting times to avoid heat—critical for kid and elder safety and comfort.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Feasible multi-generational itinerary with heat-avoidance and buffers
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses if a 7-day schedule includes morning/evening beach time, midday rest, realistic transit times, and parallel age-appropriate options (e.g., kids’ workshops vs elders’ spa/tea) to prevent overexertion and ensure everyone participates.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Dining practicality near the accommodation
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for a shortlist of walkable/short-ride restaurants with kids’ options and elder-friendly light dishes, approximate prices, peak-season reservation tips/queue strategies, and inclusion of familiar flavors (e.g., Sichuan and seafood) to ensure mealtime ease.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget transparency and cost-control strategy
actionability_practicality.explanation: Requires an itemized one-week estimate (transport, lodging, food, local transport, activities) with July surcharges, concrete savings levers (early booking, weekday check-in, multi-night discounts, cards/coupons), and refundable booking recommendations.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Weather and seasonality contingency planning
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates rain/typhoon backup plans (indoor activities nearby), monitoring tools (apps/alerts), flexible cancellation policies, travel insurance suggestions, and heat-mitigation measures (UV index timing, hydration), which are vital in July.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Accessibility and medical readiness
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks proximity (in minutes) to clinics/hospitals and pharmacies, emergency numbers, and wheelchair/stroller-friendly routes/venues, minimizing risk for elders and ensuring quick response to health issues.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Pet-care or pet-travel executable plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a clear choice path: pet-friendly accommodation/transport and beach rules if bringing the dog, or reputable Chengdu boarding options with vaccination/booking requirements if not, avoiding last-minute issues.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Booking timeline and execution checklists
actionability_practicality.explanation: Requires a July-specific booking schedule (how many weeks in advance for flights/hotels/activities), plus tailored checklists (documents, sun/med kits, kid/elder items, sand toys, repellents, cooling towels) to streamline execution.

depth_insight.criterion: Multigenerational Suitability Analysis of Destination and Beach Conditions (July-specific)
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report selects and justifies the destination/beaches using July-relevant, mixed-age criteria: climate/heat index, water temperature and surf/currents, lifeguard coverage, crowd levels, shade/restroom availability, step-free access, proximity to medical care, and trade-offs across candidate locations.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk and Seasonality Awareness with Concrete Mitigation
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates insight into July risks (extreme heat/UV, thunderstorms/hurricanes, sargassum/red tide, jellyfish, peak-season capacity) and whether the plan embeds practical mitigations (time-of-day scheduling, shaded setups/cabanas, hydration/cooling, rain/indoor backups, cancellation policies/insurance, alternate beaches).

depth_insight.criterion: Accommodation Selection Depth for Mixed Ages
depth_insight.explanation: Examines how well lodging recommendations map to multigenerational needs: beachfront vs. quiet trade-offs, room configuration (suites/connecting rooms), elevators/ADA access, shaded pool and quiet rest zones, kitchenettes/laundry, crib availability, beach wheelchairs/cabanas, and walk-time reductions for elders and kids.

depth_insight.criterion: Age-Appropriate, Balanced Activity Planning
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether activities and the weekly cadence reflect energy and rest needs: calm-water play and beginner lessons for kids, low-exertion and shaded options for elders, multigenerational experiences, parallel tracks, heat-aware scheduling, and viable indoor alternatives for weather or fatigue.

depth_insight.criterion: Dining Ecosystem Insight and Peak-Season Strategy
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates depth in dining recommendations: diversity and local flavor, children’s menus and elder-friendly options, allergy/medical diets, reservation/off-peak tactics for July crowds, proximity to lodging/beach, takeout or picnic plans to reduce waits, and cost/value considerations.

depth_insight.criterion: Travel Logistics and Accessibility Trade-off Analysis
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether transport and on-the-ground mobility burdens are analyzed: flight/drive duration and connections with kids/elders, airport proximity, transfers and car seats, parking, walkability, stroller/wheelchair suitability, beach access mats/ramps, and comparisons that justify the chosen setup.

depth_insight.criterion: Personalization and Assumption Handling
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates how the plan deals with missing family specifics by eliciting key preferences (budget, mobility, nap schedules, risk tolerance) or stating reasonable assumptions and offering adaptive options or decision points that materially tailor recommendations.

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and Local Insider Value
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards unique, high-impact insights beyond common advice: lesser-known calm coves or shaded parks, tide/wind timing tips, specific vendors (beach wheelchair or cabana rentals), crowd-avoidance tactics, and booking hacks that improve comfort and enjoyment.

logical_coherence.criterion: Articulation and Use of Goals, Constraints, and Assumptions
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the plan explicitly states user goals (all-ages suitability, July beach, comfort, convenience, diverse dining, age-appropriateness) and any assumptions (budget range, mobility limits, transport mode), and then consistently uses them as decision criteria throughout the plan.

logical_coherence.criterion: Destination Selection Logic Aligned with July and Family Profile
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the chosen destination(s) are justified by seasonality (July weather, crowd levels, sea conditions), accessibility for elders, and activity options for children, forming a clear premise that supports all subsequent choices.

Assesses whether the plan presents a coherent day-by-day structure with logical pacing (e.g., acclimatization early, peak activity midweek, lighter/rest days interspersed), clear dependencies (booking times, tides), and age-appropriate parallel or shared options.

Checks that travel times, walking distances, and transfers are realistic, buffers are included (heat/rest, meal times), and locations are co-located to reduce backtracking—e.g., lodging near the beach and dining options near activities.

Evaluates whether key recommendations (beaches, hotels, restaurants, activities) are supported by relevant operational details such as distance in minutes, opening hours, accessibility features (ramps, shade), and seasonal notes, avoiding unsubstantiated claims.

Assesses whether transitions explicitly reference prior decisions (e.g., how the hotel location informs dining and activity choices), ensuring each section logically follows from and constrains the next without gaps or contradictions.

Checks for logically reasoned contingencies tied to July-specific risks (heat waves, afternoon storms, high UV, crowds, jellyfish), including time-of-day scheduling, backup indoor activities, reservation strategies, and proximity to medical services.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Organization and Readable Layout for a One-Week Itinerary
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear section hierarchy (e.g., overview, accommodations, daily schedule, dining, activities by age group, transport, budget, packing/safety) with descriptive headings, bullet lists, consistent section order, and adequate white space so readers can quickly locate what they need.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity, Concision, and Actionable Instructions
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether sentences are plain and unambiguous, instructions are stepwise and imperative (e.g., 'Book by…', 'Walk 5 min to…'), and wording avoids vagueness or fluff; names, addresses, and times are stated precisely to reduce reader effort and execution errors.

clarity_readability.criterion: Use and Explanation of Travel/Local Terminology
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether necessary terms (e.g., Blue Flag beach, lifeguarded, ADA-accessible, boardwalk, half-board, UV index, rip current, tide table) are either avoided or briefly defined at first use to prevent comprehension barriers for non-experts.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Visual Aids (Maps, Timelines, Tables, Icons)
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether visuals are present and well-designed for this task: annotated maps with walking/drive times, daily timelines, comparison tables (lodging/dining), simple budget tables, and clear icons for accessibility, shade, stroller-friendly, lifeguards; each with titles, legends, readable labels, and captions that tie to the text.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency of Time/Date/Units, Names, and Contact/Link Presentation
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates standardized formats (e.g., July 14–20, 8:00 AM), dual units for distances (km/mi) if relevant, consistent place names across text and visuals, and clean presentation of links/contacts (clickable URLs, phone numbers, reservation notes) to avoid confusion.

clarity_readability.criterion: Emphasis and Callouts for Critical Information
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether peak-season constraints and safety items (e.g., reservation windows, UV peak hours, heat/rest breaks, rip-current flags) are highlighted using callouts, bolding, or icons without clutter, positioned near relevant steps so readers don’t miss them.

clarity_readability.criterion: Accessibility and Mixed-Age Readability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks readability choices that aid elders and on-the-go readers: sufficient font size/contrast, short paragraphs, no color-only signaling, descriptive link text, alt text for images, and mobile-friendly layout so the plan is easy to read on phones and print.

task: I plan to spend one week at the beach with my family in July. The destination should be suitable for all ages—active children and elderly family members who need rest. Please create a personalized travel plan recommending comfortable beaches, convenient accommodations, diverse dining options, and age-appropriate activities so that everyone can enjoy the trip.

goal_alignment.criterion: Multigenerational fit with split-track schedule and shared family anchors
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan explicitly balances active teen activities (e.g., supervised water sports/tech-infused experiences), restful/accessible options for elders (shade, low-slope beaches, gentle walks, spa), and arts/culture for wife/daughter, while building daily shared anchor times (family breakfasts/dinners, sunset walks). This directly serves the family structure and Huang Zhi’s strong family orientation.

goal_alignment.criterion: Destination rationale tailored to July from Shanghai
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate if the chosen beach destination is justified via July-specific climate/sea conditions (rain/typhoon patterns, UV, crowd index), direct/short flight options from Shanghai, visa ease, time-zone comfort for elders, and Mandarin-friendly services. This matches the user’s rational, risk-averse decisions and peak-season realities.

goal_alignment.criterion: Accommodation alignment: luxury, accessibility, family convenience, and wellness
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether accommodations offer beachfront comfort, quiet rooms/villas, connecting or multi-bedroom options, kids’ club, accessible paths/elevators, shaded areas, lifeguard proximity, on-call medical support, quality dining on-site, club lounge/concierge, and amenities for his routines (lap pool, coastal running route, yoga/Pilates). Reflects high-end, health-focused, multigenerational needs.

goal_alignment.criterion: Age-appropriate, interest-matched activity portfolio with contingencies
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if activities map to each member: supervised snorkeling/intro diving/paddleboarding for the son (optionally with marine-science/tech angles), art workshops/galleries for daughter and wife, gentle cultural excursions for elders, and fitness/wellness for Huang Zhi; plus heat/monsoon indoor backups and morning/evening scheduling to avoid midday sun.

goal_alignment.criterion: Safety and risk management for heat, sea, and weather
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify explicit plans for sun/UV management, hydration/shade, lifeguard beaches, sea-condition checks, certified operators, storm/typhoon contingency, medical facility mapping (15–20 minutes), emergency contacts, and flexible bookings. Aligns with his risk-averse profile and responsibility toward family.

goal_alignment.criterion: Dining personalization to Zhejiang/light taste and healthy variety
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether dining includes light Chinese (Zhejiang) options, vegetarian/healthy menus, and occasional French/Japanese picks, plus private rooms or in-villa dining for comfort with elders, and kid-friendly choices—reflecting his culinary preferences and health goals.

goal_alignment.criterion: Logistics efficiency and comfort for elders and teens
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for seamless transfers (direct flights where possible, minimal layovers), expedited airport services, EV/comfortable transfers, stroller/wheelchair readiness, short distances within resort, Mandarin-capable staff, WeChat/Alipay acceptance, and realistic time buffers. Supports elder comfort and family ease.

goal_alignment.criterion: Decision-style support with transparent comparisons and metrics
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan presents an executive summary, destination shortlists or justification with data (flight times, climate stats, crowd levels), cost/time trade-offs, and pros/cons, matching his MBA-trained, data-driven decision-making.

goal_alignment.criterion: Integrated sustainability and responsible travel elements
goal_alignment.explanation: Look for eco-certified resorts, reef-safe sunscreen guidance, refill stations, reduced plastics, community-based cultural experiences, and gentle wildlife policies—aligning with his recent green focus and desire to educate children responsibly.

goal_alignment.criterion: Communication style and daily rhythm resonance
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report is concise, structured, and professional, with morning planning/exercise slots and family dinners emphasized—matching his morning productivity habit and family communication time.

goal_alignment.criterion: Pet consideration (include or plan for premium boarding)
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if the plan either identifies pet-friendly options if bringing the dog or suggests trusted premium boarding services in Shanghai, reflecting attention to real-life constraints for this pet-owning family.

content_alignment.criterion: Seasonality- and Risk-Aligned Destination & Beach Selection from Shanghai
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether proposed beach destinations explicitly account for July climate (typhoon/monsoon patterns, sea conditions, UV/heat), prioritize direct flights or minimal transfers from Shanghai, and note visa/entry ease—matching the user’s risk-averse, pragmatic style and elder comfort needs.

content_alignment.criterion: Multi-Generational and Interest-Tailored Activity Mix
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether activities are curated for each family member: STEAM/marine-science or tech maker experiences for the 15-year-old son; hands-on art/music workshops and gallery visits for the 12-year-old daughter and art-curator spouse; low-exertion, shaded relaxation and gentle sightseeing for elders; plus shared family experiences by the sea.

content_alignment.criterion: Luxury, Personalized, and Family-Friendly Accommodations (with Accessibility and Eco Credentials)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether lodging options match high-end brand expectations and personalization (suites/connecting rooms, butler/club services), include accessibility features for elders, on-site kids/teen programs, quiet work-friendly spaces, and verifiable sustainability practices—aligned with the user’s premium and eco-aware preferences.

content_alignment.criterion: Dining Profile Match: Zhejiang/Light Chinese, Vegetarian/Healthy, and Select Fine Dining Variety
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether dining content prioritizes Chinese (especially Zhejiang-style, light-flavor) options, highlights vegetarian/healthy choices, and includes curated French/Japanese fine dining that suits family palates and ages, with options like private dining or early family dinners.

content_alignment.criterion: Coastal Cultural and Art Immersion
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the plan includes coastal galleries, design-forward spaces, artist villages, or craft workshops suited to an art-curator spouse and a daughter who enjoys painting/music, integrating culture into the beach context rather than generic tourist stops.

content_alignment.criterion: Wellness and Fitness Integration (Run/Swim/Yoga/Meditation)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether content highlights safe morning run routes, lap-worthy pools or calm swim zones, yoga/Pilates/meditation offerings, and spa/thermal facilities—matching the user’s established exercise and mental wellness habits.

content_alignment.criterion: Privacy, Comfort, and Heat/Crowd Mitigation at the Beach
content_alignment.explanation: Checks for private or less-crowded beaches, shaded cabanas, gentle entries, and comfort features (e.g., beach clubs, reserved zones) that fit a multi-generational family and peak-season July conditions.

content_alignment.criterion: Sustainability and Low-Impact Marine Experiences
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether hotels/activities cite credible eco-certifications, conservation partnerships, reef-safe practices, sustainable seafood, and low-impact marine activities—aligned with the user’s growing focus on sustainability.

content_alignment.criterion: Language/Service Readiness and Elder Support
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether destinations/venues are Mandarin-friendly or offer high-touch service with translation support, easy internal transport, nearby medical facilities, and short walking distances—tailored to elders’ comfort and the family’s service expectations.

content_alignment.criterion: Pet Strategy Alignment (Travel vs. Boarding)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan responsibly addresses the large-breed dog’s feasibility (airline restrictions, quarantine) and suggests pet-friendly domestic options or reputable Shanghai boarding services—reflecting awareness of the family’s pet reality.

presentation_fit.criterion: Executive summary and decision highlights at the top
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report opens with a concise, one-screen overview (key destination(s), travel window, quick pros/cons, headline logistics) so a busy, MBA-trained reader can grasp the plan in minutes and share decisions with family.

presentation_fit.criterion: Logical structure and scannability (clear hierarchy and day-by-day layout)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether the content follows a business-like outline with clear headings and numbered sections, and provides a day-by-day itinerary using consistent morning/afternoon/evening blocks for quick morning planning and easy WeChat sharing.

presentation_fit.criterion: Audience tagging for age suitability
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that activities, dining, and amenities are consistently labeled (e.g., [Kids], [Elders], [All]) or with simple icons, allowing rapid identification of who each item suits in a multi-generational trip.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology clarity with bilingual place names
presentation_fit.explanation: Ensure plain, professional language with brief explanations for travel terms (e.g., Blue Flag, half-board), and list place names in both Chinese/English to streamline understanding and booking for a Shanghai-based user.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use of compact tables and visual aids for key comparisons
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether flight times, transfer durations, distance to beach, weather (°C/UV), and amenity summaries are presented via clean tables or simple charts, matching the user's data-driven reading habits.

presentation_fit.criterion: Localization of formats for China-based usage
presentation_fit.explanation: Verify consistent use of CNY, kilometers, Celsius, 24-hour time, and China-friendly date formats, with mobile-oriented paragraph length and bulleting that suit WeChat reading and quick copying.

presentation_fit.criterion: Risk and accessibility information surfacing style
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether safety, medical proximity, weather/season alerts, and accessibility notes are surfaced via callout boxes or icons rather than buried in text, aligning with a risk-aware, family-responsible decision process.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tone and cultural fit (professional, family-centered, non-hyperbolic)
presentation_fit.explanation: Check for a calm, respectful, businesslike tone that emphasizes family harmony and avoids slang or marketing hype, matching the user's Confucian values and executive background.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visual consistency and clean design
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess consistent iconography, bullet styles, spacing, and section layouts to deliver a premium, uncluttered aesthetic that supports quick comprehension and reflects high-end taste.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Season and destination suitability for July with risk mitigation
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report selects/filters destinations based on July weather (rainy/monsoon/typhoon seasons, UV/heat index, sea conditions) and provides concrete mitigations (indoor alternatives, shaded facilities, jellyfish season notes, lifeguard presence). This directly impacts feasibility and comfort for all ages.

actionability_practicality.criterion: End-to-end travel logistics and entry feasibility from Shanghai
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report provides viable flight plans (direct or one-stop with total travel time), time zone impact, visa/entry requirements for Chinese passports with processing timelines and document checklists, and airport transfer options suitable for elders. Without this, the plan may be impossible to execute.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Accommodation suitability for a multi-generational family
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether recommended hotels/resorts offer connecting rooms/suites or villas, step-free access/elevators, shaded/short paths to beach, kids club and quiet areas/spa, medical/onsite nurse availability, and Chinese-friendly service/policies. This ensures comfort and practicality for all family members.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Executable day-by-day itinerary with heat/energy pacing
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for a realistic 7-day schedule that time-boxes activities to mornings/evenings, includes midday rest/indoor options, states approximate walking distances/transfer times, and offers split-track (active vs. rest) plans and family regrouping slots. This makes the plan usable on-site for elders and teens.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Age-appropriate activities with booking details
actionability_practicality.explanation: Rates whether activities specify age/skill prerequisites, intensity level/duration, safety standards (certified operators, ratios), prices, reservation lead times in peak season, cancellation terms, and provider contacts. This enables immediate booking and reduces last-minute failures.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Dining practicality and reservation guidance
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report provides diverse dining near the accommodation (Chinese/Zhejiang, vegetarian, Japanese/French), children-friendly options, elder-friendly seating, price ranges, peak-season reservation windows, and backup options (room service or delivery). Ensures meals are easy and satisfying.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Cost transparency and peak-season policy clarity
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether per-night rates, taxes/fees, service charges, activity costs, transfers, and expected total trip range are clearly stated, along with deposit/cancellation/refund policies. Even with high budget, transparency drives confident decisions and vendor selection.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Health, safety, and emergency preparedness
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses inclusion of nearby hospitals/clinics, resort medical capabilities, emergency numbers, lifeguard coverage, rip current/flag systems, heat/sun protection guidance, reef-safe sunscreen notes, and travel insurance recommendations. Essential for risk-averse, elder-inclusive travel.

actionability_practicality.criterion: On-site mobility and transfer practicality
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for suitable airport transfers (MPV/van), travel times at typical traffic, resort carts/buggy availability, elevator/ramps, and local transport options (ride-hailing, private driver), plus mapping/parking guidance. Reduces friction for elders and ensures punctuality.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Actionable sustainability alignment
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report recommends eco-certified resorts/tour operators, practical low-friction steps (refill stations, towel policies), wildlife-ethical activities, and reef-safe materials—with minimal burden on the family. Matches user’s eco preference in a do-able way.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Pet care or pet travel plan feasibility
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report clarifies pet policies for destinations/hotels and offers reputable Shanghai pet boarding/sitting options with vaccination/booking requirements and costs. Ensures family logistics are complete even if the dog is not traveling.

depth_insight.criterion: July-season, multi-generational destination suitability and trade-off analysis
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report evaluates and compares destinations specifically for July conditions (heat/UV, rainfall, sea state, sargassum/jellyfish risk, crowding) and for multigenerational needs (gentle surf, lifeguards, accessibility, nearby medical care), then justifies a best-fit choice with explicit trade-offs.

depth_insight.criterion: Evidence-backed justification with local, time-specific data
depth_insight.explanation: Checks the use of concrete, verifiable inputs—e.g., July UV index and water temperature ranges, lifeguard hours, tide times impacting tidepools, beach accessibility features, typical crowd patterns, reservation lead times—so recommendations are grounded rather than generic.

depth_insight.criterion: Safety, accessibility, and health risk mitigation insight
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates proactive identification of risks (heat, UV, rip currents, storms) and tailored mitigations for kids and elders (shade/cabana strategy, early/late beach windows, hydration/rest schedules, ADA beach access, beach wheelchairs, short walking distances).

depth_insight.criterion: Age-tailored activity portfolio, pacing, and contingencies
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses depth in curating age-appropriate and shared activities (e.g., gentle water play, tidepooling, shaded walks, sunset cruises), pacing the week with alternating high/low energy days, scheduling around naps/heat, and providing realistic bad-weather/rough-sea backups.

depth_insight.criterion: Accommodation–location optimization for convenience and comfort
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for insightful selection of lodging based on door-to-sand proximity, elevators/ground-floor options, quiet rooms, kitchenette and laundry, connecting rooms, shaded pools, on-site services, and a clear rationale on comfort vs cost vs convenience for multigenerational needs.

depth_insight.criterion: Peak-season dining strategy with diversity and practicality
