depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan goes beyond naming restaurants to ensure feasibility in July: proximity to lodging, kid-friendly menus and elder-friendly seating times, reservation tactics, takeout/grocery delivery plans, dietary accommodations, and highlighting local specialties.

depth_insight.criterion: Logistics burden minimization and cost–value trade-offs
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses insight into reducing travel and daily friction (airport choice, transfer times, walkability, shuttles, parking, stroller/boardwalk suitability), gear strategy (rent vs bring), aligning check-in/out with family rhythms, and identifying peak-season cost savers without sacrificing comfort.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a coherent structure that moves from user needs and July constraints to selection criteria, destination choice, lodging/dining/activity recommendations, and finally a day-by-day plan. A sound framework prevents fragmented reasoning and ensures each part builds on the previous.

logical_coherence.criterion: Itinerary Feasibility and Temporal Coherence (7-day cadence)
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the daily schedule is internally consistent and executable: realistic travel and transition times, no overlapping commitments, logical geographic routing, heat-aware timing (e.g., avoiding midday sun), and a balanced cadence of active and rest days for children and elders.

Evaluates whether each user requirement (all-ages suitability, comfortable beach, convenient accommodations, diverse dining, age-appropriate activities) is explicitly mapped to specific plan elements, showing clear derivation from premises to recommendations.

Examines whether the destination choice is justified using explicit criteria relevant to July and mixed-age needs (climate/heat, sea conditions, crowding, accessibility/mobility, healthcare proximity, travel time). This tests the rigor of the core decision-making step.

Checks that important claims (e.g., calm waters for kids, flat beach access for elders, walking distance to dining) are backed by relevant facts or references and that the cited information directly supports the specific claims, avoiding unsupported assertions.

logical_coherence.criterion: Logical Handling of Constraints and Trade-offs
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the report identifies key constraints (e.g., budget, mobility, July crowding/heat, travel duration) and addresses trade-offs explicitly and consistently across sections (e.g., prioritizing proximity and shade over lower price).

logical_coherence.criterion: Section Transition Smoothness and Logical Connectivity
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether transitions explain how outputs from one section inform the next (e.g., destination criteria shaping lodging choice; lodging location constraining dining/activity radius), preventing logical jumps between sections.

clarity_readability.criterion: Scannable Organization and Sectioning for a Family Itinerary
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report is organized with a clear hierarchy (e.g., Overview, Day-by-Day Itinerary, Accommodations, Dining, Activities, Logistics/Transport, Safety & Accessibility), uses descriptive headings, and provides navigational aids (table of contents, section anchors). For the itinerary, evaluates day-by-day headings and time blocks, plus labels such as “For Kids,” “For Seniors,” and “For All” so each reader can quickly locate relevant parts.

clarity_readability.criterion: Layout Cleanliness and Accessibility
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses visual cleanliness and ease of reading: adequate white space, consistent font sizes, paragraph spacing, color contrast, and readable line length. Also checks mobile/print-friendly formatting and accessible design (e.g., not relying on color alone; icons with text labels; alt text for images). A clean, accessible layout reduces fatigue for elderly readers and makes on-the-go consultation practical.

clarity_readability.criterion: Plain, Fluent Language and Jargon Handling
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether the writing is concise, free of ambiguity, and uses plain language with consistent terminology. Any necessary terms (e.g., Blue Flag beach, ADA-accessible, resort fee, half-board, SPF, rip current) are briefly defined when first introduced. Clear, friendly tone and actionable wording (e.g., “Book,” “Allow,” “Avoid”) help readers execute plans without confusion.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Visuals (Maps, Tables, Schedules)
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether visuals are used appropriately to simplify complex information: maps with clear markers, tables comparing accommodations/dining options, and schedule blocks for daily plans. Visuals should have informative titles, legends, labels, units, and minimal clutter; they must be readable on both desktop and mobile. This speeds comprehension of routes, time/distance, and option trade-offs.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Key Practical Details
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether critical, time-sensitive, or decision-driving details are prominently and consistently highlighted (e.g., reservation windows, opening hours, peak sun times, accessibility features, age/height restrictions, dietary tags). Uses badges, callouts, bullets, or bolding to surface what matters at a glance, improving quick decision-making during planning and on-site.

clarity_readability.criterion: Summaries, Quick-Start, and Checklists
clarity_readability.explanation: Looks for an executive summary/TL;DR at the start and daily snapshot boxes (e.g., morning/afternoon/evening highlights), plus end-of-report checklists (packing, documents, reservations). These elements enable rapid orientation and reduce the cognitive load for busy families preparing for the trip.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency of Notation and Labels
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses consistent use of time formats (e.g., all in 24-hour or 12-hour with AM/PM), dates, currencies, distances (with dual units where helpful), and label sets (icons for kid-friendly, senior-friendly, accessibility). Consistency across the report minimizes misinterpretation and speeds scanning.

task: I will be traveling to Europe on a business trip within the next month, specifically staying in Berlin, Germany for about one week from October 15 to 22. My budget is within ¥30,000, including all transport and accommodation costs. Please prepare a detailed travel plan that includes suitable flights, local lodging, ground transport arrangements, a schedule for business meetings, and suggestions for leisure activities. My business meetings need to be scheduled over three working days in central Berlin.

goal_alignment.criterion: Budget integrity with CNY-denominated, exchange-aware cost control
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether the plan presents a full CNY budget roll-up (flights, accommodation, ground transport, estimated food/city taxes/activities) using an explicit EUR→CNY rate, shows scenario ranges, and preserves a prudent contingency while staying within ¥30,000. This fits his cost-effectiveness and rational spending style and ensures feasibility.

goal_alignment.criterion: Flight strategy optimized for business readiness and risk mitigation
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate if flight options are compared on total travel time, layovers, arrival times conducive to jet lag recovery before meetings, historical punctuality, fare rules (changes/cancellation), baggage, and seat comfort. Inclusion of buffers for first meeting day aligns with his risk-averse, efficiency-first approach.

goal_alignment.criterion: Accommodation fit to comfort profile and meeting geography
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether hotels are mid-to-high-end with gym access and breakfast, reliable Wi‑Fi, quiet rooms, and proximity to central Berlin (Mitte) or fast S/U-Bahn lines. Pricing transparency, flexible rates, and early check-in options reflect his comfort needs and planning rigor.

goal_alignment.criterion: Engineered three-day meeting schedule with time-zone and productivity design
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for a concrete meeting timetable across three working days in central Berlin with realistic travel times, venue suggestions (coworking/meeting rooms), buffers, light lunch windows, and consideration of Shanghai time for remote calls. This matches his meticulous planning and business constraints.

goal_alignment.criterion: Ground transport optimization mapped to actual routes and times
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify that airport transfers (BER to city), zone selection (AB/ABC), passes (e.g., BVG 7-day), routes (S/U-Bahn lines), stop names, peak/off-peak timing, and taxi contingencies are specified with time and cost estimates. This supports his efficiency and risk-averse commuting to meetings.

goal_alignment.criterion: Leisure curation aligned to cultural interests and healthy dining
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether after-work and weekend activities reflect his interest in city culture/history (e.g., Museumsinsel, Berlin Wall sites, Reichstag pre-booking), include time-appropriate evening plans, running-friendly parks, and light/healthy/Japanese/vegetarian dining nearby. Ensures resonance with his lifestyle.

goal_alignment.criterion: Analyst-style presentation with data-backed comparisons and clear rationale
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for option sets (flights/hotels) compared on key metrics, explicit trade-offs, source attributions, and a decisive recommendation path. Structured timelines, checklists, and summary tables reflect his data-driven, independent decision-making workflow.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk controls, compliance, and contingencies
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess inclusion of Schengen visa steps/timing for a China passport holder, insurance requirements, strike/operational disruption contingencies, cancellation policies, buffer scheduling, and an expense/receipt checklist for reimbursement. Aligns with his risk management mindset.

goal_alignment.criterion: Health and routine maintenance across time zone and season
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify jet lag plan (sleep timing, light exposure), October weather-aware packing, hotel gym usage, safe running routes (e.g., Tiergarten/Tempelhofer Feld) with time windows, and healthy meal timing compatible with meetings and his evening reading routine. Supports sustainable productivity.

content_alignment.criterion: Budget-fit and Cost-Effectiveness Modeling in RMB/EUR
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether all major items (round-trip Shanghai–Berlin flights, 6–7 nights hotel, local transport) are costed in RMB with stated EUR assumptions, and whether options/trade-offs (e.g., direct vs 1-stop, shifting hotel tier) are quantified to keep total within ¥30,000. This matches his rational, data-driven, cost-sensitive profile.

content_alignment.criterion: Accommodation Fit: Mid-to-High-End, Gym + Breakfast, Central Berlin Proximity
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether hotel options explicitly satisfy his comfort preference (gym access, breakfast buffet), are in/near central business areas (e.g., Mitte/Friedrichstrasse/Potsdamer Platz) to minimize commute, and note transit convenience. This directly targets his stated accommodation preferences.

content_alignment.criterion: Finance/Fintech Relevance and Networking Opportunities
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan surfaces Berlin-relevant finance/fintech touchpoints (e.g., coworking spaces with professional crowd, startup hubs, relevant meetups/events during his dates) to enrich business value beyond meetings, aligning with his occupation and interest in industry forums.

content_alignment.criterion: Health and Fitness Integration
content_alignment.explanation: Check for integration of running routes (e.g., Tiergarten, Tempelhofer Feld), hotel gym specifics, nearby yoga options, and scheduling that preserves exercise frequency. This aligns the trip with his routine and stress-management methods.

content_alignment.criterion: Decision-Support Depth (Comparative Metrics and Trade-offs)
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether content provides comparative data (flight duration/layover risk, hotel review scores/amenities, transit time vs cost), cost breakdowns, and sensitivity to FX changes. This depth matches his analytical decision style and desire for data-backed choices.

content_alignment.criterion: Ground Transport Optimization for Efficiency and Cost
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether local transport content is tailored (e.g., weekly AB pass vs pay-as-you-go, airport transfer options with time/cost, walking/bike-share suitability near hotel/meetings) rather than generic tips, fitting his efficient, cost-effective travel habits.

content_alignment.criterion: Business Schedule Alignment with Time Zone and Routine
content_alignment.explanation: Check if meetings are allocated across three working days with realistic commute buffers, jet lag-aware timing after arrival, and evening blocks reserved for his preferred focused time. This respects his structured planning and productivity patterns.

content_alignment.criterion: Dietary Alignment: Light/Japanese/Vegetarian Options Near Key Locations
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan highlights suitable dining options (light, Japanese, vegetarian) around the hotel and meeting areas, and notes hotel breakfast fit. This directly reflects his lifestyle preferences.

content_alignment.criterion: Cultural-Historical and Light Nature Leisure Tailored to Interests
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether leisure suggestions emphasize city culture/history (e.g., Museum Island, Berlin Wall sites, Reichstag dome) and easy-access nature/outdoor options (e.g., Grunewald/Wannsee) scheduled around his evenings/weekend time. This matches his travel style and emerging nature interest.

content_alignment.criterion: Risk-Aware Content (Low-Risk Choices and Financial Protections)
content_alignment.explanation: Check for content that anticipates and mitigates risks (e.g., safer neighborhoods/hotel areas, travel insurance coverage types, buffer time for delays, FX/payment considerations) aligned with his risk-averse profile and finance background.

presentation_fit.criterion: Clear, modular structure aligned to the task (Flights, Lodging, Ground Transport, Business Schedule, Leisure, Budget, Assumptions)
presentation_fit.explanation: For a report-like reading habit, well-labeled sections and a logical order let the user navigate instantly to the needed component without wading through long prose.

presentation_fit.criterion: Financial presentation clarity (currency labeling, exchange rate note, subtotals and totals by category/day)
presentation_fit.explanation: A data-driven, cost-conscious user needs costs shown in RMB with EUR references, visible assumptions, and clean totals so budget conformance can be verified at a glance.

presentation_fit.criterion: Time and schedule clarity (24-hour clock, weekday labels, time-zone notation for flights and meetings)
presentation_fit.explanation: Unambiguous time formatting with local time-zone tags prevents scheduling errors during a time-bound business trip, matching the user’s precise planning style.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use of option-comparison tables for key decisions (flights/hotels/transport) with consistent columns
presentation_fit.explanation: Side-by-side matrices with consistent fields (price, duration, location, amenities) support fast, rational comparison and reflect the user’s habit of structured evaluation.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology and bilingual annotation fit (define acronyms; Chinese with key English/German terms in parentheses)
presentation_fit.explanation: Clarifying transport zones and system names (e.g., U-Bahn, S-Bahn, AB/BC) reduces cognitive load in a foreign city while maintaining professional tone.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-friendly readability (concise paragraphs, bullet lists, meaningful subheadings, line breaks)
presentation_fit.explanation: The user likely reads on mobile in the evening; scannable formatting and short blocks enable quick intake and easy screenshotting.

presentation_fit.criterion: Executive summary and checklists (TL;DR budget/schedule at top; pre-trip and on-trip checklists)
presentation_fit.explanation: A brief upfront summary plus checklists improves first-pass understanding and operational readiness without digging through the full document.

presentation_fit.criterion: Assumptions, sources, and timestamps clearly labeled
presentation_fit.explanation: Marking 'as-of' dates for prices and stating sources/assumptions aids trust and interpretability for a risk-averse, evidence-oriented reader.

presentation_fit.criterion: Consistent visual cues/tags for key attributes and constraints (e.g., [Gym], [Breakfast], [Near U2], [Refundable], [Risk Note])
presentation_fit.explanation: Textual tags make critical amenities and caveats pop during scanning, aligning with the user’s preference for efficient, tag-like cues.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget feasibility with itemized RMB breakdown and buffer
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report provides realistic, source-backed prices for flights, accommodation, and local transport in RMB with the exchange rate/date noted, includes taxes/fees, stays within ¥30,000, and reserves a reasonable contingency buffer so the user can commit without budget overrun risk.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Booking readiness and specificity of options
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan lists concrete, immediately bookable options: flight numbers, carriers, routes and layovers; hotel names/addresses, room type with breakfast and gym, cancellation policies; meeting venue names with reservation methods; and direct links or exact search filters so the user can execute without extra research.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Schedule and timing viability (arrival, working days, buffers, jet lag)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses if arrival precedes the first meeting day, meetings are scheduled over three working days in central Berlin, and the itinerary includes realistic transfer and setup buffers, check-in/out constraints, and jet lag acclimation so commitments are achievable on time.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Local transport operability (airport transfer, passes, routes)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for step-by-step instructions from BER airport to hotel, selection of appropriate BVG zones/passes (e.g., ABC/weekly), route times, payment options, and last-mile directions between hotel, meeting venues, and leisure spots to ensure smooth, punctual mobility.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Business meeting operability (venues, equipment, booking steps, costs)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines whether the report provides practical arrangements for three business days: specific central venues or meeting rooms/co-working spaces, availability of Wi‑Fi/AV, booking procedures, estimated costs, and travel times between sessions to make meetings executable.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk and contingency planning (visa, insurance, cancellations, alternatives)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies inclusion of Schengen business visa lead times and document checklist, travel insurance guidance, clear cancellation/change policies, backup flight/hotel/transport options, and time buffers to mitigate common disruptions and enable confident booking.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Leisure plan executability (proximity, timing, tickets, fitness-friendly)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Rates whether the leisure suggestions fit around the work schedule, are near the hotel/meeting areas, include opening hours, ticketing/reservation steps, durations, and costs, and optionally support the user’s fitness (e.g., hotel gym hours, safe running routes like Tiergarten).

depth_insight.criterion: Constraint Validation and Assumption Rigor (budget currency, origin city, visa/timeline)
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report explicitly identifies and resolves critical uncertainties (e.g., whether ¥ denotes JPY or CNY, unspecified departure city, Schengen visa/lead time) and tests overall feasibility against the October 15–22 window and current market prices. This demonstrates deep understanding of constraints that determine plan viability.

depth_insight.criterion: End-to-End Budget Modeling and Optimization
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report provides an itemized, realistic total-cost model (flights, lodging, airport transfers, local transport, activities) and justifies choices via cost–benefit trade-offs, contingency buffers, and alternative options to keep within ¥30,000 while preserving reliability and productivity.

depth_insight.criterion: Flight Choice Rationale and Timing Strategy
depth_insight.explanation: Checks depth in comparing routes and fare types (layovers, arrival times, airport choice), baggage/cancellation policies, and risk buffers to ensure arrival allows recovery before meetings. Insight is shown by quantifying trade-offs among cost, duration, disruption risk, and meeting readiness.

depth_insight.criterion: Lodging Location Strategy and Access Analysis
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the lodging choice is justified by proximity to central Berlin business areas (e.g., Mitte/Tiergarten/Friedrichstrasse), transit access (U/S-Bahn lines), Wi‑Fi/workspace needs, noise/safety, cancellation policy, and nightly rate versus budget, reflecting deep place-based analysis.

depth_insight.criterion: Ground Transport Strategy and Product Selection
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the analytical depth in mapping airport transfers (BER in Zone C), daily commutes, and selecting optimal tickets/passes (AB vs ABC, 7-day options, tourist cards) with cost-effectiveness calculations and travel time estimates; includes contingencies (e.g., strike workarounds).

depth_insight.criterion: Operational Realism of the Three-day Meeting Schedule
depth_insight.explanation: Measures whether meeting days are sequenced and geoclustered to minimize travel, include buffer times, align with business hours, and account for jet lag and potential delays; also whether specific venues (e.g., coworking/meeting rooms) are chosen with practical justifications.

depth_insight.criterion: Insightfulness of Leisure Plan (date- and location-aware)
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether leisure activities are curated by opening hours, required reservations, and geographic clustering (e.g., Museum Island, Berlin Wall sites, Potsdam day trip) to reduce transit overhead and maximize value on the weekend versus weekdays of Oct 15–22.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Identification and Contingency Planning
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report anticipates key risks (flight delays, transit strikes, weather, Sunday closures) and proposes concrete mitigations (refundable bookings, schedule buffers, backup routes, insurance), reflecting forward-looking, decision-useful insight.

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and High-value Practical Tips
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards non-obvious, actionable recommendations (e.g., BVG app usage, seat selection to expedite immigration, optimal SIM/eSIM choices, restaurant booking norms, cultural/business etiquette) that enhance efficiency and experience beyond standard planning.

logical_coherence.criterion: Explicit Constraint Extraction and Traceability to Decisions
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether key constraints (Oct 15–22 window, three working days of meetings in central Berlin, total budget ≤ ¥30,000 including transport and lodging) are explicitly listed and each major decision (flights, hotel, transport, schedule) is traceably justified as meeting those constraints.

Verifies that all times align logically: flight arrival/departure with check-in/out windows, transfer durations, meeting days falling on weekdays within the date range, no overlaps between business and leisure, and inclusion of realistic buffers for airport transfers and intra-city travel.

logical_coherence.criterion: Budget Logic and Cost Aggregation Consistency
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether all required cost components (flights, accommodation, ground transport) are itemized, currency conversions are stated and applied consistently, totals are correctly summed, and the final total demonstrably fits within ¥30,000.

Evaluates whether choices (airports, carriers, hotel location, transport modes) are supported by relevant facts such as typical fares, travel times, distances, and network coverage, avoiding unsupported assertions.

Checks that each section explicitly references dependencies from previous sections (e.g., arrival time informing airport transfer plan and first-day schedule; hotel location informing commute times), with smooth transitions that prevent logical gaps.

Assesses whether the report states and consistently applies decision criteria (e.g., budget vs. travel time, proximity to central Berlin) and shows how alternatives were considered and rejected based on those criteria.

Checks that key assumptions (fare volatility, transit operating hours, meeting locations) are explicit and that contingency steps (e.g., backup transport for delays) logically preserve the feasibility of the plan.

clarity_readability.criterion: Executive Summary and Quick-Glance Essentials
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report opens with a concise summary card listing trip dates (Oct 15–22), origin/destination, selected flights, hotel name/location, total estimated cost vs ¥30,000 budget, and the three business meeting days—so the traveler can grasp the plan in under a minute.

clarity_readability.criterion: Sectioning and Day-by-Day Structure
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether content is clearly segmented (Flights, Lodging, Ground Transport, Business Schedule, Leisure) and each day’s plan uses a consistent template with time blocks, locations, and addresses—enabling quick navigation and preventing missed items.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity and Actionability
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether instructions are concise and unambiguous (24-hour times, explicit meeting locations, step-by-step transit directions), avoiding vague phrasing and excessive jargon so the plan can be executed without rereading.

clarity_readability.criterion: Terminology and Local System Explanations
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that essential terms and abbreviations are defined on first use (e.g., BER, S-Bahn/U-Bahn, fare zones AB, validation rules) and used consistently, lowering the learning curve for non-local users.

clarity_readability.criterion: Numeric, Time, and Currency Presentation Standards
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether dates and times use a consistent, timezone-aware format (e.g., CEST, 24h), costs are shown with clear currencies (CNY/EUR) and conversion rates, and totals/subtotals are labeled to show what is included—improving budget and schedule readability.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Tables, Timelines, and Maps
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether flight/hotel/transport details are organized in readable tables; schedules are visualized (e.g., simple timelines) with captions/legends; maps or map links are placed near relevant text and remain legible when printed or viewed on mobile.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Constraints and Key Actions
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks use of visual emphasis (headings, bold, icons, callouts) for critical items such as budget cap, booking deadlines, check-in/out times, meeting addresses, and transfer buffers—so key risks and actions stand out at a glance.

task: I will be traveling to Europe on a business trip within the next month, specifically staying in Berlin, Germany for about one week from October 15 to 22. My budget is within ¥30,000, including all transport and accommodation costs. Please prepare a detailed travel plan that includes suitable flights, local lodging, ground transport arrangements, a schedule for business meetings, and suggestions for leisure activities. My business meetings need to be scheduled over three working days in central Berlin.

goal_alignment.criterion: Budget control with chain-hotel preference and transparent RMB breakdown
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether total transport + accommodation stays within ¥30,000 with a clear RMB itemized breakdown (fares, taxes, fees), and whether lodging choices prioritize reputable business-chain hotels near central Berlin. Evaluate value-for-money trade-offs, loyalty usage, and flexible/refundable rates that fit his risk-averse, quality-focused profile.

goal_alignment.criterion: Date-accurate flight plan with time-zone and jet-lag adaptation
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether flights for Oct 15–22 are realistic (Shanghai–Berlin), minimize fatigue, and align arrival/departure times with meeting days. Look for aisle seat/comfort tips, buffer times, and a jet-lag strategy that protects his morning productivity and overall efficiency within the one-month booking window.

goal_alignment.criterion: Three working days of central Berlin meetings aligned to his work rhythm
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate if the schedule places meetings in the afternoon across three actual weekdays in central districts (e.g., Mitte), preserving mornings for high-efficiency prep. Includes travel-time estimates, buffers, exact addresses or nodes, and contingency for overruns—matching his planning style and efficiency goals.

goal_alignment.criterion: Hotel location strategy and ground transport optimization
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify that the hotel is strategically located near key transit (S/U-Bahn) to cut commute risk/time; that AB zone passes or ticket bundles are chosen based on his itinerary; airport transfers are reliable; and walking times/transit routes are specified—serving his pragmatic, time-efficient nature.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk management and compliance (visa, insurance, cancellation, contingencies)
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether Schengen business visa requirements for a Chinese passport are addressed with realistic timelines, along with travel insurance, refundable bookings, backup routes for rail/airport strikes, and emergency contacts—aligned with his risk-averse decision style and one-month time constraint.

goal_alignment.criterion: Professional and networking value for a marketing manager
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess inclusion of market-relevant site visits (e.g., retail corridors, startup hubs), potential events, and LinkedIn-based outreach windows. This reflects his career development focus and habit of learning from cases and industry trends.

goal_alignment.criterion: Leisure personalization to interests, cuisine, and season
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether cultural/historical activities (Museum Island, Berlin Wall, Reichstag) and wellness options (Tiergarten running routes, yoga drop-ins) are time- and weather-appropriate, and whether dining includes high-quality Chinese or refined options—matching his taste and desire for deep experiences.

goal_alignment.criterion: Decision-ready, data-driven presentation
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for structured comparisons (flight/hotel options with pros/cons and quantified time/cost), RMB/EUR conversions, clear timelines, checklists (packing, documents), and booking links—matching his MBA-style, analytical, efficiency-oriented decision-making.

goal_alignment.criterion: Family communication and personal wellbeing safeguards
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess inclusion of family call windows calibrated to the ~6-hour time difference, hotel gym access, simple in-room stretches for neck/shoulder care, safe running times, and thoughtful souvenir ideas for children—reflecting his family-first values and health habits.

content_alignment.criterion: Business-day structure aligned to user's time-use pattern and central Berlin focus
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether meeting days are clustered into three working days in central Berlin and scheduled to respect his preference for morning preparation and afternoon meetings, minimizing intra-day transit and enhancing efficiency—key to his pragmatic, plan-driven work style.

content_alignment.criterion: Accommodation fit with high-end chain preference and value-for-money within budget
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether hotels are reputable business chains in or near Mitte with business amenities (gym, workspace, reliable Wi‑Fi) and positioned as cost-effective choices that keep total spend within ¥30,000, matching his quality-plus-economy mindset.

content_alignment.criterion: Flight selection tuned to efficiency, reliability, and Shanghai origin within budget
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether flight options from PVG consider non-stop or single-stop reliable carriers, arrival/departure times that mitigate jet lag and protect meeting productivity, and explicit reasoning about time vs. cost—aligned with his risk-averse, efficiency-first profile.

content_alignment.criterion: RMB-based cost breakdown and trade-off rationale
content_alignment.explanation: Looks for transparent, RMB-denominated estimates for major cost buckets (flights, hotel, local transport) and data-driven trade-offs (e.g., fare class vs. schedule, hotel location vs. nightly rate), reflecting his MBA background and rational decision process.

content_alignment.criterion: Marketing-oriented networking and market exposure opportunities
content_alignment.explanation: Examines whether the plan includes Berlin-relevant marketing/innovation activities (e.g., LinkedIn-found meetups, startup hubs, industry talks, market observation routes) tailored to his role and habit of engaging with industry forums, enhancing professional value.

content_alignment.criterion: Deep cultural and historical leisure curation for self-guided exploration
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether leisure choices emphasize Berlin’s core cultural/historical themes (e.g., Museum Island, Cold War sites, Bauhaus/design) in self-guided formats and slotted around business days, matching his preference for deep, efficient experiences.

content_alignment.criterion: Dining curation balancing Chinese comfort and business-appropriate fine dining
content_alignment.explanation: Checks that dining suggestions include at least one reputable Chinese option (ideally Jiangnan-leaning or high-quality Chinese) for comfort plus refined local/modern venues suitable for client dinners, aligning with his culinary preferences and business needs.

content_alignment.criterion: Health and exercise continuity (jet lag, running/yoga options, ergonomic care)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the content helps him maintain routines (safe running routes like Tiergarten, nearby yoga/gym, timing for workouts) and acknowledges neck/shoulder care and jet-lag strategies—personalized to his health habits and needs.

content_alignment.criterion: Tooling and workflow alignment (LinkedIn, local transit/payment/connectivity)
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses inclusion of tools that match his digital habits (LinkedIn event discovery, BVG/DB apps, eSIM/roaming, card/China-friendly payment notes) to support efficient time use and professional engagement during the trip.

content_alignment.criterion: Family-conscious touches (souvenir and brief kid engagement ideas)
content_alignment.explanation: Looks for subtle, time-efficient suggestions that reflect his family focus (e.g., art/dance-themed gifts for his daughter, building toys/basketball-related items for his son), adding personalized resonance without diluting the business purpose.

presentation_fit.criterion: Executive summary and layered structure tailored for a busy manager
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether the report opens with a concise executive summary (dates, budget cap in CNY, trip objective, 3 business days in central Berlin) and then uses clear sections (Flights, Lodging, Ground Transport, Business Schedule, Leisure, Cost Overview, Notes) to reduce search time.

presentation_fit.criterion: Schedule visualization and time presentation (day-by-day, 24-hour, time zones)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether the itinerary is organized in a calendar/day-by-day view with 24-hour times, local time-zone tags (e.g., CEST/UTC+2), and clear sequencing so cross-time-zone planning and meeting coordination are immediately understandable.

presentation_fit.criterion: Professional, concise Chinese business tone with selective bilingual labels
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks that the language is crisp and non-promotional, primarily in Chinese with key international terms in parentheses (e.g., Shanghai Pudong PVG, Berlin BER, U-Bahn/S-Bahn) to align with his foreign-enterprise context and avoid ambiguity.

presentation_fit.criterion: Numeric and currency formatting clarity
presentation_fit.explanation: Reviews whether costs are shown with CNY as default and EUR in parentheses, consistent number formatting (thousand separators), subtotals and totals in tables, and fee labels—supporting quick budget digestion without judging the amounts.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology and acronym handling
presentation_fit.explanation: Judges whether travel/transport acronyms (BER, S-Bahn/U-Bahn, BVG, OTA, layover) are briefly explained at first mention, reducing cognitive load while preserving professional precision.

presentation_fit.criterion: Standardized templates for recurring items (flights, hotel, meetings, transfers)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses the use of uniform, labeled fields for each item (e.g., flight number, dep/arr local time with TZ, terminal, baggage; hotel address and check-in/out; meeting address, nearest transit, estimated transit time) to enable fast comparison and scanning.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile- and screenshot-friendly formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks for compact bullet lists, concise tables that fit typical phone screens, clear headings, labeled links (e.g., ‘Google Maps route’), and key information front-loaded per line, facilitating quick reading and easy screenshot sharing.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget feasibility with transparent, itemized cost plan and buffers
actionability_practicality.explanation: The report should present an itemized total cost (flights, lodging, ground transport, estimated meals/incidental fees) with exchange-rate assumptions, show how it fits under ¥30,000, and include a contingency buffer and cost-lock strategies (e.g., advance purchase, refundable rates). This lets the user confidently commit without budget overrun.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Actionable flight itinerary (specific options, timing, and booking feasibility)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Provides specific round-trip flight options for the exact dates with departure/arrival times, layover durations, total travel time, airport details (BER terminals), baggage rules, and links or instructions to book refundable/flexible fares within budget. This enables immediate purchase and aligns arrival with rest and meeting start.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Operational meeting schedule for three central Berlin workdays
actionability_practicality.explanation: Gives a calendar-ready plan with proposed workdays, time blocks, buffer times, venue addresses in central Berlin (e.g., Mitte), door-to-door travel times between locations, and contingency slots. This makes the business core of the trip executable and reduces risk of delays affecting meetings.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Practical lodging selection near meeting area with business amenities and policies
actionability_practicality.explanation: Recommends specific chain hotels in/near Mitte with commute times to meeting sites, business amenities (Wi‑Fi, desk, early check-in/late checkout options), breakfast availability, cancellation/refund terms, and invoicing/VAT receipt readiness for reimbursement. This ensures comfort, efficiency, and policy compliance.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Executable ground transport plan (airport transfer, local transit, routes)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Details airport-to-city transfer choices with time/cost, local ticket/pass recommendations (zones coverage for BER and city), and step-by-step route examples (U/S‑Bahn/tram/walking) with estimated durations at peak times. This removes friction in daily movement and keeps the schedule reliable.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Visa/entry and insurance readiness with timelines and documents
actionability_practicality.explanation: Outlines Schengen visa requirements, document checklist (invitation, itinerary, proof of funds, insurance meeting minimum coverage), appointment timelines, and expedited strategies. Also includes travel insurance recommendations. Without this, the trip cannot proceed, so clear steps are critical.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk and contingency planning for disruptions
actionability_practicality.explanation: Provides plan B options for flight delays/cancellations (alternative routes, flexible tickets), local transport disruptions (taxi/ride-hail fallback), and meeting adjustments (virtual/hybrid, backup venues/contact lists). This preserves business objectives under common European travel risks.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Leisure and personal time practicality aligned to schedule and preferences
actionability_practicality.explanation: Suggests nearby, time-bounded activities (e.g., evening museum visits, runs in Tiergarten, quality dining including Chinese options) with opening hours, reservation tips, and costs that fit between work commitments without inflating budget or causing fatigue.

depth_insight.criterion: Budget Interpretation, Conversion, and Costed Trade-off Analysis
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the plan correctly interprets ¥ (e.g., CNY vs JPY), converts to EUR with realistic rates, explicitly allocates the budget across flights, lodging, and ground transport (including taxes/fees like city tax and airport transfers), and justifies quantified trade-offs and a contingency buffer to keep the trip feasible within ¥30,000.

depth_insight.criterion: Flight Routing and Timing Strategy Aligned to Business Productivity and Budget
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether flight choices weigh total travel time vs price, arrival/departure timing relative to the three meeting days, jet-lag recovery, reliability, layover/visa risks, fare flexibility, and baggage needs, articulating why the chosen itinerary maximizes business value within the budget rather than merely minimizing fare.

depth_insight.criterion: Lodging Location-Value Assessment and Business Suitability
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether lodging is selected for proximity to central Berlin meeting areas and transit hubs, with analysis of workspace quality, Wi‑Fi, noise, safety, cancellation terms, and nightly rate. Depth is shown by comparing alternatives and justifying the best value choice under budget constraints.

depth_insight.criterion: Ground Transport Optimization (Zones, Passes, First/Last-Mile) with Cost Justification
depth_insight.explanation: Examines whether the plan maps the itinerary to Berlin’s zone system (A/B/C) and products (e.g., weekly/daily passes, airport supplements), compares public transport vs taxis for tight windows, and quantifies the chosen mix to minimize cost and time without risking meeting punctuality.

depth_insight.criterion: Business Meeting Schedule Design (Time-zone, Buffering, Clustering, Feasibility)
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the three working days are placed on feasible weekdays within Oct 15–22, with realistic start times after arrival for jet-lag recovery, geographic clustering of venues, inter-meeting travel time, and buffers—showing locally informed judgment that protects meeting success.

depth_insight.criterion: Seasonality- and Location-aware Leisure Curation with Time-efficient Routing
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether leisure suggestions fit evenings/weekend windows, account for mid-October weather/daylight and common closures (e.g., Monday museum, Sunday retail), are geographically clustered to reduce transit time, and add distinctive Berlin experiences beyond generic lists.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk and Contingency Planning (Delays, Strikes, Cancellations, Visa/Transit)
depth_insight.explanation: Checks for anticipation of disruptions (flight delays, rail/airport strikes, tight connections), visa/transit considerations relevant to likely origin, and practical mitigations (flexible fares, alternate routes, backup remote meeting options, insurance) consistent with the budget and schedule.

depth_insight.criterion: Assumptions Transparency and Scenario/Sensitivity Analysis
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether key unknowns (departure city, airline preferences, currency) are spelled out, assumptions are reasonable, and sensitivities (exchange rates, fare swings, alternative origin cities) are quantified with backup options that still meet the budget and core requirements.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a logical structure from constraints and objectives (dates Oct 15–22, Berlin, ¥30,000 budget, three working days of meetings) to option screening, selection, integrated itinerary, and final budget/schedule summary. A coherent framework prevents ad hoc decisions and ensures each section builds on the previous.

logical_coherence.criterion: Explicit Constraint Mapping and Requirement Satisfaction
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether all stated requirements are explicitly listed and traced to plan elements: flights match dates/time zones, lodging is in/near central Berlin, three meeting days are scheduled on working days in central Berlin, and total transport+accommodation costs stay within ¥30,000. Any trade-offs or deviations must be identified and justified.

logical_coherence.criterion: Feasibility and Internal Consistency of Timeline and Budget
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses logical consistency of time and costs: no overlapping activities; realistic buffers for airport transfer, check-in/out, and intra-city travel; meeting times feasible given locations; arithmetic accuracy of totals; currency definition and conversions clearly applied; the final plan executable without contradictions.

logical_coherence.criterion: Justified Selection Logic with Evidence Linkage
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether choices (flights, lodging location, ground transport passes, meeting venues, leisure slots) are supported by relevant facts (price, duration, proximity to central Berlin, transfer times), and whether the report shows how these facts lead to the chosen option over alternatives.

logical_coherence.criterion: Cohesive Transitions and Dependency Signposting Across Sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether transitions make dependencies explicit (e.g., how flight arrival time constrains day-1 activities; how hotel location drives transport choices; how meeting schedule shapes leisure windows), avoiding logical jumps between sections.

Checks that assumptions (e.g., departure city/time zone handling, baggage needs, class of service, working hours for meetings, definition of 'central Berlin') are stated where data are uncertain and are used consistently throughout the schedule and budget.

clarity_readability.criterion: Task-Aligned Structuring and Day-by-Day Timeline
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report is organized into clear sections matching the user’s needs (Flights, Lodging, Ground Transport, Business Meetings Schedule, Leisure, Budget) and provides a readable day-by-day plan (Oct 15–22) with day-of-week labels and time blocks so the traveler can follow the itinerary at a glance.

clarity_readability.criterion: Visual Hierarchy and Scanability (Headings, Lists, Emphasis)
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses use of a consistent heading hierarchy, bullet/numbered lists, spacing, and emphasis (bold/keylines) to surface critical constraints (¥30,000 cap, meeting days in central Berlin) and action items, enabling quick scanning on desktop or mobile.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity, Concision, and Actionability
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether sentences are concise, unambiguous, and use an instructional, actionable tone (e.g., 'Book Flight X, depart 10:45 (local time)') with correct grammar and consistent style, reducing the chance of operational mistakes during booking and travel.

clarity_readability.criterion: Terminology and Localization Clarity (Transit, Time Zones, Currency)
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that travel-specific terms are explained on first use and used consistently (e.g., BER airport, BVG zones AB/BC, U-Bahn vs S-Bahn, refundable fare, non-stop/layover), that times include time-zone labels (CEST/CET vs origin), and that currencies (¥/CNY vs €/EUR) are clearly indicated.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structured Data Presentation for Logistics and Budget
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether complex information is placed in clear tables: flight options (flight no., depart/arrive airports and local times, layover duration), hotel options (address, distance to Mitte, rate/night), ground transport (mode, route, duration, fare), and a budget table with category subtotals and a visible total vs the ¥30,000 cap.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Visual Aids (Maps, Timelines, Calendars, Charts)
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether visuals are used appropriately to improve comprehension—e.g., a simple calendar or Gantt-like timeline for Oct 15–22, a map showing hotel relative to central Berlin meeting area (Mitte), and a budget breakdown chart—using clear legends, labels, and color choices that remain readable when printed or viewed on mobile.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency of Notation and Supporting Metadata
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks consistent date/time formats (YYYY-MM-DD, 24-hour clock, day-of-week), unit conventions (km/min), clearly stated exchange rate used for ¥↔€ conversions, standardized address formatting with postal codes, and clearly labeled links to booking pages or maps, reducing ambiguity across the document.

task: I will be traveling to Europe on a business trip within the next month, specifically staying in Berlin, Germany for about one week from October 15 to 22. My budget is within ¥30,000, including all transport and accommodation costs. Please prepare a detailed travel plan that includes suitable flights, local lodging, ground transport arrangements, a schedule for business meetings, and suggestions for leisure activities. My business meetings need to be scheduled over three working days in central Berlin.

goal_alignment.criterion: End-to-end budget control with transparent trade-offs (≤¥30,000 incl. flights + lodging)
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the plan provides total cost roll-ups, currency assumptions, and option comparisons that keep flights and hotel within the ¥30,000 cap, highlighting cost–performance logic suitable for a rational, data-driven founder.

goal_alignment.criterion: Jet-lag-aware, central-Berlin three-working-day meeting plan with buffers
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether meetings are scheduled on three business days (not weekends), in central areas (e.g., Mitte/Tiergarten/Friedrichstraße), with recovery time after arrival, realistic travel buffers, and time-zone-aware slots.

goal_alignment.criterion: Lodging fit: star-rated, quiet, workspace-enabled, central, and commute-efficient
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the hotel meets his preference for star-rated, quiet environments with reading/work spaces, strong Wi-Fi, gym access, and ≤20–25 min transit to meeting venues to match his efficiency habit.

goal_alignment.criterion: Flight strategy optimized for time efficiency, recovery, and reliability
goal_alignment.explanation: Reviews route choices (nonstop/one-stop), arrival/departure times that enable rest before meetings, on-time performance, baggage alignment with light-travel preferences, and flexibility (change/cancel terms).

goal_alignment.criterion: Ground transport plan tailored to Berlin (BER transfers, passes, time maps)
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks specificity on BER–city transfers (e.g., FEX/S-Bahn), zone/weekly tickets, first/last mile, and travel-time mapping between hotel and meeting sites, supporting punctuality and cost control.

goal_alignment.criterion: Decision transparency and tool integration (Notion/ICS-friendly, quantified comparisons)
goal_alignment.explanation: Determines whether options are presented with metrics (price/time/risk) and exportable structures (tables/timelines/ICS blocks) that plug into his GitHub/Slack/Notion workflow.

goal_alignment.criterion: Connectivity and work-readiness (Wi‑Fi guarantees, eSIM, power, workspace backup)
goal_alignment.explanation: Verifies inclusion of eSIM/roaming plan, Type F adapter, reliable hotel Wi‑Fi, and backup coworking options to ensure uninterrupted Slack/Notion usage and video calls.

goal_alignment.criterion: Health, routine, and family alignment
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses provision for morning runs/gym or climbing gym options, low-sugar/Chinese dining nearby, and a daily Berlin-time slot aligned with Beijing bedtime for his daughter’s story-time.

goal_alignment.criterion: Leisure personalization and time-fit near base
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether leisure suggestions match his interests (tech/startup hubs, museums, concerts, parks/lakes) and are scheduled in realistic evening/half-day windows near lodging to minimize transit.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk and compliance (Schengen business visa, insurance, contingencies)
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for a visa timeline within one month, required documents (invitation, insurance), refundable booking choices, and contingency buffers for delays or meeting changes.

content_alignment.criterion: AI/Tech Ecosystem and Central Berlin Business Context Relevance
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan curates Berlin-specific AI/startup touchpoints (e.g., relevant co-working hubs, VCs/accelerators, university/industry labs, meetups/newsletters) and proposes central Berlin meeting venues/routes that suit an AI founder’s networking and deal-making, reflecting his professional identity and community engagement habits.

content_alignment.criterion: Accommodation Fit to Quiet, Work-Friendly, Star-Rated, and Eco Preferences
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether hotel options match his preference for star-rated quality, quiet/soundproof rooms, strong in-room workspace, gym access, reading/lounge areas, and eco-certification or sustainability practices, with proximity to central Berlin for meetings and parks for runs.

content_alignment.criterion: Budget Calibration in CNY with Cost–Quality Balance
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether flight and lodging selections are priced/estimated in CNY and structured to fit within ¥30,000 all-in while preserving his value-oriented, rational decision style (e.g., direct vs. one-stop trade-offs, fare classes, realistic nightly rates), showing cost-effectiveness rather than generic options.

content_alignment.criterion: Schedule Alignment with Morning Creative Peak and Family Routine
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the daily plan and three meeting days place intensive meetings late morning/afternoon to preserve his morning deep work window, and whether it thoughtfully includes time zone-aware slots for evening family connection (e.g., daughter’s bedtime reading), reflecting his routine and priorities.

content_alignment.criterion: Leisure Curation Aligned to Tech/Futurism, Sci‑Fi Aesthetic, and Nature
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether leisure content prioritizes tech-forward venues (e.g., future/science/technology museums, planetarium), modern cultural experiences resonating with film-score/sci‑fi tastes, and accessible nature (parks, lakes, forests) that match his metropolitan-and-nature travel preference.

content_alignment.criterion: Health, Fitness, and Low-Sugar Dining Integration
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether the plan integrates runnable routes (e.g., parks near hotel), quality gyms or climbing gyms, and low-sugar dining or Cantonese/Chinese options aligned to his diet and exercise routine, indicating content selection that respects his wellness goals.

content_alignment.criterion: Mobility Efficiency and Sustainability Preferences
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether ground transport choices reflect his efficiency and eco-consciousness (e.g., BER airport rail options like FEX, AB zone passes, U/S‑Bahn connectivity, bike/e-mobility), and whether flight choices consider direct routes or lower-emission options consistent with his values.

content_alignment.criterion: Work Infrastructure and Connectivity Readiness
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content anticipates his heavy digital workflow by highlighting in-flight Wi‑Fi availability, hotel/co-working bandwidth and meeting rooms, EU power/adapters, eSIM/roaming options, and backup work settings, matching his advanced tech usage and need for uninterrupted productivity.

presentation_fit.criterion: Section structure mirrors the task modules and supports quick navigation
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that the report uses clear, numbered sections for Flights, Lodging, Ground Transport, Meeting Schedule (3 working days in central Berlin), and Leisure, plus a day-by-day overview. This structure matches the user's Notion-like, modular reading habit and reduces search time.

presentation_fit.criterion: Executive summary/dashboard at the top for at-a-glance comprehension
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report opens with a concise summary of key constraints and metrics (dates, budget in CNY with EUR reference, planned meeting days, total estimated spend, timezone note). This aligns with the user's data-driven, time-efficient style.

presentation_fit.criterion: Timeline/calendar-style daily schedule with explicit timezone and 24-hour format
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether each day’s plan is shown in a timeline or calendar-ready format (e.g., 08:30–10:00 CEST), distinguishing transit, meetings, and leisure. Clear timezone labeling mitigates coordination errors for an international traveler.

presentation_fit.criterion: Table-based comparisons for flights and hotels with decision-focused columns
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that flights/hotels are presented in compact tables including cost, duration, transfer count, cancellation policy, location/commute time, and a highlighted recommendation. This suits the user's habit of scanning structured, comparable data.

presentation_fit.criterion: Consistent terminology and unit conventions with brief clarifications
presentation_fit.explanation: Verify use and first-use expansion of terms/codes (e.g., PEK/BER, S-Bahn/U-Bahn), 24-hour time, metric distances, and CNY/EUR with clear notation and a mini legend if needed. This reduces cognitive load for a technical reader.

presentation_fit.criterion: Professional, concise, non-promotional tone with succinct rationales
presentation_fit.explanation: Ensure the language is objective and to-the-point, avoiding flowery or marketing copy. Short rationale tags (e.g., 'Why this flight') support analytical decision-making without drifting into verbosity.

presentation_fit.criterion: Bilingual labeling for on-the-ground usability (Chinese + English/German)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether critical items (hotel names/addresses, stations, venue names) include both Chinese and English/German forms, aiding recognition in signage and apps while matching the user's bilingual professional context.

presentation_fit.criterion: Workflow compatibility with Notion/Slack/Mobile (links, checklists, copy-paste blocks)
presentation_fit.explanation: Check for clean, copyable blocks, live links to booking pages/maps, concise checklists, and optional calendar-friendly text. This presentation increases usability within the user’s daily tools.

presentation_fit.criterion: Clear visual hierarchy and emphasis on key numbers and actions
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess use of headings, numbering, bullet points, and consistent emphasis to surface critical info (deadlines, amounts, times). This enhances scanability without relying on heavy graphics.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget feasibility with transparent cost breakdown and buffer
actionability_practicality.explanation: The report should provide a clear subtotal for flights and hotel (taxes/fees included), currency conversion assumptions, and a contingency buffer so the total stays within ¥30,000 while leaving room for local transport. Without a concrete, verified total, the plan is not immediately actionable.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Concrete, bookable flight plan (options, timing, policies, realism)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Provide at least one primary and one backup flight option with carriers, flight numbers, layovers, total duration, arrival/departure times aligned to the stated dates, typical prices, baggage inclusion, and change/refund policies. This enables immediate booking and reduces risk of infeasible or overpriced itineraries.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Lodging suitability and bookability near central Berlin
actionability_practicality.explanation: Recommend specific hotels (name, address) in central areas (e.g., Mitte) with quiet rooms, strong Wi‑Fi, desk, gym, breakfast options, and flexible cancellation. Include nightly rate, total cost, and distance to meeting locations/transit so he can book confidently for productivity and rest within budget.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Operational feasibility of the three‑day meeting schedule
actionability_practicality.explanation: Lay out three weekday meeting blocks with local times, venue addresses, travel times between sites, and buffers for delays/jet lag. Ensure no weekend/holiday conflicts and that start/end times are realistic given arrivals and commutes, so the schedule is actually keepable.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Ground transport practicality (airport transfers and daily commuting)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Detail BER–hotel transfer options, transit passes (zones, prices, validity), route numbers, typical travel times, payment methods (contactless/cards), and ride‑hailing availability. This removes ambiguity on how to get around on time and within cost expectations.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Visa and compliance readiness within the timeline
actionability_practicality.explanation: Outline Schengen business visa requirements for a Chinese passport (documents, appointment/processing times), mandatory travel insurance coverage, and practical steps. Without this, the plan may be impossible to execute within a month.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Practical leisure plan aligned to schedule, location, and interests
actionability_practicality.explanation: Suggest nearby, time‑boxed activities with opening hours, ticket/reservation steps, and costs that fit around meetings (e.g., evening runs, museums not closed that day, climbing gyms). This ensures leisure is doable without jeopardizing work commitments.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk and contingency planning for disruptions
actionability_practicality.explanation: Include realistic plan B elements: alternative flight routes, cancellation deadlines, buffer times, backup meeting modes (video/coworking), and notes on potential strikes. This reduces execution risk and supports decision‑making under uncertainty.

actionability_practicality.criterion: End‑to‑end, day‑by‑day executable itinerary with times and addresses
actionability_practicality.explanation: Provide a chronological schedule (arrival, check‑in, meetings, transfers, leisure) with concrete times, durations, and addresses to avoid conflicts and enable immediate calendar entry and booking sequencing.

depth_insight.criterion: Budget Feasibility and Optimization (All-in Costed Plan)
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the plan builds a realistic, itemized cost model (flights, 7 nights lodging, airport/city transport) within ¥30,000, includes currency conversion assumptions, allocates budget across components, and proposes actionable trade-offs or savings without undermining business goals.

depth_insight.criterion: Flight Strategy Depth (Productivity, Risk, and Cost Trade-offs)
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates how well flight options are analyzed beyond price—arrival/departure timing vs. jet lag, layover risk, on-time performance, baggage policies, and alignment with meeting days—to maximize business productivity while respecting the budget.

depth_insight.criterion: Accommodation Strategy and Location Intelligence
depth_insight.explanation: Looks at how the lodging choice balances cost, proximity to central Berlin (e.g., Mitte/Tiergarten), transit access, safety, and business amenities (Wi‑Fi, workspace), including cancellation flexibility and acknowledgment of local lodging taxes or exemptions.

depth_insight.criterion: Business Meeting Schedule Realism and Operational Feasibility
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether the three working days are scheduled with realistic slotting (local business hours, buffers), geographic clustering to reduce transit time, and provision for booking suitable venues (e.g., coworking/meeting rooms) in central Berlin.

depth_insight.criterion: Ground Transport Optimization and Cost-Time Analysis
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses the selection and justification of local transport products (e.g., AB 7-day pass vs. single fares/Deutschlandticket), concrete airport-city routes, estimated travel times/costs, and last-mile planning tailored to the itinerary.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Management and Contingency Planning
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates buffers for delays/jet lag, backup transport or meeting formats, cancellation windows, and mitigation of local risks (e.g., potential rail strikes), ensuring the plan remains viable under plausible disruptions.

depth_insight.criterion: Assumption Handling and Scenario Sensitivity
depth_insight.explanation: Measures how missing inputs (e.g., departure city, exact meeting locations) are handled via explicit assumptions, with sensitivity ranges or alternative pathways that keep the plan robust and within budget.

depth_insight.criterion: Leisure Plan Curation with Seasonal and Spatial Fit
depth_insight.explanation: Examines whether leisure suggestions are time- and place-aware (weekend/evening slots, proximity to meetings/hotel), account for mid-October Berlin specifics (weather, likely events), and include booking/queue considerations.

depth_insight.criterion: Local Regulatory and Practical Value-Adds
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses inclusion of high-impact local knowledge that improves the plan’s value (e.g., Berlin business lodging tax exemption procedures, Sunday retail closures affecting shopping plans).

depth_insight.criterion: Use of Realistic Price Benchmarks and Evidence
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether prices (flights, hotels, transit passes) reflect current market ranges for October Berlin travel and cite assumptions or sources, strengthening the credibility of recommendations.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report explicitly maps the task’s constraints (dates, location, budget scope, three working meeting days) to its section structure and keeps these constraints visible as organizing principles throughout.

logical_coherence.criterion: Constraint-Driven Decision Rationale for Key Components
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether choices for flights, lodging, ground transport, meeting schedule, and leisure are logically derived from stated constraints and objectives, with clear reasons linking constraints to decisions.

logical_coherence.criterion: Quantitative Feasibility and Internal Consistency (Budget and Time)
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan includes coherent calculations (cost roll-ups vs. ¥30,000 cap; duration buffers, transfer times) and avoids contradictions (e.g., budget overrun, overlapping times).

logical_coherence.criterion: Temporal and Spatial Coherence of the Itinerary
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks alignment of timelines and locations: arrival/departure vs. meeting days; hotel proximity to central Berlin; transit times between venues; feasible sequencing without conflicts.

Assesses whether key assertions (fares, hotel rates, travel times, distances) are supported by concrete figures or verifiable references, ensuring conclusions rest on stated facts rather than assumptions.
Evaluates whether transitions explicitly carry forward dependencies (e.g., flight arrival informs meeting schedule; hotel choice informs transport plan), preventing logical jumps between sections.
Checks that critical assumptions (exchange rate, booking date/prices, fare class, working days definition) are stated so that the reasoning scope and validity conditions are clear.

clarity_readability.criterion: Day-by-Day Itinerary and Section Layout Scannability
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether the plan is organized with a clear heading hierarchy and a daily schedule (Oct 15–22) that groups flights, lodging, meetings, ground transport, and leisure into easily scannable blocks with bullets/whitespace. Readers should be able to locate any day, time block, or component in seconds.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Precision, Brevity, and Actionability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that instructions use concise, unambiguous wording (e.g., 24-hour times, explicit locations, imperative steps like 'Buy AB zone day ticket at BER T1'), avoiding vague modifiers. Clear sentences and consistent phrasing make tasks directly executable and reduce misinterpretation.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistent Notation for Times, Dates, Currency, and Addresses
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether times use a consistent 24-hour format with time zone labels (e.g., CEST), dates are unambiguous (YYYY-MM-DD), currencies are labeled (CNY/EUR) with clear decimals, and addresses include standardized elements (street, number, postcode, city, country) plus identifiers (airport/line codes). This consistency prevents errors during travel.

clarity_readability.criterion: Budget and Currency Presentation Clarity
clarity_readability.explanation: Looks for a transparent cost breakdown (flights, lodging, local transport, other), totals vs. per-day costs, what is included/excluded, stated exchange rate and date, and visual emphasis on the ¥30,000 cap (e.g., subtotal/total lines). Clear budgeting lets the reader quickly verify affordability.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Tables, Timelines, Maps, and Other Visual Aids
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether tables (for flights/hotels/costs), day timelines, and map links/thumbnails are used appropriately, correctly labeled, and legible (legends, units, captions), enhancing comprehension without clutter or decorative excess. Visuals should complement, not duplicate, the text.

clarity_readability.criterion: Definition and Use of Technical Terms and Local Concepts
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that local terms (e.g., U-Bahn/S-Bahn, fare zones AB/BC, BER, city tax) and any travel jargon are defined at first use or in a brief glossary, and then used consistently. Explaining terms lowers the knowledge barrier for non-local readers and improves understanding.

clarity_readability.criterion: Prioritization and Labeling of Key Information (Business vs. Leisure, Critical Notices)
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether business meeting schedules are clearly separated from leisure sections and whether critical notices (check-in/out times, cancellation deadlines, meeting venue details, contact info) are highlighted via callouts, bold labels, or icons so important items are not missed.

task: I will be traveling to Europe on a business trip within the next month, specifically staying in Berlin, Germany for about one week from October 15 to 22. My budget is within ¥30,000, including all transport and accommodation costs. Please prepare a detailed travel plan that includes suitable flights, local lodging, ground transport arrangements, a schedule for business meetings, and suggestions for leisure activities. My business meetings need to be scheduled over three working days in central Berlin.

goal_alignment.criterion: Budget integrity and value optimization within ¥30,000 (CNY) for flights + lodging
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the plan anchors all key costs in CNY with a transparent breakdown (flights, hotel, ground transport), includes realistic price ranges and a contingency buffer, and explains value-for-money trade-offs. This matches his rational, cost-performance mindset and ensures feasibility under the stated ceiling.

goal_alignment.criterion: Meeting-centric weekday scheduling in central Berlin with efficiency buffers
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses if three business days are firmly placed on working days in central Berlin (Mitte/Potsdamer Platz/Friedrichstrasse areas), with morning slots (his peak productivity), realistic travel times, and buffers for overruns and jet lag. Directly supports his results-oriented style and the task’s core requirement.

goal_alignment.criterion: Chengdu–Berlin flight routing aligned to reliability, jet lag management, and timeline
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether proposed flights minimize risk (sensible minimum connection times, reputable hubs), time arrivals to allow rest before meetings, and offer 1–2 solid alternatives with clear pros/cons. This reflects his logistics mindset and need for dependable arrival for work.

goal_alignment.criterion: Business-grade, centrally located, and sustainable lodging selection
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks if the hotel is in central Berlin with fast transit access, quiet rooms, strong Wi‑Fi, in-room desk, breakfast, and ideally green certifications or sustainability practices. Fits his business comfort preference and eco-conscious tendency while controlling costs.

goal_alignment.criterion: Ground transport plan optimized for BVG zones and eco-first mobility
goal_alignment.explanation: Verifies that the plan correctly uses AB/ABC zones (including BER in C) with the right product (weekly pass vs singles), provides door-to-door time/cost estimates, and includes backup (taxi/ride-hail) for time-critical legs. Aligns with his efficiency and low-carbon preferences.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk and contingency management (visa/compliance, buffers, disruptions, insurance)
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the plan highlights Schengen visa timeline/documents, change/cancellation policies, realistic buffers, and contingency actions for common disruptions (e.g., rail strikes, missed connections). This mirrors his conservative risk posture and supply chain problem-solving approach.

goal_alignment.criterion: Leisure plan matched to immersive culture, health, and light travel
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for historically rich, high-yield activities near the hotel (museums, Cold War heritage walks), healthy dining spots, and low-footprint options (walking, cycling). Includes a jogging route or gym access to support his exercise routine and stress relief.

goal_alignment.criterion: Decision-ready presentation with comparisons and actionable checklists
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan presents 1–2 flight and hotel options with pros/cons, key metrics (time, cost, distance), and concise checklists (eSIM/payment/apps). May annotate key times in both CEST and China time. Matches his fast, data-driven decision style.

goal_alignment.criterion: Family connectivity and wellbeing considerations
goal_alignment.explanation: Looks for suggested daily call windows that suit China time, rest/adjustment guidance for jet lag, and small touches (e.g., gift ideas nearby) to maintain family bonds. Supports his evening family habit and mental well-being on a demanding trip.

content_alignment.criterion: Origin- and Route-Specific Flight Curation (Chengdu↔Berlin, realistic hubs and timings)
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether flight options are anchored to Chengdu (CTU/TFU) and Berlin (BER) with plausible connections (e.g., FRA/MUC/HEL/IST/DOH), durations, and time windows matching the Oct 15–22 window. This ensures the content selection is personalized to his actual origin rather than generic Europe itineraries.

content_alignment.criterion: RMB Budget Translation and Value-for-Money Cost Breakdown
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether transport and hotel options are costed in RMB with clear ranges and trade-offs (comfort vs price, refundable vs non-refundable), aligning with his rational, price–performance decision style and the ¥30,000 cap.

content_alignment.criterion: Business-Hotel Fit in Central Berlin
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether lodging choices match his mid-to-high-end business preference and are located in central areas (e.g., Mitte/Potsdamer Platz/Alexanderplatz/Friedrichstrasse) with quiet rooms, reliable Wi‑Fi, breakfast, and gym access—content tailored to his work-focused stays.

content_alignment.criterion: Sustainability-Aligned Transport and Stay Choices
content_alignment.explanation: Checks for low-carbon content choices (public transit over taxis, weekly BVG passes, hotel eco certifications, lower-emission routing/offset options), reflecting his growing eco-consciousness and light-travel preference.

content_alignment.criterion: Schedule Optimization Respecting Morning Productivity and Jet Lag
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether meeting days are placed and time-blocked to leverage his morning efficiency and accommodate the UTC+8→CEST shift (buffers on arrival day, morning slots for key meetings), aligning content to his personal time preference.

content_alignment.criterion: Logistics-Minded Comparisons and Risk Trade-offs
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the plan compares routes and transfers using factors a supply chain manager values (connection reliability, minimum connection times, hub efficiency, transfer risks), providing depth aligned to his analytical background.

content_alignment.criterion: China-User Digital/Payment Ecosystem Fit
