content_alignment.explanation: Checks if the content references tools and options that fit his habits (BVG app/eSIM recommendations, WeChat/Alipay/UnionPay acceptance notes, practical app ecosystem alignment) to reduce friction for a China-based traveler.

content_alignment.criterion: Leisure Tailored to Immersive Culture and Fitness Habits
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether leisure suggestions emphasize Berlin’s history/culture (Museum Island, Berlin Wall sites, walking/biking) and include morning jogging routes or yoga-friendly options—matching his immersive style and exercise routine.

content_alignment.criterion: China-Origin Traveler Specifics at Appropriate Depth
content_alignment.explanation: Checks for inclusion of China-relevant travel requirements and norms (Schengen business visa considerations, insurance expectations, invitation letters) and RMB-centric info without overloading irrelevant details—personalized to his context and timing.

content_alignment.criterion: Relevance Filter to Business Traveler Context
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the content avoids off-target advice (e.g., party hostels, backpacker itineraries, family-with-kids logistics) and stays Berlin- and business-trip focused, ensuring high signal-to-noise for his needs.

presentation_fit.criterion: Professional, concise language tone suitable for a supply chain manager
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report uses businesslike, objective, and succinct wording, avoids promotional fluff, and presents points with clear logic so the user can scan and decide quickly.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology and localization clarity (IATA codes + full names, bilingual labels where helpful)
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that travel/logistics terms (e.g., BER, TXL legacy references, fare classes, U/S-Bahn) are used correctly with brief clarifications, airport/city names show both the code and full name, and key items are localized (Chinese with necessary English/German names) to reduce ambiguity.

presentation_fit.criterion: Clear structure and navigation (headings, numbering, TOC, section chunking)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether the report is organized into intuitive sections (flights, hotel, local transport, meeting schedule, leisure), with numbered headings, a mini table of contents, and chunked paragraphs/bullets that support scanning and screenshotting on WeChat/DingTalk.

presentation_fit.criterion: Executive summary with key metrics upfront
presentation_fit.explanation: Determine if a one-screen summary presents critical KPIs (dates, total estimated cost, flight duration, hotel location, commute times, meeting days) so the user can grasp essentials in his high-efficiency morning review.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use of comparison tables/matrices for flights, hotels, and transport options
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether options are displayed in structured tables (columns for time, duration, transfers, cost, location, commute, cancellation policy) with consistent units and optional eco-friendly badges for easy trade-off comparison.

presentation_fit.criterion: Time/date and time-zone formatting consistency
presentation_fit.explanation: Check for 24-hour time, clear date formats (e.g., 10-15 Tue), explicit time zones (CST vs. CEST) and noted time difference to avoid scheduling errors across Chengdu and Berlin.

presentation_fit.criterion: Transparent cost presentation format (currency units, conversion, subtotals, totals)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate if costs are shown with currency symbols (¥/€), conversion rate used, line items with subtotals and a clear total, improving budget comprehension without judging the amounts themselves.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visual scheduling of the three meeting days and daily timeline
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the meeting schedule and daily plan are presented as time blocks or a calendar-style view with durations and buffers, improving at-a-glance understanding of each day’s flow.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget adherence with itemized, realistic cost breakdown
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides an itemized total cost (international flights, accommodation by night, airport transfers, local passes) in CNY with stated FX assumptions, cites realistic price ranges/sources, and includes a 5–10% buffer while staying under ¥30,000.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Visa and entry feasibility within the one-month window
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the plan includes a concrete Schengen business visa path (documents list, insurance ≥ €30,000 medical coverage, VFS/consulate appointment steps, expected processing times, invitation/itinerary requirements) and a timeline ensuring approval before departure or offering viable expedite options.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Flight plan specificity and bookability
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report lists 2–3 concrete round-trip flight options on the exact dates with departure/arrival times, layovers, total durations, baggage allowance, on-time reliability, refund/change rules, and direct booking channels so the user can immediately purchase.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Lodging practicality near central Berlin
actionability_practicality.explanation: Judges if the report proposes 2–3 specific business hotels in central Berlin (e.g., Mitte/Potsdamer Platz) with nightly rates, cancellation policy, amenities (fast Wi‑Fi, desk, quiet rooms, breakfast), walking/public transit time to meeting locations, and booking links.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Ground transport operability (airport transfers and daily commuting)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for step-by-step airport-to-hotel routes, recommended passes (e.g., Berlin AB weekly pass/Deutschlandticket) with prices, route times between hotel and meeting sites, relevant apps (BVG, DB Navigator), and practical payment/connectivity tips (contactless cards, eSIM).

actionability_practicality.criterion: Meeting schedule realism for three working days
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks that the plan allocates three central Berlin workdays with morning meeting slots (user’s peak productivity), realistic travel buffers, exact or sample venues (client sites/co-working), and time for prep/materials, ensuring the schedule is executable on the ground.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Actionable leisure plan aligned to time windows
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether leisure suggestions fit around the work schedule (evenings/weekend windows), include proximity to hotel/meetings, opening hours, duration, ticket prices/links, and options matching interests (culture, local cuisine, light/eco activities).

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk and contingency measures
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates inclusion of buffers (connection times, meeting slack), alternative flight/hotel options, travel insurance guidance beyond visa minimums, disruption playbooks (transit strikes, delays), and key contacts (112, embassy/consulate), improving resilience of the plan.

depth_insight.criterion: Budget Realism and Optimization Under the ¥30,000 Cap
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report builds a credible, itemized cost model (flights, lodging, local transport) using current market benchmarks and exchange-rate assumptions, compares alternatives, and justifies choices that keep the total within budget while preserving business viability.

depth_insight.criterion: Calendar- and Time-Aware Schedule Design
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether arrival/departure times, hotel check-in/out, jet lag, meeting blocks, buffers, and weekday/weekend constraints (e.g., Sunday retail closures, museum schedules) are integrated to produce a feasible three-working-day meeting plan and effective use of remaining time.

depth_insight.criterion: Lodging and Meeting Location Strategy
depth_insight.explanation: Examines the depth of analysis in choosing neighborhoods/hotels (e.g., Mitte, Potsdamer Platz, Friedrichstrasse) to minimize transit times and improve reliability for central Berlin meetings, balancing safety, noise, amenities, and price with quantified access metrics.

depth_insight.criterion: Ground Transport System Optimization
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses mastery of Berlin transport (BER rail options, S/U-Bahn, AB vs ABC zones, passes vs single tickets, taxi/rideshare trade-offs) with break-even calculations and reliability considerations to minimize total cost and time.

depth_insight.criterion: Assumption Management and Scenario Tailoring
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether unspecified inputs (e.g., origin city, airline preferences) are handled via explicit, reasonable assumptions or branching scenarios, with quantified impacts on cost and schedule to maintain plan validity.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk and Contingency Planning
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses the inclusion of practical risk controls (visa/insurance needs, strike/operational disruption contingencies, cancellation policies, connectivity plans), with buffers and fallback options that protect meeting success and budget.

depth_insight.criterion: Context-Aware Leisure Curation
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether leisure suggestions are time-boxed to actual free windows, aligned with locations near lodging/meetings, account for opening hours/reservations, and provide distinctive Berlin value with time/cost estimates.

depth_insight.criterion: Original, High-Value Practical Insights
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards unique, actionable tips (e.g., Sunday trading rules, packing for October weather, VAT, coworking day passes, eSIMs) that materially enhance the trip beyond standard recommendations.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a coherent structure that starts from constraints/assumptions (dates, budget cap, central Berlin requirement), then builds a day-by-day itinerary, logistics (flights, lodging, ground transport), and budget summary, ensuring each later section depends on earlier premises.

Verifies that all elements satisfy the fixed constraints without contradiction: travel dates (Oct 15–22), three working days of meetings in central Berlin, and total budget ≤ ¥30,000 including transport and accommodation; checks consistency of check-in/out dates, nights counted, and locations claimed as 'central'.

Assesses whether the timeline is feasible end-to-end: flight arrival aligns with hotel check-in; transit times allow on-time arrival to meetings; meetings are assigned to three working days; leisure activities fit around business commitments without creating overlaps or impossible sequences.

Evaluates whether cost items (international flights, accommodation, ground transport) are itemized with currency conversions, add up correctly, and logically support the budget conclusion (≤ ¥30,000); checks that upgrades or add-ons are accounted for rather than implicitly assumed.

Checks that major decisions (flight selection, hotel location relative to central Berlin, transport modes) are justified by concrete, relevant evidence (e.g., example flight numbers/times/prices, hotel address and distance to meeting area, transit durations), and that the evidence logically supports the choice.

Assesses whether sections explicitly connect (e.g., the meeting schedule references the hotel location and transit plan; budget references the specific options chosen), preventing logical jumps and making the reasoning chain traceable across the report.

clarity_readability.criterion: Sectioning and Navigability of Layout (Travel-Plan Oriented)
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether the report uses a clear, consistent hierarchy and travel-appropriate sections (e.g., Executive Summary, Flights, Accommodation, Ground Transport, Business Meetings, Leisure, Budget, Contingencies) and, when lengthy, a table of contents and day-by-day timeline. Good navigability lets readers quickly find operational details under time pressure.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity, Precision, and Professional Tone of Language
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether instructions are unambiguous, concise, and action-oriented (e.g., use of 24-hour time with time zone, explicit steps for transfers), with correct grammar and minimal typos. Clear language prevents misinterpretation in time-sensitive logistics.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency and Standardization of Formats (Dates/Times/Currencies/Addresses)
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for consistent date formats (e.g., 2025-10-15 or 'Oct 15, 2025'), 24-hour times with time zone labels (CEST/CET), currency notation with stated conversion rate (¥ and €), proper airport/rail codes (BER, ICE), and complete addresses with postal codes. Standardization reduces cognitive load and operational errors.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Tables, Timelines, and Visual Aids
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether budgets are in clear tables with subtotals/totals, daily schedules in timelines, and routes shown via simple maps or map links; visual elements have captions, labels, and are legible and colorblind-safe. Good visuals improve scanability and comprehension of dense logistics.

clarity_readability.criterion: Use and Explanation of Domain/Local Terms
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether transport and booking terms (e.g., BVG zones AB/BC, U-/S-Bahn, BER T1-2, Sparpreis, city tax, PNR) are introduced with brief definitions or tooltips/footnotes upon first use. Clear term explanations lower the barrier for non-local readers.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Key Constraints, Assumptions, and Actionable Callouts
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks use of emphasis devices (summary boxes, bullets, bold) to foreground critical constraints (budget cap ¥30,000, Oct 15–22 dates, three central-Berlin workdays), assumptions (exchange rate), and time-sensitive items (check-in/check-out, cancellation deadlines, visa/insurance reminders). Highlighting helps readers grasp essentials at a glance.

task: I will be traveling to Europe on a business trip within the next month, specifically staying in Berlin, Germany for about one week from October 15 to 22. My budget is within ¥30,000, including all transport and accommodation costs. Please prepare a detailed travel plan that includes suitable flights, local lodging, ground transport arrangements, a schedule for business meetings, and suggestions for leisure activities. My business meetings need to be scheduled over three working days in central Berlin.

goal_alignment.criterion: Budget adherence with RMB breakdown and contingency (incl. reimbursement readiness)
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan keeps total flights + lodging within ¥30,000, provides an RMB-denominated, tax/fee-inclusive breakdown, shows trade-offs (e.g., premium economy vs. standard), sets a 10–15% buffer, and highlights invoice-friendly vendors and cancellation/refund policies for company reimbursement. This fits his cost-effectiveness, planning discipline, and need for clean documentation.

goal_alignment.criterion: Jet-lag-aware, central Berlin business schedule with buffers and commute realism
goal_alignment.explanation: Check that three working days of meetings are scheduled in central Berlin (Mitte) with realistic door-to-door commute times, time-zone adaptation (e.g., mid-morning starts on first meeting day), built-in buffers between meetings, and contingency slots. This matches his morning preference, risk aversion, and need for reliable execution.

goal_alignment.criterion: Lodging choice fit: central, gym-equipped, quiet, high Wi‑Fi, value
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the recommended hotel is in/near Mitte with fast transit access, a proper gym, quiet rooms, early breakfast hours, strong Wi‑Fi, and solid hygiene/service—at a value-aligned price point. This mirrors his business-hotel preference, health routine, and quality standards.

goal_alignment.criterion: Flight plan from Suzhou/Shanghai optimized for reliability, arrival time, comfort, and cost
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether flight options depart from PVG/SHA (or NKG as backup), favor reliable hubs and layovers (not too tight), aim for daytime or early evening arrival in Berlin to ease adjustment, and consider seat selection/ergonomics (aisle, lumbar support) within budget. This respects his risk management, health, and pragmatic style.

goal_alignment.criterion: Ground transport plan (BER transfers, ticket types, routes, costs, alternatives)
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for clear instructions from BER to the hotel (e.g., FEX/RE/S‑Bahn), AB/ABC ticketing or 7-day pass options with approximate costs, key S/U‑Bahn lines for meetings, and taxi alternatives for time-critical legs. This supports his action-oriented, self-guided travel style.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk and compliance safeguards (visa, insurance, contingencies, local constraints)
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan flags Schengen business visa steps/timelines, mandatory insurance coverage, strike/delay contingencies, buffer days, Sunday/holiday closures, and critical document checklists. This aligns with his risk-averse planning and ensures feasibility under time constraints.

goal_alignment.criterion: Leisure personalization to history + Industry 4.0/tech interests with feasible booking
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether leisure time includes Museum Island/Berlin Wall for history and relevant tech/industry visits (e.g., Deutsches Technikmuseum, Siemens/Fraunhofer hubs, optional Tesla Grünheide if practical), with prebooking notes and proximity to base. This resonates with his professional curiosity and personal interests.

goal_alignment.criterion: Dining plan balancing Chinese comfort (esp. Shaanxi) and authentic Berlin cuisine
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for nearby Shaanxi/Chinese options (e.g., around Kantstraße) alongside well-chosen German dishes/venues, with opening hours and reservation tips to reduce uncertainty. This addresses long-trip comfort while enabling curated local culinary exploration.

goal_alignment.criterion: Health and routine support (jet lag, running routes, lumbar care, sleep hygiene)
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess inclusion of morning run routes (e.g., Tiergarten/Spree/Tempelhofer Feld), gym usage windows, jet-lag strategies (light exposure, meal timing), and lumbar-friendly travel tips. This fits his exercise habits and back health needs, improving trip performance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Executable presentation and tool ecosystem fit
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether the plan is structured into day-by-day tasks with time-boxed actions, addresses in Chinese/English/German for taxis, and recommends practical apps (BVG/DB Navigator/Google Maps offline/eSIM options; WeChat/DingTalk calendar integration). This matches his preference for clear, tool-supported execution.

content_alignment.criterion: Budget and Currency Sensitivity in CNY with Cost-Effectiveness Rationale
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether flight and hotel options are selected and costed explicitly in CNY with clear price bands and trade-offs, reflecting his rational, planning-oriented style and ¥30,000 cap. Alignment requires content that prioritizes value-for-money choices suitable for a quality-focused yet frugal decision-maker.

content_alignment.criterion: Flight Routing and Timing Tailored to Suzhou/Shanghai Base and Daily Rhythm
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether flights originate from PVG/SHA to BER with efficient routing (minimal layovers), timed to his morning/evening preference and including arrival-day buffer/jet-lag-aware timing considerations—content choices that respect his routine and comfort rather than generic flight listings.

content_alignment.criterion: Accommodation Fit: Business Hotel with Gym, Hygiene, and Centrality to Meetings
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether lodging options match his business-hotel preference (gym access, high hygiene standards) and are located in central Berlin (e.g., Mitte/Tiergarten/Potsdamer Platz) near likely meeting areas, aligning with his practical, efficiency-focused needs and quality standards.

content_alignment.criterion: Industry 4.0 and Technology-Oriented Leisure Selection
content_alignment.explanation: Judges whether leisure content includes Berlin-specific technology and manufacturing sites (e.g., Futurium, Deutsches Technikmuseum, science/industry campuses like Adlershof or Siemensstadt, factory tours if available), matching his professional identity and interests in smart manufacturing.

content_alignment.criterion: Cultural and Personal Hobby Alignment (Pottery, Calligraphy, Gardening, Classical Music)
content_alignment.explanation: Checks inclusion of activities resonant with his hobbies—such as KPM Royal Porcelain tours/workshops, Asian art collections (Humboldt Forum), Berlin Botanical Garden, and concerts at the Philharmonie/Konzerthaus around the trip dates—showing content tailored to his non-work passions.

content_alignment.criterion: Dining Recommendations Balancing Local Berlin/German Cuisine and Quality Chinese/Shaanxi Options
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan proposes both authentic local cuisine experiences and reliable Chinese/Shaanxi restaurants for comfort, located conveniently near the hotel/meeting zones, fitting his habit of exploring local food while respecting his taste anchors.

content_alignment.criterion: Fitness and Health Routine Integration
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the content thoughtfully integrates fitness options (hotel gyms, morning/evening running routes like Tiergarten/Tempelhofer Feld/Spree paths) and a sensible activity density acknowledging long-haul recovery—aligned to his running habit and lumbar care.

content_alignment.criterion: Ground Transport Plan Matching Pragmatic, Cost-Conscious Style
content_alignment.explanation: Checks for selection of simple and economical Berlin transport options (e.g., AB weekly tickets, BER airport trains like FEX/RE to central stations) and positioning accommodation along key S-/U-Bahn lines near central meeting areas, aligning content to his efficiency and value mindset.

content_alignment.criterion: Self-Guided, Modular Itinerary Structure
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the itinerary is presented as flexible modules (e.g., optional museum/industry visits, concert evenings, running sessions) that empower independent choices, matching his self-guided planning style without enforcing rigid tours.

presentation_fit.criterion: Macro structure and navigability (executive summary, numbered sections, clear headings/TOC)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report starts with a 5–8 line executive summary highlighting dates, budget cap, and key arrangements, then uses numbered sections (Flights, Lodging, Ground Transport, Meeting Schedule, Leisure) with consistent heading levels for fast scanning—matching a technical director’s preference for structured documents.

presentation_fit.criterion: Schedule and time presentation clarity (24-hour format, weekday labels, time zone handling, chronological day-by-day plan)
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that all times use 24-hour format with weekday annotations (e.g., Tue 15 Oct, local time), explicit CET/CEST vs Beijing time where relevant, buffers between appointments/commutes, and a chronological daily timeline—reducing scheduling ambiguity for business meetings.

presentation_fit.criterion: Cost and currency presentation (RMB as primary, EUR secondary, exchange rate note, totals/subtotals)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether costs are shown primarily in RMB with EUR in parentheses, a stated exchange rate/date, and clear subtotals (flights, hotels, transport) and totals, enabling a cost-conscious manager to audit at a glance without doing conversions.

presentation_fit.criterion: Language tone fit for a senior technical audience (concise, neutral, non-promotional, logically sequenced)
presentation_fit.explanation: Judge whether the tone is precise and professional (no flowery or marketing language), with logical connectors and concise sentences, matching the user’s rational, engineering-oriented reading style.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology usability and bilingual naming (Chinese first, with English/German originals; acronyms defined on first use)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess the use of Chinese as the main language while including original place/transport names (e.g., S-Bahn, BVG, ‘AB区’) for searchability in Berlin; acronyms and local terms are briefly defined on first mention to avoid comprehension gaps.

presentation_fit.criterion: Comparative option tables for key decisions (flights, hotels, transport passes) with essential fields and highlights
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that options are presented in compact tables (e.g., depart/arrive times, duration, stops; hotel distance to center/gym availability; transport zones/validity), with consistent columns and brief pros/cons notes—supporting quick trade-off assessment without wading through prose.

presentation_fit.criterion: Map/address presentation and wayfinding aids (full addresses, postal codes, station names/lines, map links)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether addresses include bilingual names, postal codes, nearest stations with line numbers, zone info (AB/BC), and clean links to official maps—so the reader can navigate efficiently in Berlin using familiar map apps.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-friendly readability and microstructure (bullets, short paragraphs, checklists, consistent units)
presentation_fit.explanation: Judge whether content is broken into bullets and short paragraphs, uses checklists for pre-trip and daily tasks, keeps consistent units (km, minutes), and avoids dense blocks—matching DingTalk/WeChat reading habits and reducing on-the-go cognitive load.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget compliance with itemized, tax-inclusive cost model and buffer
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report provides a CNY/EUR itemized budget (flights, hotels with taxes/city tax, ground transport, data/roaming, insurance, contingency) that totals within ¥30,000 and reserves a realistic buffer for overruns.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Booking-ready international flight plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report lists concrete flight options from the Shanghai/Suzhou area to Berlin (BER) with dates/times aligned to Oct 15–22, total travel time, layover lengths, baggage allowance, fare rules/cancellation/refundability, and estimated prices so the user can book immediately.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Booking-ready lodging plan aligned to meeting area
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses if the hotel recommendations specify exact properties in central Berlin or with direct transit to meeting locations, nightly rates with taxes, cancellation terms, breakfast availability, gym access, and invoice availability, enabling immediate reservation.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Schengen visa and travel insurance readiness with deadlines
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies the report includes a step-by-step visa checklist (appointment timing, documents, itinerary proof, financials) and compliant insurance (≥€30,000 medical coverage) with a timeline that fits the trip starting ~one month from now.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Meeting schedule feasibility and buffer design
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks that three working-day meetings in central Berlin are scheduled on actual weekdays within the stay, account for jet lag, include door-to-door transit times, and build in buffers around arrivals/departures to avoid missed meetings.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Ground transport practicality (airport transfers and in-city mobility)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines whether the plan provides concrete routes and costs from BER to the hotel, recommends an appropriate Berlin fare product (e.g., 7-day AB pass), includes last-mile options, and names reliable apps/tools for navigation.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Leisure activity feasibility within time windows
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates if leisure suggestions fit around the meeting schedule and flight times, specify locations, opening hours, travel times, and any booking needs, minimizing risk of schedule conflicts or excessive costs.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk and contingency planning
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses presence of practical backups (alternative flights/routes, flexible fares, hotel late check-in, weather plan), emergency contacts, and guidance for disruptions to maintain trip objectives within budget.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Reimbursement and documentation readiness
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report indicates how to obtain valid receipts/invoices for flights, hotels, transport passes, and insurance, facilitating smooth corporate reimbursement.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Health and comfort considerations for long-haul travel
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies inclusion of actionable measures like seat selection guidance, reasonable layover durations, stretch/jet lag tips, hotel gym access, and nearby running routes to support the user's routine and reduce strain.

depth_insight.criterion: Budget Feasibility and Cost Modeling Depth
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the plan builds an itemized, quantified cost model (flights, lodging, airport transfers, local transit, estimated incidentals) with currency conversion, volatility buffers, and trade-offs, demonstrating the trip is realistically within ¥30,000 and showing what changes keep it feasible if prices move.

depth_insight.criterion: Flight Strategy and Time–Productivity Optimization
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether flight options are analyzed beyond price—arrival/departure timing relative to meetings, layover risk, on-time performance, recovery from jet lag, baggage and changeability—showing insight into minimizing disruption and maximizing readiness for three workdays.

depth_insight.criterion: Lodging Selection Strategy and Location Optimization
depth_insight.explanation: Examines how the plan selects neighborhoods and specific lodging by analyzing commute times to central Berlin meeting areas, price vs. amenities (work desk, reliable Wi‑Fi, early check-in, breakfast), safety/noise, and cancellation flexibility to preserve both cost-efficiency and business productivity.

depth_insight.criterion: Ground Transport Plan Sophistication and Cost-Effectiveness
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for a data-driven choice between AB/ABC passes vs single tickets, airport transfer options (FEX/S‑Bahn vs taxi/ride-hail), route/time estimates between hotel and meetings, and contingencies—quantifying costs and reliability for the meeting days.

depth_insight.criterion: Business Meeting Schedule Insight and Cultural/Operational Fit
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether three central Berlin workdays are structured with realistic durations, buffer times for transit, venue availability (e.g., coworking/meeting rooms), sequencing by proximity, and local business norms (punctuality, lunch windows), reflecting deep operational understanding.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk and Contingency Planning
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan anticipates and mitigates key risks—visa/entry timelines, fare/availability volatility in a near-term window, transport disruptions/strikes, missed connections—and proposes fallback options (refundable fares, alternative routes, hybrid/remote meeting backup).

depth_insight.criterion: Handling of Uncertainties and Assumption Impact Analysis
depth_insight.explanation: Judges how the report addresses unknowns (origin city, whether ¥ denotes CNY or JPY, personal preferences) by stating working assumptions and analyzing how these affect flight/lodging choices and budget feasibility, offering conditional paths where outcomes materially diverge.

depth_insight.criterion: Leisure Plan Originality Integrated with Schedule and Budget
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether leisure suggestions are curated (seasonal, time-of-week aware—e.g., Sunday retail closures, museum hours), geographically coherent with meetings/hotel, include time and cost estimates, and add distinctive value without jeopardizing the budget.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report explicitly maps all required constraints (Oct 15–22 in Berlin, three working days of meetings in central Berlin, total budget ≤ ¥30,000, inclusion of flights/lodging/ground transport/meetings/leisure) into a clear plan structure (arrival/departure, day-by-day outline, sections for each required component). This ensures the argument starts from the stated premises and organizes the plan accordingly.

logical_coherence.criterion: Temporal Feasibility and Sequencing of the Itinerary
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the timeline is logically coherent: flight times align with check-in/out, meeting days are exactly three weekdays in central Berlin with no overlaps, travel buffers are included, ground transit durations are realistic, and leisure activities do not conflict with business commitments. This tests the reasoning chain from time-related premises to feasible daily conclusions.

Assesses whether all cost elements (international flights, lodging, local transport, incidental fees) are itemized, currency conversions are consistent, totals are correctly summed, and the plan stays within ¥30,000. Logical rigor requires that the budget conclusion follows from complete, non-contradictory arithmetic and stated cost assumptions.

Checks that each key choice (flight, lodging location, ground transport mode) is justified by criteria derived from the task (dates, central Berlin proximity for meetings, time efficiency, cost) and that cited data or reasonable estimates directly support the choice. This links claims about suitability to concrete supporting facts.

Evaluates whether transitions explicitly connect sections (e.g., flight arrival informs Day 1 schedule; lodging location informs meeting commute times) and whether dependencies are referenced rather than assumed. Smooth transitions prevent logical jumps and maintain continuity of reasoning across the plan.

Checks whether crucial assumptions (e.g., departure city/airport, meeting locations within central Berlin, class of flight, local transit passes) are stated and consistently applied throughout scheduling and budgeting, avoiding contradictions later in the report.

clarity_readability.criterion: Section Structure and Navigability Aligned to Task
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear hierarchy (e.g., Overview, Budget Summary, Flights, Lodging, Ground Transport, Business Meetings, Leisure, Checklist) with logical ordering and consistent headings so readers can locate needed information quickly.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity, Concision, and Professional Tone
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether sentences are concise, free of ambiguity, and free of grammatical errors; uses plain, direct instructions suitable for business use without slang or verbosity, improving quick comprehension under time pressure.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency of Time/Date/Timezone and Currency Notation
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether dates use an unambiguous format (e.g., 2025-10-15), times use a consistent 24-hour clock with time zones (e.g., CEST), and currencies are consistently labeled with conversion notes (e.g., ¥ vs. €) to prevent execution mistakes.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Tables and Visual Aids for Execution
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks if itinerary, flight, lodging, budget, and meeting schedules are presented in clear tables or timelines with complete fields (e.g., flight number, terminals, local times, addresses, contact info, costs), and if any maps or route diagrams are labeled and legible.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Constraints and Key Actions
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether critical constraints and actions (¥30,000 budget cap, meeting days vs. leisure days, check-in/out times, transport pass validity zones) are surfaced via callouts, bullet points, or summaries for rapid grasp.

clarity_readability.criterion: Definition and Consistent Use of Travel-Specific Terms and Codes
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether domain terms and abbreviations (e.g., IATA codes, BVG, AB/BC zones, U-/S-Bahn, OTA) are correctly used and briefly explained on first mention to lower comprehension barriers for non-experts.

task: I plan to take a one-week deep cultural trip to Morocco next spring with a budget of ¥20,000. I especially want to experience the traditional souks of Marrakech and Fes, take a Moroccan cooking class, participate in local artisan workshops, and stay in local guesthouses to experience authentic life. I would like a professional guide for in-depth cultural tours while keeping a few days free for independent exploration. Please design a detailed itinerary including cultural activities, guesthouse recommendations, intra-city transport options, and safety tips.

goal_alignment.criterion: Complete inclusion of all requested elements with optimized 7-day routing
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the itinerary explicitly covers Marrakech and Fes souks, one cooking class, artisan workshops, local guesthouses (riads), professional guided in-depth tours, and a few free days; also verifies the route minimizes backtracking and fits a one-week window.

goal_alignment.criterion: Budget discipline and transparent cost breakdown in CNY for a student traveler
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates itemized costs (lodging, guides/classes, intercity/intra-city transport, meals, tips, SIM, contingencies) in CNY with stated assumptions (e.g., airfare treated clearly), cost-saving options, and total staying within ¥20,000 with a sensible buffer.

goal_alignment.criterion: Safety, scam-avoidance, and solo-male risk management in medinas and transport
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses tailored guidance on fake guides, taxi practices, night navigation, souk bargaining boundaries, ATM/cash handling, emergency contacts, SIM for connectivity, and etiquette (including Ramadan context), reflecting a risk-averse young solo traveler.

goal_alignment.criterion: Balanced mix of professional guided tours and free exploration with rationale and vetting
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks that specific days are assigned to professional guides (e.g., medina history, tanneries, crafts) with vetted operators and clear pricing, while other days are structured for self-guided discovery; explains why each activity is guided or independent.

goal_alignment.criterion: Authentic guesthouse (riad) recommendations optimized for safety, cleanliness, and value
goal_alignment.explanation: Looks for named riads/guesthouses in safe, accessible medina-adjacent areas with price ranges, cleanliness and Wi‑Fi notes, cancellation policies, and alternatives for peak periods, matching the user’s budget-first, cleanliness-priority preference.

goal_alignment.criterion: Transport optimization within and between cities for a cautious planner
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates clarity and realism of intercity routing (e.g., ONCF train Marrakech–Fes with time estimates and booking tips) and intra-city options (petit taxis, walking routes, typical fares, first/last mile to riads), including time/cost estimates and practical tips.

goal_alignment.criterion: Depth and specificity of cultural immersion (cooking class and artisan workshops)
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for reputable, hands-on options with concrete schedules, content (dishes/crafts), ethical cooperatives, and educational context (history/techniques of crafts), aligning with the user’s desire for in-depth cultural learning.

goal_alignment.criterion: Structure and cognitive fit for a CS student (time-boxing, checklists, decision logic)
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the plan uses clear day-by-day time slots, buffers, task lists, and contingencies (if-else branches for sold-out trains/closed sites), matching the user’s analytical, planning-heavy style and boosting executability.

goal_alignment.criterion: Tech enablement and China-friendly readiness
goal_alignment.explanation: Looks for guidance on SIM/eSIM choices and data needs, offline maps/translation/currency tools, plug types and power, and realistic payment advice (cash vs cards; China wallet limitations), reducing friction for a tech-savvy Chinese student.

goal_alignment.criterion: Spring timing intelligence (weather, daylight, Ramadan/holiday impacts)
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks that the plan reflects spring conditions (temperature, daylight), potential Ramadan/Eid effects on hours and meals, and booking lead-time tips, ensuring feasibility for the stated travel season.

content_alignment.criterion: Authenticity and specificity of Marrakech/Fes souk, artisan, and cooking experiences
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the content curates specific souk submarkets (e.g., dyers/leather/tile), hands-on artisan workshops, and market-to-kitchen cooking classes with concrete distinctions between Marrakech and Fes, aligning with his explicit cultural targets and desire for authentic life rather than generic attractions.

content_alignment.criterion: Guesthouse (riad) curation aligned to student budget, cleanliness, Wi‑Fi, and medina proximity
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether recommended stays match his cost-effectiveness and cleanliness priorities, provide reliable Wi‑Fi and quieter environments for an introverted student, and are well-located for souk immersion, with reasoning on trade-offs (authenticity vs comfort).

content_alignment.criterion: Guide-led vs self-exploration balance tailored to his planning preference
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the itinerary content meaningfully allocates deep cultural tours with a professional guide while preserving free days for independent exploration, reflecting his self-guided habit and stated need for both depth and autonomy.

content_alignment.criterion: Safety and risk management customized to a young, risk-averse solo traveler in Moroccan medinas
content_alignment.explanation: Checks for targeted safety content on souk/tannery tout scams, taxi norms, pickpockets, food/water hygiene, night-time movement, and navigating crowded medinas—aligned to his cautious nature and first-hand context rather than generic safety lists.

content_alignment.criterion: Learning-oriented cultural depth for an analytical university student
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the content provides brief but substantive context on crafts (e.g., zellige, leather tanning), market structures, cooking staples/spices, and workshop pedagogy levels, satisfying his curiosity and preference for deeper understanding.

content_alignment.criterion: Digital-tool alignment (maps, translation, booking suited to Chinese travelers)
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the content favors app-based navigation, translation, and booking channels practical for a Chinese student (e.g., offline maps, eSIM guidance, platforms with Chinese/English support), matching his high digital literacy and research habits.

content_alignment.criterion: Intra-city transport options aligned to budget and comfort
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether recommended transport modes within Marrakech/Fes (walking routes, petit taxis with meter/price norms, limited public transit where relevant) are selected for cost-effectiveness and ease, not an exhaustive but unfocused list.

content_alignment.criterion: Language and cultural mediation (Mandarin/English guide availability and phrase aids)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the content anticipates Arabic/French language barriers by suggesting English/Mandarin-capable guides or practical phrase aids for bargaining and etiquette, enhancing authentic interactions for a Chinese traveler.

content_alignment.criterion: Spring seasonality and cultural calendar relevance (e.g., Ramadan impact)
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the content accounts for spring climate and potential Ramadan effects on opening hours, meal timing, and market rhythms, personalizing recommendations to his actual travel window.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tone and language style fit (neutral, precise, instruction-oriented)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether the report avoids flowery travel-blog prose and uses concise, objective, step-by-step language appropriate for an analytical CS student who prefers clear instructions over marketing-style descriptions.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structural clarity and navigability
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates presence of a clear hierarchy (overview/TL;DR, day-by-day plan, and separate sections for lodging, intra-city transport, safety, booking workflow), with headings and internal navigation cues to match the user's task-list planning habit.

presentation_fit.criterion: Scannability and succinctness
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks use of bullet/numbered lists, short paragraphs, and labeled key data points so the user can screenshot and reference quickly without wading through long text blocks.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology and bilingual labeling of places/terms
presentation_fit.explanation: Looks at whether Moroccan terms (riad, medina, souk, petit/grand taxi, ONCF) are briefly defined on first use and whether place names are shown in Chinese plus English/romanization to reduce on-the-ground confusion in a French/Arabic context.

presentation_fit.criterion: Quantitative formatting consistency (time, currency, units, naming)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses consistent use of 24-hour time, metric units, and clearly formatted prices with RMB↔MAD conversion and uniform naming conventions, supporting a budget-conscious, detail-oriented reader.

presentation_fit.criterion: Lightweight visual aids and schedule cues
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates use of simple, screenshot-friendly visuals like compact 2–3 column tables (time | activity | location | est. cost), ASCII timelines, and clearly labeled map links rather than heavy graphics that hinder mobile use.

presentation_fit.criterion: Safety information presentation clarity
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks whether safety guidance is isolated into a clearly titled section with categorized bullet points (street, transport, health), prioritized warnings, and highlighted emergency contacts, aligning with the user's cautious profile.

presentation_fit.criterion: Guided vs. free-day demarcation and labeling
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether the itinerary explicitly tags time blocks as [Guided] or [Free], and indicates flexible/reschedulable segments, helping the user plan autonomy and focus time at a glance.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget adherence with transparent, itemized cost breakdown
actionability_practicality.explanation: The report should calculate a clear total within ¥20,000, itemizing flights, lodging (per-night), guide fees, classes/workshops, intercity and local transport, meals, tips, SIM/insurance, and a 10–15% contingency, with exchange-rate assumptions and cheaper fallback options.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Time-feasible itinerary structure with buffers and preserved free days
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether day-by-day scheduling includes realistic travel times (airport–riad, medina navigation, train between cities), avoids backtracking, builds buffers around prayer times and closures, and explicitly protects a few free days for independent exploration.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Bookability and vendor specificity for key components
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks that guides, cooking classes, artisan workshops, and guesthouses are named with contact methods (website/WhatsApp/email), language availability (English at minimum), price quotes, booking lead times, deposit/cancellation terms, and alternative options if fully booked.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Accommodation operability in medinas (location, access, and suitability)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Guesthouse (riad) recommendations should include walkable proximity to souks, neighborhood safety notes, check-in/out and luggage storage, Wi‑Fi, quietness, exact addresses with wayfinding from taxi drop-off, nightly price/taxes, and cleanliness standards.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Intercity and intra-city transport operability
actionability_practicality.explanation: Provides concrete steps for Marrakech–Fes travel (e.g., ONCF train classes, how to buy tickets, example departures), local mobility (petit taxi norms, expected fares, when to negotiate, walking routes, airport transfers), and alternatives (bus/coach) with time/cost estimates.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Context-specific safety and risk mitigation
actionability_practicality.explanation: Offers actionable souk tactics (handling touts, bargaining, pickpocket prevention), night travel guidance, food/water hygiene, safe cash/ATM use, insurance coverage reminders, emergency contact info, and location of nearby clinics/embassies relevant to Marrakech and Fes.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Seasonal and calendar practicality (spring and Ramadan/Friday impacts)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report accounts for spring weather/daylight, potential Ramadan/Eid schedule changes, Friday prayer closures, adjusted opening hours for souks/classes, and packing/clothing guidance accordingly.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Execution logistics for priority cultural activities
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies that souk visits, the cooking class, and artisan workshops are placed at optimal times, specify meeting points and durations, note what’s included (e.g., market tour, materials), and present backups if a class or workshop is full or rescheduled.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Pre-trip operational checklist and booking timeline
actionability_practicality.explanation: Includes a stepwise timeline (e.g., when to book flights/riads/guides/classes, confirm intercity tickets), visa/entry notes for a Chinese passport, insurance purchase, payment methods, currency preparation, and document readiness to reduce last-minute risk.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Digital tool readiness and offline contingencies
actionability_practicality.explanation: Recommends specific tools (offline maps, translation, ONCF app, expense tracker), eSIM/SIM options with setup steps, and offline copies of addresses in French/Arabic and booking confirmations to ensure operability without constant connectivity.

depth_insight.criterion: Cultural Authenticity and Contextualization of Experiences
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether recommendations go beyond tourist checklists to explain the cultural meaning of Marrakech/Fes souks and crafts, identify ethical/community-rooted workshops, and suggest when/where to visit specific guild areas for genuinely immersive encounters.

depth_insight.criterion: Budget Realism and Value Optimization
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan translates ¥20,000 into a realistic cost breakdown (lodging, guide, classes/workshops, intercity transfer, meals, tips), includes buffers, and proposes tactics to maximize cultural value per yuan (e.g., choosing riads with included breakfast, group vs private tours, targeted splurges).

depth_insight.criterion: Itinerary Feasibility and Timing Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Checks if the schedule reflects on-the-ground constraints and advantages (e.g., morning vs evening souk dynamics, Friday prayer impacts, potential Ramadan timing shifts, crowd avoidance, rest pace), and smartly sequences guided days to build confidence for later independent exploration.

depth_insight.criterion: Guide and Workshop Vetting Depth
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report specifies how to identify and verify professional, licensed guides and authentic hands-on workshops (selection criteria, expected rates, language, specialization, anti-kickback practices) and how to align them with the traveler’s interests.

depth_insight.criterion: Intra-city Transport and Medina Navigation Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether advice goes beyond listing options to provide actionable nuance—petit taxi norms and typical fares, when to walk vs ride, car-free medina access, porter use for luggage, night-time movement—so the traveler can make informed choices on the spot.

depth_insight.criterion: Context-Specific Safety and Risk Mitigation
depth_insight.explanation: Examines whether safety guidance is tailored to Morocco: common souk scams, unsolicited ‘guides’ (e.g., tannery hustles), pickpocketing patterns, gender-aware tips, food/water hygiene, and concrete countermeasures (what to do, who to contact).

depth_insight.criterion: Intercity Transfer Strategy (Marrakech–Fes) with Time–Cost–Comfort Analysis
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the plan compares options (train classes, duration, departure timing, last-mile to riads) and recommends a choice with rationale that balances comfort, budget, and time for a one-week schedule.

depth_insight.criterion: Original, Seasonally Aware, and Tailored Enhancements
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards distinctive, practical ideas tied to spring conditions and personal goals (e.g., Ramadan implications for dining/class timing, hammam etiquette, cooperative visits supporting artisans, ethical bargaining and photography etiquette) that add real experiential value.

logical_coherence.criterion: Trip-Specific Overall Framework and Traceability to Requirements
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a systematic structure (assumptions and constraints → itinerary by day → logistics for each day → budget roll-up → safety) and explicitly maps each user requirement (Marrakech/Fes souks, cooking class, artisan workshops, local guesthouses, guided and free days, one-week duration, ¥20,000 budget) to concrete plan elements. This ensures the plan is logically derived from stated premises.

Assesses whether the day-by-day schedule respects realistic travel times (e.g., Marrakech–Fes transfer), opening hours, duration of activities, walking time in medinas, and includes buffers to avoid overlap or impossible sequences. Also evaluates geographic sequencing to minimize backtracking and cross-day consistency (locations and times align).

Evaluates whether costs for lodging, guides, workshops, classes, intra-city and intercity transport, meals, and contingencies are logically estimated, currency assumptions are stated, per-day/per-item costs aggregate to a total within ¥20,000, and nights/costs align with the itinerary. Prevents contradictions between budget and plan.

logical_coherence.criterion: Justification and Evidence for Key Choices
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that recommendations (e.g., riad locations near souks, choosing train vs. bus, selecting workshop providers, sequence of cities) are supported by relevant facts or checkable references (typical durations, known routes, typical class lengths), avoiding unsupported assertions. Ensures arguments are grounded by evidence.

logical_coherence.criterion: Resource Allocation Logic (Guided vs. Independent Days and Buffers)
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the plan explicitly allocates which days/segments are guided versus independent in a way that matches activity complexity (e.g., souk navigation, artisan visits) and includes time buffers and booking lead times where needed. Ensures the schedule operationalizes the user's preference for both guided depth and free exploration.

Evaluates whether transitions between sections (itinerary → transport → lodging → budget → safety) are logically connected, with explicit cross-references (e.g., a listed workshop is reflected in the day’s schedule, transport times align with check-in). Identifies and resolves dependencies to avoid logical jumps.

logical_coherence.criterion: Risk-to-Safety Causality and Integration into the Plan
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether safety tips are logically derived from itinerary-specific risks (e.g., crowded souks, night travel, medina navigation, cash handling) and whether mitigations are built into the plan (daylight transfers, meeting points, SIM/Maps, guide usage in complex areas). Ensures safety advice is not generic but causally linked.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Formatting and Layout Hierarchy
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear, consistent heading hierarchy (e.g., Day 1–Day 7, city sections), logical section order, adequate white space, and consistent typography to make the itinerary easy to follow at a glance.

clarity_readability.criterion: Scannability and Information Chunking
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether key operational details (time blocks, locations, costs, addresses, booking links, contact numbers) are presented in bullets, callouts, or tagged lines (e.g., Time, Location, Cost), enabling quick retrieval during planning and on-the-go use.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity, Concision, and Tone
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether sentences are concise, free of ambiguity and unnecessary jargon, with a professional yet friendly tone; instructions (e.g., transport steps) are actionable and use plain language.

clarity_readability.criterion: Terminology and Local Naming Clarity
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that local terms and variants (e.g., riad, medina, souk, petit/grand taxi, MAD; Marrakech/Marrakesh; Fes/Fez) are introduced with brief explanations and consistent usage, optionally with transliteration/Arabic names to reduce confusion onsite.

clarity_readability.criterion: Format Consistency for Time, Currency, and Contact Details
clarity_readability.explanation: Verifies consistent, unambiguous formats (e.g., 24-hour times with ranges, dates with month/day, currency shown as MAD and ¥ with exchange note, standardized address lines with map links, phone format with country code), minimizing misinterpretation.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Visual Aids and Data Presentation
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether tables, timelines, checklists, and simple maps are clear, labeled, and placed near relevant text (e.g., budget table with JPY/MAD columns and exchange date, intra-city transport comparison table), enhancing comprehension.

clarity_readability.criterion: Navigation Aids and Document Usability
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates presence and clarity of an executive summary, table of contents or section index, internal cross-references, and day-by-day overviews that help readers locate sections quickly.

task: I plan to take a one-week deep cultural trip to Morocco next spring with a budget of ¥20,000. I especially want to experience the traditional souks of Marrakech and Fes, take a Moroccan cooking class, participate in local artisan workshops, and stay in local guesthouses to experience authentic life. I would like a professional guide for in-depth cultural tours while keeping a few days free for independent exploration. Please design a detailed itinerary including cultural activities, guesthouse recommendations, intra-city transport options, and safety tips.

goal_alignment.criterion: Complete coverage of user-stated nonnegotiables in a coherent 7-day plan
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the itinerary explicitly includes both Marrakech and Fes souks, at least one Moroccan cooking class, at least one artisan workshop, stays in local guesthouses/riads, professional guided in-depth tours plus clearly marked free days, and provides intra-city transport options and safety tips. Coherence means a logical city order and day-by-day flow without unrealistic transfers—crucial for a self-guided, analytical planner.

goal_alignment.criterion: Budget fidelity and cost-performance tailored to ¥20,000
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates itemized cost breakdown (flights, lodgings, guide fees, classes/workshops, intercity/intracity transport, meals, tickets, buffers) with currency conversion and price ranges grounded in current norms. Includes cost-saving trade-offs (e.g., train vs flight, riad tiers) and review-backed value selections to fit Xu Yang’s rational, budget-tracking behavior and low risk appetite.

goal_alignment.criterion: Cultural depth and artisan immersion aligned with academic interests
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether souk routes and activities are framed with historical/social context (e.g., medersas, museums, social history of trade), and whether workshops (zellige, leather, calligraphy, weaving) are reputable, small-group/community-based with learning outcomes. Cooking class content should include foundational techniques and dishes (e.g., tagine, salads) and hygiene considerations, satisfying his in-depth learning and light-meal preferences.

goal_alignment.criterion: Balanced guided vs independent time and pacing matched to habits
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for 2–3 guided (half/full) days for deep cultural interpretation and 2–3 flexible free days with curated options. Time-of-day design should respect his morning light activity and evening reflection habits, avoid overpacking, and include buffers—matching his efficiency and autonomy without sacrificing depth.

goal_alignment.criterion: Intercity and intra-city transport feasibility with realistic buffers
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether ONCF train/bus timings, ticketing, and class choices are realistic; station-to-riad medina access (walking/porters) is addressed; petit taxi practices (metering, typical fares) are explained; and map/offline navigation tools are provided. This minimizes uncertainty for a low-risk, logistics-conscious traveler.

goal_alignment.criterion: Local, scenario-specific safety and risk management
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses concrete, Morocco-specific guidance: avoiding fake guides, handling touts and haggling, pickpocket prevention in medinas, licensed guide verification, food/water hygiene, modest dress and etiquette, emergency numbers, travel insurance, and digital safety (eSIM/ATM use). Tailored to Xu Yang’s conservative risk profile and preference for authoritative precautions.

goal_alignment.criterion: Accommodation fit: authentic riads with study-friendly amenities and value
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks if recommended guesthouses/riads have strong recent reviews, authentic design, quiet rooms, reliable Wi‑Fi, desk space, good locations near medina gates, and clear price ranges. This aligns with his cost-effective, culturally rich lodging preference and need for evening reflection/reading.

goal_alignment.criterion: Season- and calendar-aware planning (spring/Ramadan/Eid)
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan accounts for spring weather, possible Ramadan/Eid impacts on opening hours/meal availability, Friday prayer rhythms, and suggests modest etiquette and schedule adjustments—ensuring realistic expectations for the specified travel window.

goal_alignment.criterion: Evidence-based, structured, and tool-ready presentation
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses clarity and logic (checklists, day-by-day blocks, cost tables, time estimates), references to official/authoritative sources (e.g., ONCF, museum sites), and shareable assets (map pins, booking sequence) that fit Xu Yang’s data-driven decision style and use of planning tools.

goal_alignment.criterion: Ethical and sustainable travel and media/consent practices
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for guidance on fair bargaining (non-exploitative), supporting cooperatives/fair-trade artisans, avoiding unethical animal activities, minimizing waste, and respectful photography/consent—resonating with his social responsibility orientation and eco-conscious preferences.

content_alignment.criterion: Socio-historical depth on souks/medinas tailored to an analytical researcher
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the content goes beyond sights to provide historical, economic, and cultural context (e.g., guild structures, UNESCO status, artisan economies, marketplace norms), matching the user’s research mindset and desire for in-depth understanding.

content_alignment.criterion: Hands-on artisan workshops and cooking classes aligned to learning style and light-diet preference
content_alignment.explanation: Checks if workshops/classes emphasize process and technique (not just shopping), are run by reputable artisans/cooperatives, and can accommodate lighter/less-oily options, resonating with his new cooking hobby, analytical learning, and taste.

content_alignment.criterion: Budget transparency and value optimization within ¥20,000
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the itinerary curates cost-effective choices with realistic price ranges for activities, guides, transport, and guesthouses, reflecting his frugal student budget and data-driven budgeting habit.

content_alignment.criterion: Guesthouse (riad) selection matching authentic + comfortable + quiet evening preferences
content_alignment.explanation: Focuses on whether lodging picks are culturally characteristic yet comfortable, quiet for evening reading, safe (medina-edge or well-signed areas), with strong Wi‑Fi and good reviews—aligning with his accommodation values.

content_alignment.criterion: Guided vs. independent structure aligned to self-guided planning style
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether professional guide days deliver deep cultural interpretation while leaving modular free days with curated optional tracks, matching his desire for autonomy and efficiency-focused planning.

content_alignment.criterion: Safety and risk-mitigation content tailored to low-risk profile and souk realities
content_alignment.explanation: Checks for targeted guidance on common local risks (fake guides, pickpockets, haggling norms, food hygiene, Ramadan etiquette) suitable for a cautious first-time traveler, not generic safety boilerplate.

content_alignment.criterion: Intra-city mobility choices enabling efficient, low-stress independent navigation
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the content favors walkable neighborhood clustering, clear petit-taxi practices, and low-cognitive-load routes that empower independent exploration consistent with his planning habits.

content_alignment.criterion: Sustainability and ethical consumption embedded across activities
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the itinerary integrates ethical artisan cooperatives, fair-trade shopping, and avoidance of exploitative attractions, aligning with his eco-conscious and socially responsible values.

content_alignment.criterion: Data-backed recommendations and digital tool integration
content_alignment.explanation: Checks for use of typical price ranges, peak/quiet time windows, and recommended apps/maps/budget tools, matching his data-driven decision style without overemphasizing generic how-to steps.

content_alignment.criterion: Language facilitation and documentation etiquette
content_alignment.explanation: Looks for content noting Mandarin/English-capable guides where feasible and clear photo-consent etiquette in souks, aligning with his communication sensitivity and likely desire to document responsibly.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structural clarity and navigation (executive summary + modular sections + clear headings)
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks whether the report starts with a brief summary of key plans/cost cues and uses clear section headers (Itinerary, Guesthouses, Transport, Safety) so an analytical reader can locate information quickly on mobile or desktop.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology and bilingual annotation for local terms and places
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether travel/culture terms (e.g., souk, medina, riad, hammam, petit/grand taxi) are used accurately and briefly explained with transliteration/parenthetical notes to reduce comprehension friction in a foreign-language context.

presentation_fit.criterion: Scannability and mobile readability (bullets, short sentences, labeled callouts)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses use of bullet lists, concise sentences, and simple emphasis labels like “Tip:”/“Warning:” to fit mobile reading habits from Zhihu/Weibo and facilitate quick scanning without dense blocks.

presentation_fit.criterion: Time-boxed day-by-day itinerary formatting with [Guided]/[Free] tags
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks that each day is segmented by time blocks (morning/afternoon/evening) and clearly labeled as [Guided] or [Free], matching the user’s need to balance guided cultural immersion with independent exploration.

presentation_fit.criterion: Numerical information formatting (currency, time, units) with consistency
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether costs show both MAD and RMB, exchange assumptions are briefly noted, and travel times/distances are consistently formatted, aligning with the user’s budgeting and data-oriented habits.

presentation_fit.criterion: Standardized listing format for guesthouses and providers
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks for a consistent field order (price range/night, location relative to medina, distance, cancellation notes, user-rating source) to enable quick comparison without narrative clutter.

presentation_fit.criterion: Safety information presented as categorized checklists with highlighted essentials
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether safety tips are grouped by category (street scams, transport, health, dress code) in checklist form with emergency numbers and key alerts emphasized for rapid in-situ reference.

presentation_fit.criterion: Source attribution and link hygiene (concise labels, dates for time-sensitive info)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates presentation of sources with clear anchor text and dates where relevant, supporting credibility for a research-oriented reader without overloading the main text.

presentation_fit.criterion: Lightweight visual/verbal wayfinding for routes and intra-city transport
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks use of simple text-friendly route notations (arrows, station names, estimated durations) instead of heavy graphics, improving on-the-go usability in low-visual environments.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget compliance with transparent, itemized breakdown and contingency
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report converts prices to RMB, itemizes all major costs (flights, lodgings, guides, classes/workshops, intercity and local transport, meals, tips), shows a total within ¥20,000, and includes a 10–15% buffer. This enables Xu Yang to judge affordability and make trade-offs confidently.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Executable day-by-day plan with realistic pacing, buffers, and free-day modules
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the itinerary provides time blocks (morning/afternoon/evening), realistic durations (e.g., Marrakech–Fes travel time), built-in slack, and optional activities for free days. This ensures he can follow the plan on the ground without time crunches and still enjoy independent exploration.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Bookable vendors for depth activities (licensed guides, cooking classes, artisan workshops)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report lists concrete providers (names/links/rating ranges), languages offered, prices, booking channels accessible from China, and cancellation terms for guided tours, cooking classes, and workshops. This allows immediate booking and reduces uncertainty.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Transport practicality (intercity and intra-city with first/last-mile guidance)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for specific transport modes (train times/classes, taxi types), estimated durations and costs, booking steps (e.g., ONCF), and clear instructions from airport/train to riads inside medinas. This prevents execution failures in complex local logistics.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Safety and risk mitigation protocols tailored to Moroccan medinas
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for concrete, situational guidance (verifying licensed guides, avoiding fake guides, pickpocket prevention, night routes, cash handling, travel insurance coverage scope). Actionable safety steps are critical given low risk tolerance.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Booking timeline and checklists (lead times, order of actions, payment methods)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a week-by-week booking sequence (flights, riads, guides/classes), typical lead times, payment options usable from China, document readiness, and confirmation tracking. This converts the plan into clear next actions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Seasonality and timing constraints addressed (spring weather, Ramadan/holiday impacts, opening hours)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for adjustments to operating hours, meal scheduling, class availability, and clothing/gear for spring. Accounting for seasonal factors prevents cancellations and mis-timed visits, increasing feasibility.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Language and communication readiness (guide language, WhatsApp/SIM/eSIM, translation tools)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report specifies language of services, recommends local SIM/eSIM and WhatsApp (common with hosts/guides), and offers translation aids. This reduces friction in coordination and on-the-spot problem solving.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Riad (guesthouse) practicality and access considerations
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if recommended riads balance authenticity and cost, and include practical notes (medina gate meeting points, porter arrangements, stairs/access, noise, breakfast, heating/AC, proximity to souks). These details determine real-world comfort and ease of arrival.

depth_insight.criterion: Culturally Immersive Curation and Justification
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the itinerary thoughtfully selects and sequences Marrakech/Fes souk experiences, cooking class, and artisan workshops to deepen cultural understanding, and explains why each choice (e.g., specific quarters, craft lineages, host types) best serves authentic immersion.

depth_insight.criterion: Budget Realism and Value Optimization under ¥20,000
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan states explicit budget assumptions (e.g., flight inclusion), provides credible price ranges (riads, guides, classes, transport), allocates funds across categories, and makes reasoned trade-offs and tactics to maximize cultural value per yuan.

depth_insight.criterion: Seasonality and Cultural-Calendar Awareness
depth_insight.explanation: Checks if spring conditions and potential Ramadan/Eid effects on hours, meals, and class availability are anticipated, with schedule adaptations (timing of souk visits, meal planning, booking windows) that preserve experience depth.

depth_insight.criterion: Logistics Insight and Pacing Feasibility
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for nuanced reasoning about intercity and intra-city movement specific to Morocco (train/bus trade-offs; petite taxis; medina walking constraints), realistic time estimates, minimized backtracking, and energy-aware pacing integrated into daily plans.

depth_insight.criterion: Authenticity and Ethical Engagement with Artisans
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report differentiates genuine cooperatives/workshops from commission-driven stops, embeds bargaining and photography etiquette, and selects cooking/workshop hosts that support ethical, community-based participation.

depth_insight.criterion: Professional Guide Strategy and Integration
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates criteria for choosing licensed, thematic guides, rationale for which days are guided vs. independent, and how guided tours are used to build cultural context that enhances later self-exploration.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Identification and Embedded Safety Mitigations
depth_insight.explanation: Judges the depth of Morocco-specific risk mapping (faux guides, taxi practices, medina night navigation, scams, food/water hygiene) and how mitigations are embedded into activity timing, routing, and vendor/area choices.

depth_insight.criterion: Scaffolding for High-Quality Independent Exploration
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether the plan provides structured tools for free days—self-guided routes, optimal visit windows, negotiation playbook, key phrases, decision rules for purchases—so independence remains immersive, safe, and purposeful.

logical_coherence.criterion: Task-Aligned Structural Framework (Itinerary → Transport → Lodging → Budget/Constraints → Safety)
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report organizes content in a logical order tailored to this task: sets constraints (7 days, ¥20,000, spring), then provides a day-by-day plan, followed by intra-city transport options, guesthouse recommendations, budget/constraint checks, and safety guidance. A clear scaffold ensures each subsequent section builds on prior premises.

Evaluates whether daily schedules and the Marrakech↔Fes transfer are time-realistic (travel durations, check-in/out, activity opening hours), avoid backtracking, and place guided tours and free days at sensible points. Ensures no impossible jumps (e.g., double-booked times) and that the sequence reduces friction.

Checks that each user-stated requirement (Marrakech and Fes souks, cooking class, artisan workshops, riad/guesthouse stays, professional guide plus free days) is explicitly mapped to concrete plan elements, and that all choices remain consistent with the ¥20,000 budget and one-week timeframe without contradictions.

Assesses whether recommendations (e.g., specific riads near medinas for authenticity, choosing petit taxis or walking in old cities, timing of guided cultural tours) are supported by premises relevant to context (proximity to souks, typical traffic patterns, spring conditions), rather than asserted without rationale.
