clarity_readability.criterion: Definition and Consistent Use of Technical Terms and Metrics
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether F&B and marketing/finance terms (e.g., COGS, ASP/AOV, CAC, LTV, contribution margin, occupancy cost ratio, SKU, BOM, POS, LTO; nutrition units like kcal/protein g) are explained at first use or in a glossary, with example formulas where calculations appear. This reduces misinterpretation and lowers the reading barrier.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity and Effectiveness of Tables, Charts, and Diagrams
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks if visuals are legible and self‑explanatory: titles that state the message, labeled axes/units, readable legends, color‑blind‑safe palettes, and appropriate chart types (e.g., break‑even line chart, six‑month Gantt, delivery radius/map). Tables (budget, menu nutrition, price ladder) should have clear headers, aligned numbers, and subtotals to make quantitative content easy to absorb.

clarity_readability.criterion: Numerical Formatting, Units, and Assumption Readability
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses consistency and clarity of numbers: currency formatted with ¥ and thousand separators, clear time bases (per day/month), rent per m² vs per month labeled, percentages with stated bases, and a simple, readable assumptions/notes block near each table or chart. This prevents confusion and misreading of key figures.

clarity_readability.criterion: Emphasis and Scannability of Key Information
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether crucial points (target price per head, calorie ranges, lunch throughput targets, budget cap, opening timeline checkpoints) are surfaced via executive summary, section summaries, callout boxes, bullet lists, and consistent highlight styles (bold/italics), enabling quick grasp of essentials without wading through dense text.

task: I plan to start a new food and beverage business focusing on healthy fast food within the next six months, with available start-up funds of approximately ¥500,000, including venue rental. My target market is primarily fitness enthusiasts and white-collar workers seeking light, healthy meals. We aim to offer convenient low-calorie healthy meals at a mid-to-high price point, averaging ¥40–¥60 per person.For the menu, I want to focus on salad bowls, energy bowls, and healthy burgers. Our service model will be mainly dine-in plus delivery. For location, I prefer commercial CBD areas and office building clusters to attract a steady flow of customers. I plan to finalize site selection and renovation within three months, aiming to officially open in six months.Please provide a business plan including market research, menu design, marketing strategies, and a detailed financial budget.

goal_alignment.criterion: Shanghai-CBD-grounded budget, site selection, and timeline feasibility
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan uses Shanghai CBD/office-cluster benchmarks for rent (including deposit and key money norms), fit-out costs, equipment, and working capital within a ¥500k cap; lays out a 3-month site+renovation and 6-month opening schedule tied to permit lead times; and clarifies trade-offs (size, frontage, ventilation) to remain feasible.

goal_alignment.criterion: Segment-specific menu engineering and price fit (fitness + white-collar at ¥40–¥60)
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if salads/energy bowls/healthy burgers are designed for macros/calorie transparency, high-protein options, quick assembly, and local palate; includes combo logic to land AOV in ¥40–¥60 after tax and platform fees; and integrates low-caffeine/herbal beverage pairings aligning with the user’s lifestyle.

goal_alignment.criterion: Dual-channel operations design for dine-in + delivery
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate if the plan optimizes lunch-peak throughput (station layout, prep, holding, ticket times), delivery menu fit (travel stability, sogginess mitigation), packaging specs, and aggregator strategy (Meituan/Ele.me fees, promotions) plus private-domain (WeChat) for repeat, reflecting her frequent use of delivery services.

goal_alignment.criterion: Persona-leveraged brand and spatial aesthetics
goal_alignment.explanation: Judge whether the brand story/visuals draw on her Melbourne coffee/design background and art curation, producing a distinctive, photography-friendly identity and interior that resonates with her community-building style and can generate organic UGC on Xiaohongshu/Instagram.

goal_alignment.criterion: Sustainable sourcing and eco-friendly packaging with unit economics
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if the plan proposes local/seasonal suppliers, farmers’ market ties, and recyclable/compostable packaging that align with her zero-waste values while modeling the cost impact to keep COGS compatible with a ¥40–¥60 price point.

goal_alignment.criterion: Marketing strategy aligned to her creator channels and communities
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether marketing leverages Xiaohongshu/Bilibili short-video content she already produces, includes art/fitness community events (evening salons, yoga/gym collaborations), micro-KOLs, office-building partnerships, and corporate catering—matching her habits and audience touchpoints.

goal_alignment.criterion: Localized competitive and locational analysis
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if the plan benchmarks direct competitors in Shanghai CBD (menu, price, wait times, delivery reviews), maps micro-areas (e.g., near metro exits, gyms, office lobbies), and articulates a clear differentiation strategy against established healthy fast-casual brands.

goal_alignment.criterion: Operations design acknowledging her time constraints and experience
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan builds SOPs, training checklists, cross-utilized roles, and a manager-led daily run that frees her evenings; leverages her café experience for procurement and service standards; and includes KPI dashboards (throughput, COGS, labor, NPS) for rational decision-making.

goal_alignment.criterion: Financial model clarity and risk-sensitivity for a risk-averse owner
goal_alignment.explanation: Judge if there is a detailed P&L, cash flow, break-even by daypart, aggregator fee impact, CAC/LTV for delivery vs dine-in, and sensitivity scenarios (rent +10%, sales -20%, delivery mix +15%) with contingency reserves to protect the ¥500k budget.

goal_alignment.criterion: Regulatory and food safety pathway specific to Shanghai
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify inclusion of required permits and timelines (business license, food operation permit, fire/ventilation/grease trap, staff health certificates), kitchen flow for hygiene and allergen labeling, tied to the 3–6 month critical path.

content_alignment.criterion: Shanghai CBD/Office-Cluster Market Fit and Competitor Mapping within ¥40–¥60
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether market research is localized to Shanghai CBD and major office clusters (e.g., Lujiazui, Jing'an, Xintiandi), includes lunch traffic patterns, gym/yoga density, white-collar behavior, and competitor benchmarks (brands, pricing, formats) aligned to the ¥40–¥60 range, using relevant local data sources (Dianping/Meituan heatmaps, Baidu LBS, industry reports). This matches her city, target users, and pricing, ensuring precise content relevance.

content_alignment.criterion: Menu-Nutrition Alignment to Fitness/White-Collar Needs and Local Palate
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether menu design centers on salad/energy bowls and healthy burgers with clear macro/calorie targets (e.g., 25–35g protein options, <600 kcal lunch builds), plant-based and low-carb choices for yoga/wellness, allergen notes, and flavor profiles suited to Shanghai/East China tastes. Reflects her health-oriented lifestyle and audience needs while respecting price architecture.

content_alignment.criterion: Sustainability Integration in Sourcing and Packaging
content_alignment.explanation: Checks for content on eco-friendly packaging (compostable/recyclable, delivery-suitable), supplier options (local/organic farms, farmers’ markets), and waste-reduction programs that align with her zero-waste and sustainability preferences. Aligns with her values and purchasing behavior, making the plan personally resonant.

content_alignment.criterion: Brand and Spatial Aesthetics Consistent with Melbourne-Influenced, Art-Forward Taste
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the plan dedicates content to brand narrative, visual identity, and spatial cues (materials, moodboard references, collaborations with local artists) that echo her design training and current café aesthetic. Ensures content respects her high bar for design and art integration.

content_alignment.criterion: Platform-Native Social/Digital and Community Marketing Fit (Xiaohongshu/Instagram/Bilibili + Gym/Yoga Partnerships)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether marketing content aligns with her actual channels and communities: XHS seeding/KOC strategies, IG visual storytelling, Bilibili behind-the-scenes, partnerships with yoga studios/gyms and office parks, and evening event formats she already excels at. Matches her habits and strengths for higher content relevance.

content_alignment.criterion: Delivery-Ecosystem Alignment (Ele.me/Meituan) and Packaging Performance
content_alignment.explanation: Checks for content specific to China’s delivery platforms—menu engineering for travel stability, condensation control, portioning, SOPs for peak lunch delivery, rating/algorithm levers, and corporate group orders. Aligns with her dine-in + delivery model and user behavior (frequent delivery use).

content_alignment.criterion: Financial Framing Tailored to ¥500k Budget, Mid-High Price Point, and Shanghai Cost Context
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether financial content uses Shanghai-specific rent/CAPEX benchmarks, realistic COGS targets for healthy ingredients, and price architecture in the ¥40–¥60 band, with risk-aware scenario framing. Matches her risk-averse investment style and the tight budget constraint.

content_alignment.criterion: Operational Depth Specific to Healthy Fast Food for an Experienced Café Operator
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether operations content avoids generic beginner tips and focuses on category-specific needs (prep line design, cold chain, HACCP/sanitation for salads, central kitchen vs. on-site prep, SKU engineering), while acknowledging her limited personal time as an owner-operator. Aligns depth to her existing expertise.

content_alignment.criterion: Timeline-Structured Content Matching 3-Month Site/Reno and 6-Month Opening
content_alignment.explanation: Checks if the plan’s content is segmented to her stated phases (site selection, design/renovation, pre-opening prep) with milestones that reflect her schedule. Ensures the content cadence fits her timeline preferences without focusing on execution details.

content_alignment.criterion: Synergy with Existing Café Brand, Audience, and Lifestyle Community
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the content proposes cross-promotion with her café’s customer base, social channels, and event know-how (e.g., pop-ups, member crossover), consistent with her artistic/lifestyle positioning. Personalizes the plan to her unique assets to reduce CAC and build early traction.

presentation_fit.criterion: Information hierarchy and navigability (TOC, numbered headings, executive summary, milestone segmentation)
presentation_fit.explanation: For a time-constrained operator, a clear structure with an upfront executive summary, distinct sections, and easy navigation enables rapid grasp of market, menu, marketing, and budget without wading through walls of text.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visualization quality for finance and timeline (tables/charts that are labeled, legible, and minimalist)
presentation_fit.explanation: A 6‑month launch with a detailed budget requires Gantt/timelines, breakeven charts, and unit economics tables that are clean, properly titled, axis-labeled, and consistent, allowing quick interpretation and reducing cognitive load.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology and acronym handling (brief definitions and bilingual pairing for key F&B/finance ops terms)
presentation_fit.explanation: As a bilingual entrepreneur, she can handle professional terms (e.g., COGS, SOP, SKU, FOH/BOH, CAC, LTV) if acronyms are expanded and briefly defined; pairing Chinese/English where appropriate prevents misreadings.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tone and style alignment (professional, concise, non-hype, design-aware language)
presentation_fit.explanation: She prefers rational, evidence-based communication with tasteful, creative sensitivity. The report should avoid buzzwords and marketing fluff, keeping sentences tight and analysis-focused while acknowledging brand aesthetics.

presentation_fit.criterion: Aesthetic coherence (minimalist layout, consistent typography, ample white space, restrained color palette)
presentation_fit.explanation: Given her strong design standards, a clean visual system (fonts, spacing, iconography) enhances readability and credibility, aligning the report’s look-and-feel with boutique, creative sensibilities.

presentation_fit.criterion: Localization of units and formatting (RMB ¥, sqm/㎡, kcal, °C, local date/time formats)
presentation_fit.explanation: Using local currency, area, and measurement conventions removes friction and ensures immediate, accurate comprehension in a Shanghai operating context.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-first readability (short paragraphs, bullet lists, non-wide tables, summary callouts)
presentation_fit.explanation: She often consumes content on mobile. Chunked text, bullets, and tables that don’t overflow screens enable on-the-go review without losing key details.

presentation_fit.criterion: Document scaffolding and references (page numbers, internal links, exhibit/appendix labels, captions, footnotes for assumptions)
presentation_fit.explanation: Professional document hygiene supports quick cross-referencing, clarifies where details live (e.g., appendices), and builds trust by clearly noting assumptions and sources.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Financial feasibility and budget realism within ¥500,000
actionability_practicality.explanation: The report provides an itemized capex/opex and working capital plan (rent deposit, renovation, equipment, licenses, initial inventory, platform onboarding, marketing, eco-packaging) with a 10–15% contingency; includes a 12-month P&L, break-even (daily covers at ¥40–¥60), cash-flow, and sensitivity to key variables (rent, COGS, delivery mix).

actionability_practicality.criterion: Implementation timeline and critical-path practicality (6-month opening)
actionability_practicality.explanation: There is a dependency-aware schedule with milestones and task owners for site selection, lease negotiation, design/permits, construction, equipment procurement, supplier onboarding, hiring/training, licensing, platform setup, soft opening; includes buffers and go/no-go gates to reliably meet the six-month target.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Shanghai CBD site selection and lease practicality within budget
actionability_practicality.explanation: The report includes a site scorecard (rent/sqm, white-collar density, footfall, venting feasibility, power load, grease trap, visibility), realistic rent/deposit assumptions, negotiation targets (rent-free, deposit months, signage rights), and trade-offs (smaller footprint, fringe CBD) to fit the ¥500,000 total budget.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Menu engineering and operational design at target price point
actionability_practicality.explanation: Standardized recipes with BOMs and target COGS per item, prep workflows for salads/energy bowls/burgers, equipment list matched to menu, allergen/nutrition info for fitness users, service-time targets for lunch rush, and seating/line design to achieve throughput and margins at ¥40–¥60.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Delivery channel economics and SOPs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Clear assumptions on Meituan/Ele.me commissions, packaging costs, delivery radius, prep-time SLAs, differentiated delivery pricing/menu, order throttling, and integration with POS/KDS, ensuring delivery orders remain profitable and operationally manageable.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Staffing model, delegation, and SOPs aligned to owner’s time constraints
actionability_practicality.explanation: A practical org chart, headcount and shift plan for peaks, labor budget (sales per labor hour targets), hiring/training timelines, SOP manuals (recipes, food safety, opening/closing, inventory), and a management plan (store manager role, dashboards) enabling Guo to oversee without full-time on-site presence.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Licensing and compliance roadmap specific to Shanghai
actionability_practicality.explanation: A step-by-step list of required permits (business license, food business license, staff health certificates, fire/environmental requirements, property usage compliance), expected durations/costs, sequencing with design/build, and responsible parties to avoid approval bottlenecks.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk assessment, sensitivity analysis, and contingency actions
actionability_practicality.explanation: Identification of top risks (rent overruns, sales ramp delay, supplier volatility, delivery fee changes), quantified sensitivities, contingency budget, and concrete mitigations (backup suppliers, phased hiring, menu cost-down options, alternate micro-locations, delivery-first fallback).

actionability_practicality.criterion: Go-to-market plan with partnerships, calendar, budget, and KPIs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Executable marketing plan: pre-opening seeding on Xiaohongshu, gym/office partnerships, corporate lunch trials, subscription meal plans; includes a 12-week calendar, channel budgets, offer mechanics, and measurable targets (CAC, trial-to-repeat, daily orders, AOV).

actionability_practicality.criterion: Lean validation/pilot before full commitment
actionability_practicality.explanation: A concrete plan to pilot menu/pricing (e.g., pop-up at her current café or short-term stall), with sample size, data to collect (COGS, prep time, feedback), success thresholds, and how learnings will adjust the final menu, pricing, and capex decisions.

depth_insight.criterion: Micro-market Demand Sizing and Daypart/Delivery Modeling
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report quantifies addressable demand around shortlisted CBD/office clusters (office worker counts, gym memberships), models lunch vs dinner splits, delivery radius coverage, weekly/seasonal patterns, and states defensible assumptions tied to this concept.

depth_insight.criterion: Target Customer Insight and Competitive White-space Positioning
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates depth of psychographic and use-case insights (e.g., high-protein/low-carb goals, willingness to pay, convenience expectations) and how they translate into a distinctive positioning versus direct/indirect competitors in CBD and delivery platforms.

depth_insight.criterion: Menu-engineering Linking Nutrition, Operations, and Cost
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether menu design aligns with nutritional goals while optimizing SKU count, cross-utilization, prep/serve times, sourcing, and allergen management to meet target food cost and throughput constraints.

depth_insight.criterion: Pricing Architecture and Unit Economics Depth
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether pricing within ¥40–¥60 is justified by contribution margins (per SKU and mix), bundling/add-ons, expected AOV, delivery commissions, packaging costs, and yields concrete break-even throughput and peak-hour capacity targets.

depth_insight.criterion: Site Selection Model and Rent-to-Sales Feasibility
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the site is chosen via data-backed scoring (footfall, office density, gym proximity, competitor saturation, rider access) and whether rent/deposit/fit-out are feasible under ¥500,000 with a realistic rent-to-sales ratio.

depth_insight.criterion: Dine-in + Delivery Operations Model and Timeline Feasibility
depth_insight.explanation: Checks depth of kitchen workflow, batching, pickup/rider flow, lunch-rush capacity planning, staffing, and SOPs, and whether the plan addresses critical-path steps (permits, renovation, equipment lead times, hiring/training) to open in six months.

depth_insight.criterion: Marketing Strategy with Quantified Acquisition and Retention
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether channel choices (e.g., Meituan/Ele.me promos, gym partnerships, office group orders, corporate catering, content) are tied to CAC/LTV estimates, retention mechanisms, and local seeding tactics relevant to CBD workers and fitness users.

depth_insight.criterion: Financial Budget Rigor, Phasing, and Sensitivity Tests
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates detail and realism of CapEx (fit-out, equipment, deposits), OpEx (labor, rent, utilities, platform fees, wastage), working capital and cashflow phasing, plus sensitivity analyses on order volume, COGS, commissions, and rent to ensure runway.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Identification and Mitigation Playbooks
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether major risks (food safety, regulatory permits/health claims, seasonality for salads, delivery platform policy shifts, supply disruptions) are anticipated with specific, actionable mitigation measures.

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and Actionable Context-specific Insights
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards non-generic, high-value ideas tailored to this concept and constraints (e.g., warm bowls/soups for winter, pre-order for lunch peaks, office subscription packs, gym co-branded SKUs, micro-location rider access design) that can materially improve outcomes.

logical_coherence.criterion: Cohesive Business Plan Framework and Sequencing
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a logical order (market research → positioning/targeting → menu/pricing/location/service model → operations → marketing → financial model → risks/milestones) and whether later sections explicitly reference earlier analyses.

logical_coherence.criterion: Derivation of Strategic Choices from Market Research
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether menu focus, price band (¥40–¥60), service model (dine-in + delivery), and CBD/office location are explicitly justified by target segment evidence and stated needs, avoiding unsupported jumps from research to decisions.

logical_coherence.criterion: Consistency and Traceability of Assumptions Across Sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether key assumptions (AOV, daily traffic, conversion, seat turnover, delivery commission, rent per m², renovation duration) are stated once and used consistently in operations, marketing, and financials without contradictions.

Checks that material claims (e.g., CBD footfall stability, fitness enthusiasts’ willingness to pay, delivery share at lunch) are backed by relevant data or justified benchmarks, ensuring arguments rest on appropriate support.

logical_coherence.criterion: Financial Logic Integrity and Unit Economics Traceability
logical_coherence.explanation: Tests whether revenue and costs flow correctly from operational assumptions (orders × AOV → revenue; COGS → gross margin; fixed costs incl. CBD rent; delivery fees; staffing; capex and depreciation; cash flow consistent with 3- and 6-month milestones).

Assesses whether the plan respects the ¥500,000 budget (including deposit and initial rent, equipment, renovation) and the 3-month site/renovation and 6-month opening timelines, given CBD costs and permitting steps.

Evaluates whether each section clearly signals why it follows (e.g., how positioning informs menu engineering; how operations capacity informs staffing; how marketing funnel informs sales forecast), avoiding logical gaps.
Checks that identified risks map to specific variables (rent overrun, delivery commission changes, slower ramp) with scenario or sensitivity impacts and logically derived mitigation measures.

logical_coherence.criterion: KPI Logic and Feedback Loop Coherence
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether KPIs (CAC, repeat rate, AOV, prep time, seat turnover) logically derive from assumptions, tie back to financial projections, and form a clear loop for adjusting operations/marketing.

clarity_readability.criterion: Document Structure and Navigability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks if the report has a logical, easy-to-follow structure for this business plan: a clear table of contents, numbered headings/subheadings mapped to required sections (market research, menu design, marketing strategies, financial budget), page numbers, cross-references, and section summaries. Also looks for scannability aids (bulleted key takeaways, executive summary, timeline overview) so readers can quickly locate and digest relevant parts.

clarity_readability.criterion: Formatting and Visual Cleanliness
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates typography and layout hygiene: consistent heading hierarchy, font sizes, spacing, indentation, alignment, and adequate white space; consistent use of styles for tables and callouts; clean page layout for long-form reading. Good aesthetics reduce fatigue and make dense sections (e.g., budgets, menu lists) easier to read.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity and Concision
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses sentence-level clarity: concise, unambiguous wording; consistent tone and tense; minimal grammatical errors; avoidance of overly promotional language; audience-appropriate reading level. For this plan, it should clearly state actions (e.g., site selection within 3 months), avoid run-ons, and make instructions and recommendations directly readable.

clarity_readability.criterion: Use and Explanation of Technical Terms
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether industry and analytical terms are defined on first use and used consistently thereafter: e.g., COGS, gross margin, SKU, AOV, CAC, LTV, EBITDA, breakeven, capex/opex, SLA, HACCP, kcal/macros. Also verifies consistent abbreviations and translations if bilingual terms are used. Proper handling of jargon lowers comprehension barriers.

clarity_readability.criterion: Numeric Transparency (Units, Assumptions, and Sources)
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether all numbers are easy to interpret: currency noted (¥), time basis stated (per day/month/year), units for nutrition (kcal, g protein), consistent decimal/rounding rules, scenario labels (base/optimistic), and clear annotation of key assumptions and data sources via footnotes or assumption boxes. This improves readability of budgets, pricing, and timeline metrics without judging their correctness.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Tables, Charts, and Other Visuals
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether visuals aid understanding and are well-designed: appropriate chart/table choice for the content (e.g., break-even chart, budget tables, channel mix charts, Gantt timeline for 6-month plan, location catchment map, menu with calories), clear titles, labeled axes/units, readable legends, annotations of key data points, color-blind-safe palettes, and no clutter (e.g., avoid 3D). Visuals should make complex sections immediately graspable.

My target market is primarily fitness enthusiasts and white-collar workers seeking light, healthy meals. We aim to offer convenient low-calorie healthy meals at a mid-to-high price point, averaging ¥40–¥60 per person.For the menu, I want to focus on salad bowls, energy bowls, and healthy burgers. Our service model will be mainly dine-in plus delivery. For location, I prefer commercial CBD areas and office building clusters to attract a steady flow of customers. I plan to finalize site selection and renovation within three months, aiming to officially open in six months.Please provide a business plan including market research, menu design, marketing strategies, and a detailed financial budget.

goal_alignment.criterion: Beijing CBD–specific customer and competitive insight at the 40–60 RMB price point
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the research grounds market sizing and positioning in Chaoyang/CBD realities (e.g., Sanlitun/Guomao/office clusters), maps lunch/dinner and gym-adjacent flows, profiles white-collar and fitness segments, and benchmarks competitors and substitutes at 40–60 RMB. goal_alignment.criterion: Budget and site feasibility under ¥500,000 with explicit risk controls

goal_alignment.criterion: Transparent financial model and unit economics tailored to Beijing operations

goal_alignment.criterion: Menu–nutrition–operations coherence (healthy credibility with lean execution)

goal_alignment.criterion: Authentic sustainability/ESG integration with measurable practices

This matches her eco-conscious, ESG interest and helps differentiate in CBD white-collar/fitness markets.

goal_alignment.criterion: Content-driven brand and community marketing leveraging her writer identity

goal_alignment.criterion: Timeline, permitting, and project management specific to Beijing within 6 months

goal_alignment.criterion: Writer-friendly structure, evidence, and decision tools

This matches her independent, analytical decision-making style and facilitates consulting her close creative/media circle.

content_alignment.criterion: Beijing Chaoyang/CBD-Specific Market Intelligence Depth
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether market research zooms into Beijing office clusters (e.g., Guomao, Sanlitun, Wangjing), white-collar lunch/dinner patterns, fitness studio ecosystems, and competitor mapping of healthy fast-casual brands in Beijing, using local foot-traffic proxies and delivery platform behaviors—matching the user’s location and need for in-depth, evidence-based insights.

content_alignment.criterion: Financial Realism for ¥500,000 in CBD with Risk-Averse Framing
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the financial content uses Beijing-specific rent/renovation/equipment benchmarks, deposits, and working capital needs, includes conservative assumptions and sensitivity/buffer analysis, and proposes staged/low-capex entry options (e.g., sublease, small footprint, pop-up) to align with her cautious risk profile and budget constraint.

content_alignment.criterion: Sustainability/ESG Operational Content
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the plan includes concrete sustainable choices (local/seasonal sourcing, waste reduction, compostable or reusable packaging, incentive mechanisms for reusables, measurable ESG KPIs) that align with her eco-friendly consumption values rather than generic ‘green’ statements.

content_alignment.criterion: Menu Tailoring to Intermittent Fasting, Yoga/Pilates, and Tea Culture within ¥40–¥60
content_alignment.explanation: Determine whether menu design addresses IF time-window bundles, macro/calorie transparency for fitness users, plant-forward/light options for yoga/Pilates, and tea pairings (low-caffeine/herbal/functional teas), all tuned to Beijing tastes and the stated price band—matching her dietary habits and target customer needs.

content_alignment.criterion: Channel and Community Strategy Aligned with WeChat/Weibo/Douban and Literary Storytelling
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether marketing content prioritizes platforms she uses (WeChat private domain, Weibo threads, Douban groups), leverages editorial storytelling, long-form explainers, and collaborations with yoga/Pilates studios and bookstores/cafés in Chaoyang—reflecting her media literacy and networks.

content_alignment.criterion: Founder-Strength Leverage: Journalism-Driven Research and Brand Building
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan includes content that taps her journalism skills—customer interview guides, narrative brand frameworks, thought-leadership articles, and research cadences—so the strategy selection is tailored to her strengths rather than generic founder profiles.

content_alignment.criterion: Delivery and Packaging Choices Reflecting Eco Concerns and Office-Cluster Behaviors
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether delivery content covers office group preorders, delivery radius/time windows for freshness, salad/bowl integrity, and eco-friendly packaging trade-offs—reconciling her personal skepticism of delivery with the business’s dine-in + delivery model.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structured, layered organization with an executive summary
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report opens with a concise executive summary and follows a clear business-plan flow (market → positioning → operations/menu → marketing → financials → risks → milestones), with visible headings/subheadings. As a journalism-trained reader, she expects a logical hierarchy that supports scanning and deep dives.

presentation_fit.criterion: Professional, precise, non-hype language and localization
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether the tone is calm, evidence-oriented, and free of marketing fluff, using Mainland Chinese business language (or bilingual with Chinese as primary) and correct currency notation (¥). This matches her writerly sensitivity to clarity and cultural context.

presentation_fit.criterion: Clarity of financial and operational visualizations
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that budgets, unit economics, breakeven, and traffic projections are shown via clean tables/charts with labeled axes, units, currency, and readable legends. Proper formatting lets a non-specialist parse numbers quickly without misinterpretation.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology management (definitions of F&B and finance acronyms)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether acronyms and sector jargon (e.g., SKU, COGS, CAC, ROI, break-even) are briefly defined on first use or via a compact glossary/footnotes. This preserves professionalism while maintaining accessibility for a reader outside F&B operations.

presentation_fit.criterion: Timeline visualization for 3- and 6-month milestones
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether key milestones (site selection, lease, renovation, licensing, hiring, soft/opening) are depicted in a clear Gantt-style timeline with dates and dependencies. Visual timing helps her plan and track progress at a glance.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-friendly readability and scannability
presentation_fit.explanation: Check for short paragraphs, bullet lists, adequate line spacing, high-contrast color choices, and charts that remain legible on small screens. She reads on WeChat at night, so mobile-optimized formatting reduces fatigue and improves comprehension.

presentation_fit.criterion: Document navigation aids (TOC, hyperlinks, cross-references, appendices)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess the presence and clarity of a table of contents, section numbering, internal links, and labeled appendices for detailed tables. This supports efficient navigation and re-finding of information in a long plan.

presentation_fit.criterion: Consistency of units, currency, and formatting conventions
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate consistent use of ¥, per-person metrics, time frames, and number formatting (e.g., thousands separators), with assumptions noted in captions/footnotes. Consistency minimizes cognitive friction and errors without changing content.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Financial plan feasibility within ¥500,000 (capex, opex, and working capital) with 12-month cashflow
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report itemizes start-up costs (rent deposit and advance, renovation, equipment, licenses, initial inventory, packaging, IT/POS), monthly operating expenses (rent, labor, utilities, platform fees, marketing), and provides a 12-month cashflow with a working capital buffer; enables the user to judge affordability and runway.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Site selection feasibility for Beijing CBD/office clusters within budget
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether rent benchmarks (¥/m²/month), typical deposit terms, required area, power/exhaust constraints, and alternative submarkets are provided with screening criteria and a lease negotiation checklist, ensuring the user can choose a viable location without exceeding budget.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Timeline and critical path to open in six months
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether there is a week-by-week (or Gantt-like) plan covering site selection, design/fit-out, licensing, procurement, hiring/training, platform onboarding, soft opening, and launch, with clear dependencies and slack for delays.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Licensing and compliance roadmap specific to Beijing F&B
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for a concrete list of required permits (e.g., business license, food business license, staff health certificates, fire/exhaust compliance), expected timelines, document requirements, sequence, and costs so the user can navigate approvals without rework.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Menu-engineering and kitchen operations feasibility (SOPs, equipment, throughput)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies standardized recipes with gram cost and kcal, controlled SKU count, prep workflows, equipment list/layout, batch prep plans, and target service times, ensuring consistent quality and speed for salad bowls, energy bowls, and healthy burgers.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Pricing and item-level unit economics (incl. target COGS and margins)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Requires per-SKU cost sheets, target COGS%, gross margin, and contribution margin; ensures ¥40–¥60 price points yield sustainable margins after ingredient costs and expected wastage.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Delivery operations plan (platforms, commissions, packaging, SLA, radius)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report details Meituan/Ele.me onboarding, commission assumptions, delivery-optimized menu, packaging specs and cost per order, delivery radius/time windows, and KPIs, ensuring delivery is profitable and reliable.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Staffing plan and founder role practicality
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks role design (kitchen lead, line/prep, cashier/barista, delivery coordination), headcount by shift, Beijing wage benchmarks, training SOPs, and a realistic plan for the founder’s time and responsibilities given limited F&B experience.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Go-to-market playbook with channel tactics, budget, and KPIs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates concrete pre-opening and first-90-days tactics (e.g., office tower samplings, gym partnerships, WeChat community deals, corporate meal plans), monthly spend, CAC targets, and measurable goals (orders/day, repeat rate), enabling execution and iteration.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Demand validation and pilot testing before full commitment
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for a step-by-step MVP (pop-up, co-working lunch test, pre-sale on WeChat) with success thresholds (e.g., orders/day, conversion, feedback) to de-risk site and menu decisions for a risk-averse founder.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk mitigation and contingency planning
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for scenario analysis (rent overrun, licensing delay, supplier issues), contingency budget (e.g., 10–15% capex), multi-sourcing, and Plan B options (smaller site/dark kitchen), improving resilience and decision readiness.

depth_insight.criterion: Evidence-based Demand Sizing and Segment Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report quantifies CBD/office-area demand for healthy meals among white-collar and fitness segments (e.g., TAM/SAM/SOM for lunch/dinner, delivery vs dine-in mix, willingness-to-pay at ¥40–¥60), using local data, benchmarks, or primary research plans and extracting actionable insights beyond generic claims.

depth_insight.criterion: Location and Catchment Economics Analysis
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates depth in analyzing candidate CBD/office-cluster sites via rent-to-sales ratio targets, foot traffic and conversion assumptions, anchor tenants, weekday vs weekend flows, and delivery radius/coverage heatmaps, leading to informed judgments on site viability within the budget and timeline.

depth_insight.criterion: Menu Engineering and Nutrition-to-Operations Fit
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether menu design links nutritional targets (calories/macros) to costed recipes, SKU rationalization, prep/hold times, cross-utilization of ingredients, and delivery holdability/packaging—showing insight into balancing health claims, taste, speed, and waste control.

depth_insight.criterion: Channel-specific Unit Economics and Pricing Strategy
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses depth in modeling per-item and per-order margins by channel (dine-in vs delivery), including food cost %, packaging, platform commissions, labor allocation, add-ons/bundles, AOV, and price elasticity—ensuring the ¥40–¥60 positioning is economically sustainable.

depth_insight.criterion: Financial Plan Realism and Robustness within ¥500,000
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the budget separates capex/renovation, deposits, equipment, pre-opening, working capital, and marketing; includes ramp-up, cash runway, break-even, seasonality, and sensitivity analyses (e.g., traffic, conversion, commission changes), demonstrating resilient planning.

depth_insight.criterion: Competitive Landscape and White-space Positioning
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for rigorous benchmarking versus direct and indirect competitors on price, nutrition, speed, delivery coverage, ratings, and brand; identifies underserved needs and defensible differentiation beyond generic 'healthy' positioning.

depth_insight.criterion: Operational Throughput and Capacity Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report quantifies peak-hour order capacity, queue times, staffing per station, equipment constraints, prep workflows, and delivery SLA impacts—translating operations into service-level and cost implications.

depth_insight.criterion: Go-to-Market Strategy with ROI Logic
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether marketing tactics (gym/corporate partnerships, subscriptions, referrals, platform promotions) are tied to CAC, expected AOV/frequency, LTV, and a test-and-learn plan—providing insight into acquisition and retention efficiency specific to the target segments.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Identification and Mitigation Specificity
depth_insight.explanation: Checks for nuanced risks (food safety and licensing, supply volatility, delivery platform dependency, taste acceptance, seasonality, staff turnover, rent escalation) with concrete mitigations (HACCP processes, dual suppliers, menu pivots, contingency reserves).

depth_insight.criterion: Original, Context-aware Differentiating Insights
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards unique, actionable ideas grounded in the local context (e.g., pre-order/pickup lanes for office lunch peaks, macro-customization, daypart-specific SKUs, corporate meal plans, data-driven menu rotation) that go beyond standard playbooks.

logical_coherence.criterion: End-to-end framework aligned with task requirements
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that the report follows a logical sequence from objectives and constraints (budget, timeline, target segment, price band) to market research, then to strategic choices (location, menu, pricing, service model), operational plan, marketing plan, and financial budget/rollout. Ensures all required sections are present and ordered so that each decision rests on prior analysis.

Evaluates whether key decisions—CBD/office cluster location, menu categories, ¥40–¥60 pricing, dine-in + delivery mix, and marketing channels—are explicitly derived from stated premises (fitness/white-collar needs, calorie constraints, convenience, budget and 6-month schedule). Looks for if–then logic and avoidance of non sequiturs (e.g., not choosing channels unrelated to the target segment’s behaviors).

Assesses whether each important claim (e.g., CBD footfall stability, segment willingness to pay, delivery demand share, renovation duration) is tied to relevant data, benchmarks, or method outputs. Focuses on the presence and relevance of support to anchor arguments, preventing unsupported jumps from assertion to decision.

Checks the internal logic from demand forecasts → capacity and seating/throughput → staffing schedules → prep times → COGS → unit margins → P&L/cash flow. Verifies that capex, rent, deposits, fit-out, equipment, and working capital fit within ¥500,000 and that the schedule (site selection + renovation in 3 months; opening in 6 months) coherently maps to spending phases and hiring/training milestones.

Evaluates whether menu design (salad/energy bowls, healthy burgers) aligns with kitchen workflow, ingredient cross-utilization, delivery packaging, and service speed; whether pricing is consistent with COGS and the target segment’s willingness to pay; whether location criteria reflect the service model (dine-in plus delivery radius/SLA). Identifies contradictions between sections.

Assesses whether sections explicitly reference prior findings (e.g., market insights informing menu/pricing) and preview how outputs feed into subsequent plans (e.g., capacity informing budget). Smooth transitions reduce logical gaps and help readers follow how conclusions are constructed step-by-step.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structured Layout and Navigability for a Business Plan
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear heading hierarchy aligned to common business plan sections (Executive Summary, Market Research, Menu Design, Operations, Marketing, Financial Budget, Timeline, Risks), plus a table of contents, page numbers, consistent styles, and adequate white space so readers can quickly locate information.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity, Concision, and Professional Tone of Language
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether sentences are straightforward, free of ambiguity and run-ons, use active voice, avoid unnecessary jargon, and employ bullet points and short paragraphs to present steps (e.g., site selection and renovation) clearly for non-experts.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency and Explanation of Technical Terms and Acronyms
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether domain terms (e.g., COGS, gross margin, AOV, CAC, CAPEX/OPEX, break-even, delivery take rate, SKU, POS, HACCP) are introduced with first-use definitions or a glossary, used consistently (e.g., ‘salad bowl’ vs ‘bowl salad’), and do not assume prior knowledge.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Data Tables and Visualizations
clarity_readability.explanation: Reviews whether key tables/charts are legible and self-explanatory: titled, labeled axes, correct units (¥, %, kcal, g), readable fonts, and notes for assumptions/sources. Examples include competitor price bars, menu nutrition-price matrix, contribution margin waterfall, break-even chart, monthly cash flow table, sensitivity chart, and a 6-month Gantt timeline placed near relevant text.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Key Metrics, Assumptions, and Summaries
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks use of executive summary, section summaries, callouts, and bolding to surface critical numbers and assumptions (e.g., ¥40–60 per capita, target gross margin, rent-to-revenue cap, delivery commission, daily covers, seat turnover, milestone dates) so decision-makers can digest the essentials quickly.

clarity_readability.criterion: Numerical and Unit Formatting Consistency
clarity_readability.explanation: Verifies consistent formatting of currency (¥ with thousand separators), decimal precision, percentages, date/time ranges (e.g., monthly vs annual), calories/macros (kcal, g protein), portion sizes, and clear labeling of approximations/ranges to avoid misreading of financials and menu nutrition.

task: I plan to start a new food and beverage business focusing on healthy fast food within the next six months, with available start-up funds of approximately ¥500,000, including venue rental. My target market is primarily fitness enthusiasts and white-collar workers seeking light, healthy meals. We aim to offer convenient low-calorie healthy meals at a mid-to-high price point, averaging ¥40–¥60 per person.For the menu, I want to focus on salad bowls, energy bowls, and healthy burgers. Our service model will be mainly dine-in plus delivery. For location, I prefer commercial CBD areas and office building clusters to attract a steady flow of customers. I plan to finalize site selection and renovation within three months, aiming to officially open in six months.Please provide a business plan including market research, menu design, marketing strategies, and a detailed financial budget.

goal_alignment.criterion: Financial feasibility under ¥500,000 for Shanghai CBD with dine-in + delivery economics
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the budget models rent (including typical Shanghai CBD deposit/advance practices), renovation, equipment, licensing, initial inventory, marketing, platform commissions, and 3–6 months working capital. Must show SKU-level COGS, target food cost %, labor %, and delivery commission impacts to maintain margins at ¥40–¥60.

goal_alignment.criterion: Data-driven market sizing and pricing validation for white-collar and fitness segments in Shanghai
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether market research quantifies lunch/evening demand in CBDs, office worker density, gym proximity, competitor benchmarks, and price elasticity around ¥40–¥60. Should present sources/assumptions transparently for a data analyst to scrutinize or rerun.

goal_alignment.criterion: Location selection scoring model tailored to CBD/office clusters and gym adjacency
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for a multi-factor site scoring framework (rent per m², footfall in 11:30–13:30 peak, office headcount, gym density within 1 km, delivery heatmaps, competitor saturation, ventilation/grease trap feasibility, landlord F&B policies) with weights and thresholds Liu Lu can apply.

goal_alignment.criterion: Operations blueprint for lunch-peak throughput and delivery SLA
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify a concrete plan for assembly-line workflow, equipment list (prep tables, refrigeration, grills/combi ovens), staffing by shift, capacity targets (orders/hour at lunch), delivery radius and SLA (e.g., ≤25–30 min), packaging to preserve quality, and dine-in seat turnover assumptions.

goal_alignment.criterion: Menu engineering aligned to fitness macros and Italian/Japanese taste at target COGS
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if menu items (salad/energy bowls, healthy burgers) include macro/calorie labeling (e.g., ≥25–35g protein, 400–650 kcal), flavor profiles reflecting Italian/Japanese preferences, allergen/sodium considerations, and per-SKU COGS/margin to fit the ¥40–¥60 band.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk control and sensitivity analysis aligned with conservative risk appetite
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess best/base/worst-case P&L, break-even covers/day, cash runway under demand and rent variance, mitigation levers (menu pricing, staffing flex, smaller footprint/ghost kitchen), and staged investment/pilot testing to limit downside.

goal_alignment.criterion: Project timeline and resource plan meeting 3-month site/renovation and 6-month opening
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for a milestone-based Gantt (site shortlist → LOI → permits → design → build → hiring → soft/open), critical-path identification, buffer times for permitting/build delays, and task owners/tools (templates suited to Notion/Trello).

goal_alignment.criterion: Compliance and licensing roadmap for Shanghai F&B
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan lists required permits (business license, food service permit, sanitation, fire safety, signage, environmental/ventilation requirements), sequencing, typical lead times, and approximate costs to protect schedule and budget.

goal_alignment.criterion: Channel and growth strategy fit: gyms, corporates, delivery platforms, and owned media
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for targeted tactics: gym partnerships and cross-promos, corporate lunch programs, WeChat mini-program/CRM, delivery platform merchandising and commission economics, and thought-leadership content on LinkedIn/Zhihu leveraging her data storytelling.

goal_alignment.criterion: Sustainability and packaging strategy grounded in local feasibility
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess packaging/material choices balancing eco-preference with Shanghai recycling realities, cost and heat retention for delivery, supplier options, waste-reduction SOPs, and messaging that resonates with fitness/white-collar audiences.

goal_alignment.criterion: KPI framework and analytics/experimentation plan
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine whether the plan defines KPIs (AOV, food cost %, labor %, occupancy %, delivery mix, daily covers, repeat rate), data capture (POS/Meituan/Ele.me), dashboard schema, cohort tracking, and A/B testing cadence—aligning with her data-driven workflow.

goal_alignment.criterion: Analytical communication style and decision tools tailored to a data analyst
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate clarity and structure: explicit assumptions, formulas, tables, checklists, and editable models/templates compatible with Notion/Trello; concise narrative avoiding hype and enabling independent verification.

content_alignment.criterion: Shanghai CBD Micro-Market Specificity and Competitor–Price Mapping
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether market research is anchored in Shanghai CBD/office-cluster realities (e.g., subdistrict rent ranges, office/gym density, footfall/delivery demand by daypart) and benchmarks against local healthy fast-casual players in the ¥40–¥60 band. This ensures the content reflects Liu Lu’s chosen geography and pricing context rather than generic China-wide insights.

content_alignment.criterion: Data-Centric Rigor Aligned to Analyst Skillset
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan frames decisions with quantifiable models and metrics Liu Lu values: unit economics, KPIs (AOV, repeat rate, CAC/LTV), cohort/retention analyses, A/B testing plans, and references to relevant data sources (delivery platform analytics, POI/footfall data). This aligns with her evidence-driven, tool-fluent background and deepens analytical engagement.

content_alignment.criterion: Menu Nutrition Depth and Flavor Preference Integration
content_alignment.explanation: Check if menu design provides per-item macro breakdowns (calories, protein, carbs, fats), high-protein/low-calorie options, and integrates Italian/Japanese flavor profiles within salad bowls, energy bowls, and healthy burgers. This matches her dietary habits and taste, increasing personal relevance of the menu content.

content_alignment.criterion: Channel Strategy Specific to Dine-in + Delivery in CBD Context
content_alignment.explanation: Determine whether the content addresses CBD lunch and post-work gym traffic patterns, delivery packaging for quality retention, platform ranking levers, pickup flow near office lobbies, and building delivery constraints. This aligns content to her service model and her own frequent delivery usage.

content_alignment.criterion: Financial Assumptions Tailored to ¥500k Budget and Conservative Risk Profile
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether budgeting and forecasts use Shanghai-appropriate rent/fit-out norms, deposits, contingency buffers, and sensitivity scenarios (e.g., slower ramp, higher rent) consistent with a conservative investor. The content should test break-even and payback in the ¥40–¥60 price band, reflecting her cost-conscious decision style.

content_alignment.criterion: Project Planning Artifacts Matching Her Structured Workflow
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan includes a structured roadmap (e.g., stage-gates for site selection, design, permits, renovation, soft open) and task frameworks compatible with her planning habits (e.g., Kanban/Gantt logic). This content reflects her time-managed, detail-oriented working style.

content_alignment.criterion: Sustainability and Supplier Transparency Content
content_alignment.explanation: Check for inclusion of eco-friendly packaging options, sourcing transparency, and sustainability trade-offs (cost vs. impact). This aligns with her interest in environmental protection and preference for high-quality, responsible products.

content_alignment.criterion: Community Partnerships and Professional-Channel Fit
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether marketing content emphasizes gym partnerships, office wellness/corporate meal plans, and professional platforms she uses (LinkedIn/Zhihu), matching both her networks and the target segments’ discovery channels. This increases personal relevance beyond generic social advertising.

presentation_fit.criterion: Logical structure and navigability aligned with the requested business plan sections and timeline
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a clear, modular outline (executive summary → market research → menu design → marketing → financial budget → timeline/roadmap), uses numbered headings, and provides a readable timeline mapping to the 3- and 6-month milestones, enabling quick navigation for a structured reader.

presentation_fit.criterion: Professional, concise, evidence-led tone with clear logical flow
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether the language avoids hype and vague claims, favors concise sentences, and presents arguments step-by-step (claim → data point → implication), matching a data analyst’s preference for objectivity and clarity.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology management: correct use of F&B/business terms with brief definitions and consistent acronyms
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether domain terms (e.g., COGS, CAC, LTV, table turnover, aggregator take-rate) are used correctly and defined on first mention, acronyms are consistent, and any localized terms are clarified, reducing cognitive load while maintaining professionalism.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visualization quality and suitability for analytical readers
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks that charts/tables are used appropriately (e.g., market sizing, price positioning, unit economics, break-even, sensitivity), with labeled axes, units, legends, readable fonts, and cited sources—facilitating fast and accurate interpretation by a Tableau-savvy reader.

presentation_fit.criterion: Numerical transparency and annotation of assumptions
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether key numbers are accompanied by explicit units (RMB, per-person, per-month), stated assumptions, ranges or scenarios, and brief formula notes/footnotes, so the reader can quickly understand and audit the calculations from the presentation alone.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tool-compatibility and reusability of presented materials
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether tables are copy-friendly (clean columns/headers, CSV-like formatting), checklists are provided, and timelines/Gantt are expressed in a text or simple visual form suitable for direct import into Notion/Trello, aligning with the user’s workflow.

presentation_fit.criterion: Localization of language, currency, and context in presentation
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks for consistent RMB currency, metric units, Shanghai/CBD context cues, and platform naming (e.g., Meituan/Ele.me) in labels and examples to improve immediacy and reduce translation effort for a Shanghai-based reader.

presentation_fit.criterion: Information hierarchy and scannability
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether the report uses an executive summary, key takeaways, bullet lists, callouts, and section summaries to support quick evening reading and efficient review by a detail-oriented, time-managed user.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Capital and cash‑flow feasibility within ¥500,000
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report provides a line‑item pre‑opening budget (deposit and advance rent, renovation, equipment, licenses, POS/IT, initial inventory, packaging, pre‑opening marketing) plus a defined working capital runway for at least 3–6 months, with monthly cash‑flow projections tied to price (¥40–¥60) and realistic order volumes. Spreadsheet-ready and editable numbers are required to be actionable.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Site selection feasibility and scoring model for CBD/office clusters
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report offers a quantifiable location scoring framework (rent/sqm and rent‑to‑sales %, weekday lunch footfall, office headcount within 5–10 min, proximity to gyms, competitor density, delivery radius coverage, ventilation/grease trap feasibility for food permit), sample benchmarks for Shanghai CBD rents and deposits, lease negotiation steps, and a shortlisting process that can be executed within 3 months.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Operational plan practicality (staffing, equipment, throughput, SOPs)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates if the report details kitchen layout constraints, equipment list with costs and utilities, prep/holding methods, staffing plan with role counts and wage benchmarks, shift scheduling for lunch peaks, and SOPs for mise en place, food safety, and waste control—so the store can reliably serve dine‑in plus delivery during peak windows.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Menu engineering with costed recipes and margin targets
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether salad bowls, energy bowls, and healthy burgers have standardized recipes, portion sizes, supplier quotes, per‑item food cost and target food‑cost % (e.g., ≤30–35%), cross‑utilization to reduce SKU complexity, and price points that fit ¥40–¥60 while remaining profitable and delivery‑suitable.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Regulatory and permit pathway with timelines and premises checklist
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines if the plan lays out a step‑by‑step path for business license and food service permit, fire and environmental approvals (ventilation/chimney/grease trap), construction compliance, required documents, realistic lead times, and a pre‑lease premises checklist to avoid signing locations that cannot be licensed.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Break‑even and sensitivity analyses for decision thresholds
actionability_practicality.explanation: Reviews whether the report provides unit economics, fixed/variable cost breakdown, break‑even revenue/orders per day, and sensitivity scenarios (rent, delivery commission, AOV, conversion, labor rates). Clear assumptions and scenario toggles enable go/no‑go and pricing decisions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Delivery channel readiness (platform setup, menu, packaging, SLAs)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for concrete steps to onboard Meituan/Ele.me (fees, timelines), menu optimization for delivery, packaging specs and per‑order cost, photography requirements, delivery radius selection, peak management (staging/pickup shelves), and impact of commissions on pricing and margin.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Marketing execution plan with budgets, channels, and measurable KPIs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the plan includes executable campaigns for CBD white‑collars and fitness enthusiasts (office partnerships, gym tie‑ins, group orders, Xiaohongshu/Dianping tactics), an opening calendar, budget allocations, expected CAC/LTV and conversion targets, and practical tracking methods.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Project plan, checklists, and tooling readiness for a 6‑month launch
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies the presence of a time‑bound Gantt/critical path for site, permits, build‑out, hiring, onboarding to delivery platforms, and soft opening; weekly task checklists; decision gates; and templates compatible with Notion/Trello plus a KPI tracker outline for daily ops/finance.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk and contingency planning with buffers and triggers
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether key risks (lease/permit delays, over‑budget renovation, supplier disruptions, demand shortfall) are identified with mitigation actions, contingency budget (e.g., 10–15%), alternative options (e.g., smaller footprint or delivery‑first), and quantitative kill/adjust triggers.

depth_insight.criterion: Market Demand, Segmentation, and Daypart Insights (CBD fitness and white-collar)
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report quantifies local demand with evidence (e.g., office worker counts, gym memberships within 1 km, weekday lunch footfall), analyzes daypart/seasonality patterns, and validates willingness-to-pay at ¥40–¥60 via surveys/benchmarks. Depth is shown by linking drivers (health trends, convenience needs) to revenue potential rather than generic market stats.

depth_insight.criterion: Location and Site Economics Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the site analysis uses micro-location data (hourly footfall, proximity to gyms/offices, delivery radius/ETAs) and rent benchmarks to derive feasible sales targets and occupancy cost ratios (e.g., rent as % of sales), including ventilation/utility feasibility. Insight is shown by translating location facts into economic implications.

depth_insight.criterion: Menu Engineering and SKU-level Unit Economics
depth_insight.explanation: Examines whether the menu is engineered to hit nutrition targets and operational constraints while achieving margins: ingredient sourcing and cross-utilization, prep times, batchability, waste control, packaging for delivery, and contribution margins per SKU after platform fees. Depth is reflected in costed recipes and margin targets tied to the ¥40–¥60 price band.

depth_insight.criterion: Operational Model and Capacity for Dine-in + Delivery Peaks
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses analysis of throughput and service times at peak lunch (orders/min, ticket times), staffing model, kitchen line design, and delivery integration without compromising dine-in quality. Insight includes quantified capacity, bottleneck identification, and mitigation (par levels, mise en place, hot/cold holding).

depth_insight.criterion: Pricing and Revenue Architecture
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether pricing goes beyond list prices to include bundles, add-ons, upsell design, channel price differentials to offset platform fees, minimum order thresholds, and elasticity assumptions grounded in comps/tests. Insight is shown by modeling AOV drivers and their margin impact.

depth_insight.criterion: Go-to-Market and Differentiation Strategy with CAC/LTV Logic
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses originality and rigor in customer acquisition and retention: gym/office partnerships, corporate catering, aggregator ads, referral/loyalty mechanics, and differentiation (e.g., macro-tracked meals, subscriptions). Depth is shown by CAC vs first-order contribution, repeat rates, and competitor whitespace analysis.

depth_insight.criterion: Financial Model Rigor and Scenario/Sensitivity Analysis (¥500k constraint)
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates a bottom-up financial plan: capex (renovation/equipment/deposits), pre-opening, working capital, monthly P&L, cash flow, breakeven and payback. Insight is shown by scenario tests (sales ramp, delivery mix, rent levels, food cost inflation) and buffers ensuring runway within ¥500k.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Identification and Practical Mitigation
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the plan anticipates key risks (perishability and waste, supply volatility, food safety, delivery platform policies/commissions, CBD weekend demand slump, weather impacts on salad demand) and proposes concrete mitigations (supplier contracts and backups, dynamic menu rotation, demand forecasting, multi-channel mix, compliance checklist).

logical_coherence.criterion: Task-Appropriate Logical Structure of Business Plan
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a logically ordered framework tailored to this F&B startup: objectives and constraints → market research → positioning (target users, price ¥40–¥60, value proposition) → menu/operations/location/channel decisions → marketing strategy → financial model and budget (≤¥500,000 incl. rent) → risks → implementation plan. Each section should set up the next, preventing disjointed or circular argumentation.

logical_coherence.criterion: Causal Chain from Market Research to Strategy and Financial Outcomes
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether conclusions (menu focus on salad/energy bowls/healthy burgers, CBD site, dine-in + delivery, mid-to-high pricing) are explicitly derived from market findings about fitness enthusiasts and white-collar demand, and whether these choices logically lead to capacity plans, revenue forecasts, and profitability. Avoids non sequiturs (e.g., pricing chosen without linking to willingness-to-pay or competition).

logical_coherence.criterion: Explicit Linkage of Claims/Decisions to Evidence or Stated Assumptions
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether each key claim or decision cites either relevant evidence (e.g., local foot traffic, competitor pricing, delivery platform share) or clearly stated assumptions when data are unavailable, and labels them as such. This prevents unsupported assertions while not judging data depth.

logical_coherence.criterion: Assumption Documentation and Flow-Through to Calculations
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that all critical assumptions (rent level for CBD/office clusters, renovation and equipment costs, seating and kitchen throughput, operating hours, AOV ¥40–¥60, COGS %, delivery commission, staffing levels and wages) are explicitly listed and traceably used in formulas for revenue, costs, break-even, and cash flow.

logical_coherence.criterion: Cross-Section Numerical Consistency and Constraint Compliance
logical_coherence.explanation: Verifies that numbers are consistent across sections and comply with constraints: total startup spend ≤¥500,000; staffing costs match hours; capacity and expected orders align with kitchen/seating; COGS and price yield feasible gross margins; delivery fees and packaging are included; no contradictions between market sizing, operations, and the financial model.

logical_coherence.criterion: Timeline and Dependency Logic vs 3- and 6-Month Targets
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan sequences tasks with dependencies (lease → permits → renovation → equipment → hiring/training → menu testing → soft opening) and whether durations feasibly meet site selection/renovation within three months and opening by six months, with contingency logic for critical path risks.

logical_coherence.criterion: Risk-Driver to Mitigation Mapping with Triggers
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether identified risks (rent overruns, permit delays, delivery commission impact, footfall seasonality) are logically tied to root drivers and mapped to specific mitigations and thresholds (e.g., rent cap per m², minimum daily orders, break-even AOV) that would trigger actions or pivots.

logical_coherence.criterion: Section Transitions and Cross-Referencing of Earlier Results
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether transitions explicitly reference preceding findings (e.g., using market insights to motivate pricing or channel choices, pointing to assumptions tables when numbers appear), avoiding logical jumps between sections. Focus is on logical linkage, not stylistic polish.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Organization and Navigability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for a clear hierarchy of numbered headings (e.g., 1, 1.1), a table of contents with page numbers, consistent section ordering (market research → menu → marketing → financials), and uniform formatting (fonts, spacing, margins). Good structure lets readers quickly locate key parts like the financial budget or site-selection timeline.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Fluency, Precision, and Audience Fit
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether sentences are concise and grammatical, use active voice, avoid ambiguity, and keep jargon minimal. Tone should be professional but accessible for non-experts (e.g., landlords, investors). Clear, error-free language reduces cognitive load and prevents misinterpretation.

clarity_readability.criterion: Definition and Consistent Use of Technical/Industry Terms
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether key terms (e.g., COGS, gross margin, CAPEX/OPEX, SKU, CAC, AOV, break-even, kcal, macros, allergens) are defined upon first use and applied consistently thereafter. Proper definitions lower the barrier for readers unfamiliar with F&B and finance terminology.

clarity_readability.criterion: Visual Design Quality of Charts, Tables, and Diagrams (Analytical)
clarity_readability.explanation: Looks at whether analytical visuals (market size charts, location heat maps, Gantt timeline, break-even/cash-flow charts) have clear titles, labeled axes with units, readable legends, appropriate chart types, legible fonts, and colorblind-safe palettes. Well-designed visuals communicate complex information at a glance.

clarity_readability.criterion: Numerical Clarity: Units, Assumptions, and Source Notes
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks consistent currency and units (e.g., ¥ with thousand separators, kcal, %), sensible decimal precision, explicit time bases (per day/month/year), and transparent assumptions/sources via footnotes. This prevents confusion in budgets, pricing, and forecasts and ensures numbers can be reliably interpreted.

clarity_readability.criterion: Emphasis and Scannability of Key Information
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates use of summaries, bullet lists, callouts, and typographic emphasis (bold/italics) to highlight key KPIs, milestones, and takeaways. Adequate white space and consistent list/table styles improve skimming and retention, especially for long sections such as marketing plans and budgets.

clarity_readability.criterion: Domain-Specific Presentation: Menu and Operations Readability
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses clarity of menu and operational content: menu categories and item names, aligned pricing, concise descriptions, calorie/macros, and allergen icons; readable process diagrams for dine-in and delivery workflows; legible floor plan legends. These domain visuals make the concept and operations easy to grasp without interpreting raw text.

task: I plan to start a new food and beverage business focusing on healthy fast food within the next six months, with available start-up funds of approximately ¥500,000, including venue rental. My target market is primarily fitness enthusiasts and white-collar workers seeking light, healthy meals. We aim to offer convenient low-calorie healthy meals at a mid-to-high price point, averaging ¥40–¥60 per person.For the menu, I want to focus on salad bowls, energy bowls, and healthy burgers. Our service model will be mainly dine-in plus delivery. For location, I prefer commercial CBD areas and office building clusters to attract a steady flow of customers. I plan to finalize site selection and renovation within three months, aiming to officially open in six months.Please provide a business plan including market research, menu design, marketing strategies, and a detailed financial budget.

goal_alignment.criterion: Chengdu CBD micro-market and site selection personalization
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan localizes to Chengdu’s CBD/office clusters (e.g., High-tech Zone/Financial City, Chunxi Road/Taikoo Li areas), providing rent benchmarks, weekday lunch footfall, office worker density, gym proximity, and competitor mapping to justify site shortlist and expected ticket/throughput.

goal_alignment.criterion: Financial feasibility under ¥500,000 with conservative risk control
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether capex/opex are itemized (deposits, renovation, equipment, licenses, initial inventory, opening marketing, working capital), include a 10–15% contingency, and provide break-even analysis, cash runway, and sensitivity tests (traffic down, platform commission up, COGS variance), reflecting the user’s conservative investing and family responsibilities.

goal_alignment.criterion: Supply chain and operations design leveraging the user’s expertise
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for a robust sourcing strategy (produce, lean proteins, whole grains), cold-chain and storage plans, batch prep vs made-to-order logic, inventory turns and waste targets, lunch-peak throughput modeling, and SOPs (prep, HACCP, station layout), aligning with a supply chain manager’s strengths and desire for efficiency.

goal_alignment.criterion: Menu-market fit with Sichuan-adapted healthy flavors, nutrition transparency, and price discipline
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether salad/energy bowls and healthy burgers include locally resonant, low-oil Sichuan flavor options; provide calorie/macros transparency for fitness users; design bundles for ¥40–¥60; and manage COGS to hit target gross margin while maintaining perceived value for white-collar diners.

goal_alignment.criterion: Delivery-first service and packaging strategy preserving quality and unit economics
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate Ele.me/Meituan strategy (commission impact, geo-fencing radius, promised delivery time), packaging that maintains texture (crisp greens, non-soggy burgers), leakage/condensation control, and channel price/portion differentiation to protect margins, reflecting the user’s frequent delivery use and operations focus.

goal_alignment.criterion: Six-month execution timeline with Chengdu compliance and critical path control
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for a Gantt-style plan hitting site finalization and renovation within 3 months and opening by month 6, including permitting (food business license, hygiene), equipment lead times, hiring/training, pilot soft opening, and risk mitigations on critical path items.

goal_alignment.criterion: Decision-ready structure with quantified KPIs and clear logic flow
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess presence of an executive summary for quick morning review and dashboards/KPIs (daily covers, AOV, COGS%, labor%, rent%, waste%, CAC, LTV, delivery mix), scenario comparisons, and concise reasoning to match the user’s data-driven, efficiency-oriented cognition.

goal_alignment.criterion: Sustainability and eco-friendly operations alignment
