goal_alignment.explanation: Check for biodegradable/recyclable packaging, supplier ESG screening, food waste reduction (donations/discounts near close), and energy-efficient equipment, with messaging that aligns to the user’s growing eco-friendly preferences and potential brand differentiation.

goal_alignment.criterion: Owner role, staffing, and risk safeguards suited to a busy family professional
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan designs a staffing model (store manager, kitchen lead, peak-time part-timers) and reporting cadence that limit owner’s daily time burden, includes insurance and liability coverage, and outlines financial safeguards to protect family cash flow.

goal_alignment.criterion: Targeted marketing to white-collar and fitness channels with measurable ROI
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess specificity of B2B office lunch subscriptions, corporate welfare programs, gym partnerships/challenge meal plans, WeChat group pre-orders, and community operations, with clear offers, budget, and CAC/ROI tracking tailored to the stated target segments.

content_alignment.criterion: Chengdu CBD/office-cluster market and site specificity
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether market research is localized to Chengdu’s key CBDs and office clusters (e.g., rent bands, daytime population, footfall rhythms, competitor mapping in healthy fast food, delivery penetration), aligning to the user’s geographic context and site preference. Depth should match a data-oriented reader, not generic national-level summaries.

content_alignment.criterion: Supply chain and back-of-house operations depth aligned to a supply chain professional
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan includes advanced, concrete operations content (supplier vetting criteria, cold-chain/produce handling, batch prep/lean workflow for lunch peaks, inventory and waste KPIs, demand forecasting for office-heavy weekdays, delivery SLA/packaging integrity) commensurate with the user’s expertise, rather than surface-level kitchen tips.

content_alignment.criterion: Financial model rigor within the ¥500,000 budget and Chengdu cost structure
content_alignment.explanation: Check that the financial section tailors unit economics, rent/deposit/renovation/equipment allocations, delivery commission effects, COGS targets, staffing costs, cash runway, and sensitivity analyses to the stated budget and local cost realities, matching the user’s rational, risk-aware investment approach.

content_alignment.criterion: Menu localization: Sichuan palate meets fitness macros at ¥40–¥60
content_alignment.explanation: Determine whether menu design balances Sichuan-flavor preferences (e.g., low-oil spicy profiles) with low-calorie, high-protein goals, transparent macros/calorie ranges, and ingredient availability in Chengdu—while staying within the specified price band and categories (salad bowls, energy bowls, healthy burgers).

content_alignment.criterion: Risk management and compliance tailored to Chengdu F&B context
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate inclusion of city-relevant licensing/permit pathways, food safety frameworks (e.g., HACCP concepts), insurance, and scenario-based risk considerations (rent volatility, delivery policy changes, food safety incidents) that suit the user’s conservative, risk-managed decision style.

content_alignment.criterion: Channel and segment alignment: white-collar and fitness cohorts in local digital ecosystem
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether marketing and service model content targets Chengdu white-collar and gym-goer behaviors using relevant channels (WeChat OA/miniprogram CRM, Ele.me/Meituan, corporate group orders, partnerships with nearby gyms/office parks), reflecting the user’s app usage and target segments.

content_alignment.criterion: Sustainability and ESG-aligned operational choices
content_alignment.explanation: Check for inclusion of eco-friendly packaging options, waste reduction/ingredient sourcing practices, and supplier ESG considerations that align with the user’s growing focus on environmental responsibility and interest in green products.

content_alignment.criterion: Milestone granularity aligned to a six-month launch and efficiency preference
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the timeline content is structured at a decision-relevant granularity (e.g., gate-based milestones for site selection and renovation within three months) to match the user’s planning-oriented, efficiency-focused style without devolving into step-by-step execution.

presentation_fit.criterion: Decision-first executive summary with key metrics and roadmap
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks whether a one-page executive summary upfront highlights KPIs (e.g., per-customer spend, target daily covers), key assumptions, and a 6-month milestone roadmap so the user can grasp the plan quickly in morning reading.

presentation_fit.criterion: Concise managerial tone and China-localized language
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether the writing is pragmatic, non-hype, and concise, using Mandarin/China-localized expressions and avoiding fluff, matching a supply chain manager’s preference for efficient, results-oriented communication.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology and acronym clarity
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether F&B and O2O terms (e.g., Meituan/Ele.me, SKU, COGS, CBD) are used correctly and briefly defined on first use, with consistent terminology throughout to lower cognitive load.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structural organization and navigability
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks for a clear TOC, numbered headings/subheadings, consistent section ordering, cross-references, and appendices so the reader can jump between market, menu, marketing, and financial sections efficiently.

presentation_fit.criterion: Numerical presentation standards and assumption transparency
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether all figures use consistent RMB symbols, units (㎡, per-cover), aligned tables, and clearly separated assumptions/notes; sensitivity inputs are labeled as assumptions to distinguish facts from modeled values.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visualization suitability and mobile readability
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether charts (e.g., break-even, cost structure), tables, and a Gantt-style timeline are legible on mobile (WeChat/DingTalk), with clear labels, captions, and minimal colors to support screenshot saving.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use of lists, checklists, and matrices to reduce cognitive load
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks for bullet lists, step checklists, and simple matrices (e.g., risk likelihood–impact) that make complex information scannable without changing content, fitting the user’s efficiency-first reading habit.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Financial feasibility: itemized CAPEX/OPEX and cashflow runway within ¥500,000
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the plan provides an itemized budget (deposit/lease prepay, renovation, equipment, licenses, initial inventory, marketing, working capital), monthly OPEX (rent, wages, utilities, delivery commissions), and a cashflow timeline that supports opening in 6 months and at least 3–6 months of runway without exceeding ¥500,000.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Unit economics and pricing viability at ¥40–¥60 per person
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report calculates per-SKU food cost, target food cost ratio (e.g., 28–35%), gross margin, and contribution margin by channel (dine-in vs delivery after 18–26% commissions), proving that the proposed price band can cover costs and support breakeven in Chengdu.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Implementable 6-month roadmap with milestones, dependencies, and buffers
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies a Gantt-style timeline covering site selection, lease, design, permitting, renovation, hiring, onboarding, platform setup, soft opening, and grand opening, including critical path, responsible owners, and 10–20% time buffers for delays.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Site selection decision framework and feasibility for Chengdu CBD/office clusters
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan specifies quantitative criteria (rent per m²/day, required usable area, power/ventilation for hot equipment, foot traffic counts by daypart, office worker density, lease terms), provides a shortlisting and on-site validation method, and shows the rent level fits the budget and breakeven model.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Menu and kitchen operational feasibility (recipes, equipment, throughput)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for standard recipes with yields and prep times for salad/energy bowls and healthy burgers, allergen/nutrition info, batch-prep plans, required equipment list and layout, and throughput estimates that meet lunch peak demand without compromising cost targets.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Compliance and licensing path in Chengdu with steps, durations, and costs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report outlines all required permits (business license, food service permit, fire safety, sanitation, staff health certificates, waste oil disposal contracts), expected processing times, documents, sequencing relative to renovation, and associated fees.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Delivery (O2O) enablement plan with platform integration and SLAs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates concrete steps to launch on Ele.me/Meituan (store setup, menu mapping, pricing, promotions), packaging specs and cost per order, delivery radius and prep-time SLAs, peak-hour capacity planning, and tactics to improve rating/search rank.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Marketing plan with measurable actions, budget, and KPIs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for a channel-by-channel launch and growth plan (gym partnerships, corporate group orders, property management collaborations, WeChat mini-program membership, opening promos), with monthly budgets, CAC targets, conversion and retention KPIs, and A/B testing cycles.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Supply chain and vendor plan with dual sourcing and inventory controls
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report proposes vetted local produce/meat vendors, dual-sourcing for key SKUs, delivery windows, SLAs, price terms, par levels, waste-reduction tactics, and sustainable packaging options with cost impact—structured so Zhou Jun can execute swiftly.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Staffing model and SOPs for operations
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies a staffing plan (roles, headcount per shift, wages/benefits), training schedule (food safety, prep SOPs, service scripts), scheduling for lunch peaks, and daily/weekly checklists enabling consistent operations.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk, sensitivity analysis, and contingency triggers
actionability_practicality.explanation: Ensures the plan stress-tests key variables (rent levels, sales ramp, ingredient price volatility, delivery commission changes), defines break-even under downside cases, and lists actionable contingencies (menu simplification, renegotiating terms, price adjustments, promo shifts) with trigger thresholds.

depth_insight.criterion: Market Demand Sizing and Segment-Specific Insights
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report quantifies addressable demand in the chosen CBD/office clusters for fitness enthusiasts and white-collar workers by daypart (breakfast/lunch/dinner), estimates conversion from footfall/office headcount/gym density, and tests willingness-to-pay against the ¥40–¥60 price band using data (e.g., office population, gym memberships, nearby aggregator order volume). This depth is essential to validate that the concept and pricing can achieve sufficient volume.

depth_insight.criterion: Competitive Landscape Analysis and Defensible Positioning
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report moves beyond listing competitors to map direct/indirect substitutes (healthy QSR chains, salad/bowl brands, convenience stores, meal-prep, delivery-only brands), analyzes aggregator marketplace dynamics (ranking, promo intensity), identifies unmet needs (e.g., macro transparency, speed, customizable bowls, delivery integrity), and articulates a defendable value proposition that justifies a mid-to-high price.

depth_insight.criterion: Location and Site Economics Rigor
depth_insight.explanation: Checks for a data-backed site selection framework that scores candidate sites on rent (and deposit multiples), usable area, footfall, office worker density, proximity to gyms, delivery radius addressability, and rider access; and calculates breakeven daily covers at assumed AOV and gross margin. Insightful work shows how site economics fit within the ¥500,000 budget and the trade-offs between higher rent and higher throughput.

depth_insight.criterion: Menu Engineering and Item-Level Unit Economics
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether menu design ties to target macros/calories, standardized builds, and precise BOM costing per SKU; sets margin targets; considers prep time/line flow; maximizes ingredient cross-utilization; and anticipates seasonality and supplier risks. Deep insight links nutrition promise to achievable gross margins at ¥40–¥60 without compromising speed or quality.

depth_insight.criterion: Operations and Channel (Dine-in + Delivery) Capacity–Economics Alignment
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan quantifies peak-hour throughput, expected dine-in vs delivery mix, station design and staffing needed to hit ticket-time targets, packaging choices and costs, delivery radius/SLAs, and aggregator commission/promo impacts on unit economics. Insight is shown by designing menu/processes to maintain product quality in delivery while protecting margins.

depth_insight.criterion: Financial Model Depth and Sensitivity to Key Drivers
depth_insight.explanation: Examines whether the report presents a detailed allocation of the ¥500,000 (lease deposit, renovation, equipment, initial inventory, working capital), month-by-month cash flow/P&L, unit economics (COGS%, labor%, overhead), breakeven and payback, and sensitivity/scenario tests for rent, demand, commissions, and COGS inflation with contingency actions. This rigor demonstrates realistic viability under uncertainty.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Identification and Mitigation Specific to Concept and Timeline
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report identifies and proactively mitigates concept-specific risks: permitting and lead times, exhaust/grease-trap needs for burgers, food safety and nutrition claims compliance, WFH-driven lunch variability, cold-weather seasonality for salads, fresh produce price volatility, and staffing constraints. Depth is shown by concrete mitigation tactics aligned to budget/timeline.

depth_insight.criterion: Original, High-Value, Implementable Innovations Under Constraints
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan proposes distinctive, feasible tactics that improve odds of success within ¥500,000 and six months, such as corporate lunch subscriptions, gym co-branding, pre-order/pickup shelves for office towers, limited hot line to reduce ventilation capex, ingredient cross-utilization, or delivery menu variants to preserve texture. Valuable originality goes beyond generic marketing tips.

logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the report follows a logical, business-plan-specific sequence (market research → positioning/targeting → menu/pricing → location strategy → operations/staffing → marketing → financial budget/break-even → rollout/milestones) such that each section sets up the next, avoiding circular or out-of-order reasoning.

logical_coherence.criterion: Traceability of Key Decisions to Stated Premises
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that major choices (e.g., CBD location, dine-in + delivery model, ¥40–¥60 price range, chosen menu items) are explicitly linked to earlier premises (target segment, demand patterns, competitive set, budget constraints), ensuring decisions are logically derived rather than asserted.

Assesses whether claims about demand, foot traffic, lunch peak concentration, delivery commission rates, competitor pricing, CBD rent levels, and expected order volume are supported by relevant and appropriate data or references, preventing unsupported leaps in reasoning.

Verifies that key assumptions (e.g., AOV, COGS by item, delivery mix and commission %, rent, wages, hours, conversion rates) are explicitly stated and used consistently across market analysis, operations, and financial modeling, with no hidden or contradictory assumptions.

logical_coherence.criterion: Financial Reasoning Coherence (Unit Economics → P&L/Cash Flow)
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether per-item unit economics (ingredient cost, packaging, labor share, delivery fees) logically roll up to gross margin, volumes to revenue, fixed costs to operating expenses, and then to break-even orders/day and cash runway within the ¥500,000 budget, including capex/fit-out and working capital.

logical_coherence.criterion: Operational–Capacity Alignment with Service Model and Location
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that expected demand (e.g., lunch peaks in office clusters) is consistent with kitchen throughput, staffing levels, equipment capacity, and delivery integration; also that location constraints (e.g., exhaust, grease trap, loading, delivery pickup) are logically incorporated into the operating model.

logical_coherence.criterion: Timeline and Dependency Logic for 6-Month Opening
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the critical path (site selection → lease → permits → fit-out → equipment delivery → hiring/training → soft launch) is identified with realistic durations and dependencies to meet the 3-month site/renovation and 6-month opening targets, including contingency buffers.

logical_coherence.criterion: Smoothness of Section Transitions and Cross-References
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether sections explicitly connect back to prior assumptions and forward to implications (e.g., pricing choices referenced in financials; delivery mix referenced in staffing and budget), avoiding logical jumps and ensuring the reader can follow the reasoning chain end-to-end.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Organization and Navigability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for a clear table of contents, logical sectioning (market research, menu design, marketing, financial budget, timeline), consistent heading hierarchy/numbering, page numbers, and cross-references so readers can quickly locate needed information.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity, Concision, and Professional Tone
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether the text uses precise, unambiguous, and concise language (short sentences, active voice), consistent terminology (e.g., using the same name for menu categories throughout), and correct grammar/spelling to reduce cognitive load.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency and Clarity of Units, Metrics, and Assumptions
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether all figures use consistent units and formats (e.g., ¥ with thousand separators, per-person/per-day clearly labeled), calories in kcal, grams for macros, and whether key assumptions and time bases are explicitly stated next to the numbers or as footnotes.

clarity_readability.criterion: Readability of Financial Tables and Budgets
clarity_readability.explanation: Looks at whether startup budget, P&L, cash flow, and break-even tables are well-structured with clear row/column labels, subtotals/totals highlighted, aligned decimals, negative numbers indicated, and brief footnotes explaining line items for quick comprehension.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Charts and Timeline Visuals
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether charts (e.g., cost breakdown donut, sales mix bars, break-even line chart) and a 6‑month Gantt timeline have clear titles, labeled axes/units, legible legends, consistent color coding, and minimal clutter to convey key points at a glance.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Key Information and Scannability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for executive summary boxes, bullet lists, callouts for critical metrics (budget ¥500k, price ¥40–60, target opening in 6 months), and sufficient white space so readers can scan and retain the main takeaways quickly.

clarity_readability.criterion: Use and Explanation of Technical Terms/Acronyms
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether industry terms and acronyms (e.g., COGS, AOV, CAC, SKU, gross margin) are defined at first use or in a brief glossary, and whether any localized terms are clarified to avoid jargon-induced confusion.

clarity_readability.criterion: Menu Presentation Readability
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether menu items are grouped clearly (salad bowls, energy bowls, healthy burgers), with readable pricing, portion descriptors, concise ingredient lists, nutrition info (kcal, protein), and simple dietary/allergen icons, enabling quick understanding of the offering.

task: I plan to develop a personal media account within the next six months, aiming to build a distinctive personal brand in a niche area, attract a stable audience base, and, if possible, monetize. Please create a systematic creative plan to help me identify my target audience, choose a niche, and define my content value proposition; provide brand tone guidelines; design differentiated content themes and creative expression styles; and prepare a 3-month content topic calendar.Propose creative directions for storytelling, serialized content, and interactive formats to ensure sustained audience interest. Develop a detailed account growth strategy, including posting frequency, platform selection, and multi-platform coordination; suggest specific methods for increasing audience engagement, building a fan community, and iterating content. Provide recommendations for tracking and optimizing key performance metrics, and develop a feasible monetization pathway.Taking into account my time, budget, and skill level, create a reasonable resource allocation plan; provide competitive analysis and differentiation strategies, assess potential risks, and propose mitigation measures. Please recommend excellent domestic and international personal media case studies for reference.

goal_alignment.criterion: Persona-anchored niche positioning and value proposition
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan defines a niche that leverages Xu Yang’s graduate expertise in new media/public opinion and his ethics (social responsibility, non-sensationalism), translating academic insight into clear public value and explicit differentiation from generic social commentary.

goal_alignment.criterion: Platform mix and cadence tailored to usage habits and time constraints
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if the strategy prioritizes Zhihu/Weibo/WeChat (and optionally Bilibili) with platform-specific formats, realistic posting frequency aligned to evening focus time and academic workload, and clear cross-posting/atomization plans to maximize reach with minimal extra effort.

goal_alignment.criterion: Data-driven audience research and KPI iteration leveraging Python skills
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan specifies concrete methods (e.g., mining Zhihu topics, Weibo trend analysis, lightweight surveys), sets platform-specific KPIs (reads, saves, repost/comment ratios, completion rate), and proposes dashboards/A-B tests using Python/low-cost tools to guide iterative refinement.

goal_alignment.criterion: Sustainable content architecture with serialized pillars and interactive formats
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if the plan outlines coherent content pillars (evergreen + timely), serialized series (e.g., algorithm watch, rumor/verification), storytelling angles, and interactive mechanisms (AMAs, polls, reading clubs), plus a feasible 3-month topic calendar that fits a student’s output capacity.

goal_alignment.criterion: Resource and workflow feasibility under student budget and bandwidth
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify that the plan proposes a weekly time budget, batching and templates, low-cost gear/software, lightweight production pipelines, and automation (e.g., scripts for data collection/visualization) to ensure consistent output without overwhelming academic commitments.

goal_alignment.criterion: Brand tone and communication guidelines aligned with analytical, accessible style
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether tone guides a calm, evidence-based voice that explains complex issues in plain language, includes citation and visualization standards, avoids sensationalism, and resonates with his logical communication style and credibility goals.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk management in China’s public-opinion and data-compliance context
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for explicit topic boundaries, fact-checking SOPs, platform policy awareness, privacy and data-scraping compliance, crisis response playbooks, and content review processes that mitigate reputational and platform risks.

goal_alignment.criterion: Engagement and community-building tailored to rational discourse
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan proposes community mechanisms (e.g., Zhihu live Q&A, WeChat study groups with rules, reading clubs, campus micro-events) that suit an audience seeking thoughtful discussion rather than pure entertainment.

goal_alignment.criterion: Monetization pathway aligned with academic credibility and low risk tolerance
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess if monetization options (paid columns, digital handbooks/templates, workshops/webinars, book affiliate links, tipping) are phased to audience growth, protect credibility, and avoid controversial sponsorships or high-risk tactics.

goal_alignment.criterion: Relevant competitive mapping and actionable differentiation insights
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether domestic and international reference cases (e.g., China-focused media analysis creators and global explainers) are selected for relevance, and whether the plan extracts concrete practices and a distinct differentiation angle (methodology transparency, data viz standards).

content_alignment.criterion: Niche and Value Proposition Fit with New Media/Public Opinion Expertise
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the proposed niche and content value proposition explicitly leverage Xu Yang’s academic domain (media literacy, public opinion analysis, platform governance, misinformation, tech-society) instead of generic topics, ensuring the content core aligns with his strongest interests and credibility base.

content_alignment.criterion: Analytical Depth and Evidence Orientation
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content plan prioritizes evidence-backed analysis (data, citations, case studies) and method transparency suitable for a data-literate graduate student, avoiding superficial hot-take commentary.

content_alignment.criterion: Platform–Format Alignment with Usage Habits
content_alignment.explanation: Check if topic types and formats are matched to Weibo (timely threads), Zhihu (long-form Q&A/essays), WeChat (deep-dive newsletters), and possibly Bilibili (lecture/documentary-style explainers), reflecting Xu Yang’s platform habits and audience consumption patterns.

content_alignment.criterion: Target Audience Definition Reflecting Academic–Professional Circles
content_alignment.explanation: Determine whether the plan precisely targets peers and professionals interested in media/public opinion (students, journalists, policy/PR/tech folks) and aligns content depth and jargon to their knowledge level.

content_alignment.criterion: Brand Tone and Ethical Stance Consistency
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the recommended tone emphasizes rationality, social responsibility, rule-awareness, and non-sensationalism, matching Xu Yang’s values and enhancing trust with a critically minded audience.

content_alignment.criterion: Topic Architecture and 3-Month Calendar Alignment with Interests
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content themes and calendar prioritize social issues, tech–society intersections, media literacy, and public opinion case analyses, while allowing occasional personal angles (Beijing cultural context, coffee, outdoor) to humanize the brand without diluting the core.

content_alignment.criterion: Interactive and Community Formats Suited to Rational Discourse
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the plan proposes interactions that fit Xu Yang’s communication style and audience (AMAs with experts, structured debates, polls tied to public opinion data, reading groups), avoiding clickbait engagement tactics.

content_alignment.criterion: Metrics and Optimization Plan Leveraging Data Skills
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether suggested KPIs and analysis methods go beyond vanity metrics to include saves/shares, dwell time, comment quality/sentiment, topic network analysis, and basic Python workflows that align with Xu Yang’s analytical strengths.

content_alignment.criterion: Monetization Pathways Aligned with Low-Risk, Credibility-First Profile
content_alignment.explanation: Determine whether monetization options (paid research briefs/newsletters, workshops, community memberships, consulting, book/tool affiliates) preserve academic integrity and suit a conservative risk appetite, avoiding misaligned sponsorships.

content_alignment.criterion: Localization and Regulatory/Contextual Sensitivity
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether examples, case references, and content choices reflect Chinese platform norms, policy context, and Beijing vantage, demonstrating sensitivity to domestic discourse and compliance considerations.

content_alignment.criterion: Case Study Relevance and Transferability
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether recommended domestic/international personal media cases come from adjacent domains (data journalism, media literacy, public opinion analysts) with clear takeaways applicable to Xu Yang’s niche.

presentation_fit.criterion: Analytical, evidence-based tone (avoid hype and vague marketing language)
presentation_fit.explanation: The report should read like a rigorous plan: neutral, precise, and supported by data or citations where claims are made, fitting Xu Yang’s academic background and preference for factual, logical reasoning.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology calibration with brief first-use definitions of frameworks/acronyms
presentation_fit.explanation: Use domain-correct growth and branding terms (e.g., STP, AARRR, ICP, JTBD, KPI/OKR) and provide concise parenthetical explanations on first use to maintain readability without oversimplifying for a graduate-level reader.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structured organization and logical flow tailored to the multi-part task
presentation_fit.explanation: A clear hierarchy (executive summary → objectives → audience/niche/value → brand tone → content pillars → 3-month calendar → growth/engagement → measurement → monetization → resources → competition → risks → cases), numbered headings, and cross-references help navigate a long, complex plan.

presentation_fit.criterion: Scannability and modularity for mobile reading and screenshotting
presentation_fit.explanation: Use bullets, numbered steps, short paragraphs, and clearly labeled callouts (e.g., ‘Decision’, ‘Rationale’, ‘Checklist’) to support evening reading habits and easy capture of key points.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use of planning-friendly visual frameworks and text-friendly visuals
presentation_fit.explanation: Include matrices (Platform × Content Pillars), timelines/Gantt-style roadmaps, content calendars, and funnels presented in simple tables/ASCII so they are legible without rich formatting and align with his data/tool proficiency.

presentation_fit.criterion: Localization of presentation to China’s platform ecology and conventions
presentation_fit.explanation: Name and format platform-specific sections (WeChat, Weibo, Zhihu, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, Douyin), use RMB and local date formats, and provide brief context when citing international cases to reduce cognitive friction.

presentation_fit.criterion: Clarity and consistency in KPI/metric displays
presentation_fit.explanation: Metrics should be presented with labels, definitions, formulas, units (e.g., %, RMB), and baseline vs. target columns, reflecting a dashboard-like presentation that suits his data-oriented habits.

presentation_fit.criterion: Presence of an executive summary and section-level micro-summaries
presentation_fit.explanation: A top-level TL;DR and brief section summaries provide quick orientation and help align with his efficiency-focused weekly planning routine.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Sustainable workload and skill-fit feasibility
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the plan matches Xu Yang’s weekly time budget and skills (writing/data analysis), with explicit time estimates per content type, weekly hour targets, and SOPs that avoid heavy video editing or costly production. A plan he can sustain alongside graduate studies is essential for execution.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Executable phased roadmap and content calendar specificity
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for a step-by-step 6‑month roadmap broken into phases (setup, launch, optimize), plus a concrete 3‑month topic calendar with titles, formats, hooks, and CTAs. Includes checklists/templates so he can start producing immediately without ambiguity.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Platform-specific tactics and cross-platform repurposing clarity
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether posting frequency, best content formats, posting windows, and distribution tactics are clearly specified for Zhihu, Weibo, WeChat (and optional Bilibili), including repurposing recipes (e.g., long-form to threads/Q&A) to maximize output per unit effort.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Engagement and community-building SOPs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for concrete interactive formats (AMAs, polls, comment prompts), collaboration pipelines, and WeChat group operations (onboarding rules, content cadence, moderation, growth loops) with schedules and responsibilities to drive retention and referrals.

actionability_practicality.criterion: KPI tracking, analysis workflow, and iteration cadence
actionability_practicality.explanation: Requires defined early-stage KPIs (e.g., saves/shares, completion rate, follow conversion), realistic benchmarks, data collection methods, tool stack (native analytics + spreadsheets/Python), weekly/biweekly analysis rituals, A/B tests, and decision thresholds for pivots.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Resource and budget plan with concrete costs and time allocation
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies a detailed list of tools, equipment, and subscriptions with costs and free alternatives, plus a weekly time allocation and contingency buffer, aligning to a frugal student budget and ensuring the plan is affordable and resourced.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Monetization pathway staged feasibility and compliance
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether monetization is sequenced by audience maturity (e.g., pilot paid briefings, digital products, workshops), includes pricing ranges, validation steps (surveys/preorders), minimal upfront cost, and compliance with platform/university norms.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk and compliance mitigation for sensitive topics and operations
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for concrete guardrails for public opinion topics (content boundaries, citation standards, pre‑publish checklists), platform policy awareness, crisis response playbook, harassment management, backups, and workload burnout prevention.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Differentiation playbook with actionable positioning and examples
actionability_practicality.explanation: Ensures the report translates brand positioning and tone into practical rules (messaging pillars, dos/don’ts, example headlines and openings) that Xu Yang can apply consistently to stand out without guesswork.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Transferable case studies with explicit application steps
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether domestic/international cases are accompanied by distilled tactics and a step-by-step mapping of how Xu Yang can adapt them to his niche, avoiding superficial inspiration-only references.

depth_insight.criterion: Audience Insight and Niche Selection Rigor
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the plan uncovers non-obvious audience segments, pain points, motivations, behaviors, and willingness to pay using evidence (e.g., user interviews, platform analytics, search/social signals) and selects a niche with explicit criteria (size, growth, saturation, monetization fit), articulating why this niche is winnable.

depth_insight.criterion: Distinctive Content Value Proposition and Positioning
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan articulates a sharp, defensible value proposition tied to audience jobs-to-be-done and the creator’s unique assets or unfair advantages, including explicit trade-offs (what not to cover) that sharpen differentiation and increase perceived value.

depth_insight.criterion: Competitive Landscape Analysis and White-space Identification
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for depth in mapping direct/adjacent competitors’ themes, formats, engagement patterns, and monetization, extracting actionable white-space opportunities and counter-positioning strategies rather than merely listing competitors.

depth_insight.criterion: Content Strategy Depth Across the Audience Journey
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether content themes, storytelling/serialization, and interactive formats are designed to move users from discovery to retention and conversion, with a 3-month calendar that reflects strategic sequencing, seasonality, testing slots, and content atomization/repurposing logic.

depth_insight.criterion: Platform Selection and Growth Mechanism Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses understanding of platform–audience fit and growth levers (format economics, recommendation algorithms, watch-time/CTR/saves signals), rationale for posting frequency, and multi-platform laddering (hub–spoke, canonical vs derivative content) to compound reach.

depth_insight.criterion: Engagement and Community-building Mechanics
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates depth in designing interaction loops (rituals, prompts, UGC, challenges), progression from lurker to advocate, moderation and governance, and mechanisms that create a community flywheel that sustains interest beyond one-off content.

depth_insight.criterion: KPI Framework and Learning Loop Design
depth_insight.explanation: Examines whether a coherent North Star metric and input metrics are chosen for the stage, with instrumentation plans (cohorts, retention, quality signals), hypothesis-driven experiments (A/B, content pilots), and clear decision rules for iteration and scaling.

depth_insight.criterion: Monetization Path Feasibility and Unit Economics
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses a phased monetization roadmap aligned with trust depth (ads/affiliates → products/services → community/education), including pricing hypotheses, CAC/LTV considerations, capacity constraints, and platform-dependency hedges.

depth_insight.criterion: Resource–Budget–Skill Allocation Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates realism and leverage in mapping time, budget, and skills to priorities (e.g., batching, templates, outsourcing/automation), with ROI-based prioritization and an operational pipeline that makes the plan executable within constraints.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Foresight and Mitigation Strategies
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for specific identification of platform/policy shifts, burnout, legal/IP, brand safety, and growth stall risks, with concrete hedges (owned channels, content buffer, diversification, legal templates) and trigger-based contingency plans.

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and Forward-looking Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards novel, contrarian, or cross-domain creative directions; trend sensing; scenario planning; and insights that go beyond best practices to create a durable edge or early-mover advantage.

logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the report progresses in a logical order from objectives → audience and niche → value proposition → brand tone → content pillars/formats → 3-month calendar → growth/distribution → engagement/community → KPIs/iteration → monetization → resources → risks/case studies. Sound sequencing ensures each decision is built on prior premises rather than introduced ad hoc.

Checks that key choices (e.g., niche selection, platform mix, posting cadence, monetization path) explicitly cite assumptions or evidence such as audience demographics, platform benchmarks, competitive gaps, or resource estimates. Traceability prevents arbitrary recommendations and anchors reasoning in stated premises.

Evaluates internal consistency across components—for example, whether the chosen audience and pain points logically inform the niche and value proposition; whether brand tone and content themes serve that proposition; and whether monetization fits audience needs and content format. Misalignments indicate logical contradictions.

logical_coherence.criterion: Causal Mechanism from Strategy to Outcomes (Acquisition–Engagement–Conversion Funnel)
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the plan articulates clear cause–effect pathways linking actions to outcomes (e.g., storytelling hooks → watch time → follows; newsletter lead magnets → email list growth → product sales). Without explicit mechanisms, the move from tactics to goals is speculative.

logical_coherence.criterion: KPI Hierarchy and Actionable Decision Rules
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks for a logical metric structure (north-star, input/process metrics) and decision rules that tie metric movements to specific actions (e.g., if 7-day retention < X, adjust content pillar mix; if conversion < Y, test CTA variants). This ensures iterative reasoning is disciplined, not arbitrary.

logical_coherence.criterion: Calendar Instantiation of Strategy (Topics → Pillars → Objectives → Cadence)
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the 3-month topic calendar concretely maps strategic pillars to weekly themes, objectives, and posting frequency, with phased logic (e.g., Month 1 test, Month 2 scale, Month 3 optimize). This tests the logical bridge from strategy to executable schedule.

logical_coherence.criterion: Multi-Platform Role Definition and Coordination Logic
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether each platform has a defined role in the funnel (e.g., discovery vs. depth vs. conversion), with repurposing flows and cross-promotion rules spelled out. This ensures distribution choices are functionally justified, not duplicative or conflicting.

Checks whether the plan translates constraints into clear prioritization and scope (e.g., batch production to fit time, template use for skill limits, staged monetization for budget). Logical feasibility prevents internally inconsistent workloads.

logical_coherence.criterion: Risk Causality and Targeted Mitigation Logic
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether risks are linked to specific causes (e.g., algorithm volatility → distribution risk) and whether mitigations address those causes (e.g., email list hedge, content diversification) with triggers for activation. This preserves logical integrity under uncertainty.

logical_coherence.criterion: Transitional Coherence and Forward Linkages Between Sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether sections explicitly carry forward conclusions (e.g., 'Given Persona A prefers short video on Platform X, we set Y posting cadence and Z content style'), avoiding logical jumps. Smooth transitions make the reasoning chain continuous and auditably connected.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Signposting and Mapping to Requested Deliverables
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear heading hierarchy and table of contents, and explicitly labels sections to match each item the user requested (e.g., Target Audience, Niche, Value Proposition, Brand Tone, Content Themes/Styles, 3-Month Content Calendar, Storytelling/Serialization/Interaction, Growth Strategy, Engagement/Community, KPI Tracking, Monetization, Resource Allocation, Competitive Analysis, Risks/Mitigations, Case Studies). This reduces search effort and improves navigability.

clarity_readability.criterion: Formatting and Layout Cleanliness
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses typography, spacing, list usage, and alignment; consistent heading levels; readable line length; adequate white space; and clear section breaks. A clean, consistent layout improves visual comfort and reduces cognitive load in a long, operational plan.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity and Action-Oriented Instructions
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether sentences are concise, unambiguous, and use active voice and precise verbs for steps (e.g., 'Define ICP in 3 bullets' vs. vague wording). Clear, directive phrasing makes execution easier without requiring interpretation.

clarity_readability.criterion: Terminology and Acronym Handling
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether platform names (e.g., Douyin/TikTok, Xiaohongshu/RED), marketing acronyms (KPI, CTR, CPM, LTV, CAC), and frameworks are expanded and defined on first use and used consistently thereafter. Proper term handling prevents confusion for mixed-experience readers.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Visuals, Tables, and Calendars
clarity_readability.explanation: Reviews whether key artifacts (e.g., 3‑month content calendar, cross‑platform coordination matrix, KPI dashboard, growth funnel, timeline) are well-labeled, use appropriate chart types, include legends/notes, and are easily scannable. Visual clarity is critical for planning and ongoing execution.

clarity_readability.criterion: Scannability and Highlighting of Key Information
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates the use of summaries, bullet lists, step checklists, callout boxes (e.g., 'First 30 Days'), and visual emphasis (bold/labels) for critical points, deadlines, and CTAs. Good scannability helps readers quickly grasp and act on essentials.

clarity_readability.criterion: Style and Notation Consistency
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks consistent use of terms, date/currency formats, numbering, and capitalization (e.g., consistent platform names, recurring section labels). Consistency reduces reader confusion across sections and artifacts.

clarity_readability.criterion: Accessibility and Localization Clarity
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses readable font sizes, color contrast, clear figure captions/alt text, and localization notes (e.g., bilingual platform names, unit/currency clarifications). Accessibility and localization broaden comprehension across audiences and contexts.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity of Case Study References and Citations
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether domestic/international case studies are cited with consistent fields (platform, handle/name, region, link) and a one‑line descriptor of relevance. Clear citations allow quick verification and further learning.

task: I plan to develop a personal media account within the next six months, aiming to build a distinctive personal brand in a niche area, attract a stable audience base, and, if possible, monetize. Please create a systematic creative plan to help me identify my target audience, choose a niche, and define my content value proposition; provide brand tone guidelines; design differentiated content themes and creative expression styles; and prepare a 3-month content topic calendar.Propose creative directions for storytelling, serialized content, and interactive formats to ensure sustained audience interest. Develop a detailed account growth strategy, including posting frequency, platform selection, and multi-platform coordination; suggest specific methods for increasing audience engagement, building a fan community, and iterating content. Provide recommendations for tracking and optimizing key performance metrics, and develop a feasible monetization pathway.Taking into account my time, budget, and skill level, create a reasonable resource allocation plan; provide competitive analysis and differentiation strategies, assess potential risks, and propose mitigation measures. Please recommend excellent domestic and international personal media case studies for reference.

goal_alignment.criterion: Persona-anchored niche, audience, and value proposition fit
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan proposes a niche that explicitly leverages Zhao Li’s Advertising/New Media and Psychology background, product-ops mindset, aesthetic skills, and interests (healthy living/vegetarian, yoga/Pilates, art, cat, Beijing urban life). It should define a clear target audience (e.g., value-conscious urban women seeking low-stress, behavioral-science-backed creative wellness) and a crisp value proposition that differentiates her from generic lifestyle creators.

goal_alignment.criterion: Feasible, low-stress execution design aligned to schedule and capacity
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan operationalizes a cadence that fits her 7–9 AM productivity window, weekday job, and weekend batching; includes content banking, buffer weeks, and burnout safeguards; and sets realistic workload given her low stress tolerance and gym routine. It should show phased milestones across the 6-month horizon.

goal_alignment.criterion: Platform selection and multi-platform coordination tailored to her usage
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if Xiaohongshu and Weibo are prioritized with platform-native formats (save-worthy cards, carousels, threads), while podcast/Bilibili are secondarily fed via smart repurposing pipelines; minimal focus on TikTok/Douyin consistent with her decreased use. Cross-platform scheduling, asset templates, and linkages should be specified.

goal_alignment.criterion: Brand tone and voice congruent with personality and emotional needs
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if the brand tone is warm, supportive, aesthetically sensitive (influenced by Nanjing cultural taste), rational and non-hustle, and includes gentle encouragement—matching her creative yet low-stress profile and need for emotional support. Incorporating authentic motifs (e.g., ‘Meimei’ the cat) without overuse strengthens resonance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Differentiated content pillars leveraging her unique intersections
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether content pillars combine behavioral science, creative wellness, and urban-lifestyle practicality, with clear guardrails to avoid niche sprawl. The plan should articulate what she will not cover and how pet/lifestyle/travel elements support, not dilute, the core proposition.

goal_alignment.criterion: Actionable 3-month content calendar with serialization and storytelling
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate the specificity of a 12-week calendar (posting frequency per platform, topic titles, hooks, seasonal/Beijing-relevant angles), serialized arcs (e.g., habit-building series), and storytelling devices designed to drive saves/comments. The schedule must align with her time blocks and include interactive touchpoints.

goal_alignment.criterion: Engagement and community-building using behavioral design
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for psychologically sound engagement mechanisms (low-pressure challenges, accountability check-ins, social proof, prompts) that reflect her Psychology background and create a safe, encouraging environment. Offline-light options (cafes/yoga studios) within her 5 km radius can be proposed without overcommitting.

goal_alignment.criterion: Product-ops-grade metrics, feedback loops, and iteration
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether KPIs are platform-specific (e.g., Xiaohongshu save/collect rate, cover CTR, completion; Weibo engagement depth; podcast retention; Bilibili retention), tied to hypotheses, and supported by A/B tests, content audits, and a backlog/roadmap—appealing to her product ops mindset.

goal_alignment.criterion: Phased, low-risk monetization path aligned to assets and audience
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate a stepwise monetization plan starting with audience trust (no hard sells), then affiliates aligned with her lifestyle (healthy foods/yoga gear), digital products (habit trackers, templates, art prints), and small-group workshops/consults—considering her risk aversion and potential employer policies.

goal_alignment.criterion: Resource allocation realism (budget, tools, time, workflows)
goal_alignment.explanation: Check that the plan recommends a lean tool stack (lighting/mic/gimbal, Canva/Notion), time-blocking for scripting/production/editing, light outsourcing if needed, and automation to fit her frugal spending and schedule—showing cost ranges and ROI expectations.

goal_alignment.criterion: Competitor/case study relevance and concrete differentiation
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether domestic and international references are similar in niche and stage (creative wellness/productivity/vegetarian lifestyle), and whether the plan extracts transferable tactics and articulates specific differentiation for Zhao Li rather than citing mismatched mega-influencers.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk identification and mitigation tailored to her constraints
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if the plan addresses creator burnout, platform policy changes, privacy, employer conflicts, and family expectations for stability. Mitigations should include a content bank, contingency cadence, clear topical boundaries (e.g., avoiding employer-sensitive topics), and mental health safeguards.

content_alignment.criterion: Niche–Value Proposition Anchored in Zhao Li’s Assets and Interests
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan proposes a niche and clear value proposition that authentically integrates her strengths (advertising/new media + psychology + product ops), existing creative assets (illustration/hand-drawn), and genuine interests (healthy living, vegetarianism, yoga/pilates, mental health, sustainability, aesthetics, pet companionship), rather than generic creator niches.

content_alignment.criterion: Platform-Specific Alignment with Actual Usage (Xiaohongshu, Weibo, Podcasts/Long-Video) over Douyin
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content strategy prioritizes and tailors to her primary platforms (e.g., Xiaohongshu note SEO, tags, save-worthy carousels; Weibo topic mechanics; Bilibili/YouTube long video; Ximalaya/Apple Podcasts), and de-emphasizes Douyin per her current behavior, ensuring content is chosen and packaged for the right discovery mechanics.

content_alignment.criterion: Depth Calibrated to Professional Background (Advanced Frameworks and Psych-Informed Content)
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether the report uses advanced audience/strategy frameworks (JTBD, segmentation, value proposition canvas), funnel/retention metrics, and experiment design, plus psychology principles (habit formation, cognitive load, intrinsic motivation) appropriate for someone trained in new media and user behavior—avoiding beginner-only content.

content_alignment.criterion: Monetization Pathways Matched to Skills, Values, and Risk Appetite
content_alignment.explanation: Determine whether monetization content favors low-risk, skills- and values-aligned options (digital illustration packs, Notion trackers, print-on-demand, workshops/courses, ethical affiliate with organic/fitness brands, selective sponsorships) and avoids inventory-heavy, high-risk models inconsistent with her profile.

content_alignment.criterion: Feasible Content Scope Given Time, Budget, and Stress Tolerance
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether content types and complexity are scoped for her morning windows and weekends, allow batching/repurposing, and minimize gear costs—so that the content plan is sustainable and does not overwhelm her low stress tolerance.

content_alignment.criterion: Wellness-Integrated Content Themes (Mental Health, Morning Routines, Gentle Habits)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether content pillars include mental well-being, morning routines, yoga/pilates journeys, vegetarian/healthy eating experiments, and sustainable living—topics she consistently consumes and needs, thus boosting personal resonance.

content_alignment.criterion: Format Selection Fit (Vlogs, Creative Shorts/Carousels, Micro-Podcasts/Long-Form)
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether the plan selects content forms she prefers and consumes (creative Vlogs, Xiaohongshu carousels/long images, short creative videos, micro-podcast episodes, occasional long-form deep dives) to match her habits and attention patterns.

content_alignment.criterion: Community and Interaction Concepts Aligned to Her Behaviors
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether serialized and interactive content leverages her participation in creative communities: co-creation prompts, 21-day yoga/vegetarian challenges, UGC around her cat ‘Meimei,’ and light offline touchpoints in cafes/yoga studios that align with her lifestyle.

content_alignment.criterion: Localization to Beijing/Nanjing and Chinese Platform Ecosystem
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether content choices reflect local realities (Beijing venues, vegetarian spots, yoga studios; Nanjing cultural aesthetics), domestic platform norms, and brand ecosystems—enhancing authenticity for a China-based audience.

content_alignment.criterion: Relevance and Transferability of Case Studies
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether domestic and international examples come from adjacent niches and similar creator profiles (female-led healthy/creative lifestyle, illustration, psychology-informed creators on Xiaohongshu/Weibo/Bilibili/Podcast), with clear takeaways mapped to her context.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tone and voice fit: professional, supportive, and non-hype
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks whether the report uses clear, professional language with an encouraging, low-pressure tone (suited to a young internet ops professional with low stress tolerance), avoiding salesy hype and alarmist wording.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structural mapping to the task blueprint
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether the report’s sectioning mirrors the requested components (target audience, niche, value proposition, brand tone, themes, 3‑month calendar, storytelling/serialization/interaction, growth strategy, engagement/community/iteration, KPI tracking, monetization, resources, competition, risks, cases) with a navigable TOC and consistent headings.

presentation_fit.criterion: Scannability and cognitive load management
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates use of bullet lists, numbered steps, short paragraphs, emphasized key phrases, section summaries, and estimated reading time to enable fast morning consumption and reduce overwhelm.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology calibration and micro-definitions
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks that industry acronyms/terms (e.g., CAC, LTV, CTR, funnel) are used appropriately for her level, with brief parenthetical definitions and minimal buzzword stuffing; Chinese-first phrasing with English terms in parentheses when helpful.

presentation_fit.criterion: Quality and readability of visual planning aids
presentation_fit.explanation: Reviews whether calendars, timelines, canvases (e.g., positioning canvas), funnels, and content matrices are presented with clear labels, legends, and ‘how to read’ notes, and remain legible when screenshotted.

presentation_fit.criterion: Platform-specific formatting and labeling
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether recommendations are separated and labeled by platform (e.g., Xiaohongshu, Weibo, Bilibili, podcast) with native format cues (card-style bullets, thread outlines, episode rundown) and platform UI terms that match Chinese usage.

presentation_fit.criterion: KPI and analytics presentation clarity
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates if metrics are organized in clean tables with definitions, simple formulas, measurement cadence, and example rows, making it easy to grasp tracking at a glance without heavy analytics prose.

presentation_fit.criterion: Presence and usability of templates/worksheets
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks for fill-in-the-blank templates (persona sheet, content pillars, posting schedule) designed as copyable, compact modules with clear field labels and instructions.

presentation_fit.criterion: Consistency and clarity in case study formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether domestic and international cases are presented in a uniform schema (platform, niche, format pattern, why it works, transferable takeaway) with clear source labeling/handles for easy reference.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Phased 6-month roadmap with milestones and weekly time plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report defines clear monthly/biweekly milestones (e.g., follower/engagement targets, content volume) and converts them into an achievable weekly schedule aligned to a full-time job and her morning/weekend time windows, so she can start immediately without re-planning.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Resource and budget feasibility (tools, outsourcing, ad spend) with explicit costs and time allocation
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the plan lists a concrete tool stack (free/low-cost options), optional micro-budgets for ads or freelancers, expected costs, and when to outsource based on workload—fitting her frugal mindset and risk-averse profile while keeping production sustainable.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Executable 3-month content calendar (topics, formats, hooks, CTAs, prep requirements)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the calendar specifies publish-ready topics, formats (short video, carousel, audio), hooks, calls-to-action, and required prep (assets, research) so she can produce without ambiguity or extra planning each week.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Platform strategy and publishing plan with multi-platform repurposing SOP
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies that the report selects primary platforms (e.g., Xiaohongshu/Weibo with possible podcast/long-form), sets posting frequency and optimal time slots that match her routine, and provides a step-by-step repurposing workflow to reduce workload and maximize reach.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Production pipeline and SOPs (batching, scripting, templates, turnaround times)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines whether the plan supplies concrete SOPs for ideation-to-posting (batching on weekends, script/storyboard templates, editing checklists, file naming/storage) with realistic time estimates to lower stress and ensure consistency.

actionability_practicality.criterion: KPI framework with numeric targets, tools, review cadence, and optimization playbook
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for defined metrics per platform (e.g., save rate, completion rate), baseline/target values, analytics tools, weekly/biweekly review cadences, and specific optimization tactics (A/B tests, hook iterations) with pivot rules when targets are missed.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Feasible, staged monetization pathway with thresholds and revenue expectations
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report outlines progressive monetization (e.g., digital goods/commissions, affiliate, brand deals) with follower/engagement thresholds, basic revenue models, compliance notes, and preparatory steps, aligned to her current skills (art/hand-drawn) and risk appetite.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Community-building and engagement playbook with cadences and interaction SOPs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan specifies recurring interactive formats (Q&A, polls, challenges), response times, moderation rules, and fan group setup to grow a loyal audience with manageable effort.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk management and contingency plans tailored to stress tolerance and schedule
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for concrete mitigations for burnout (content batching buffers, rest weeks), content drought (evergreen backlog), platform policy changes, and schedule disruptions (travel/home visits), with explicit triggers to activate contingencies.

depth_insight.criterion: Granular Audience Insight and Jobs-to-be-Done
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report goes beyond demographics to articulate psychographics, motivations, pain points, JTBD, willingness to pay, and consumption habits, revealing specific unmet/underserved needs the brand can own.

depth_insight.criterion: Niche Selection Rigor and Defensibility
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether niche choice is justified via market sizing, competition intensity, search/trend signals, platform fit, and creator’s unfair advantages, identifying clear white-space and trade-offs.

depth_insight.criterion: Value Proposition Differentiation and Benefit Ladder
depth_insight.explanation: Checks if the report defines a unique, outcome-oriented promise supported by reasons-to-believe and a benefit ladder that clearly distinguishes from competitors and is testable.

depth_insight.criterion: Platform-specific Strategy and Algorithmic Levers
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates depth of insight into each platform’s format economics (hook/retention dynamics, metadata, cadence, distribution features) and multi-platform coordination to compound reach.

depth_insight.criterion: Content System Depth: Pillars, Serialization, and Funnel Mapping
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether content pillars, serialized arcs, and the 3-month calendar are derived from audience needs and funnel goals (TOFU/MOFU/BOFU), balancing evergreen vs. reactive content to build habit.

depth_insight.criterion: Engagement and Community Design via Behavioral Science
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for insight-driven mechanisms (rituals, identity cues, reciprocity, UGC prompts, cohorts) that convert passive viewers to active community members and sustain participation.

depth_insight.criterion: KPI Framework and Learning Agenda
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses the selection of north-star and diagnostic metrics (e.g., hook rate, AVD, retention, cohort activity), the use of benchmarks, and a clear experimentation plan to iterate hypotheses.

depth_insight.criterion: Monetization Pathway Viability and Unit Economics
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether monetization options are audience–value aligned, phased by readiness triggers, and supported by funnel math/unit economics (pricing, conversion rates, CAC proxies, breakeven).

depth_insight.criterion: Resource-constrained Planning and ROI Prioritization
depth_insight.explanation: Checks realism and insight in allocating time/budget/skills, including batching, workflow design, outsource-vs-DIY trade-offs, and prioritization by marginal ROI.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Assessment and Contingency Triggers
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses identification of key risks (platform policy, volatility, fatigue, legal/compliance) and pre-defined leading indicators, pivot criteria, and mitigation playbooks.

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and Practical Novelty of Insights
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the presence of non-obvious, actionable ideas or contrarian but well-supported approaches that materially improve outcomes beyond generic best practices.

depth_insight.criterion: Case Study Synthesis and Transferability
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether domestic/international cases are analyzed for underlying mechanisms and translated into context-relevant principles rather than superficial imitation.

logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether objectives (brand, audience, monetization) logically cascade into strategies (niche focus, content pillars, platform choices) and then into concrete tactics (calendar, posting cadence, engagement mechanisms), with explicit linkage so each tactic references the strategy and objective it serves.

Checks that time, budget, skills, and platform constraints are stated upfront and consistently referenced in downstream decisions (e.g., posting frequency, format complexity), avoiding hidden assumptions that break the reasoning chain.

Evaluates whether the audience definition leads logically to niche selection and a clear value proposition (who/what need/unique benefit), using explicit decision criteria and avoiding leaps from persona to claims about differentiation.

Assesses whether content pillars and creative expressions (storytelling, serialization, interactive formats) logically derive from the value proposition and map to a 3‑month calendar, ensuring topics ladder back to pillars and series arcs progress coherently.

logical_coherence.criterion: Platform Selection and Growth Strategy Logic
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether platform choices, posting frequency, and multi‑platform coordination are justified by audience distribution, platform mechanics, and content format fit, with clear sequencing/repurposing logic across channels.

Checks that assertions about audience behavior, platform algorithms, cadence norms, or monetization readiness are supported by cited benchmarks, case studies, or test results, ensuring decisions rest on substantiated premises.

logical_coherence.criterion: Metrics Framework and Feedback Loop Integration
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether KPIs map to objectives (awareness, engagement, retention, conversion), include measurement methods and thresholds, and feed into defined iteration routines that adjust content, cadence, or platform tactics.

Evaluates internal consistency between workload implied by the plan and stated time/budget/skills, and whether dependencies (tools, setup steps) are recognized and ordered logically, avoiding contradictory requirements.

Checks that proposed monetization methods match audience maturity and content trust, with clear milestones/triggers for when to introduce each revenue stream, preventing premature or misaligned monetization.

Assesses whether identified risks are linked to specific mitigations and contingency triggers (what signal prompts which action), ensuring a logical response chain rather than a disconnected risk list.

Evaluates whether sections explicitly reference preceding decisions (e.g., 'based on the audience persona...') to connect steps and avoid logical jumps, guiding the reader through the reasoning flow across the report.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Scannability and Navigation Aids
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear heading hierarchy, table of contents, section numbering, and consistent section labels that map to the user’s requested components (e.g., target audience, niche, calendar, KPIs). Effective cross-references (e.g., “see Section 6 KPI Dashboard”) let readers quickly find relevant parts.

clarity_readability.criterion: Formatting and Layout Aesthetics
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses readability features such as appropriate paragraph length, bullet lists for steps, adequate white space, consistent typography, and clean tables. A tidy layout lowers cognitive load, makes long plans less daunting, and supports quick scanning.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity, Concision, and Instructional Tone
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether instructions are written in plain, unambiguous language with active voice and concise sentences (e.g., “Post 3x/week on TikTok” rather than vague phrasing). Clear directives improve immediate executability without interpreting intent.

clarity_readability.criterion: Terminology and Acronym Management
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that technical terms and acronyms (e.g., CTR, ER, CAC, LTV, A/B test, native post vs. cross-post) are defined at first use or listed in a glossary, and that terms are used consistently across sections and platforms to avoid confusion.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Visualizations for Plans and Metrics
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether calendars, roadmaps, matrices (platform × content type × frequency), funnels, and KPI dashboards have clear labels, legends, units, and timeframes; are easy to read on screen; and are explicitly referenced and explained in the text.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Priorities, Milestones, and Key Takeaways
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses the use of summaries, callouts, and milestone markers (e.g., Month 1 setup, Month 2 optimization) so readers can grasp priorities and deadlines at a glance, aiding planning and follow-through.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency in Labels, Units, and Date/Time Formats
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks consistent use of platform names, posting frequency units (per day/week), time zones, date formats, number formats, and currencies; this prevents misinterpretation in scheduling, budgeting, and KPI tracking.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity and Usability of Templates, Checklists, and Examples
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether provided templates (e.g., content calendar, KPI tracker, resource allocation, risk register) include clear field names, brief instructions, and example entries, making them immediately usable without guesswork.

clarity_readability.criterion: Accessibility and Device Readability
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether charts avoid color-only encoding, have sufficient contrast, include alt-text/notes, and whether layouts remain readable on mobile and when printed, ensuring broad accessibility.

task: I plan to develop a personal media account within the next six months, aiming to build a distinctive personal brand in a niche area, attract a stable audience base, and, if possible, monetize. Please create a systematic creative plan to help me identify my target audience, choose a niche, and define my content value proposition; provide brand tone guidelines; design differentiated content themes and creative expression styles; and prepare a 3-month content topic calendar.Propose creative directions for storytelling, serialized content, and interactive formats to ensure sustained audience interest. Develop a detailed account growth strategy, including posting frequency, platform selection, and multi-platform coordination; suggest specific methods for increasing audience engagement, building a fan community, and iterating content. Provide recommendations for tracking and optimizing key performance metrics, and develop a feasible monetization pathway.Taking into account my time, budget, and skill level, create a reasonable resource allocation plan; provide competitive analysis and differentiation strategies, assess potential risks, and propose mitigation measures. Please recommend excellent domestic and international personal media case studies for reference.

goal_alignment.criterion: Persona-specific niche and audience definition anchored in AI founder perspective and company goals
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan picks a niche that leverages Zhang Yu’s credibility (e.g., applied AI productization in China, MLOps/LLMOps for startups, enterprise AI adoption) and segments target audiences (CTOs/heads of product in traditional industries, founders, senior engineers/students). It should articulate ICPs, pain points, and jobs-to-be-done and align them to the company’s lead-gen and brand goals.

goal_alignment.criterion: Content value proposition and differentiation clarity for an expert, B2B-leaning audience
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan communicates a unique promise (turning cutting-edge AI into deployable products with China-specific constraints, pragmatic engineering practices, founder lessons) backed by credibility signals (papers, conferences) and a clear positioning versus competing creators.

goal_alignment.criterion: Platform selection and cross-platform orchestration tailored to his digital habitat and bilingual reach
goal_alignment.explanation: Check that the plan designates platform roles and mappings (LinkedIn global networking, WeChat/知乎 longform in Chinese, Bilibili for tech videos, GitHub for code artifacts, newsletter as owned channel), sets a Notion/site as canonical hub, defines localization/bilingual rules, and specifies cross-post workflows and UTM tracking.

goal_alignment.criterion: Cadence and production schedule aligned with morning creativity and family-first evenings/weekends
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify a sustainable timetable: morning drafting/ideation blocks, weekly batching for video recording, minimal evening work, and weekend protection. It should include realistic per-platform frequencies, editing lead times, travel buffers, and scheduler/automation use.

goal_alignment.criterion: Differentiated themes, storytelling arcs, serialized content, and interactive formats leveraging founder journey and technical depth
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan proposes recurring series (e.g., build-in-public feature logs, conference debriefs, MLOps pitfalls), narrative structures, and interactive formats (AMA, live coding, polls) that fit a professional tech audience and Zhang Yu’s strengths.

goal_alignment.criterion: Three-month topic calendar specificity, prioritization, and feasibility
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the calendar lists concrete weekly topics with sequencing and theme rotation, matches expected events (industry summits/travel), balances depth vs. accessibility, includes contingencies, and is feasible within his time constraints.

goal_alignment.criterion: Engagement and community design suitable for professional audiences
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for a value-centric community plan (e.g., curated WeChat groups/Discord, GitHub Discussions), membership criteria, moderation guidelines, office hours, feedback loops, and co-creation mechanisms that encourage high-signal interactions.

goal_alignment.criterion: KPI framework and experimentation/iteration loop aligned to data-driven decision style and business funnel
