goal_alignment.explanation: Verify clearly defined OKRs, leading/lagging metrics (e.g., saves/shares, newsletter growth, MQLs/demos), dashboards (Notion/BI), A/B tests for titles/thumbnails/CTAs, review cadence, and decision thresholds for iterating content and channel strategy.

goal_alignment.criterion: Monetization pathways aligned with founder ethics and startup synergy
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether monetization emphasizes company lead-gen, enterprise workshops/training, paid cohort courses, sponsorship policies that protect credibility, and digital products (templates/code). It should include readiness milestones and pricing logic.

goal_alignment.criterion: Resource allocation and workflow leveraging his tools, time, and budget
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for a pragmatic content OS in Notion, GitHub integration for code content, use of transcription/automation tools, and selective outsourcing (editing, thumbnails, translation). Include budget ranges, SOPs, RACI, and a burnout risk check.

goal_alignment.criterion: Competitive analysis and case study relevance with actionable differentiation
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan analyzes both domestic and international personal creators analogous to an AI founder, extracts format/cadence/monetization insights, and translates them into concrete differentiation moves for Zhang Yu.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk, compliance, and reputation safeguards tailored to China AI and founder responsibilities
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for NDA/IP-safe content boundaries, regulatory/platform compliance checklists, claims verification, pre-publication review flow, crisis response plan, and ethical sponsorship/affiliate policies to protect brand and business.

goal_alignment.criterion: Brand tone and communication style alignment with persona
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether tone guidelines reflect authoritative yet approachable, optimistic, rational communication; specify platform-specific registers, bilingual style choices, jargon management for mixed audiences, and tasteful humanization (family/fitness/pet) without diluting technical authority.

content_alignment.criterion: Niche–User Fit and Authority Anchor
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the chosen niche and value proposition explicitly leverage Zhang Yu’s AI founder credibility (e.g., applied AI, MLOps/productization, engineering leadership, China-specific AI deployment) so the content naturally signals authority to the intended tech audience.

content_alignment.criterion: Appropriate Technical Depth and Rigor
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether topics go beyond surface tips to include code-level insights, architecture trade-offs, evaluation frameworks, and concrete AI/ML operational details that match his Tsinghua/industry background without drifting into purely academic abstraction.

content_alignment.criterion: China Ecosystem and Policy Grounding
content_alignment.explanation: Check that content reflects China’s market realities and regulations (e.g., PIPL, CAC algorithm guidance), domestic cloud/compute landscape, Shenzhen/Hangzhou ecosystems, and local enterprise needs—ensuring high local relevance rather than generic global advice.

content_alignment.criterion: Workflow- and Tool-Aligned Content Formats
content_alignment.explanation: Judge whether content formats align with his daily GitHub/Slack/Notion use (e.g., repo-driven tutorials, Notion playbooks, reproducible experiments), reducing friction and ensuring authentic, substantive outputs that fit his habits.

content_alignment.criterion: Platform–Audience Content Fit
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan maps content types to each platform’s audience and culture he engages with (e.g., LinkedIn industry analyses, WeChat long-form case studies, Bilibili technical demos, Zhihu Q&A), maximizing relevance of topics per channel.

content_alignment.criterion: Evidence- and Metric-Oriented Framing
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate if content consistently uses data (benchmarks, A/B results, cohort metrics, deployment KPIs) to support claims, fitting his data-driven decision style and the expectations of a technical/professional audience.

content_alignment.criterion: Founder Narrative Integration with Professional Focus
content_alignment.explanation: Check that humanizing elements (founder diary, stress management, family–work balance) are included at a proportion that enhances trust and relatability while staying anchored to AI entrepreneurship and product content.

content_alignment.criterion: Tech-Community-Oriented Interactivity Fit
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether interactive content (AMAs, code reviews, office hours, repo challenges) is designed for a technically savvy audience and leverages his extroversion to facilitate meaningful, problem-solving exchanges.

content_alignment.criterion: Case Study Relevance and Transferability
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether domestic and international case studies mirror his context (AI startups, enterprise AI, open-source maintainers) and extract insights that can reasonably transfer to a Beijing-based founder’s situation.

content_alignment.criterion: Monetization Content–Brand Congruence
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether monetization-linked content (e.g., paid technical playbooks, enterprise workshops, consulting, beta access to tools) aligns with his expertise and audience expectations, avoiding mismatched ads or generic sponsorships.

content_alignment.criterion: Cross-Border and Bilingual Insight Integration
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether content bridges domestic and global AI conversations (e.g., bilingual summaries, conference takeaways, comparisons of domestic vs. overseas practices) reflecting his international academic exposure.

content_alignment.criterion: Risk-Aware Topic Selection
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether sensitive topics (AI ethics, regulatory compliance, IP/data usage) are selected and framed to minimize reputational risk and align with his moderate risk appetite and compliance needs.

presentation_fit.criterion: Scannability and layered structure (executive summary, TOC, section headings, TL;DR per section)
presentation_fit.explanation: As a busy, data-driven founder who does morning deep work, Zhang Yu needs a top-level summary and clear hierarchy to grasp the whole plan fast and drill down selectively. Presence and quality of TL;DRs, headings, numbered sections, and a table of contents determine reading speed and comprehension.

presentation_fit.criterion: Professional, concise, data-oriented tone (avoid hype and slang; crisp sentences)
presentation_fit.explanation: He values logical reasoning and evidence-based communication. A neutral, analytical tone with concise phrasing improves trust and reduces cognitive load compared with influencer-style hype or vague guidance.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology precision and brief definitions for creator-growth jargon (consistent acronyms, optional bilingual notes)
presentation_fit.explanation: While fluent in tech, he may be less familiar with marketing/social metrics. Consistent use of acronyms (e.g., CTR, ER, LTV) with one-line definitions and occasional bilingual labels prevents misinterpretation and ensures shared understanding with team members.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visual organization using tables, matrices, decision trees, and calendars (Notion/Slack-friendly)
presentation_fit.explanation: He organizes knowledge in Notion and shares via Slack. Clear tables (platform comparisons), matrices (positioning), decision trees (niche selection), and 3‑month calendars increase readability and are easy to copy/paste into tools he already uses.

presentation_fit.criterion: Platform-specific modularity with consistent section templates
presentation_fit.explanation: Present each platform in a repeatable template (audience, formats, hooks, cadence, CTA, cross-posting rules). Consistent modular blocks reduce context switching and enable quick reference during execution.

presentation_fit.criterion: KPI dashboard presentation clarity (metric name, definition/formula, tool, cadence, target/benchmark)
presentation_fit.explanation: He prioritizes measurement. A well-formatted KPI table with formulas and update cadence supports data-driven iteration and aligns the team on exactly how metrics are computed and reviewed.

presentation_fit.criterion: Timeline and resource plan visualization (phase roadmap, weekly cadence grid, budget table)
presentation_fit.explanation: Time and budget constraints require clear visual timelines (e.g., phase/milestone roadmap), weekly posting grids, and budget breakdown tables to quickly assess feasibility without parsing dense prose.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-first readability and shareability
presentation_fit.explanation: Short paragraphs, bullet lists, highlighted key numbers, and clean link formatting ensure the plan is easy to read on a phone and remains legible when pasted into Slack/WeChat or exported to Notion.

presentation_fit.criterion: Action checklists and decision gates at the end of sections
presentation_fit.explanation: Checklists for next 7/30/90-day actions and explicit decision criteria/gates translate each section into immediate steps, improving usability without changing content—this is a presentation feature that fits his execution-focused workflow.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Executable 6-month roadmap with phased milestones and decision gates
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a week-by-week or phase-based plan (discovery → prototype content → launch → growth → monetize) with clear deliverables, dates, and go/no-go or pivot criteria. Without a sequenced roadmap and checkpoints, the plan is hard to execute or adjust in time.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Time-feasible workload and schedule aligned to mornings and weekends
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the plan specifies estimated weekly hours by task (ideation, scripting, recording, editing, distribution, community), fits morning creative blocks, batches production, and protects weekends for family. Practicality depends on matching output ambitions to his true time capacity.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Platform-specific playbooks and realistic posting frequency
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report gives concrete tactics for chosen platforms (e.g., WeChat Official Account, Bilibili, Zhihu, LinkedIn, Xiaohongshu), including format specs, SEO/algorithm levers, caption/thumbnail guidelines, and feasible posting frequencies per platform. Actionability requires precise ‘how-to’ beyond generic advice.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Detailed 3-month content calendar with topics, hooks, and CTAs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for a filled calendar that lists weekly themes, specific post ideas/titles, required assets, target audience intent, and clear calls-to-action, incorporating serialized and interactive pieces and integrating travel windows. Concrete calendars remove ambiguity and accelerate execution.

actionability_practicality.criterion: KPI definition, instrumentation, and review cadence
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report specifies platform-level north star and leading indicators (e.g., watch time, saves, shares, click-through to lead magnet), sets weekly/monthly targets, and defines tooling (UTM, platform analytics, Notion dashboards) and ritualized reviews. Data-driven execution needs proper measurement set-up.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Iteration and experimentation framework
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether there is a concrete A/B testing plan for hooks/thumbnails/format lengths, a hypothesis log, sample sizes, test cadence, and explicit rules for keeping/killing ideas and pivot conditions. Practical plans must show how learning cycles will improve outputs quickly.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Resource and budget plan with tool stack and outsourcing options
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates if the plan specifies exact tools (e.g., mic/lighting, editing/transcription/scheduling software), automation, costs, and when to hire freelancers (editor, thumbnail designer, community moderator) with rate estimates and a simple RACI/ownership model. Clarity on resources enables immediate setup.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Feasible monetization pathway with stage-gated triggers
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for near-term monetization steps suited to an AI founder (lead magnets → consulting/workshops → paid newsletter/community → sponsorships), with activation thresholds (e.g., follower count, newsletter subs), pricing ranges, and lightweight funnels (booking links, intake forms). Actionability requires ‘when and how’ to turn on revenue.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Community building mechanisms and operational load
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report details private-domain setups (WeChat groups, newsletter, Slack/Discord if relevant), onboarding prompts, engagement rituals (AMAs, office hours), moderation rules, and time estimates. Sustainable engagement depends on manageable, repeatable community operations.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk, compliance, and contingency measures
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates if the plan identifies key risks (content accuracy in AI, platform policy shifts, burnout, reputational issues), includes pre-publication review checklists, sponsor disclosure practices, backup content buffers, and escalation playbooks. Practical plans prepare for disruptions that can derail execution.

depth_insight.criterion: Audience–Niche–Value Proposition Fit Analysis
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report deeply segments the target audience, surfaces unmet needs with evidence (e.g., search/social signals, community pain points), sizes and trends the niche, and justifies a niche choice aligned with the creator’s skills/time. Also evaluates the defensibility and specificity of the value proposition.

depth_insight.criterion: Competitive Landscape Depth and White-space Identification
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether competitors are mapped by content type, positioning, and monetization models, and whether the report identifies actionable gaps (formats, angles, audiences) with clear reasons these gaps are exploitable and sustainable.

depth_insight.criterion: Content Strategy Mechanisms and Editorial Depth
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether content themes, storytelling, serialization, and interactive formats are tied to psychological triggers and audience jobs-to-be-done, with clear mechanisms for retention/shareability. The 3‑month calendar should reflect hypotheses, seasonality, and learning loops, not just a topic list.

depth_insight.criterion: Platform Mechanics and Growth Lever Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates understanding of platform algorithms and distribution (e.g., hook dynamics, session metrics, watch time), posting frequency trade-offs, and multi-platform coordination/repurposing logic grounded in platform-native behaviors and user journeys.

depth_insight.criterion: Engagement and Community-Building Mechanisms
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report designs concrete interaction loops (e.g., prompts, challenges, UGC, rituals), incentive structures, and feedback systems that convert audience into a community, with rationale for how these mechanisms sustain participation and loyalty.

depth_insight.criterion: KPI Model, Benchmarks, and Experimentation Rigor
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the presence of a KPI tree linking leading to lagging metrics, realistic benchmark ranges, clear hypotheses, A/B testing or sequential testing methods, and an iteration cadence that can systematically improve outcomes.

depth_insight.criterion: Monetization Path Fit and Unit Economics Thinking
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether monetization modes (e.g., affiliates, digital products, services, memberships) match audience and content maturity, include pricing hypotheses, basic LTV/CAC proxies, and milestone gates for when to scale or pivot.

depth_insight.criterion: Resource-Constrained Feasibility and Process Design
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether time/budget/skill constraints are translated into a pragmatic production workflow, tooling/automation choices, outsourcing calculus, and prioritization that maximizes ROI without sacrificing strategic focus.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Anticipation and Concrete Mitigation
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses the identification of major risks (e.g., platform dependency, legal/compliance/IP, burnout, content fatigue, reputation) and the specificity and plausibility of mitigation playbooks and contingencies.

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and Actionable Synthesis (incl. Case Studies)
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report contributes non-obvious, contrarian (yet justified) angles and extracts transferable principles from exemplary domestic and international creators, adapting them to the specific context rather than copy-pasting tactics.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether top goals (distinctive brand, stable audience, optional monetization in 6 months) are explicitly mapped to strategies (niche positioning, content pillars, distribution), to concrete tactics (posting cadence, formats, community ops), and to KPIs/milestones, forming a traceable chain from objectives to actions to measurements.

logical_coherence.criterion: Explicit Decision Criteria and Traceable Choices (Niche, Audience, Platforms)
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the report states evaluation criteria (e.g., audience fit, competition, creator strengths, monetization fit) and applies them to compare options, showing how the final niche, target audience, and platform mix logically follow from those criteria rather than assertion.

Evaluates whether recommendations in all sections (posting frequency, content volume, community operations, budget allocations) are internally consistent and explicitly derived from the stated time, budget, and skill constraints, with these constraints propagated into the content calendar and growth plan.

logical_coherence.criterion: Evidence-Linked Justification of Assumptions and Tactics
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that key claims (e.g., platform algorithm behaviors, content format performance, monetization viability) are linked to relevant data, platform documentation, or case benchmarks, and that the cited support directly underpins the claim; unstated assumptions are minimized and identified when unavoidable.

logical_coherence.criterion: Causal Reasoning from Tactics to Outcomes with Measurement Logic
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether each tactic is tied to a hypothesized mechanism (e.g., series format → higher retention → increased distribution → follower growth → conversion) and to specific KPIs and thresholds that test the mechanism and inform iterate-or-pivot decisions.

Evaluates whether the 3‑month calendar and growth strategy are sequenced into logical phases (e.g., discovery → engagement → conversion), account for dependencies (e.g., baseline content before collaborations, community launch after content cadence is stable), and include iteration cadences.

logical_coherence.criterion: Cohesive Transitions and Cross-References Between Sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether sections explicitly connect (e.g., audience insights leading to value proposition, then to content pillars and calendar, then to distribution and monetization), with forward/backward references that prevent logical jumps between parts of the plan.

Assesses whether identified risks are linked to likelihood/impact reasoning, have corresponding mitigation actions, and define monitoring indicators/triggers that tie back to KPIs and prompt predefined adjustments to the plan.

clarity_readability.criterion: Hierarchical Structure and Navigability Aligned to Task Scope
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear heading hierarchy and table of contents that mirror the requested components (e.g., audience, niche, value proposition, brand tone, content themes, 3‑month calendar, storytelling/interactive formats, growth strategy, engagement/community, KPI tracking, monetization, resources, competition, risks, cases). Includes consistent numbering, cross-references, and section summaries to help readers quickly locate relevant parts.

clarity_readability.criterion: Formatting and Layout Readability
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses cleanliness and consistency of visual layout: adequate white space, readable font size/line spacing, consistent heading styles, bullet/numbered lists for steps, and appropriate use of tables for calendars or checklists. A tidy layout minimizes fatigue and supports long-form reading.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity, Concision, and Action-Oriented Phrasing
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether sentences are plain and concise, with active voice and unambiguous instructions (who/what/when/how), and whether steps are expressed with clear action verbs. Minimal grammatical errors and the use of concrete examples/templates improve immediate comprehension and execution.

clarity_readability.criterion: Terminology and Acronym Handling
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether platform and marketing terms (e.g., ICP, UVP, CTR, CPM, CAC, LTV, retention, watch time) are defined on first use and/or consolidated in a brief glossary with simple examples. Proper handling lowers entry barriers for non-experts and prevents misinterpretation.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Planning Visuals (Calendars, Roadmaps, Matrices)
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates clarity of visual tools used for the 3‑month content calendar, posting frequency, platform coordination, and content themes (e.g., timelines, Gantt-like views, platform-by-format matrices). Labels, legends, color contrast, and callouts should make schedules and responsibilities instantly understandable.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity of KPI and Data Presentation
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether KPI tables/charts specify metric names, definitions, formulas, units, targets, and reporting cadence; axes and legends are clear; thresholds/benchmarks are highlighted. This enables accurate reading and consistent tracking without confusion.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting, Scannability, and Key Takeaways
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for an executive summary, per-section key takeaways, checklists, and emphasized callouts for critical deadlines and dependencies. Good scannability lets readers extract the ‘what to do next’ quickly, improving adoption and recall.

task: I plan to develop a personal media account within the next six months, aiming to build a distinctive personal brand in a niche area, attract a stable audience base, and, if possible, monetize. Please create a systematic creative plan to help me identify my target audience, choose a niche, and define my content value proposition; provide brand tone guidelines; design differentiated content themes and creative expression styles; and prepare a 3-month content topic calendar.Propose creative directions for storytelling, serialized content, and interactive formats to ensure sustained audience interest. Develop a detailed account growth strategy, including posting frequency, platform selection, and multi-platform coordination; suggest specific methods for increasing audience engagement, building a fan community, and iterating content. Provide recommendations for tracking and optimizing key performance metrics, and develop a feasible monetization pathway.Taking into account my time, budget, and skill level, create a reasonable resource allocation plan; provide competitive analysis and differentiation strategies, assess potential risks, and propose mitigation measures. Please recommend excellent domestic and international personal media case studies for reference.

goal_alignment.criterion: Persona-grounded niche and value proposition clarity
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan defines a focused niche that leverages Lin Nan’s Jiangnan-influenced aesthetics, travel photography, process expertise, and cat ‘Mo Mo’ as narrative assets; specifies target segments (e.g., aspiring illustrators, art/lifestyle enthusiasts, travel/culture lovers, pet-loving art fans, and potential brand clients); and states a crisp value proposition (teach/inspire with calm, poetic illustration grounded in real travel and studio craft).

goal_alignment.criterion: Platform mix and CN/INT localization aligned to current habits
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether platform choices and roles reflect his existing Bilibili/Weibo/Instagram presence and add high-fit platforms (e.g., Xiaohongshu, Douyin, Behance/Dribbble, WeChat OA) with format- and algorithm-specific tactics, evening posting windows, bilingual/caption strategy where relevant, and clear cross-posting workflows that minimize redundant effort.

goal_alignment.criterion: Content formats and serialized storytelling tailored to illustration workflow
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if the plan proposes process videos, time-lapses, live ‘draw with me,’ travel sketchbook diaries, client project breakdowns, and ‘Mo Mo’ micro-stories; includes sustainable series pillars (e.g., Jiangnan Corners Weekly, Tools Tuesday, From Photo to Illustration); and designs interactive mechanisms (prompts, polls, co-creation) to sustain interest.

goal_alignment.criterion: Feasible cadence and resource allocation respecting time/budget/skill
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine whether posting frequencies per platform fit a solo creator with 6h drawing and 1.5h social daily; whether batch production, templates (thumbnails/captions), simple filming setups, and workflow tools (content calendar, asset library) are specified; and whether budget allocations (gear upgrades, printing, ad tests) and time blocks align with his evening peak and client deadlines.

goal_alignment.criterion: Monetization pathway fit and staged validation
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess if revenue options match his brand and moderate risk profile: limited-edition prints/merch (domestic and international storefronts), commissions/licensing, selective sponsorships (art tools/coffee/lifestyle aligned with aesthetics), workshops/courses/memberships (e.g., knowledge platforms or creator memberships), live tipping; includes price ladders, pre-sale/waitlist tests, and safeguards against over-commercialization.

goal_alignment.criterion: Platform-specific KPI system and iteration loop
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan defines actionable KPIs by platform (e.g., save rate and CTR on Xiaohongshu/IG, watch time and retention on Bilibili/Douyin, shares/coins/favorites on Bilibili, live concurrent and retention), sets baselines and review cadences, and prescribes A/B testing (hooks, thumbnails, openings) with monthly iteration rituals.

goal_alignment.criterion: Brand tone, visual identity, and communication style resonance
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether tone keywords (calm, poetic, craftsmanly, warm) and a visual system (Jiangnan palette, typography, logo/watermark, Mo Mo as mascot) are defined; and if the report’s suggested expression style is visual-first, low-jargon, and emotionally attuned to an introverted artist audience.

goal_alignment.criterion: Community building and engagement suited to an introverted creator
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess if engagement tactics emphasize depth over volume: small WeChat/Discord groups, monthly live critique/office hours, comment moderation norms, low-pressure prompts (#MoMoSketch), and occasional offline meetups at art markets/salons—designed to feel safe, meaningful, and sustainable.

goal_alignment.criterion: Competitive analysis and sharp differentiation
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine whether the plan benchmarks relevant domestic/international illustrator creators (process, travel sketchers, educator-illustrators), extracts tactics, and articulates a whitespace positioning (Jiangnan-culture-infused travel illustration + process pedagogy + cat companion narrative) with clear points of difference.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk assessment and mitigation for creator operations
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for concrete safeguards around copyright/reference use, client NDA handling, platform policy shifts, negative comments/reputation, burnout/anxiety (buffers, off-weeks), data/asset backups, and simple legal/contract templates—tailored to a solo illustrator’s realities.

goal_alignment.criterion: 3-month editorial calendar synced to travel and city events
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the calendar sequences series around quarterly travel (mini-arcs), Shanghai art/cultural events, seasonal festivals, and includes planned live streams/workshops and rest buffers—matching his real-world rhythms to maintain consistency without overload.

goal_alignment.criterion: Relevance and transferability of case studies
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether recommended domestic and international cases are illustrator-led (process/education/travel/lifestyle hybrids), explain why they fit Lin Nan’s goals, and translate their tactics into actionable steps for his brand rather than generic inspiration.

content_alignment.criterion: Niche and Value Proposition Anchored in Lin Nan’s Unique Identity
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report defines a niche that fuses freelance illustration with Jiangnan aesthetics (Suzhou influence), travel-derived visuals, and his pet motif (Mo Mo), clearly separating him from generic ‘art tips’ creators and reflecting his Shanghai context.

content_alignment.criterion: Audience Segmentation Congruent with His Reach and Communities
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether target audiences reflect realistic clusters he can serve and attract—aspiring illustrators (process/portfolio), indie brands/cafes/hospitality (commissions), pet owners (pet portraits), art-market attendees—mapped to his current platforms and offline scenes.

content_alignment.criterion: Platform and Format Fit with Current Multi-Platform Behavior
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether the plan prioritizes Weibo, Bilibili, Instagram (and optionally Xiaohongshu) with formats he already uses—process videos, time-lapses, live drawing—and adapts content selection to each platform’s norms, rather than proposing misaligned channels.

content_alignment.criterion: Depth Appropriate to a Trained, Award-Winning Illustrator
content_alignment.explanation: Determine if topics go beyond beginner tips to advanced Procreate/Photoshop workflows, composition/color theory, portfolio design, and business-of-illustration insights suited to his expertise, avoiding superficial content.

content_alignment.criterion: Storytelling and Serialization Leveraging Recurring Motifs
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether storytelling arcs exploit his quarterly travel, Shanghai café/exhibition visits, art market participation, and Mo Mo as recurring anchors, enabling distinct serials that build sustained audience interest.

content_alignment.criterion: Temporal Alignment with Creative Rhythm and Time Budget
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content cadence and pipeline respect his evening peak creativity, 1.5-hour daily social window, and batching needs, avoiding unrealistic editing or posting burdens.

content_alignment.criterion: Monetization Pathways Aligned to Skills and Values
content_alignment.explanation: Check if monetization options naturally fit his practice and audience—prints/merch, commissions (incl. pet portraits), art-supply/gear sponsorships he actually uses, advanced workshops/courses, memberships—avoiding incongruent tactics.

content_alignment.criterion: KPI and Feedback Loop Selection Tailored to Art Content
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether metrics emphasize saves/collections, shares, watch time for process videos, comment depth, DM inquiries, newsletter signups, and conversion at art markets, rather than generic vanity metrics.

content_alignment.criterion: Competitive Set and Differentiation Relevant to Illustrator Landscape
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether competitors are illustrator accounts with similar mediums/aesthetics across China and abroad and whether the differentiation leverages his Jiangnan cultural lens and travel-photo-to-illustration workflow.

content_alignment.criterion: Case Studies Relevancy and Transferability
content_alignment.explanation: Determine if recommended domestic and international personal media cases are process-driven illustrators or visual artists and if learnings are clearly transferable to Weibo/Bilibili/Instagram contexts he uses.

content_alignment.criterion: Risk Topics Specific to a Freelance Illustrator in China
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether risk identification focuses on IP/art theft, client payment terms, platform algorithm volatility (Weibo/Bilibili/Instagram), and burnout, rather than generic market risks.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structural clarity and navigability across all requested modules
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report mirrors the task’s modules with clear sectioning (e.g., target audience, niche, value proposition, brand tone, content themes, 3‑month calendar, storytelling/serialization/interaction, growth strategy, platform coordination, engagement/community/iteration, metrics, monetization, resources, competition, risks, cases), uses a table of contents, consistent headings/numbering, and an executive summary plus a “next 7 days” box so the user can quickly orient and jump to sections.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology clarity and localization for creator economy and platform analytics
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether marketing/analytics/platform terms are presented in plain Chinese with brief in‑line clarifications and English terms in parentheses where useful (e.g., 留存率(retention), 点击率(CTR), 完播率, B站“三连”, 微博“转评赞”), avoiding unexplained jargon so a non‑analyst creator can follow without extra research.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visualization appropriateness for a visual professional
presentation_fit.explanation: Check for effective use of simple, clean visual aids to convey complex ideas: content pillar maps, cross‑platform coordination matrices, growth funnels, KPI dashboard mockups, and a content calendar in a table/timeline. Visuals should be legible, minimal, and directly tied to the text to suit an illustrator’s visual cognition.

presentation_fit.criterion: Scannability and chunking (progressive disclosure)
presentation_fit.explanation: Determine if the report uses bullets, short paragraphs, callout boxes, and checklists, with overviews first and details expandable later. Priority tags (必做/可选) and time markers (Week 1–12) help the user skim during limited evening time and return for deep dives.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tone and voice alignment with a creative, introverted yet pragmatic illustrator
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the language is calm, respectful, and encouraging—avoiding hard‑sell or heavy corporate buzzwords—balancing aesthetic sensitivity with practical guidance. Occasional creative analogies are acceptable if they increase clarity without fluff.

presentation_fit.criterion: Platform‑specific presentation and formatting guides
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether per‑platform sections are clearly delineated and presented as quick‑reference cards (e.g., aspect ratios, durations, caption length, posting cadence, live formats) with consistent labels/icons so cross‑platform differences are easy to grasp at a glance.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use of concrete examples and templates to illustrate methods
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that abstract ideas are illustrated with example post outlines, sample storyboard/wireframe snippets, and template structures (e.g., caption skeletons, live session run‑of‑show) to make concepts tangible. Focus on how examples aid understanding, not on their substantive correctness.

presentation_fit.criterion: Readability and language accessibility
presentation_fit.explanation: Examine sentence length, punctuation, and consistency of terms; prefer plain Chinese with selective English glosses, minimal emoji, and consistent numbering/labels. This ensures low cognitive load for sustained reading and reference.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Phased 6-month roadmap with milestones and weekly action steps
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report provides a month-by-month (and preferably week-by-week) timeline with clear milestones (e.g., niche validation, content pillar launch, first live series, first sponsor outreach) and deliverables so Lin can execute sequentially without ambiguity.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Time-feasibility and workload fit for a solo illustrator
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether posting frequency, production pipeline, and live/interaction commitments are realistic given Lin’s 6 hours/day on illustration and 1.5 hours/day on social, including time estimates per asset, batching recommendations, and buffers to prevent burnout.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Specificity and readiness of the 3-month content topic calendar
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the calendar includes concrete topics, formats (short video, carousel, live), hooks, shot lists/storyboards, asset lists, and deadlines that Lin can follow immediately without additional planning.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Platform-specific execution and repurposing workflow
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for actionable guidance on platform selection (e.g., Bilibili, Weibo, Instagram, Xiaohongshu/Douyin), posting specs, thumbnail/title templates, hashtag/SEO practices, and a step-by-step repurposing map to adapt one asset across platforms efficiently.

actionability_practicality.criterion: KPI framework with measurable targets, tools, and decision thresholds
actionability_practicality.explanation: Requires clear metric definitions (e.g., views, ER, watch time, saves), target ranges for each platform, tracking tools (native analytics, Notion/Sheets), and explicit thresholds that trigger actions (iterate, double down, or pause).

actionability_practicality.criterion: Feasible monetization pathway with staged steps and prerequisites
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether monetization options (commissions, print sales, digital products, sponsorships, memberships, courses) are sequenced for a six-month horizon with follower/ER prerequisites, setup checklists (storefront/payment), pricing guidance, and outreach scripts appropriate for China and overseas.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Audience engagement and community-building playbook
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for concrete formats Lin can run consistently (e.g., weekly 60-min live sketch, challenge prompts, Q&A, polls), moderation scope, onboarding to a group (WeChat/QQ/Discord), engagement scripts, and cadence to build a stable core fan base.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Resource and budget allocation plan (tools, gear, ads, optional outsourcing)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for a realistic budget with itemized costs (lighting, mic, editing/subtitle tools, minimal ad test spend), time allocation blocks, and when to outsource (e.g., subtitles/thumbnail design) with cost estimates—aligned to Lin’s moderate budget and quality focus.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Content systemization: pillars, serialized formats, and templates
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report defines 3–5 content pillars leveraging Lin’s strengths (illustration process, travel-inspired art, Mo Mo, Jiangnan culture), serialized concepts with repeatable beats, and reusable templates (hooks, thumbnail styles, storyboard/checklists) to streamline production.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk identification and mitigation measures
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies that key risks (burnout, algorithm shifts, rights/IP, platform compliance, live-stream hiccups) are listed with concrete mitigation (content buffer, cross-platform backups, music licensing, disclosure templates, rest cycles).

actionability_practicality.criterion: Iteration and experimentation process
actionability_practicality.explanation: Requires a defined loop (weekly review, A/B tests on thumbnails/hooks, hypothesis log, experiment calendar) and simple analysis steps to convert insights into next-week adjustments, distinct from the KPI list.

depth_insight.criterion: Audience/Niche Insightfulness and Creator–Audience Fit
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report goes beyond demographic labels to surface psychographics, unmet needs, and consumption behaviors; estimates niche viability; and rigorously matches these with the creator’s skills, credibility, time, and budget to justify niche selection with clear tradeoffs.

depth_insight.criterion: Distinctive Content Value Proposition and Defensible Positioning
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan articulates a sharp job-to-be-done and point of view, defines what unique value the content delivers (and what it will not), and specifies defensible differentiation and moats (expertise, access, format IP) rather than generic promises.

depth_insight.criterion: Content System Design Depth (pillars, serialization, storytelling, interactivity, calendar strategy)
depth_insight.explanation: Judges whether content pillars are strategically derived from audience needs and positioning; serialized formats and storytelling arcs are designed to build habit; interactive mechanics are tied to audience motivations; and the 3‑month calendar sequences experiments, evergreen vs timely mixes, and funnel objectives.

depth_insight.criterion: Platform Mechanics and Growth Strategy Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses understanding of how chosen platforms distribute content (algorithm triggers, format fit, session goals), the rationale for posting frequency and timing, the role of each platform in the funnel, and a concrete cross-platform repurposing and cross-promotion strategy grounded in platform realities.

depth_insight.criterion: Experimentation and Measurement Framework Robustness
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan defines a KPI tree (leading vs lagging metrics) aligned to goals, sets hypotheses and test designs (content variables, sample sizes, decision rules), establishes feedback loops, and specifies optimization levers that make data actionable.

depth_insight.criterion: Monetization Pathway Realism and Unit Economics
depth_insight.explanation: Judges whether monetization options match audience profile and maturity stage; outlines a staged monetization ladder with assumptions (conversion rates, ARPU, CPM), simple unit economics/break-even logic, and risk/brand fit considerations to ensure feasibility.

depth_insight.criterion: Resource-Constrained Planning and Tradeoff Rigor
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses integration of time, budget, and skill constraints into production workflows (batching, templates, outsourcing vs in-house), tooling choices, and capacity planning, showing clear tradeoffs that protect consistency and quality within realistic limits.

depth_insight.criterion: Competitive Landscape Insight and White-space Identification
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates depth of competitor mapping (pillars, formats, cadence, engagement patterns), identification of saturation and underserved needs, and derivation of specific whitespace wedges and anti-commoditization strategies informed by evidence.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Foresight and Mitigation Strategy Depth
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses anticipation of major risks (platform policy shifts, burnout, legal/reputation issues, content fatigue) and the concreteness of mitigations (content bank, crisis playbook, diversification, compliance safeguards, workload buffers).

depth_insight.criterion: Case Study Synthesis and Transferable Principles
depth_insight.explanation: Judges whether domestic and international cases are used to extract principles and failure modes, with explicit reasoning on which elements are transferable and how to adapt them to the creator’s context, rather than superficial name-dropping.

logical_coherence.criterion: Structured Planning Framework Aligned to the Lifecycle
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a coherent end-to-end structure (diagnose audience/niche → define value proposition and brand tone → design content themes/formats → build calendar → growth/distribution → engagement/community → measurement/iteration → monetization → resources/risks/competition), so each section builds on the previous one without gaps.

Assesses whether stated objectives translate into measurable KPIs and whether platform selection, posting frequency, content mix, and budget allocation are explicitly justified by those KPIs, preventing decisions that drift from goals.

logical_coherence.criterion: Audience/Niche → Value Proposition → Content Strategy Causal Chain
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the identified audience needs and niche dynamics logically produce the value proposition, which then deterministically shapes brand tone, content themes, formats (e.g., storytelling, serials), and the topic calendar.

logical_coherence.criterion: Evidence or Explicit Assumptions Supporting Key Choices
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that critical choices (e.g., platforms, frequency, monetization path, resource split) are backed by relevant benchmarks, case references, or clearly stated assumptions, avoiding unsupported assertions.

Assesses whether steps are ordered feasibly (e.g., brand assets and audience research before calendar; pilots before scale) and dependencies are acknowledged (skills, tools, budget), ensuring the timeline is logically executable.

Evaluates whether the 3‑month topic calendar, posting cadence, CTAs, and experiments operationalize upstream strategic choices without contradiction (e.g., frequency matches resource constraints; themes reflect the value proposition).
Checks whether each identified risk is matched with a detection trigger/threshold and a concrete mitigation or fallback, demonstrating a logical contingency structure rather than a loose list.

Assesses whether metrics have review cadence and decision rules (e.g., thresholds for doubling down or pivoting) and whether learnings feed back to adjust content, channels, and monetization in a closed loop.
Evaluates the presence of explicit linkages explaining why each section follows (e.g., ‘Given audience X and value proposition Y, we choose platform Z’), preventing logical jumps between parts.

clarity_readability.criterion: Task‑Aligned Structure and Navigability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether headings, section order, and a table of contents map clearly to the requested components (audience, niche, value prop, brand tone, content pillars, 3‑month calendar, storytelling/interactive formats, growth, engagement, KPIs, monetization, resources, competition, risks, cases) with clear numbering and cross‑references so readers can locate items instantly.

clarity_readability.criterion: Scannability and Visual Cleanliness of Layout
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates use of bullet lists, step lists, checklists, and whitespace; consistent heading hierarchy; emphasis for key takeaways; and compact tables for schedules and action items to enable quick scanning without dense blocks of text.

clarity_readability.criterion: Plain, Precise, and Error‑Free Language
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether sentences are concise and unambiguous, with actionable verbs and concrete quantifiers (e.g., posting frequency, response SLAs), minimal filler, and correct grammar/spelling so instructions are immediately understandable.

clarity_readability.criterion: Definition and Consistent Use of Terms, Acronyms, and Metrics
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that platform jargon and KPIs (e.g., CTR, CPM, CAC, LTV, AARRR, content pillars, ICP, UTM) are defined on first use or in a glossary, used consistently, and, for metrics, include units and formulas to reduce comprehension barriers.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity and Informativeness of Visuals (Calendars, Roadmaps, Funnels, Dashboards)
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether calendars, timelines, funnel diagrams, platform mapping matrices, and KPI tables have clear titles, labels, legends, time frames, units, and captions; visuals should stand alone and align with the text to convey schedules and metrics at a glance.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency of Labels, Units, and Cross‑References
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses consistent naming of content pillars, platform tags (e.g., Douyin/TikTok), date and time formats, currency/units, and stable references (e.g., ‘see Section 3.2’), preventing confusion across sections.

clarity_readability.criterion: Quality of Summaries, Action Checklists, and Roadmap Overviews
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for an executive summary/TL;DR, per‑section key actions, and a one‑page 90‑day roadmap so readers can grasp priorities and next steps quickly without reading the entire report.

task: I plan to develop a personal media account within the next six months, aiming to build a distinctive personal brand in a niche area, attract a stable audience base, and, if possible, monetize. Please create a systematic creative plan to help me identify my target audience, choose a niche, and define my content value proposition; provide brand tone guidelines; design differentiated content themes and creative expression styles; and prepare a 3-month content topic calendar.Propose creative directions for storytelling, serialized content, and interactive formats to ensure sustained audience interest. Develop a detailed account growth strategy, including posting frequency, platform selection, and multi-platform coordination; suggest specific methods for increasing audience engagement, building a fan community, and iterating content. Provide recommendations for tracking and optimizing key performance metrics, and develop a feasible monetization pathway.Taking into account my time, budget, and skill level, create a reasonable resource allocation plan; provide competitive analysis and differentiation strategies, assess potential risks, and propose mitigation measures. Please recommend excellent domestic and international personal media case studies for reference.

goal_alignment.criterion: Persona-strength-led niche and audience definition
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan derives the niche and value proposition from her core advantages (journalism training, award-winning writing, social/cultural observation, feminism and eco-interest), and explicitly profiles the target audience (e.g., urban, culture-minded readers 22–35) with pains and gains matching her themes.

goal_alignment.criterion: Platform selection and format fit to current habits with clear cross-platform orchestration
goal_alignment.explanation: Check that WeChat OA (long-form), Weibo (threaded insights), and Douban (community/long comments) are prioritized, with specific cross-posting and traffic loops; heavy short-video is optional or phased. This aligns with her 5h WeChat, 2h Weibo, 1h Douban usage and text-first strengths.

goal_alignment.criterion: Brand tone and aesthetic resonance with her literary, minimalist style
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether tone guidelines are calm, reflective, ethical, and literary (in line with Murakami/Wong Kar-wai influences), with minimalist, eco-leaning visuals and a humane warmth (e.g., featuring ‘Mo Mo’ as a subtle mascot), ensuring emotional congruence with her personality.

goal_alignment.criterion: Time-aware cadence and feasible 3-month content calendar
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if posting frequency, batching, and buffer content align with her night writing and morning planning routine, solo capacity, and anxiety management. A realistic, detailed 3-month calendar should reflect workload constraints while enabling consistency.

goal_alignment.criterion: Serialized storytelling and thematic arcs aligned to her investigative interests
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for well-defined serial formats (e.g., feminist reading notebooks, Beijing literary walks, eco-minimalist living experiments, tea ritual essays) that play to her curiosity and sustain retention via cliffhangers, arcs, and subscriber-only continuations.

goal_alignment.criterion: Community-building strategy suited to an introverted, depth-first creator
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether interactive formats privilege asynchronous, thoughtful discussion (Douban topic threads, WeChat book club, monthly letters) over high-energy live formats, and include clear norms to cultivate a safe, high-quality comment culture.

goal_alignment.criterion: Engagement tactics leveraging her cognitive habits and audience’s reflective behavior
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether prompts, calls-to-action, and UGC mechanisms (e.g., annotation swaps, ‘quote-and-thought’ threads) leverage her habit of screenshotting comments and foster saves/long comments rather than shallow likes, increasing resonance with reflective readers.

goal_alignment.criterion: Platform-specific KPI framework and iteration loops for depth content
goal_alignment.explanation: Check that KPIs emphasize depth: WeChat read-through and save-to-read ratio, Weibo saves/comments per thread, Douban long-comment ratio; that qualitative feedback is coded into idea banks; and that there is a monthly iteration ritual based on these signals.

goal_alignment.criterion: Monetization path aligned with risk aversion and brand integrity
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify that monetization is staged (after audience validation) and low-risk: paid columns/newsletter, digital zines/e-books, small-group workshops, editing/consulting, carefully vetted sustainable brand partnerships; avoiding aggressive, off-brand sponcon.

goal_alignment.criterion: Resource allocation and tooling feasibility for a solo creator with limited budget
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan specifies low-cost tools (Notion/Obsidian/Canva), a weekly production workflow, batching/editing checklists, and scheduled rest to protect mental health, ensuring sustainable execution without overspend.

goal_alignment.criterion: Competitive analysis and differentiation with relevant case studies
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the analysis maps adjacent Chinese cultural commentators and international personal media writers, extracts clear gaps (e.g., Beijing place-based eco-literary angle), and selects case studies whose tactics are transferable to her text-led style.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk identification and mitigation tailored to content sensitivity and well-being
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for concrete measures on topic framing and platform compliance for feminism/social issues in China, harassment moderation, privacy, burnout prevention, and IP/citation practices, reducing operational and reputational risk.

content_alignment.criterion: Niche and Value Proposition Anchored to Writer Identity and Signature Lenses
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the chosen niche and value proposition leverage her strengths as a journalist-level writer and focus on her enduring interests (culture, social observation, psychology, feminism, environmental protection) with Beijing/Nanjing cultural textures. This ensures the core content focus is distinctly hers and attractive to like-minded readers.

content_alignment.criterion: Depth and Research Rigor Suited to an Educated, Reflective Creator and Audience
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate if content plans emphasize in-depth essays, investigative/annotated pieces, curated references, and thoughtful synthesis over superficial tips. This aligns with her academic background and her audience’s preference for substance.

content_alignment.criterion: Platform–Format Fit to Existing Habits and Production Strengths (WeChat/Douban/Weibo, Text-First)
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether the plan prioritizes text-centric platforms she already uses (WeChat OA essays, Douban group discussions, Weibo threads) and suggests any multimedia only in lightweight, essayistic forms. This aligns content types with her workflow and reduces friction.

content_alignment.criterion: Thematic Resonance with Personal Motifs and Values
content_alignment.explanation: Judge whether content themes meaningfully integrate her lifestyle/value motifs (tea ceremony, yoga/Pilates, minimalism, bookstores/cafés, urban nature, cat ‘Mo Mo’) and feminist/eco angles to create authentic, recurring storytelling anchors.

content_alignment.criterion: Localization to China’s Digital Ecosystem and Beijing Cultural Context
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether examples, hooks, and topic calendars reflect domestic platforms, cultural moments (e.g., Women’s Day, World Book Day, Earth Day), local bookstores/cafés, and Chinese audience sensibilities, ensuring relevance to her environment.

content_alignment.criterion: Target Audience Definition and Topic Fit to Her Natural Communities
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the audience persona (e.g., educated urban readers/women interested in reflective culture and mindful living) matches her current circles (WeChat/Douban) and whether topics address their psychographics and media habits.

content_alignment.criterion: Serialized and Interactive Design Aligned to Introverted, Reflective Engagement
content_alignment.explanation: Check if proposed serials (e.g., field notes, bookstore diaries, city nature essays) and interactions (asynchronous reading clubs, writing prompts) fit her style and time preferences, fostering sustained, thoughtful participation.

content_alignment.criterion: Monetization–Content Coherence with Values and Audience Trust
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether monetization proposals (paid newsletters, writing workshops, curated book/eco-product affiliates, ESG-aligned collaborations) naturally extend from the content and respect her risk-averse, value-driven stance.

content_alignment.criterion: Content Scope Feasibility Relative to Solo Capacity and Budget
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate if the content calendar and formats are realistically producible by a single writer with limited budget/time (text-first, low-cost visuals/audio), avoiding heavy daily vlogging or high-production demands that would misalign with her context.

content_alignment.criterion: Relevance of Domestic/International Case Studies to Writer-Led, Culture-Centric Creation
content_alignment.explanation: Judge whether the referenced creators are analogous (personal brands, essayists/cultural commentators, value-driven) and whether takeaways inform content choices applicable to her niche and platforms.

content_alignment.criterion: KPI Selection Oriented to Depth and Resonance
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether recommended metrics (save rate, read completion, time-on-page, high-quality comments, group retention) reflect deep engagement typical of long-form cultural content rather than vanity views alone, guiding content choices accordingly.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structural hierarchy and navigability
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report has a clear top-level outline and section numbering (from strategy to tactics to schedule to measurement), with a table of contents, headings/subheadings, and logical sequencing. This matches a journalist’s need for a rigorous argument flow and allows quick navigation across a broad, multi-part plan.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tone and voice fit for a thoughtful writer
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether the language is calm, precise, and respectful (non-hype, non-clickbait), with nuanced yet concise phrasing. This aligns with her literary sensibility and increases trust and willingness to adopt the plan.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology clarity and localization
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that platform/growth terms (e.g., CTR, retention, funnel) and Chinese platform concepts are used correctly with brief inline definitions or parenthetical notes, avoiding unexplained jargon. This supports comprehension without interrupting reading flow.

presentation_fit.criterion: Micro-scannability and summary scaffolds
presentation_fit.explanation: Look for TL;DR at the start, section-level key takeaways, numbered steps, and checklists. These devices help her scan in mornings, revisit at night, and reduce cognitive load across a long document.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visualization and artifact appropriateness
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether visuals (tables, matrices, calendars, funnels) are minimalist, labeled, and legible; legends and captions clarify meaning; no decorative clutter. Visuals should clarify scheduling and pillars without overwhelming.

presentation_fit.criterion: Platform-portable templates and format consistency
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether provided templates (post outlines, calendars, checklists) are copy-paste friendly for WeChat/Weibo/Douban and maintain consistent formatting (bullets, numbering, labels). This ensures the plan can be directly applied in her daily tools.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-first readability
presentation_fit.explanation: Check short paragraphs, adequate line spacing, restrained table width, and avoidance of dense, image-only text. Given her heavy app usage, mobile-friendly formatting prevents fatigue and preserves clarity.

presentation_fit.criterion: Citation and reference presentation quality
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether domestic and international case studies and sources are presented with clear titles, clean links, and one-sentence annotations. Good citation presentation supports credibility and efficient follow-up reading.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Specificity and executability of the 6-month roadmap and 3-month content calendar
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a week-by-week plan with exact posting frequency, suggested posting times (aligned to her nighttime productivity), mapped content pillars/series, and a prepared backlog checklist so she can start immediately and maintain cadence.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Feasibility vs time, skills, and budget
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if recommendations match her writer-first skill set (text-first, light multimedia), include realistic per-post time estimates and weekly workload (including client work buffer), and avoid costly tools; this ensures she can consistently execute without burnout or overspend.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Platform selection and multi-platform operating playbook (China-localized)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan prioritizes WeChat/Weibo/Douban with concrete setup steps, posting formats, content adaptation rules, cross-posting workflow, and compliance notes—so she can operate effectively where she already is.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Content production workflow and SOPs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for detailed SOPs (ideation → outlining → drafting → editing → visual packaging → publishing → archiving), templates/checklists, batching plans, and a lightweight tool stack; this reduces friction and increases consistency.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Audience development, interaction, and community-building mechanics
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies concrete interaction formats (e.g., weekly Q&A, reader prompts, serialized reader submissions), cadences, and community tools (e.g., WeChat groups or knowledge communities) with moderation guidelines—so engagement is systematic and sustainable for an introvert.

actionability_practicality.criterion: KPI system with targets, tracking tools, and decision rules
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report defines stage-appropriate KPIs (output, engagement, retention, growth), sets monthly targets, specifies tracking tools/links, and provides review cadences and action rules (e.g., pivot thresholds), enabling data-driven iteration.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Iteration and experimentation plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for a structured monthly experimentation slate (e.g., A/B titles, format tests, interactive pilots), explicit hypotheses, and how to translate case-study learnings into small tests, ensuring continuous improvement without large risks.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Monetization pathway feasibility with milestones
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates a staged path (e.g., tips/paid articles, paid community, brand collaborations) with prerequisite thresholds (follower/ER), revenue projections, sample outreach scripts, and brand-fit guidance compatible with her topics and risk profile.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Resource and budget allocation plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Requires a clear monthly cost breakdown (tools, optional outsourcing, small test ad spend), time allocation, and ROI checkpoints that align with her cautious budgeting and allow scale-up only when justified by metrics.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk assessment and mitigation playbooks
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for actionable mitigations for burnout (batching, rest days), platform/policy sensitivity around social topics (framing, compliance checks), harassment/moderation protocols, and contingency plans—enabling steady execution under uncertainty.

depth_insight.criterion: Audience insightfulness (segmentation, psychographics, jobs-to-be-done, pains/gains)
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report moves beyond demographic labels to reveal deep audience motivations, unmet needs, willingness-to-pay signals, and content triggers. Insight here underpins all downstream choices and determines the sharpness of the brand’s hook.

depth_insight.criterion: Niche selection rigor and strategic fit
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates analysis of market whitespace, competition density, trend momentum, and defensibility, balanced against the creator’s skills, time, and resources. High insight is shown by an explicit fit model (market gap × capability × sustainability) rather than intuition.

depth_insight.criterion: Value proposition and brand differentiation
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the plan articulates a clear, unique promise with reasons-to-believe, anchored in audience needs and contrasted against incumbents. Looks for non-obvious angles and how tone/positioning become strategic levers, not mere stylistic choices.

depth_insight.criterion: Content pillars and expression innovation
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the depth of a content architecture that maps pillars to audience jobs and funnel stages, and proposes distinctive formats/styles with repeatable mechanics. Insight is shown by linking pillars to discoverability, engagement, and conversion pathways.

depth_insight.criterion: Storytelling, serialization, and interactivity mechanisms
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the plan designs narrative arcs, episodic IP, and participatory formats (UGC, challenges, rituals) that create habit and retention, including hooks, cliffhangers, and feedback loops that compound interest over time.

depth_insight.criterion: Growth strategy grounded in platform mechanics
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates understanding of recommendation systems, SEO/keywords, watch-time/retention drivers, cadence effects, and cross-platform flywheels. Depth is shown by leveraging algorithmic edges and tailoring tactics to each platform’s physics.

depth_insight.criterion: Engagement and community flywheel design
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses a deliberate system for fostering belonging and participation (tiers, rituals, recognition, incentives), with tactics for converting casual viewers into contributors and superfans, thereby increasing retention and LTV.

depth_insight.criterion: Measurement framework and experimentation rigor
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates selection of leading and lagging KPIs across the funnel, use of cohort/retention analysis, hypothesis-driven A/B tests, and decision rules. Insight is shown by focusing on causal levers and learning velocity rather than vanity metrics.

depth_insight.criterion: Monetization pathway viability and unit economics
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses staged monetization models with prerequisites (audience size/engagement), pricing/test plans, and unit economics. Depth includes tradeoffs between ads, affiliates, digital products, services, and the roadmap to de-risk them.

depth_insight.criterion: Resource-constrained planning and tradeoffs
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates how time, budget, and skill limitations inform scope, outsourcing/automation choices, and ROI-based prioritization. Insight is shown by designing a minimal viable content portfolio and scaling plan that matches constraints.

depth_insight.criterion: External environment insight (competitive benchmarking, case study transfer, and risk mitigation)
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether competitor teardowns and domestic/international case studies yield transferable principles adapted to this niche; also evaluates identification of key risks (platform, legal, brand safety, burnout) with proactive mitigation paths.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a coherent order (diagnosis → positioning/brand → content pillars & calendar → distribution/growth → engagement/community → measurement/optimization → monetization → resources/risks → references), with each layer logically nested under the prior.

Assesses whether every key recommendation (niche choice, platform mix, posting cadence, monetization path) is explicitly linked back to stated objectives (brand distinctiveness, audience growth, monetization) and shows how actions lead to target outcomes.

Evaluates whether stated constraints (time, budget, skills) are consistently carried through to all decisions (e.g., feasible content volume, platform count, production complexity) without contradictions.

logical_coherence.criterion: Justification of Decisions with Relevant Criteria
logical_coherence.explanation: Verifies that each major choice is supported by logical criteria (e.g., audience–platform fit, niche attractiveness, algorithm dynamics, resource capacity) rather than assertion, and that criteria are applied consistently.
Checks that outputs from one section are explicitly used as inputs to the next (e.g., persona → value proposition → content themes → calendar), avoiding logical jumps or isolated modules.
Assesses whether KPIs map to objectives, include thresholds or decision rules, and trigger specific optimization actions and timelines, forming a clear feedback loop rather than standalone reporting.

Evaluates whether the plan identifies key alternatives (e.g., multi-platform vs. single-platform focus, depth vs. frequency), articulates decision criteria, and provides fallback scenarios for uncertainty.
Checks numerical consistency (e.g., total content pieces implied by frequency × platforms × formats matches resource capacity; monetization timeline aligns with projected audience milestones).

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Layout and Navigability Across Required Sections
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether all requested modules (audience, niche, value proposition, brand tone, content themes/styles, 3-month calendar, storytelling/serialization/interaction, growth strategy, engagement/community/iteration, KPI tracking/optimization, monetization, resource allocation, competitive analysis/differentiation, risks/mitigation, case studies) are clearly segmented with a consistent heading hierarchy, numbering, TOC, and cross-references so readers can quickly locate information.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity, Brevity, and Actionable Instructions
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether the prose is concise, unambiguous, and uses actionable phrasing (e.g., imperative steps, owner, deadline, frequency). Avoids vague qualifiers and run-on sentences; uses concrete wording that makes execution steps easy to follow.

clarity_readability.criterion: Definition and Consistent Use of Technical Terms and Acronyms
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether domain terms (e.g., ICP, CTR, CPM, CAC/LTV, DAU/MAU, retention/cohorts, UGC, A/B testing, SEO, content pillars, funnel) are defined at first mention, optionally summarized in a mini-glossary, and used consistently with units and time windows to prevent misinterpretation.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity and Usability of Operational Artifacts (tables, calendars, checklists, templates)
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that the 3-month content calendar, resource plans, community playbooks, and risk registers are readable and self-explanatory: clear column headers (e.g., date, platform, topic, format, CTA, asset, owner, status), consistent date/number formats, legends for color-coding, and example-filled templates that make immediate use straightforward.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness and Self-Containment of Data/Charts for KPIs
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether KPI visuals (e.g., trend lines, funnels, cohort charts, bar charts vs targets) are legible with labeled axes, units, time frames, target/benchmark lines, and captions that state the key takeaway in plain language; visuals should be placed near the relevant text and not over-stylized.

clarity_readability.criterion: Scannability and Emphasis of Key Points
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates the use of bullets, numbered steps, callouts, one-idea-per-paragraph structure, adequate whitespace, and consistent emphasis (bold/italics) to highlight key numbers, decisions, and deadlines, enabling quick skim-reading without losing core instructions.

clarity_readability.criterion: Executive Summary and Quick-Start Guide Readability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for a concise front-matter summary that surfaces objectives, target audience, niche, content pillars, posting cadence, initial KPI targets, and a first-week checklist so readers can grasp the plan’s essentials and begin execution rapidly.

task: I would like to update my skincare products within the next month to choose ones that best suit my skin type and needs. My skin is combination type—my T-zone tends to be oily, especially by afternoon when it becomes noticeably shiny, while my cheeks are quite dry and feel tight after washing. Recently, I’ve been concerned about dullness and the appearance of fine lines, and I hope to see improvements through skincare.My skincare goals are mainly anti-aging and brightening. My total budget is under ¥2,000. I want to first establish a healthier skin condition before further targeting specific concerns. I have both morning and evening skincare routines, usually including facial cleanser, toner, and face cream.Please provide me with a skincare plan, including basic steps and product recommendations, and compare the ingredients, effects, and prices of different brands to help me find the most suitable choice.

goal_alignment.criterion: Budget adherence and transparent cost-effectiveness breakdown
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the plan strictly stays under ¥2,000 with an itemized cost list in RMB, shows per-ml/per-month value, and prioritizes spending across steps (e.g., invest more in sunscreen/actives) to match a rational, cost-conscious student’s decision style.

goal_alignment.criterion: Barrier-first and zonal strategy for combination skin
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the report identifies barrier instability as a root issue and proposes ‘stabilize first, then target’, with distinct care for oily T-zone (e.g., niacinamide/zinc, light textures, optional BHA spot use) and dry cheeks (gentle low-pH cleanser, ceramides/cholesterol/fatty acids, occlusive layering).

goal_alignment.criterion: Age-appropriate anti-aging and brightening ingredient design
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses if the plan emphasizes prevention for 19–22 (daily high-UVA sunscreen, antioxidants), gentle brightening (niacinamide, TXA, arbutin), cautious low-strength retinoid introduction, and avoids overly aggressive acids—directly addressing dullness/fine lines without undue irritation.

goal_alignment.criterion: Comparative, evidence-based multi-brand options with China availability
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for at least 2–3 brand options per step with side-by-side comparison of key ingredients, mechanisms, expected effects, and prices in RMB, and confirms availability on common China retail channels—aligning with her research-driven, comparison-shopping habits.

goal_alignment.criterion: Day/night routine tailored to a student’s time budget
goal_alignment.explanation: Verifies a quick, efficient AM routine (≤5 minutes) with reapplication strategy for campus, and a slightly richer PM routine leveraging her reflective evenings, plus clear weekly add-ons (e.g., mask) that fit a dorm schedule.

goal_alignment.criterion: Adaptation to Beijing climate, pollution, dorm life, and exercise
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates context sensitivity: recommendations for dry, heated winters and pollution (hydration/barrier support, antioxidants), low- or no-fragrance choices and compact, dorm-friendly packaging, plus sweat-resistant sunscreen and on-the-go reapplication for running/outdoor activities.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk management and one-month onboarding plan
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the report gives a phased introduction schedule (one new product every 3–4 days), patch testing, retinoid ramp-up, avoiding conflicting actives on the same night, and red-flag guidance—matching her risk-averse, productivity-focused needs.

goal_alignment.criterion: Logical structure and evidence alignment with law-student reasoning
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for clear claims-evidence-conclusion flow, concise ingredient rationales (why it works for her), and minimal marketing fluff—resonating with her analytical, detail-oriented decision style.

goal_alignment.criterion: Practical usage details and purchasability
