actionability_practicality.criterion: Room-by-room prioritized shopping list and phased execution plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for an essentials-vs-optional list per room with quantities, a phased purchasing timeline (what to buy before move-in vs later), and fallback options at different price bands so the user can act immediately despite limited time.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Purchase channels, availability, lead times, and delivery/installation sequencing
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies that the report provides concrete purchase channels available in Shanghai (e.g., JD/Tmall/Suning/IKEA/local showrooms), typical lead times, stock notes, and a logical delivery/installation order (e.g., measure/order curtains early, large furniture before décor, appliance installation appointments) to reduce delays.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Pet- and child-friendly materials and safety measures
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for specific, purchasable material/feature guidance (scratch-resistant and easy-clean fabrics, removable washable covers, low-pile rugs, rounded corners, anti-tip brackets, cord management, non-toxic finishes) that reduce maintenance and hazards in daily use.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Cost-performance and total cost of ownership (TCO) justification
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether recommendations include clear reasons to choose one option over another based on durability, maintenance effort, and estimated annual energy/water costs, helping the user make rational, long-term cost-effective decisions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Sustainability and health standards implemented practically within budget
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the plan translates sustainability/health into concrete, accessible specs (e.g., E1/E0 formaldehyde boards, low-VOC finishes, FSC options, high energy-efficiency labels) that can be purchased locally without breaching the budget.

depth_insight.criterion: Budget Allocation Strategy and Trade-off Justification
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report proposes a defensible split of the ¥100,000 across furniture, appliances, and décor, with reasoned trade-offs (e.g., where to splurge vs save), contingency reserves, and alignment to the household’s must-haves (fridge, washer) and modernization goals.

depth_insight.criterion: Space–Function Fit and Scale for an 89㎡ Modern 3BR
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates depth of analysis on sizing, circulation, and multifunctional choices tailored to a compact 89㎡ layout (e.g., storage beds, modular sofas, dining table size), and how selections layer with white walls/gray floors to maintain modern cohesion.

depth_insight.criterion: Appliance Constraint Handling and Technical Rigor
depth_insight.explanation: Measures how thoroughly the report addresses refrigerator/washing machine fit (exact dimensions, door swing, ventilation), energy efficiency, noise, water/electrical/venting needs, and limited smart-connectivity (offline controls, 2.4GHz, ecosystem compatibility).

depth_insight.criterion: Comparative Evaluation of Brands, Materials, and Design Styles
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether recommendations compare options on price bands, material quality (e.g., solid wood vs engineered board, fabric durability), build/feature differences, design language, and provide clear reasoning for chosen brands/models for this modern style.

depth_insight.criterion: Cost-Performance and Total Cost of Ownership Analysis
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates depth in assessing lifetime value beyond sticker price—energy use, durability, maintenance, warranty/after-sales, modular expandability, and bundled discounts—to justify selections within the tight budget.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk and Feasibility Controls
depth_insight.explanation: Checks for insight into measurement tolerances, delivery/installation constraints (elevator, doorways), lead times, color/finish matching risk with existing hard finishes, and fallback options if size or connectivity constraints fail.

depth_insight.criterion: Original, Actionable Market Insights
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards unique tactics that increase value—e.g., seasonal sales timing, bundle negotiations, high–low mix strategies, outlet/sample options, or modular custom solutions—tailored to the user’s constraints and budget.

logical_coherence.criterion: Overall analytical framework aligned to scope and objectives
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report begins by structuring the task around the stated inputs (89㎡, 3BR1LR, modern style, ¥100k budget, required refrigerator and washer, kitchen connectivity/size limits) and organizes the plan top-down (e.g., category and room breakdown) so all subsequent decisions map back to defined goals.

logical_coherence.criterion: Budget allocation logic and internal consistency
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the report allocates the ¥100k across furniture, appliances, and décor (and optionally by room), explains the rationale (e.g., must-haves vs nice-to-haves, usage frequency, durability), and maintains internal consistency (subtotals roll up correctly; trade-offs are explicitly handled when adding/removing items).

Assesses whether constraints (kitchen smart connectivity limits, appliance dimensions, space planning, delivery clearances, power/load considerations) are converted into explicit selection criteria and filters, and whether all recommended items are shown to satisfy these constraints.

Checks that each recommendation is justified by relevant, comparable attributes (price bands, materials, design style fit with modern white/gray, cost-performance) and that comparisons use a consistent attribute set across brands/options to avoid cherry-picking.

logical_coherence.criterion: Reasoning chain from premises to recommendations
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the report states decision criteria (e.g., durability for sofa, capacity and door opening for fridge, noise for washer) and shows a stepwise derivation from these premises to specific product/brand choices without logical leaps.

Verifies that item-level prices aggregate correctly to room/category totals and to the overall ¥100k, that unit assumptions (e.g., quantity per room) are explicit, and that any contingency/reserve is accounted for without double-counting.

Checks for explicit assumptions (e.g., standard bed sizes per room, typical appliance capacities for 89㎡, price ranges) and ensures they are applied consistently across sections without contradiction.

logical_coherence.criterion: Transitions and hierarchical coherence between sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether section sequencing is logically justified (e.g., from overall budget strategy to room plans to item comparisons to purchase order), with transitions that explain why the next step follows from prior decisions, avoiding abrupt topic shifts.

clarity_readability.criterion: Task-tailored structure, layout, and navigation
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear hierarchy and navigation aligned to the shopping workflow (e.g., overview, budget summary, room-by-room BOM, appliance shortlist, size/fit checklist, purchasing timeline, appendices), with consistent headings, bullet lists, white space, and section numbering for quick scanning.

clarity_readability.criterion: Plain, precise, and consistent language
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether wording is concise and unambiguous (short sentences, active voice), avoids marketing fluff, and keeps naming/labels consistent across items (e.g., same unit names, brand/series/size order), reducing misinterpretation during purchase decisions.

clarity_readability.criterion: Budget and numeric presentation clarity
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates if budget data are shown in clear tables with subtotals and totals (furniture/appliances/décor), percentages, unit prices vs. set prices, ranges, delivery/installation costs, and consistent currency/number formatting, enabling immediate understanding of the ¥100,000 allocation.

clarity_readability.criterion: Specification and measurement readability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks if dimensions, clearances, and installation requirements are presented in standardized spec blocks (units, counter-depth, door swing, water/power points), with simple fit checklists for fridge/washer and notes on smart connectivity constraints, to prevent sizing/compatibility errors.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of tables, charts, and visual aids
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether comparison tables (brand vs. price/material/features), budget charts, lead-time timelines, color swatches/mood boards, and annotated floor sketches are well-labeled (titles, captions, legends, units), readable on screen/print, and chosen with appropriate chart types.

clarity_readability.criterion: Explanation and standardization of technical terms
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether key terms (e.g., E1/E0 formaldehyde, solid wood vs. veneer, HPL, inverter, energy-efficiency grades, decibels, W/D capacity, Wi‑Fi bands, Matter/Zigbee) are defined at first use or in a glossary, with consistent abbreviations and units to lower comprehension barriers.

clarity_readability.criterion: Emphasis and retrievability of key constraints and priorities
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks use of callouts/highlights/icons to surface must-haves (refrigerator, washer), kitchen size/connectivity limits, and the total budget cap, ensuring readers can quickly locate constraints and priorities without hunting through dense text.

task: I have just completed the hard furnishing of my home and am now ready to move on to the soft furnishing stage. My home is 89㎡, with three bedrooms and one living room. The hard furnishing style is modern, featuring white walls and gray floors. I now want to create a shopping plan that includes furniture, home appliances, and accessories.My total soft furnishing budget is about ¥100,000, and I want to allocate it reasonably among furniture, appliances, and décor. I need a refrigerator and washing machine, but I also have some limitations regarding the kitchen’s smart appliance connectivity and size. Please recommend suitable furniture brands, appliance options, and accessories, comparing their prices, materials, design styles, and cost performance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Deep persona–task understanding and translation of implicit needs
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report identifies and operationalizes Liu Lu’s unique context: data-driven decision style, evening study routine, modern white/gray base, 89㎡ three-bedroom use patterns for a single professional, pet ownership, eco-consciousness, and back discomfort. Check if these are explicitly translated into requirements (quiet/efficient appliances, ergonomic home office, pet-friendly materials, eco labels, flexible storage, lighting for evening reading) rather than generic advice.

goal_alignment.criterion: Rational budget allocation and cost-performance modeling within ¥100,000
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan provides a clear, justified split among furniture, appliances, and decor tailored to a modern 89㎡ layout, with itemized price bands, alternatives (good/better/best), and sensitivity for promotions. It should include life-cycle cost thinking (energy/water usage, warranty length, expected lifespan) to fit her rational, long-term value mindset and monthly big-purchase rhythm.

goal_alignment.criterion: Appliance selection aligned with kitchen size and smart-connectivity constraints
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether refrigerator and washing machine recommendations concretely address footprint limits common in Shanghai kitchens (e.g., 600mm width cabinetry, depth/ventilation clearances), noise targets for evening study, capacity needs for a single owner with a cat, energy label Class 1, and smart features that work offline or within a compatible ecosystem. Expect measurement checklists, model examples with prices, and explicit compatibility notes.

goal_alignment.criterion: Functional zoning and measurement-driven space planning for a single professional with a pet
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report proposes a coherent room-by-room plan: ergonomic home office (chair/desk specs for back support), multi-purpose guest/fitness/storage use, dedicated pet zones (scratch-resistant, easy-clean, anti-tip), kitchen workflow for cooking hobbies, and circulation clearances. Recommendations should be dimension-based and suited to 89㎡ three-bedroom layouts.

goal_alignment.criterion: Style/material cohesion with modern boutique-hotel warmth, pet- and eco-safety
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether brands/materials match the modern white/gray hard-finish base while introducing boutique-hotel textures (lighting layers, textiles, wood tones) and ensuring durability: cat-scratch-resistant upholstery, washable rugs/covers, low-VOC and OEKO-TEX/FSC credentials. This ties her aesthetic preference to practical, sustainable, long-term choices.

goal_alignment.criterion: Data-analytic decision support: structured comparisons, metrics, and tools
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for comparison matrices with explicit criteria (price, materials, dimensions, efficiency, noise, warranty), pros/cons, and prioritized shortlists. Look for decision frameworks (e.g., MoSCoW), measurement and delivery checklists, and Notion/Trello-ready tables/templates, aligning with her analytical workflow and need for evidence-backed decisions.

goal_alignment.criterion: Local procurement feasibility for Shanghai (availability, logistics, after-sales)
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether recommendations consider Shanghai-specific availability, installation constraints (elevators, door widths), delivery fees/lead times, reputable purchase channels (e.g., JD/Tmall flagship), and after-sales/warranty service coverage, plus alignment with local sales events to match her monthly big-purchase habits.

goal_alignment.criterion: Maintenance, durability, and life-cycle practicality
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan includes cleaning/maintenance guidance (washable textiles, replaceable covers, scratch-resistant finishes), consumables schedules (filters), and durability expectations that minimize future hassle and align with her preference for high-quality, long-lasting products.

content_alignment.criterion: Data-Rich Comparative Depth and Cost-Performance Framing
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report provides quantified comparisons (RMB price bands, material grades, energy labels, noise dB, warranty years, expected lifespan, maintenance costs) and a clear TCO/cost-performance rationale, matching the user’s analytical decision style and need for evidence-backed choices.

content_alignment.criterion: Fit-to-Space and Smart-Connectivity Constraints for Kitchen/Laundry
content_alignment.explanation: Check if refrigerator/washer recommendations explicitly address exact dimensions, depth/door swing clearances, ventilation gaps, plumbing/power constraints, and favor offline/manual-control or minimally connected models given limited smart connectivity, preventing ecosystem lock-in.

content_alignment.criterion: Pet-Friendly and Easy-Maintenance Material Choices
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether content accounts for living with a cat: scratch-resistant upholstery (e.g., microfiber/performance fabrics), tight-weave/washable rugs, easy-clean finishes, concealed cable management, and integrated pet solutions (e.g., hidden litter box furniture) without sacrificing modern aesthetics.

content_alignment.criterion: Budget Allocation Tailored to Long-Term Value Priorities
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the budget split across furniture/appliances/decor reflects the user’s cost-effectiveness focus (e.g., invest in durability-critical items like mattress, ergonomic chair, core appliances; economize on easily upgradable decor) and includes trade-off logic aligned with her usage.

content_alignment.criterion: Ergonomic and Health-Conscious Specifications
content_alignment.explanation: Determine if the content integrates ergonomic benchmarks relevant to her back discomfort and work habits (e.g., chair adjustability ranges, desk height options, sofa seat depth/height, mattress firmness guidance, anti-fatigue kitchen mats) and low-noise appliances for comfort.

content_alignment.criterion: Sustainability and Indoor Air Quality Considerations
content_alignment.explanation: Check for coverage of low-VOC/low-formaldehyde materials (E1/E0, relevant certifications), FSC/Greenguard-type references, energy-efficiency Grade 1 appliances, water-saving features, and guidance that aligns with her interest in environmental protection and healthier living.

content_alignment.criterion: Aesthetic Coherence with Modern Minimal Base and Boutique-Hotel Cues
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether brand/style/material selections align with her modern hard-furnishing baseline (white walls, gray floors) and taste for refined boutique aesthetics—neutral palette, texture layering (wood/metal/fabric), clean lines—avoiding incongruent styles.

content_alignment.criterion: Shanghai/China Market Specificity (Brands, Prices, After-Sales)
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether content references locally relevant brands/models, realistic China price bands (e.g., JD/Tmall), warranty/installation norms, and service networks common in Shanghai, ensuring recommendations are locally obtainable and supportable.

content_alignment.criterion: Lifestyle Scenario Coverage (Single Professional, Cooking, WFH, Guests, Fitness)
content_alignment.explanation: Determine if the plan reflects her real-life usage: WFH/study setup, storage for fitness gear, optional cooking-supportive appliances within budget/space limits, convertible guest room for parents, and layouts that maintain efficiency and order.

presentation_fit.criterion: Macro structure aligned to decision flow (exec summary → budget allocation → category/room sections → constraints)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether the report opens with a concise TL;DR and budget split, then organizes content by Furniture/Appliances/Décor and room-by-room packs, with a dedicated section highlighting kitchen connectivity/size constraints—matching a data analyst’s top-down reading habit.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use of compact comparison tables for key choices
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether furniture and appliances are compared in tables with clear columns (price range, dimensions, capacity, materials, style, energy label, noise, connectivity, warranty). Tables should be screenshot-friendly and easy to paste into Notion/Trello.

presentation_fit.criterion: Metric precision and unit consistency
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks that all specs use consistent, standard units (mm/cm, L, dB, kWh/yr), with clear dimension notation (W×D×H) and fit/clearance guidance. This supports accurate, data-driven decisions and minimizes misinterpretation.

presentation_fit.criterion: Concise, analytical tone and terminology scaffolding
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether language is neutral and non-promotional, uses precise terms correctly, and provides brief parenthetical explanations for technical terms (e.g., inverter motor, laminate vs. solid wood) to reduce cognitive load without dumbing down.

presentation_fit.criterion: Localization to Mainland China consumer context
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks for ¥ pricing, China Energy Label levels, locally available brands/models, and notes on local installation norms and app/connectivity ecosystems—ensuring the presentation feels native and immediately interpretable in Shanghai.

presentation_fit.criterion: Decision aids and visual emphasis cues
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates the presence of pros/cons bullets, badges/tags (e.g., [Best Value], [Compact], [Low Noise], [Pet-friendly]), and callouts for critical constraints (kitchen cutout sizes, 2.4 GHz Wi‑Fi, doorway widths), enabling quick triage and prioritization.

presentation_fit.criterion: Scannability and tool-friendly formatting (Notion/Trello)
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks for copy-pasteable checklists (with quantity, target price, status), consistent delimiters, and simple tables/headings that import cleanly—reflecting the user’s habit of organizing purchases as tasks.

presentation_fit.criterion: Budget visualization and traceability
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether the budget split is shown with a simple visual (pie/bar) or clear numeric breakdown, includes subtotals per category/room, and indicates price assumptions/date—improving financial clarity without content creep.

presentation_fit.criterion: Clear visual hierarchy and readability
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks the consistent use of headings, numbering, short paragraphs, and whitespace so sections are easily navigable and skimmable during evening review sessions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget realism and category allocation with total cost control
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report presents a clear allocation of the ¥100,000 budget across furniture, appliances, and décor; provides itemized costs (including delivery/installation, accessories, warranties) and a contingency buffer so the total stays within budget.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Physical and technical constraint compliance (dimensions, utilities, smart connectivity)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies that recommended furniture and appliances fit the 89㎡ layout and door/elevator clearances, and that appliances match kitchen size limits, electrical load, water/drain requirements, ventilation, and stated smart connectivity limitations.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Specificity and comparability of recommendations (models, specs, price, materials)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides concrete SKUs/models with up-to-date prices, key specs (capacity, materials, finishes), and side-by-side comparisons to enable fast, evidence-based decisions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Local availability and serviceability in Shanghai (channels, lead times, delivery/installation)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Ensures items are readily purchasable via mainstream Shanghai channels (e.g., Tmall/JD/brand stores), with stated lead times, delivery fees, installation options, and after-sales support.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Installation, measurement, and logistics execution plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for a practical plan covering measurement checklists, sequencing (what to order first), scheduling, vendor coordination, packaging disposal, and risk points (e.g., elevator booking) to reduce delays and returns.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Durability, maintenance, and pet-friendly practicality
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether materials and finishes are scratch-resistant/easy to clean (for a cat), removable/washable covers, stain-resistant rugs, and whether appliance noise levels suit an apartment.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Energy efficiency and lifetime operating cost considerations
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks inclusion of energy/water efficiency ratings and estimated operating costs for appliances, supporting cost-performance decisions beyond upfront prices.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Prioritization and trade-off guidance under budget changes
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides clear rules for what to upgrade/downgrade or defer (e.g., prioritize mattress/sofa/fridge/washing machine) and suggests substitutes if items are out of stock or over budget.

depth_insight.criterion: Budget Allocation Strategy and Justified Trade-offs (¥100k, 89㎡, 3BR1LR)
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report translates the total budget into a reasoned category/room allocation (furniture vs appliances vs décor), quantifies priorities, includes a contingency, and shows sensitivity (e.g., upgrade/downgrade paths) to maximize value under the stated constraints.

depth_insight.criterion: Measurement-driven Space Fit and Function Optimization
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether selections are grounded in the 89㎡ layout and ergonomic standards—accounting for exact dimensions, clearances, door swings, traffic flow, storage needs, and multifunction solutions to avoid misfits and optimize limited space.

depth_insight.criterion: Appliance Ecosystem and Size-Constrained Specification Insight (Fridge & Washer)
depth_insight.explanation: Checks if the report addresses kitchen connectivity limits and size by recommending compatible ecosystems (or robust offline modes), precise capacity/dimension specs, energy ratings, noise, venting/drainage, and realistic placement (e.g., cabinet depth, balcony stack) with trade-offs.

depth_insight.criterion: Materials, Durability, and Health/Safety Standards Evaluation
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses the depth of analysis on furniture and textile materials (e.g., E0/E1 emissions, veneer vs solid wood, fabric abrasion/stain resistance), finishes, maintenance, and safety—linking material choices to longevity and living quality in a modern home.

depth_insight.criterion: Modern Style Cohesion and Texture/Color Strategy on White/Gray Base
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan demonstrates a deliberate aesthetic strategy—balancing warm/cool tones, textures, and accent colors/metals—so furniture, appliances, and accessories form a cohesive modern look rather than ad hoc purchases.

depth_insight.criterion: Cost-Performance and Lifecycle Value (TCO, Energy, Warranty)
depth_insight.explanation: Judges whether the report goes beyond sticker price to compare total cost of ownership, energy savings, expected lifespan, warranty/after-sales, and bundle opportunities—identifying where to splurge vs save for maximum value.

depth_insight.criterion: Comparative Brand/Model Analysis with Evidence
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for substantive comparisons among brands/models across furniture, appliances, and accessories—contrasting price bands, materials, design styles, and pros/cons with evidence or benchmarks rather than superficial listing.

depth_insight.criterion: Procurement Risks and Execution Practicality Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report anticipates delivery/installation constraints (elevator/door widths, appliance hookups), lead times, return policies, and timing of purchases (e.g., promotional cycles), providing actionable risk mitigations.

logical_coherence.criterion: Explicit Problem Definition and Constraint Anchoring
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report states and systematically uses all premises (89㎡, 3BR1LR, modern white/gray style, ¥100,000 total budget, must-have refrigerator and washing machine, kitchen size/connectivity limits) as anchors for subsequent allocations and selections, ensuring conclusions trace back to initial constraints.

logical_coherence.criterion: Budget Allocation Logic and Numerical Consistency
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether category budgets (furniture/appliances/decor) are justified by reasoning (e.g., priorities, market price bands), itemized with prices, and sum correctly to the total; verifies no double counting, inclusion of delivery/installation where relevant, and a clear contingency or buffer consistent across sections.

Assesses whether the refrigerator and washing machine recommendations logically follow from explicit requirements: kitchen aperture dimensions and door swing, depth clearance, capacity needs for a 3BR household, power/water constraints, and smart-connectivity limitations (e.g., Wi‑Fi protocols, offline control), with each chosen model meeting those specs.

Checks that brand/options are compared using a consistent set of criteria (price range, materials/build quality, design style fit to modern white/gray, warranty/after-sales, cost-performance), applied uniformly so that claims (e.g., ‘better value’) are evidence-linked rather than impressionistic.

logical_coherence.criterion: Room-by-Room Functional Layout and Sizing Logic
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether furniture selection follows a logical room-by-room process with measurement-based fit (sofas to living room span, bed sizes to bedroom clearances, storage to circulation), and that adjacency and use-sequence reasoning underpins placements, preventing infeasible or conflicting recommendations.

logical_coherence.criterion: Trade-off and Prioritization Rationale Under Budget/Style Constraints
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the report explains where to invest versus economize (e.g., sofa/mattress quality over decorative accessories) given the ¥100,000 cap and modern style premise, and whether these trade-offs lead to coherent final selections aligned with the stated priorities.

Checks that the report moves in a logically progressive flow (constraints → budget allocation → room planning → item comparisons → final list) and uses cross-references (e.g., a chosen fridge citing earlier aperture and connectivity constraints) to avoid logical jumps between sections.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structured, Scannable Layout (Room-by-Room and Category Sections)
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report uses a clear hierarchy (e.g., Overview, Budget Allocation, Living Room, Bedrooms, Kitchen, Appliances, Accessories) with headings, bullet lists, and adequate white space so readers can quickly locate what to buy for each space.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity, Concision, and Accuracy of Language
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether sentences are concise and unambiguous, brand/model names are written consistently, and recommendations are stated plainly (e.g., must-have vs optional), avoiding marketing fluff and minimizing cognitive load.

clarity_readability.criterion: Use and Explanation of Technical Terms & Specs
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether necessary terms (e.g., inverter, E0 board, counter-depth, energy efficiency grades, Wi‑Fi protocols) are used correctly and briefly defined in context so non-expert readers can understand specs without external lookup.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Visual Comparisons (Tables, Matrices, Checklists)
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks if comparisons (brand/model, price ranges, materials, design styles, energy ratings) are presented in readable tables or matrices with clear labels, sorting, and captions, and if room-by-room checklists are easy to scan and act on.

clarity_readability.criterion: Budget and Numeric Presentation Consistency (Currency, Units, Ranges)
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether currency is consistently formatted (¥ with thousands separators), per-room/category subtotals sum to the total, date of price quotes is noted, and dimensions/capacities use consistent units (mm, cm, L, kg) and notation (L×W×H).

clarity_readability.criterion: Visibility of Constraints and Fit Checks
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether size/connectivity limits (e.g., max fridge width/depth, door swing clearance, washer depth, ventilation gaps, smart platform compatibility) are highlighted via callouts/warnings and supported by a measurement checklist so readers don’t miss critical constraints.

clarity_readability.criterion: Executive Summary and Quick-Buy Checklist
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for a concise TL;DR that states budget allocation percentages and a short list of top picks (e.g., 2–3 fridges and washers with model codes), plus a room-by-room checklist to enable fast decision-making.

task: I have just completed the hard furnishing of my home and am now ready to move on to the soft furnishing stage. My home is 89㎡, with three bedrooms and one living room. The hard furnishing style is modern, featuring white walls and gray floors. I now want to create a shopping plan that includes furniture, home appliances, and accessories.My total soft furnishing budget is about ¥100,000, and I want to allocate it reasonably among furniture, appliances, and décor. I need a refrigerator and washing machine, but I also have some limitations regarding the kitchen’s smart appliance connectivity and size. Please recommend suitable furniture brands, appliance options, and accessories, comparing their prices, materials, design styles, and cost performance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Constraint-accurate appliance fit (size/connectivity) for fridge and washer
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks whether recommendations specify exact product dimensions, door swing, installation clearances, ventilation needs, and match limited smart connectivity (offline-first control, 2.4GHz Wi‑Fi if used, no ecosystem lock-in), ensuring they physically fit a small Chengdu kitchen and work despite connectivity limits.

goal_alignment.criterion: Budget allocation with cost-performance transparency
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the ¥100,000 budget is split logically across furniture/appliances/décor with clear trade-offs, price bands, and value rationale (e.g., what to prioritize vs. where to save), including per-item target price ranges and total tracking to prevent overruns—aligned to a rational, value-focused buyer.

goal_alignment.criterion: Family- and pet-oriented material and safety design
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether furniture and décor choices consider a 6-year-old child and Alaskan Malamute: stain/scratch-resistant materials, washable/replaceable covers, rounded edges, anti-tip anchoring, easy-clean finishes, low noise appliances, and storage for Legos/art supplies.

goal_alignment.criterion: Procurement sequencing and logistics plan (supply-chain thinking)
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks for a structured purchasing/install timeline, lead times, coordinated deliveries, measurement checklists, return/exchange contingencies, and backup SKUs—matching the user’s supply chain mindset and desire for efficiency and risk control.

goal_alignment.criterion: Brand/channel strategy aligned to JD/Tmall and Chengdu after-sales
goal_alignment.explanation: Verifies that brand and model picks include JD/Tmall availability, typical online price ranges, warranty/installation serviceability in Chengdu, and reference to user reviews/ratings—supporting his online buying habits and risk-aware decisions.

goal_alignment.criterion: Smart ecosystem minimalism and future-proofing
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan favors standards and devices that function fully without stable internet, avoid ecosystem lock-in, and allow later upgrades (important since he updates devices every six months), with simple integration paths if connectivity improves.

goal_alignment.criterion: Style coherence with modern white/gray and warmth layering
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether selections reinforce a modern aesthetic and balance the cool white/gray shell with textures/colors (wood tones, textiles, lighting) to add warmth and family friendliness, ensuring visual consistency across rooms.

goal_alignment.criterion: Local lifestyle and climate adaptation
goal_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the plan addresses Chengdu humidity (anti-mold materials, drying/dehumidification) and Sichuan cooking (odor/grease management, fridge odor control), as well as apartment noise considerations.

goal_alignment.criterion: Sustainability and health standards compliance
goal_alignment.explanation: Verifies emphasis on low-VOC/low-formaldehyde boards (E0/E1), energy- and water-efficient appliances, durable materials to extend lifecycle, and when sensible, second-hand options—matching his eco-conscious consumption trend.

goal_alignment.criterion: Decision-support formatting and comparative rigor
goal_alignment.explanation: Assesses the presence of executive summaries, checklists, and side-by-side comparisons (price, materials, design style, specs, cost-performance, TCO) to facilitate quick, informed decisions in his preferred morning planning window.

content_alignment.criterion: Appliance Fit to Kitchen Constraints and Smart Ecosystem Compatibility
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether fridge/washer recommendations include precise dimensions (width/depth/door swing/hinge clearance), capacity trade-offs for small kitchens, and explicit compatibility with common China smart platforms (Mijia, HarmonyOS, Haier U+), reflecting the user’s stated size and connectivity limits.

content_alignment.criterion: Budget Allocation and Cost-Performance Tailored to ¥100,000 for 89㎡
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the content proposes a rational split across furniture/appliances/décor with China-market price bands and points out high-value picks, aligning with the user’s cost-performance focus and overall budget.

content_alignment.criterion: Family- and Pet-Oriented Material/Feature Selection
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether furniture and appliances account for a six-year-old child and a large dog: easy-clean and scratch-resistant finishes, rounded corners, washable slipcovers, low-noise/high-hygiene washer cycles, and storage for toys/art supplies.

content_alignment.criterion: Eco-Health Alignment (Energy/Water Efficiency and Low-VOC Materials)
content_alignment.explanation: Verify inclusion of China Energy Label Level 1 appliances, water-saving features, and furniture/décor materials with E0/CARB P2/FSC/low-VOC specs to match the user’s rising sustainability and family health priorities.

content_alignment.criterion: China Local Market and Channel Specificity
content_alignment.explanation: Check that recommended brands/models are commonly available in China with typical street prices, JD自营/Tmall旗舰店 options, clear warranty/after-sales coverage in Chengdu, and service network reliability.

content_alignment.criterion: Style Cohesion with Modern White/Gray Palette
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether selections fit a modern aesthetic—clean lines, matte/neutrals, coordinated textures—and offer color/material pairings that complement white walls and gray floors without drifting into ornate styles.

content_alignment.criterion: Data-Backed Comparisons at the Right Technical Depth
content_alignment.explanation: Confirm the report uses concrete specs and comparable metrics (price, materials, dimensions, capacity, energy kWh/year, noise dB, warranty) and summarizes user-review insights, fitting the user’s analytical decision style.

content_alignment.criterion: Space-Appropriate Planning for an 89㎡ Three-Bedroom
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether furniture sizing and categories are scaled for 89㎡ and include multifunction/storage solutions (e.g., sofa bed, lift-top table, wardrobes), while considering circulation with a large dog.

content_alignment.criterion: Maintenance and Lifecycle Considerations
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether content addresses durability, replaceable parts/surfaces (e.g., slipcovers), easy maintenance, and robust warranties, aligning with the user’s efficiency and long-term value mindset.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structural hierarchy and scannability (clear headings, numbering, room-based sections, bullet lists)
presentation_fit.explanation: A supply chain manager expects report-like organization. Clear sectioning by category/room and numbered steps reduce cognitive load and support quick scanning and bookmarking.

presentation_fit.criterion: Professional, data-driven tone with concise language and no marketing fluff
presentation_fit.explanation: A pragmatic, rational reader prefers neutral, fact-focused wording. Avoiding hype improves trust and speeds comprehension.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology clarity with brief in-line definitions for technical terms
presentation_fit.explanation: Short parenthetical explanations for terms like E1/E0 formaldehyde grade, inverter, energy label, and smart ecosystem names keep the text readable without breaking flow.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use of standardized comparison tables/matrices for furniture and appliances
presentation_fit.explanation: Side-by-side tables with consistent columns (price, material, size, style, energy rating, noise, warranty, ecosystem) match his data-comparison habit and facilitate decisions.

presentation_fit.criterion: Budget visualization and room/category breakdown (charts and mini-tables)
presentation_fit.explanation: Pie/bar charts and per-room/category subtotals provide an at-a-glance understanding of the ¥100,000 allocation, aiding planning and family discussion.

presentation_fit.criterion: Consistent units, metrics, and standards (mm/m²/L/kg, China energy/water labels) and currency formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Precision and consistency in units and labels are crucial for fit and credibility; clear ¥ formatting and ranges reduce ambiguity.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-friendly formatting for WeChat/DingTalk reading
presentation_fit.explanation: Short paragraphs, responsive/narrow tables, readable fonts, and chunked sections ensure usability on phones, aligning with his daily app usage.

presentation_fit.criterion: Decision aids visualization (scorecards, traffic-light ratings) with clear legends
presentation_fit.explanation: Simple visual ratings translate complex trade-offs (price-performance, durability, energy efficiency) into quick cues without heavy text.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visual highlighting of constraints and compatibility (dimension fit, ventilation clearance, smart ecosystem icons)
presentation_fit.explanation: Icons/labels and callouts next to items make kitchen size and smart-connectivity constraints immediately visible, reducing misreads.

presentation_fit.criterion: Family-facing one-page brief (TL;DR) with visuals for spousal review
presentation_fit.explanation: A simplified summary page with key numbers and visuals supports quick alignment with his wife, matching his consultative decision process.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget and category allocation feasibility with contingency
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report provides a clear, itemized budget split across furniture, appliances, and décor (and per room if relevant), includes delivery/installation and hidden costs, keeps the total near ¥100,000, and sets a 5–10% contingency with save/splurge levers so the plan remains executable under real prices.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Dimensional fit and layout/installation feasibility
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether each recommended large item (fridge, washer, sofa, beds, wardrobes) has exact dimensions, required clearances, and installation notes (door swing, ventilation gaps, hose routing), plus checks for doorway/elevator passage and traffic flow, ensuring the plan fits the 89㎡ space without rework.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Appliance compatibility with kitchen connectivity, size, and power
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses if refrigerator/washer options match cabinet niches and balcony/kitchen spaces, specify plug types and circuit loads, ventilation needs, Wi‑Fi/mesh/2.4G support or offline control, and ecosystem (e.g., HarmonyOS/Mijia) compatibility given the user’s smart limitations, enabling trouble-free setup.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Sourcing practicality in Chengdu (channels, delivery, installation)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report names purchase channels (JD/Tmall/Suning/local stores), indicates stock/lead times, bundles or promo windows, installation booking steps, and basic vendor selection criteria; optionally flags reliable second-hand options where sensible to improve value without risking delays.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Family- and pet-friendly material choices and durability
actionability_practicality.explanation: Reviews whether recommended furniture and textiles specify wear-resistant, easy-clean, low-VOC materials (e.g., high Martindale fabrics, removable covers, anti-scratch finishes), rounded corners/anti-tip for a child, and hair/dirt management for a large dog—ensuring practical daily use.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Energy efficiency and total cost of ownership (TCO) estimates
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies that appliances list energy efficiency grades, kWh/year, water use, noise, and estimated annual operating costs in RMB, comparing lifecycle costs so the user can judge true cost-performance beyond upfront price.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Executable procurement sequence and timeline
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines whether the report gives a step-by-step schedule (measure → confirm niches/power → order long-lead furniture/curtains → schedule appliances/installation → décor), with dependencies, acceptance checklists, and risk mitigations to reduce delays and change orders.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Decision-enabling comparisons and selection rules
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks that for key categories the report narrows to 2–3 shortlisted options with clear price/material/style/performance comparisons and explicit selection rules (e.g., choose model A if niche ≤60 cm; model B otherwise), enabling fast, confident choices.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Safety and compliance measures
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether safety is operationalized: anti-tip straps for cabinets/TV, child locks, cord management, curtain fire-retardant options where needed, compliance marks (e.g., CCC) and basic indoor air guidance—reducing practical risks during use.

depth_insight.criterion: Task-specific needs translation and space fit
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report converts the 89㎡, 3-bedroom modern home into concrete, room-by-room functional requirements (e.g., storage, circulation, seating capacity), size targets (sofa length, table dimensions, bed sizes), and lifestyle priorities, ensuring recommendations are tailored rather than generic.

depth_insight.criterion: Budget allocation depth and trade-off strategy
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the rigor of allocating ¥100,000 across furniture, appliances, and décor with itemized ranges, must-have vs nice-to-have tiers, contingency (e.g., 5–10%), and identification of high-ROI spend (e.g., sofa, mattress, core appliances) versus areas to economize, including sensitivity to price shifts.

depth_insight.criterion: Appliance constraint handling (fit, installation, and connectivity)
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether refrigerator and washing machine choices reflect precise dimensional planning (niche size, door swing, ventilation clearance), electrical and plumbing requirements, noise/energy considerations for small spaces, and smart connectivity constraints (protocol compatibility, 2.4 GHz Wi‑Fi, ecosystem interoperability) with actionable mitigation.

depth_insight.criterion: Comparative cost-performance analysis (brands, materials, specs)
depth_insight.explanation: Rates the depth of brand and model comparisons using measurable specs and materials (e.g., solid wood vs veneer, foam density, fabric/leather grades, compressor type, energy rating), warranty/after-sales coverage, reliability, and value metrics (e.g., cost per year, durability per yuan) rather than superficial brand name-dropping.

depth_insight.criterion: Design cohesion and modern style integration
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether recommendations create a cohesive modern scheme for white walls and gray floors via color/texture layering (adding warmth with wood tones, textiles), proportion and visual weight control, and small-space solutions (multifunctional pieces, storage integration) that elevate both aesthetics and function.

depth_insight.criterion: Quantitative justification and lifecycle/TCO reasoning
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the use of numbers to justify choices (capacities in L/kg, decibels, kWh/year, dimensions/clearances, expected lifespan), including lifecycle cost or payback analyses (e.g., energy savings over years), demonstrating evidence-based value rather than intuition.

depth_insight.criterion: Scenario-based alternatives and sensitivity
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for differentiated packages or pathways (e.g., heavy cooking vs light cooking; child/pet-friendly vs adult-only; value vs premium focus) that adapt the plan while staying within budget, revealing trade-offs and flexibility under varying user needs or constraints.

depth_insight.criterion: Procurement and risk/market insights
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses practical insights that improve real outcomes: timing purchases around major sales, bundle discounts, lead times, delivery/installation fees, doorway/access checks, material authenticity and VOC considerations, warranty terms, and after-sales service risks.

logical_coherence.criterion: Task-grounded analytical framework (requirements → allocation → room-by-room plan → comparisons → final list)
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report explicitly anchors on the home’s specs (89㎡, 3BR, modern white/gray), budget (¥100,000), and appliance needs/constraints, then proceeds in a structured sequence toward actionable recommendations. A coherent framework prevents fragmented analysis and ensures each later section follows from the defined task.

Evaluates whether budget allocation principles are stated and consistently applied (e.g., percentages for furniture/appliances/décor), itemized costs roll up to category totals, and totals reconcile to ¥100,000. Also checks for explicit trade-offs and prioritization when items risk exceeding budget, avoiding double-counting or hidden costs.

logical_coherence.criterion: Constraint-compliance reasoning (size, connectivity, and style fit)
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether recommendations are filtered by and justified against constraints: kitchen appliance dimensions and clearances, smart connectivity limitations/brand ecosystem compatibility, and alignment with the modern white/gray aesthetic. This ensures selections logically satisfy stated premises rather than contradict them.

logical_coherence.criterion: Consistency of comparative evaluation methodology for brands/models
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that comparisons use a consistent set of criteria (e.g., price, materials, design style, energy efficiency, cost-performance) applied uniformly across options, with an explicit rationale for rankings/choices. This avoids ad hoc cherry-picking and makes the selection logic reproducible.

Evaluates whether claims (prices, dimensions, materials, energy labels, durability) are explicitly tied to concrete specs or figures presented in the report and whether calculations are numerically consistent (e.g., dimensions fit, totals sum correctly). This guards against unsupported assertions and numeric errors.

Assesses whether transitions explain how each section derives from the previous (e.g., how allocation informs the room plan, how comparisons lead to the final shortlist) and whether later recommendations explicitly reference earlier constraints and budget allocations, avoiding logical jumps or contradictions.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structure and Layout for Scannability (Room-by-Room and Budget-Centric)
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report is cleanly structured with a clear heading hierarchy and predictable sections (e.g., Executive Summary, Budget Allocation, Room-by-Room shopping lists, Appliances with constraints, Accessories, Procurement Schedule). Uses adequate white space, bullet points, and consistent sectioning so readers can quickly locate items for the 89㎡ 3-bed layout.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity, Consistency, and Numeric Precision
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether the prose is concise, free of ambiguity and marketing fluff, and uses consistent terminology (e.g., soft vs hard furnishing). Numbers are presented clearly with correct units and symbols (¥ with thousand separators, ㎡, mm), consistent decimal precision, and clear date/time context for prices to avoid misunderstandings.

clarity_readability.criterion: Tabular Comparability of Prices and Specs
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether brand and model comparisons are presented in standardized tables with aligned columns (price, materials, dimensions, capacity, energy rating, connectivity, style), uniform units, subtotals/totals, and footnotes for assumptions (e.g., price source/date). This makes cross-brand cost-performance easy to scan without re-interpretation.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Charts and Visual Aids
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether visuals (e.g., budget allocation pie/bar charts, dimension-fit diagrams for fridge/washer, simple mood boards, delivery timeline) are legible, labeled with units and legends, colorblind-safe, and directly support the text. Visuals should clarify at-a-glance insights (space fit, budget split) rather than decorate.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity of Technical Terms and Constraint Explanations
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether domain-specific terms (e.g., inverter, counter-depth, energy efficiency ratings, Wi‑Fi/Matter connectivity, decibel levels, capacity in L/kg) are briefly defined where first used, with plain-language notes about why they matter for this home’s modern style and kitchen size/connectivity limitations.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Key Constraints, Picks, and Next Steps
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates the use of callouts, bolding, summaries, and checklists to surface must-know constraints (kitchen width/depth, doorway clearance, outlet positions), top recommended models, and immediate actions (measurements to verify, delivery/installation sequence). Clear emphasis reduces rereading and supports execution.

task: I plan to start a series of outdoor activities this summer, including hiking, camping, and mountaineering. As a beginner, I want to purchase a complete set of gear that will not only meet my basic needs but also handle different weather conditions, complex terrain, and unexpected situations.Please provide me with a detailed gear checklist, including but not limited to: backpack, waterproof clothing, hiking shoes, tent, sleeping bag, outdoor cooking equipment, navigation tools, first-aid kit, headlamp, portable water filter, and other essentials. Please consider durability, functionality, and comfort, ensuring the gear can adapt to changing outdoor environments. Also recommend suitable brands that will help me balance budget and quality.

goal_alignment.criterion: East China summer environment localization
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan tailors gear choices to Jiangsu/East China summer realities: monsoon rain and sudden storms (adequate hydrostatic head and seam-taped shells), high heat/humidity (breathable fabrics, ventilation), heavy mosquitoes (repellent/net), and wet, slippery stone trails (sticky, wet-traction outsoles). This directly maps to Li Chen’s local climate and typical routes.

goal_alignment.criterion: Multi-activity modular system for hiking/camping/non-technical mountaineering
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if the kit is designed as a modular 3-season system: layering for clothing, add-on modules (e.g., trekking poles, extra insulation) for higher elevations, pack volume ranges for day hikes vs overnights, and explicit scoping that technical mountaineering gear (e.g., crampons/ice axe/rope) requires training/guides. Ensures breadth without overbuying and aligns with beginner scope.

goal_alignment.criterion: Budget–quality balance with China-available brands and channels
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether recommendations provide tiered options (budget/mid/premium) available on JD/Taobao/Decathlon and domestic brands (e.g., Naturehike, Kailas, Toread, Fire Maple) alongside global brands (Osprey, Deuter, Salomon, MSR). Includes guidance on where to invest (footwear, pack fit, sleep) vs save (accessories), plus rental/second-hand tips and timing (e.g., 618 sales). Matches Li Chen’s frugal yet quality-oriented purchasing.

goal_alignment.criterion: Spec-driven selection thresholds and comparability
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if the plan uses quantitative specs and thresholds to guide choices (pack capacity in L; tent hydrostatic head; fabric denier; rain jacket membrane type; sleeping bag EN/ISO ratings and fill power; pad R-value; headlamp lumens/IP rating/USB-C; filter pore size and flow rate; stove fuel type and boil time). Aligns with Li Chen’s analytical style and need for rational comparisons.

goal_alignment.criterion: Beginner safety and risk mitigation
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess inclusion of a beginner-focused safety layer: first-aid kit tailored to blisters/heat/insect bites, emergency blanket, ORS; navigation redundancy (offline maps + paper map/compass basics); itinerary sharing and check-in; weather monitoring and turn-back criteria; whistle and reflective elements; sufficient power bank. Critical for a risk-averse novice facing variable conditions.

goal_alignment.criterion: Comfort and fit coaching (pack, footwear, sleep)
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for step-by-step guidance on pack fitting (torso/hipbelt adjustment), shoe sizing/lacing and blister prevention (socks, taping), and sleep comfort (pad R-value for summer, pillow options, ventilation/mesh for humid nights). This sustains performance and aligns with Li Chen’s focus on functionality and physical fitness routine.

goal_alignment.criterion: Pack weight management and load planning
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan offers base weight targets for a 3-season beginner kit, highlights multi-use items, and explains trade-offs between durability and weight. Includes water/food planning for humid heat and guidance to avoid overpacking. Supports rational decision-making and safer, more enjoyable first trips.

goal_alignment.criterion: Operational checklists, testing, and maintenance for durability
goal_alignment.explanation: Look for structured checklists (packing, pre-trip shakedown), at-home tent/pad/stove tests, and post-trip care (DWR renewal, seam checks, storage) to extend lifespan and reduce total cost. Resonates with Li Chen’s task-list habit and need to protect a student budget.

goal_alignment.criterion: Tech integration aligned with a CS student
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess recommendations for China-relevant offline navigation (e.g., Gaode/Tencent offline, 两步路), GPX route planning, USB-C rechargeable headlamps, power budgeting (10k–20k mAh), and location sharing. Matches Li Chen’s tech proficiency and leverages his comfort with digital tools.

goal_alignment.criterion: Structured, transparent communication and rationale
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the article uses clear bullet lists, mini-comparisons, and succinct reasoning for each item, referencing China-available examples rather than generic brands. This communication style aligns with Li Chen’s analytical reading habits and improves decision confidence.

content_alignment.criterion: Localization to Jiangsu/East China Conditions and China Market
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the gear choices and specs are tailored to hot, humid, rainy East China summers (e.g., breathable rain shells, quick-dry layers, mosquito/sun protection, 3-season ratings) and reference brands/models readily available in China, ensuring the content fits Li Chen’s actual environments and purchase context.

content_alignment.criterion: Budget–Value Optimization with China-Available Brands and Tiers
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the report provides cost-effective, reliable options with tiered recommendations (budget/mid), RMB price awareness, and China-available brands (e.g., Decathlon/Forclaz, Naturehike, Kailas, Fire-Maple, Nitecore/Fenix), aligning with his frugal, rational purchasing habits and need to balance quality with budget.

content_alignment.criterion: Beginner-Calibrated Technical Depth and Trade-off Explanations
content_alignment.explanation: Check if technical concepts (e.g., hydrostatic head, RET/MVTR, denier, Vibram/outsole compounds, fill power, R-value) are included with concise definitions, practical thresholds, and trade-offs so a novice can make informed choices—matching his detail-oriented, analytical mindset without overwhelming him.

content_alignment.criterion: Modular, Cross-Activity System Design
content_alignment.explanation: Determine whether the content structures gear as a modular system (layering for clothing, shared shelter/cookware, pack volume ranges) that covers hiking, camping, and entry-level mountaineering without over-specialized purchases, reflecting his need for versatility across multiple activities as a beginner.

content_alignment.criterion: Safety-Forward Gear Selection Aligned to Risk Aversion
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the checklist emphasizes safety-oriented content aligned to a cautious beginner (redundant lighting and power, reliable navigation basics, first-aid components suited to local conditions, emergency whistle/blanket, weather awareness items), focusing on what to include and why.

content_alignment.criterion: Tech and Data Integration Consistent with CS Background
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate inclusion of data-driven comparisons (spec tables/metrics), compatibility notes (USB-C rechargeable headlamps/power banks), and China-usable apps/devices for navigation and planning (e.g., offline maps, GLONASS/BeiDou support), aligning with his tech affinity and desire for measurable criteria.

content_alignment.criterion: Online Purchasing Channel Fit and Authenticity Guidance
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether recommendations consider JD/Taobao purchasing patterns (flagship stores, authorized resellers, anti-counterfeit cues, warranty/returns, sizing charts) to match his online shopping habits and reduce risk in buying technical gear.

content_alignment.criterion: Planning-Oriented Checklists and Organization Aids
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report offers structured packing lists, category breakdowns, and pre-trip gear-check aids that fit his planning discipline and help him systematically cover essentials without redundancy.

presentation_fit.criterion: Macro structure and scannability (clear hierarchy, headings, TL;DR, numbered and bulleted lists)
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks whether the report is organized by logical gear systems (e.g., backpack, layering, footwear, shelter, sleep, cook, nav, safety, lighting, water) with a quick summary/TL;DR, section headers, and list formatting so Li Chen can scan and plan efficiently like reading a README.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology clarity and unit conventions (brief defs, metric-first)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates if key outdoor terms (e.g., denier, hydrostatic head, fill power, R-value, IP rating) are briefly defined in-line and if specs use metric units (L, kg, g, cm, °C) with consistent notation, matching a beginner’s needs and Li Chen’s Chinese context.

presentation_fit.criterion: Checklist usability and priority labeling
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether items are presented as checkable lists with clear priority tags (e.g., [Must-have] vs [Optional]) and condition tags (e.g., [Rain], [Cold], [Mountaineering]) so he can quickly tailor the list to different trips without deciphering long paragraphs.

presentation_fit.criterion: Algorithmic decision rules for selection (if–then, flow, simple decision trees)
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks the presence of concise, rule-like selection guidance (e.g., pack volume by trip length, sleeping bag by night temps, waterproof ratings by rainfall) presented as if–then steps or flows, fitting his CS-style, logic-oriented reading preference.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tone and language style (concise, objective, non-marketing, consistent wording)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether the language is neutral, evidence- and spec-oriented, avoiding hype and vague superlatives, with consistent terminology—matching a rational, research-driven student’s expectations.

presentation_fit.criterion: Brand and price formatting localization (RMB, consistent template, bilingual names)
presentation_fit.explanation: Looks at whether brand/model names and prices are formatted consistently using RMB (¥) and commonly used English/Chinese names to ease JD/Taobao search, focusing on format rather than the specific products.

presentation_fit.criterion: Comparative summaries for quick selection (simple tables or tiered lists)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides compact comparison structures (e.g., budget/mid/premium tiers with 2–3 key specs) to support fast trade-off reading typical of technical blog habits.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile/offline readability and consolidated final checklist
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks for short paragraphs, chunked sections, copy-and-paste-friendly formatting, and a single consolidated checklist at the end for easy saving/printing—aligned with his mobile usage and planning behavior.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget clarity with tiered purchase plan (buy-now, upgrade-later, rent/borrow)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report provides an itemized cost breakdown, a realistic total within a student budget, and tiers (starter vs. upgrade) plus items to rent/borrow for infrequent mountaineering needs—so Li Chen can act immediately without overspending.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Purchasing feasibility on Chinese channels
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates if recommended models/brands are readily available on JD/Taobao/Decathlon China with current price ranges, avoiding hard-to-source imports and indicating after-sales channels—ensuring he can actually buy the gear.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Scenario-based packing checklists (day hike, overnight camping, entry-level mountaineering)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides clear, minimal viable loadouts by scenario with required vs optional items, enabling Li Chen to pack and go without guesswork.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Climate and regional suitability (East China summer + occasional mountain conditions)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks that recommendations match Suzhou/east-China heat, humidity, heavy rain, and mosquitoes (e.g., breathable rainwear, ventilation, sun/bug protection) and note temperature ranges for 3-season mountain use.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Fit and sizing guidance for key items
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies the report includes how to choose backpack torso/hipbelt size, hiking shoe sizing/width and break-in, sleeping bag length, and tent interior fit—reducing returns and ensuring comfort and safety.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Safety and emergency practicality
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates concreteness of first-aid contents, headlamp specs/batteries, whistle/emergency blanket, power bank and offline maps, weather/route check reminders, and plan-sharing—so a beginner manages unexpected situations.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Weight and load management targets
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for base-weight and total pack-weight targets (e.g., % of body weight), item weights for the big three (pack/tent/sleep system), and tips to keep loads beginner-manageable—improving comfort and feasibility.

actionability_practicality.criterion: System compatibility (stove fuel, cookware, water treatment, power)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks that stove fuel types and canister standards are available in China, pot sizes match stoves, filter/chemical choices suit local freshwater, and charging/power plans fit phone/GPS—preventing in-field incompatibilities.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Durability and serviceability in practice
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether material specs (fabric denier, waterproof ratings, sole compounds), simple repair kits, and warranty/service info are given so Li Chen buys gear that lasts and can be fixed affordably.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Preparation, use, and maintenance steps
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies inclusion of step-by-step pre-trip actions (boot break-in, tent setup practice, seam sealing, packing order), post-trip care, and storage guidance—so gear performs reliably when used.

depth_insight.criterion: Environment–Activity Mapping and Scenario Coverage
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report tailors gear to hiking, camping, and entry-level mountaineering across varying weather and terrain, translating likely conditions (temperature ranges, precipitation, altitude, exposure) into concrete gear requirements rather than a one-size-fits-all list.

depth_insight.criterion: Technical Performance Specs and Minimum Thresholds (with rationale)
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the use of appropriate, justified specs (e.g., hydrostatic head and breathability for shells, EN/ISO bag temp ratings and pad R-values, headlamp lumens and IP rating, water filter pore size and flow, stove fuel performance in cold/altitude, boot/sole stiffness) and explains why those thresholds matter.

depth_insight.criterion: Trade-off and Decision Framework for Key Choices
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for explicit reasoning frameworks that balance weight, durability, comfort, and cost; compare down vs synthetic, single- vs double-wall tents, canister vs liquid fuel stoves, pack volume/materials, layering strategies—showing when and why to choose each option.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk Preparedness and Contingency Depth
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses depth in addressing unexpected situations: redundancy (backup light, fire, water treatment), first-aid completeness and training cues, emergency shelter/comm devices, weather and navigation contingencies, and reference to relevant standards/certifications (UIAA/CE for helmets/harnesses, IP for lights).

depth_insight.criterion: System Integration and Compatibility Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report ensures gear works as a system: crampon–boot compatibility, helmet fit with headlamp clips, sleeping bag–pad thermal synergy, stove–pot–fuel availability, tent seasonality vs pole materials, filtration compatibility with bottles/hydration, and pack fit for load distribution.

depth_insight.criterion: Brand/Model Recommendations with Value Logic Across Budgets
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses specificity and justification of brand/model picks across budget tiers, articulating durability, warranty/reliability, materials, and when to splurge vs save to balance budget and quality, rather than name-dropping popular brands without rationale.

depth_insight.criterion: Fit and Comfort Guidance with Actionable Protocols
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates nuanced, experience-based guidance on achieving comfort: boot fit tests and lacing tricks, sock/insoles, pack torso/hip-belt sizing, sleep system fit, blister prevention, and break-in planning—key to beginner success beyond specs.

depth_insight.criterion: Acquisition Strategy, Maintenance, and Expert Heuristics
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for non-obvious, high-value insights: rent/borrow for technical mountaineering items initially, buying used and inspection checklists (stitching, delamination, pole integrity), care (DWR renewal, filter backflushing), field repair kits, and upgrade paths as skills/conditions progress.

logical_coherence.criterion: Defined Scope and Scenario Assumptions Propagate Through the Report
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report clearly states and then consistently uses the user context (beginner), activity set (hiking, camping, mountaineering), seasonal/weather range, terrain complexity, and budget–quality balance to constrain all subsequent choices (e.g., pack volume, waterproof ratings, sleeping bag temps). Without firm premises that carry through, later recommendations lack logical grounding.

logical_coherence.criterion: Structured Gear-System Framework Aligned to Outdoor Use
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether content is organized into a coherent system (carry, clothing layering, shelter/sleep, water, food/cooking, navigation/communication, lighting, first aid, repair/emergency) and whether dependencies are acknowledged (e.g., stove type dictates fuel and cookware). A system framework provides the skeleton that keeps reasoning organized and non-fragmented.

Evaluates whether each recommendation can be traced from a stated need to a measurable specification and then to a concrete item/brand (e.g., ‘summer alpine storms → 20k/20k shell → Model X’). This traceability ensures conclusions are derived rather than arbitrary.

logical_coherence.criterion: Explicit Selection Criteria and Trade-off Reasoning
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether selection criteria (durability vs weight, comfort vs packability, cost vs performance) are stated and the chosen balance is justified for a beginner targeting multi-activity use. Logical coherence requires making the rationale for trade-offs transparent.

Tests for contradictions and confirms that recommendations adhere to earlier constraints (e.g., beginner suitability, multi-activity versatility, budget–quality aims) and avoid incompatibilities (e.g., fuel–stove mismatches). Consistency is essential to preserve the integrity of the reasoning chain.

logical_coherence.criterion: Evidence Relevance and Justification via Specifications/Standards
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether claims are supported by relevant specs or standards (e.g., hydrostatic head, breathability ratings, ISO/EN temperature ratings, R-values, outsole compounds) and whether that evidence directly supports the stated need. Appropriate evidence links bolster logical derivation.

logical_coherence.criterion: Section Transitions and Progressive Reasoning Flow
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the report moves logically from assumptions to system overview, to category-level criteria, to item/brand options, and finally to an acquisition/packing plan, without logical jumps. Smooth progression helps maintain a coherent argument structure.

Checks whether identified risks (weather shifts, navigation failure, injury, water contamination) are explicitly mapped to gear mitigations and reasonable redundancies (e.g., backup navigation, spare batteries, repair kit), proportional to the stated scenarios. This shows coherent preparedness aligned with premises.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Layout and Navigability for Multi-Activity Gear Guides
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether the report uses a clean, consistent hierarchy (clear headings/subheadings per gear category and scenario), logical grouping (e.g., hiking/camping/mountaineering sections), adequate spacing, and navigational aids (table of contents, anchors, cross-references) so beginners can quickly find what they need.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity, Brevity, and Instructional Tone of Language
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether the wording is concise, unambiguous, and action-oriented (e.g., checklist-style imperatives), with correct grammar and precise wording that minimizes misinterpretation of specs, usage notes, and care instructions.

clarity_readability.criterion: Definition and Consistent Use of Technical Terms and Units
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether outdoor-specific jargon (e.g., denier, fill power, R-value, hydrostatic head, IP ratings, shank stiffness) is introduced with brief, reader-friendly definitions; units are consistent (metric/imperial with conversions where relevant); abbreviations are explained on first use or via a glossary.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Comparison Tables, Matrices, and Visual Aids
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether brand/spec comparisons (e.g., capacity, weight, temperature rating, waterproofing, price tier) are presented in clear, well-labeled tables or matrices with readable fonts, aligned columns, visible units, legends/footnotes, and accessible color choices, enabling at-a-glance decisions.

clarity_readability.criterion: Scannability: Checklists, Key Highlights, and Safety Callouts
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses the presence and readability of quick-reference elements (e.g., must-have vs optional checklists, weather/terrain-specific mini-lists, budget/standard/premium tags, safety/warning callouts). These should be visually distinct and consistent to support fast scanning and reduce errors.

task: I plan to start a series of outdoor activities this summer, including hiking, camping, and mountaineering. As a beginner, I want to purchase a complete set of gear that will not only meet my basic needs but also handle different weather conditions, complex terrain, and unexpected situations.Please provide me with a detailed gear checklist, including but not limited to: backpack, waterproof clothing, hiking shoes, tent, sleeping bag, outdoor cooking equipment, navigation tools, first-aid kit, headlamp, portable water filter, and other essentials. Please consider durability, functionality, and comfort, ensuring the gear can adapt to changing outdoor environments. Also recommend suitable brands that will help me balance budget and quality.

goal_alignment.criterion: Zhejiang-summer climate and terrain–specific adaptation
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether recommendations address Hangzhou/Zhejiang summer realities: high humidity, heavy rain/typhoons, heat management, intense sun and mosquitoes, and slippery, vegetated trails (traction, quick-dry layers, robust rain protection, sun/bug strategy, water treatment appropriate for local sources). This localizes the generic gear list to her actual environment.

goal_alignment.criterion: Sustainability-first gear curation
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the gear and brands prioritize eco-certifications (e.g., bluesign, Fair Trade), PFC-free DWR, recycled/biobased materials, repairability/warranty, durability over disposability, and include rental/second-hand options plus Leave No Trace practice. This matches her environmental studies background and willingness to pay for sustainable quality.

goal_alignment.criterion: Beginner safety scaffolding and risk management
