actionability_practicality.explanation: State base weight/pack weight targets, item weight caps for big-three (pack/tent/sleep system), pack volume fit, and airline/train constraints (e.g., no fuel on flights, blade limits). Include compact storage tips for a small apartment. This improves comfort and logistical feasibility.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Safety and risk-mitigation checklists and readiness steps
actionability_practicality.explanation: Include a beginner-friendly safety checklist (first-aid essentials, foot care, hydration plan, sun/insect protection, storm/heat protocols), pre-trip tests (pitch tent at home, water filter backflush, headlamp battery check), and boundaries (rent/guide for technical mountaineering). This reduces real-world risk.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Maintenance and repair guidance for durability and lifespan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Provide practical care steps (e.g., DWR reproofing, seam sealing, patch kits, water filter maintenance, boot care), storage guidance, and simple field repairs. This supports the user’s desire for durable, quality-aligned purchases and lowers total cost of ownership.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Phased purchasing roadmap and timeline
actionability_practicality.explanation: Offer a stepwise buy-first list (safety-critical and sizing-sensitive gear first), timing guidance (e.g., break-in shoes 2–3 weeks before trips), and an upgrade path aligned with monthly and quarterly spending cycles. This helps the user take immediate, organized action.

depth_insight.criterion: Contextual Hazard and Requirements Profiling
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report defines likely summer conditions, terrain types, trip lengths, elevation/exposure, and beginner constraints, then uses this profile to set gear requirements rather than offering a generic list.

depth_insight.criterion: Specification Rigor with Justified Performance Thresholds
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether key items include actionable specs (e.g., shell HH/breathability, pack volume ranges, boot outsole type, sleeping bag EN/ISO rating and pad R-value, tent materials, stove fuel pros/cons, headlamp lumens/runtime) and why those thresholds fit the use cases.

depth_insight.criterion: System-level Modularity and Cross-activity Adaptability
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether the gear is designed as a coherent system (layering strategy, modular shelter/cook setups, compatible components, redundancy planning) that adapts from day hikes to camping and entry-level mountaineering without unnecessary duplication.

depth_insight.criterion: Trade-off Analysis and Budget–Quality Optimization
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for explicit reasoning on where to invest (e.g., footwear, sleep system, rain shell) versus where to save, balancing durability, weight, comfort, and cost; may include tiered brand/model options and total budget allocation logic.

depth_insight.criterion: Safety, Redundancy, and Contingency Planning Depth
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates inclusion and justification of redundant navigation, first-aid contents tailored to likely injuries, emergency shelter/signaling, battery/power strategies, and weather/route fallback plans to handle unexpected situations.

depth_insight.criterion: Fit, Ergonomics, and Comfort Insight
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses guidance on achieving proper fit and comfort (boot last/width and try-on protocol, sock systems and blister prevention, pack torso/hip sizing, sleeping pad comfort trade-offs) that materially affect beginner experience.

depth_insight.criterion: Evidence-based Brand and Model Recommendations
depth_insight.explanation: Judges whether brand/model suggestions are supported by reliability data, warranty/repair support, field performance, and price-performance tiers, rather than mere brand listing.

depth_insight.criterion: Integration of Environmental/Regulatory Constraints
depth_insight.explanation: Considers whether the report anticipates constraints like fire bans (fuel choices), wildlife storage rules (bear canisters/ursacks), and area-specific requirements, and reflects them in gear choices.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report first defines the beginner’s use cases (activities, season, terrain, trip length) and then maps them systematically to gear systems (shelter, sleep, carry, apparel layering, footwear, water, cook, navigation, lighting, first-aid/repair, comms). A clear framework prevents a disjointed shopping list and ensures every item traces back to a stated need.

logical_coherence.criterion: Explicit Decision Criteria and Trade-off Logic
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the report states selection criteria (durability, functionality, comfort, weight, versatility, safety margin, compatibility, budget) and consistently applies them to justify choices per category, explicitly reasoning about trade-offs (e.g., down vs synthetic, trail runners vs boots, canister vs liquid fuel, freestanding vs non-freestanding tents). This anchors recommendations in a stable logic rather than preference.

Evaluates whether interdependent systems are reasoned as a whole: layering (base/mid/shell) vs expected weather; sleep system coupling (bag temperature rating, pad R-value, tent seasonality) vs forecast lows; stove–fuel–pot compatibility and fuel availability; navigation stack (primary + backup + power); pack volume vs trip length/season; footwear–socks–gaiters vs terrain and load. Coherent configuration avoids mismatches that undermine performance.

Checks for stated and consistently used assumptions (temperature range, precipitation/wind likelihood, altitude, terrain technicality, water availability, trip duration), plus constraints (beginner skill level, budget tiers, weight tolerance). Declared premises make the downstream reasoning auditable and prevent hidden, shifting assumptions.

Assesses whether each key recommendation (e.g., '3-season tent' or 'mid-cut waterproof boots') is immediately backed by reasons tied to the stated needs and criteria (e.g., summer storms, ankle support for load, ISO bag ratings, IPX ratings where relevant). This ensures conclusions are derived from premises rather than asserted.

Evaluates whether the report structures likely scenarios (heat, rainstorms, cold snaps, high wind, rocky terrain, night travel, water scarcity, gear failure, minor injuries) and maps each to gear mitigations and redundancies (e.g., emergency bivy, repair kit, backup fire/lighting, spare batteries, robust water treatment plan). Logical scenario mapping demonstrates preparedness reasoning.

Checks that the report guides the reader from needs to criteria, then to system categories, specific item features, brand options, and finally to a checklist—using clear transitions and cross-references. Smooth transitions reduce logical jumps and preserve the reasoning flow across sections.

logical_coherence.criterion: Internal Consistency and Constraint Compliance (incl. Budget–Brand Alignment)
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether there are no contradictions (e.g., advocating ultralight minimalism while prioritizing beginner-friendly durability/safety), and whether brand recommendations align with earlier criteria and budget tiers (e.g., not placing premium boutique brands in 'budget' tier). Consistency validates that conclusions honor the report’s own premises.

Evaluates whether the report logically distinguishes essentials from optional upgrades for a beginner and sequences purchases based on safety-critical needs and versatility, derived from the prior needs and criteria—not arbitrary preference. This adds a coherent decision path for acquisition.

clarity_readability.criterion: Information Architecture and Scannability for Gear Checklists
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether items are grouped into clear systems (e.g., backpack, clothing, footwear, shelter, sleep, cooking, navigation, safety/first-aid, lighting, water) with a consistent heading hierarchy, concise bullet lists, and labels for must-have vs optional items and scenario tags (weather/terrain). Good structure enables quick lookup and on-the-go use without reading long prose.

clarity_readability.criterion: Formatting Cleanliness, Consistency, and Unit Standardization
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that spacing, bullet/number styles, and alignment are consistent; specs use uniform notation and units (e.g., L, g/oz, mm hydrostatic head, fill power, EN/ISO temperature, R-value, lumens, IP/IPX) with metric and imperial where relevant; and that tables/lists avoid clutter. Consistency reduces confusion and prevents misreading critical specs.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity and Precision of Language
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether sentences are concise and unambiguous, avoid unnecessary jargon and marketing language, use consistent terminology, and provide clear, actionable phrasing (e.g., "Choose X when Y"). Clear language lowers the entry barrier for beginners and reduces misinterpretation in safety-relevant contexts.

clarity_readability.criterion: Explanation of Technical Terms and Specifications
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether specialized terms and ratings (e.g., denier, 3L vs 2.5L membranes, fully seam-taped, hydrostatic head, fill power, EN/ISO comfort vs limit, R-value, last/shank, lug depth, heel-to-toe drop, lumens, IPX) are briefly defined at first use or via a glossary/notes, and linked to practical implications. Proper explanations make specs meaningful and comparable.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Tables, Checklists, and Visual Summaries
clarity_readability.explanation: Measures whether comparison tables, matrices, or checklists are used where they improve understanding (e.g., brand/price/weight/temp-rating tables, budget tiers), with clear titles, labeled columns, legible ordering, and direct references in the text. Effective visuals condense multi-attribute choices and aid fast decision-making.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of Key Decisions, Safety Notes, and Beginner Tips
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether critical guidance (e.g., fit/sizing notes, weather thresholds, safety cautions, "don’t skimp" items) is visually emphasized using callouts, bolding, or summary boxes, and whether a brief summary or quick-start pack list is provided. Highlighting directs attention to high-impact information and supports novices.

task: I would like to renovate my apartment in the coming months to create a home environment that is both comfortable and stylish. My apartment is about 80㎡, with two bedrooms and one living room. I hope to use natural wood and fabric materials in the renovation to create a warm atmosphere. My total renovation budget is about ¥150,000, which I hope will also cover furniture and appliances. I aim to complete the renovation within three months, though I can be flexible if necessary.I live with my partner, and our high-frequency daily activity areas are mainly the living room and kitchen. I enjoy cooking, so my ideal layout would improve the functionality of an open-plan kitchen. In terms of existing conditions, the lighting is generally good, and the master bedroom faces south. I have the floor plan available, and can share it with you if needed. Please create a detailed design plan, including furniture selection, material recommendations, and budget guidance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Budget- and ROI-grounded plan under ¥150,000 (incl. furniture & appliances)
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan provides a transparent line-item budget (hard vs. soft costs), prioritizes spend in kitchen/living, includes a 5–10% contingency, and shows TCO/energy-savings payback (e.g., inverter AC, efficient hood/dishwasher). It should leverage sales calendars (618/11.11) and vetted second-hand options with warranty, and align payment milestones with the user’s monthly financial planning. This fits Chen Hao’s cost-effectiveness focus, risk aversion, and data-driven financial habits.

goal_alignment.criterion: Kitchen-centric open-plan functionality with fume/noise control for two-person cooking
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the design optimizes the workflow (zones/triangle, two cooks side-by-side), storage (pantry, vertical, pull-outs), and appliance set (hood ≥20 m³/min with capture efficiency, dishwasher, steam/oven combos). It should present trade-offs among open vs. semi-open (sliding glass), hood types, and easy-clean materials/backsplashes, plus acoustic strategies. This matches the user’s high cooking frequency, open-plan desire, and couple use.

goal_alignment.criterion: Feasible 3‑month execution plan with risk control and decision cadence
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for a critical-path schedule (demolition, MEP, carpentry, finishes), lead-time management (custom cabinets 30–45 days), early procurement, HOA/property management approvals, elevator protection, and contractor QA/acceptance checklists. Include evening check-ins for approvals and buffers for unexpected delays. This aligns with Chen Hao’s risk-aversion, planning style, and evening availability.

goal_alignment.criterion: Material system tailored to warm wood/fabric aesthetics, humidity, durability, and low-VOC
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess selection of engineered wood (E0/E1), moisture-resistant substrates, waterborne finishes/adhesives, anti-mold strategies for Shanghai humidity, and easy-maintenance surfaces. For fabrics, prefer tightly woven, scratch-resistant, and cleanable options anticipating a future cat. This connects aesthetic goals with health, local climate practicality, and pet readiness.

goal_alignment.criterion: Couple-centric spatial planning for daily routines, storage, and comfort
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan reflects two-person living: dual workstations or a flexible study/guest room, living room layout for shared activities, storage for books/sports gear, and a yoga/meditation corner. Include layered lighting for evening reading and bedroom sleep hygiene (warm CCT, blackout for south-facing room). This matches the couple’s high use of living/kitchen and the user’s reading/exercise habits.

goal_alignment.criterion: Data-driven presentation with clear options, specs, and assumptions
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for side-by-side option sets with costs, pros/cons, and quantified specs (e.g., hood airflow, counter heights, storage volumes), plus explicit assumptions. This mirrors Chen Hao’s analytical decision style and improves acceptance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Smart-home and energy-efficiency plan with simple payback estimates
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess inclusion of cost-effective smart basics (lighting scenes, smart switches/sensors, remote monitoring) and energy-efficient appliances with payback periods. This aligns with the user’s tech interest, travel, and ROI mindset while controlling costs.

goal_alignment.criterion: Shanghai-specific pricing, sourcing, and compliance grounding
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether recommendations reflect Shanghai market rates for labor/materials, available brands/models, reliable contractor selection norms, second-hand platform usage (e.g., Xianyu) and promo cycles, and property management rules. Local grounding enhances feasibility and cost accuracy.

goal_alignment.criterion: Health, safety, IAQ, and ergonomics strategy
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for VOC control (material selection and bake-out/venting plan), dehumidification/ventilation approaches, anti-slip tiles in wet zones, rounded edges, circuit planning for high-load appliances, and lighting tuned to improve sleep quality. This serves the user’s health focus and risk management.

goal_alignment.criterion: Future pet-readiness integration
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess provisions for a future cat: concealed litter box location/venting, balcony safety netting, claw-resistant fabrics, cat tree/shelf integration, and cleaning/storage solutions. This anticipates stated future plans and prevents costly rework.

content_alignment.criterion: Open-Plan Kitchen Functionality for a Cooking Enthusiast
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report deeply covers Chinese cooking needs in an open-plan setting: ventilation capacity (e.g., m³/h and static pressure), grease containment strategies (partial glass partitions/baffle options), workflow zoning (prep–cook–wash triangle), heat-/oil-resistant materials (quartz/compact laminate, stainless zones), storage ergonomics (pull-outs, vertical pantries), and appliance specs (range hood noise dB, induction vs gas trade-offs) tailored to his stated love of cooking.

content_alignment.criterion: Data-Transparent Budgeting and Cost-Performance Alignment (¥150k all-in)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan presents a quantified budget structure suited to a finance professional: unit price bands (materials, labor, cabinetry, appliances, furniture), A/B trade-off options within ¥150k, sensitivity ranges, and lifecycle/TCO comparisons (maintenance, warranty, energy costs) to match his rational, ROI-focused decision style.

content_alignment.criterion: Natural Wood/Fabric Selection with IAQ and Humidity Considerations
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the content goes beyond ‘warm aesthetics’ to specify species/veneers vs solid wood, fabric types (washable, tightly woven), formaldehyde/VOC standards (e.g., E0/E1, low-VOC coatings), and Shanghai humidity/mold resistance measures (engineered wood cores, SPC/engineered flooring, anti-mildew paint), aligning with his material preference and local climate.

content_alignment.criterion: Pet-Ready, Cat-Safe Design Provisions
content_alignment.explanation: Determine whether the plan anticipates a future cat: scratch-resistant fabrics (microfiber, performance linen blends), removable/washable covers, claw-friendly but durable wood finishes, window safety screens, litter box placement/ventilation/odor control, cat-safe plants, and material toxicity awareness—personalized to his adoption plan.

content_alignment.criterion: Shanghai-Localized Context (Climate, Sourcing, and Standards)
content_alignment.explanation: Assess inclusion of Shanghai-specific content: moisture control/dehumidification guidance, energy/water labels common in China, realistic local price bands, and example procurement channels/brands commonly available in Shanghai, ensuring recommendations are grounded in his actual market context.

content_alignment.criterion: Smart Home and Energy-Efficiency with TCO Perspective
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content proposes cost-effective smart home basics (lighting scenes, sensors, platform ecosystem fit) and appliance energy ratings with quantified operating cost impacts, reflecting his tech interest and cost-performance mindset rather than pushing premium features without value justification.

content_alignment.criterion: Content Emphasis on High-Use Zones (Living Room and Kitchen)
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether the report allocates disproportionate depth to living room and kitchen solutions (layout, furniture sizing for 80㎡, storage, acoustics, lighting layers), matching his stated high-frequency use and avoiding equal-depth treatment of low-priority spaces.

content_alignment.criterion: Couple Routine and Daily Rhythm Accommodation
content_alignment.explanation: See if content accounts for two adults’ routines: evening reading nook with appropriate color temperature and glare control, sleep-supportive bedroom treatments (blackout, acoustics), and organized storage for fitness gear—reflecting his habits and shared living needs.

content_alignment.criterion: Second-Hand and Value Sourcing Guidance
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan identifies categories suitable for second-hand vs new (e.g., solid wood tables yes, mattresses no), inspection checklists, and cleaning/refresh tips, aligning with his cost-effective, review-driven shopping behavior and use of second-hand platforms.

content_alignment.criterion: Risk- and Lead-Time–Aware Selection Information
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether content provides data relevant to a risk-averse decision style: typical lead times for custom cabinetry/sofas, warranty lengths, spare-part availability, and maintenance burden as selection criteria—informing choices without drifting into schedule management.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structural clarity and navigability (executive summary, logical sections, room-by-room organization, and phased timeline placement)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether the report starts with a concise executive summary and uses clear, numbered headings (e.g., Overview, Budget, Layout, Kitchen, Living Room, Bedrooms, Bathroom, Procurement, Timeline) with room-by-room subsections and a dedicated timeline section. This matches the user’s analytical and time-efficient reading habits, enabling fast scanning in evening sessions.

presentation_fit.criterion: Language tone and concision fit (objective, professional, non-marketing, concise with high signal-to-noise)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether the tone is neutral and data-backed, avoids salesy adjectives, uses active voice, and keeps sentences concise. This presentation style aligns with an investment manager’s preference for clarity and evidence over hype.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology and localization clarity (jargon explained briefly; consistent metric/RMB; China-relevant standards referenced)
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks that renovation terms (e.g., E0/E1 formaldehyde rating, veneer vs. solid wood, SPC, melamine) are used correctly with short in-text definitions; units are metric, currency is RMB, and any GB/T or local codes are referenced with brief parenthetical explanations. This reduces cognitive load and improves comprehension for a non-designer professional in China.

presentation_fit.criterion: Quantitative presentation quality (budget tables, assumptions, comparisons, and scenario formatting)
presentation_fit.explanation: Looks for well-formatted tables with category subtotals and totals, explicit assumptions/allowances, ranges where appropriate, and side-by-side comparison matrices for key options (e.g., base vs. upgrade). This format resonates with the user’s finance background and supports quick numerical evaluation.

presentation_fit.criterion: Visualization and diagram effectiveness (annotated floor-plan overlays, kitchen workflow diagrams, mood boards, labeled figures)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether visuals are used purposefully and labeled clearly (figure titles, callouts, legends). Floor-plan markups and kitchen work-triangle diagrams aid spatial understanding; mood boards and material swatches convey the warm wood-and-fabric theme efficiently.

presentation_fit.criterion: Decision-support formatting (pros/cons lists, checklists, milestones/Gantt, and clearly marked next steps)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether choices are summarized via pros/cons bullets or matrices, procurement and pre-construction tasks are in checklists, and the 3-month schedule is shown as milestones or a simple Gantt. This improves decision flow for a risk-averse, independent decision-maker.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget completeness and realism (Shanghai, 80㎡, includes furniture/appliances)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report provides an itemized BOQ and cost breakdown (labor, main/aux materials, furniture, appliances, fixtures, design/permits, logistics/installation, cleaning, soft furnishings) with realistic Shanghai price bands and a total within ¥150,000, plus trade-offs and prioritization (e.g., allocate more to kitchen/living).

actionability_practicality.criterion: Timeline feasibility with milestones and critical path
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether there is a week-by-week or milestone-based plan covering approvals, demolition, MEP rough-in, waterproofing tests, carpentry/tiling, painting, installation, and furniture/appliance deliveries, including lead times (e.g., custom cabinets), dependencies, and buffers to realistically meet ~3 months.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Regulatory and building constraints handled (open kitchen, MEP, approvals)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies that the report addresses property management/gas company rules for open kitchens (gas vs induction or partition solutions), wet-area waterproofing standards and tests, electrical capacity/load and circuit design, exhaust duct requirements, and any required permits/notifications—so plans are legally buildable.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Cooking-centric kitchen functionality and specifications
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the kitchen plan includes actionable layout metrics (work triangle/zone plan, aisle widths, counter heights), storage solutions, durable/cleanable materials, high-suction range hood specs, appliance list (dishwasher/oven/fridge), ventilation, and dedicated power/gas/water arrangements suitable for frequent cooking.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Procurement specificity and market availability in Shanghai
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for brand/model-level or equivalent spec recommendations with price ranges, warranty notes, and purchase channels (e.g., JD/Tmall/local showrooms/IKEA), delivery timelines, and second-hand options where suitable—so the user can actually order on time and within budget.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Material specifications for warm natural style, health, and maintenance
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for concrete material grades and certifications (e.g., E0/E1, low-VOC paints), moisture/mold resistance suitable for Shanghai humidity, wear/scratch resistance, fabric durability (e.g., Martindale, removable washable covers), and care/maintenance guidance to ensure long-term practicality.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Dimensional fit and circulation clearances verified
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines whether furniture/built-in sizes and placements are dimensioned against the floor plan with clearances (e.g., >900mm walkways, door swing, wardrobe depth, island/dining spacing), minimizing rework/returns and ensuring everyday usability in an 80㎡ layout.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Contractor selection and contract execution plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report provides a process to solicit and compare 3 bids against a common BOQ, standard payment schedules, key contract clauses (change orders, warranty), site supervision cadence, and acceptance checklists—enabling the user to confidently hire and manage the build.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk management and contingency planning
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for explicit budget (10–15%) and schedule buffers, substitution lists for out-of-stock items, protocols for variations, and mitigation for common risks (delays, QC failures), aligning with a risk-averse decision style and improving execution reliability.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Pet-readiness, durability, and safety provisions
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for practical adjustments anticipating a future cat (scratch-resistant textiles, cable management, balcony screens, litter box zone, non-toxic finishes), reducing future modifications and protecting finishes and safety.

depth_insight.criterion: User Needs Translation and Trade-off Prioritization
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the plan turns stated goals (warm wood/fabric style, functional open kitchen, high-use living room/kitchen, budget ¥150k, 3 months) into prioritized design drivers with explicit trade-offs (e.g., open-plan feel vs odor control, material quality vs budget, customization vs lead time). Insight is shown by justified prioritization tailored to this household.

depth_insight.criterion: Space Planning and Open-Kitchen Workflow Optimization
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates depth in reconfiguring layout to improve kitchen/living functionality: cooking workflow (work triangle, prep zones), circulation widths, storage zoning, island/peninsula choices, and addressing constraints (wet-area positions, load-bearing walls, gas regulations). Strong insight uses ergonomic benchmarks and proposes viable alternatives with rationale.

depth_insight.criterion: Materials and Finishes Selection Depth (Warm Wood/Fabric with Performance)
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether wood/fabric choices achieve warmth while meeting durability, maintenance, moisture/grease and fire resistance, and indoor air quality (e.g., E0/E1, low-VOC finishes). Insight includes kitchen/wet-zone specific solutions, texture/palette coherence, and cost-quality trade-offs tied to the budget.

depth_insight.criterion: Technical Systems and Appliance Specification for Heavy Cooking
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses rigor in specifying ventilation (hood type/capacity, ducting, make-up air), gas safety, electrical loads/circuiting for appliances, layered lighting (task/ambient), acoustic comfort, and energy efficiency suitable for an open-plan, cooking-intensive home. Depth includes quantitative targets and compliance awareness.

depth_insight.criterion: Furniture and Storage Strategy with Dimensional Fit
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether furniture choices are dimensionally appropriate for 80㎡, maintain flow, and enhance storage (e.g., built-ins, multifunctional pieces, under-bed storage). Insight is shown through ergonomic clearances, capacity planning by room, and alignment with high-use living/dining needs.

depth_insight.criterion: Budget Realism and Value Engineering
depth_insight.explanation: Judges the granularity and market realism of the budget covering renovation, furniture, and appliances within ¥150,000. Insight includes line-item estimates, brand/grade tiers, save-vs-splurge strategy, contingency, and how design decisions (e.g., custom millwork, stone counters) impact costs.

depth_insight.criterion: Timeline Feasibility and Risk Mitigation
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the plan critically evaluates three-month feasibility, identifies the critical path (e.g., cabinets, countertop, appliances, permits), and offers mitigation (pre-ordering, parallel trades, alternates with shorter lead times). Insight links schedule choices to design and budget impacts.

depth_insight.criterion: Site-specific Utilization and Constraint Awareness
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates use of existing conditions (south-facing master, good daylight) to enhance comfort and avoid glare/shading issues, while acknowledging constraints (shafts, load-bearing walls, gas riser). Insight includes identifying missing measurements/info needed from the floor plan and stating assumptions that affect feasibility.

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and Value-added Strategies under Constraints
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards unique yet practical ideas that materially improve outcomes within budget/time—for example, a sliding glass partition to balance openness and odor control, modular island solutions, or cost-savvy material substitutions that preserve the warm aesthetic. The focus is on non-generic, high-impact moves.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a logical, renovation-specific structure (brief and constraints → concept → space planning → materials/specs → MEP/appliance plan → budget → schedule → risk/contingency), enabling a stepwise, non-circular argument.

Assesses whether each client requirement (e.g., open-plan kitchen functionality, warm wood/fabric palette, ¥150k including appliances and furniture, ~3 months) is explicitly mapped to concrete design choices, with acknowledgement of building codes/structural limits where relevant.

logical_coherence.criterion: Rigor of reasoning from premises to design decisions and trade-offs
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan derives solutions logically from stated premises (e.g., cooking habits → ventilation, work triangle, storage; budget/time constraints → scope choices), explicitly articulating trade-offs and justifying selections over alternatives.

Checks that claims about materials, furniture, layout, and systems are backed by functional or technical justification (e.g., durability and maintenance of wood/fabric, ergonomic dimensions, appliance specs, ventilation strategies) rather than unsupported assertions.

Verifies that numbers and decisions align across sections: itemized costs sum within ¥150k; scope matches budget; schedule durations fit ~3 months; quantities match 80㎡; appliance loads align with MEP plan; no contradictions (e.g., ‘open-plan’ vs. closed layout).

logical_coherence.criterion: Smooth transitions and dependency logic between sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether sections connect without logical jumps and reflect real dependencies (e.g., layout guides MEP; procurement lead times inform schedule; budget informs scope), with clear signposting of how one step leads to the next.

clarity_readability.criterion: Sectioning and Navigability (Macro Structure)
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report has a clear hierarchy and TOC with distinct sections (e.g., overview, room-by-room plan, materials, furniture/appliances, budget, schedule, procurement checklist), consistent headings, and cross-references so readers can quickly find kitchen, living room, or budget details.

clarity_readability.criterion: Typographic Cleanliness and Layout Aesthetics (Micro Formatting)
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses paragraph length, line spacing, font size consistency, use of bullet lists for specs/checklists, table alignment, figure captions, and white space so long content is comfortable to read on screen or print.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity, Concision, and Instruction-Oriented Language
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether wording is simple and unambiguous, avoids run-on sentences and jargon, uses active voice and measurable specs (e.g., dimensions, color temperature, finishes), and provides actionable steps where appropriate.

clarity_readability.criterion: Terminology Explanation and Unit Conventions
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that technical terms (e.g., engineered wood vs solid wood, E0/E1 emission class, VOC, 2700–4000K lighting, IP ratings) are briefly defined in plain language; units are metric and consistent (mm, m², ¥); and any standards or ratings are decoded at first mention.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Spatial/Design Visuals
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses clarity of floor plans, annotated kitchen workflow (work triangle), lighting layout diagrams, elevations, mood boards, and material palettes with legends, scales, labels, and readable color/line coding to convey spatial intent at a glance.

clarity_readability.criterion: Budget and BOM Table Readability
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether budget/BOM tables have clear categories (e.g., finishes, carpentry, furniture, appliances, lighting, labor), line items with units/quantities, subtotals, tax/contingency notes, totals in ¥, and any footnotes or links, enabling quick cost scanning.

clarity_readability.criterion: Timeline and Process Visualization Clarity
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for a readable phase/timeline (e.g., Gantt-style) showing key stages, dependencies, lead times, milestones, and buffers within ~3 months, so readers can understand the sequence and timing without deciphering dense text.

clarity_readability.criterion: Emphasis and At-a-Glance Cues
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses use of summaries, callouts, icons, highlights, and checklists to surface priorities (must-have vs optional), risk/lead-time flags for appliances/materials, and key decisions, improving skimmability for busy readers.

task: I would like to renovate my apartment in the coming months to create a home environment that is both comfortable and stylish. My apartment is about 80㎡, with two bedrooms and one living room. I hope to use natural wood and fabric materials in the renovation to create a warm atmosphere. My total renovation budget is about ¥150,000, which I hope will also cover furniture and appliances. I aim to complete the renovation within three months, though I can be flexible if necessary.I live with my partner, and our high-frequency daily activity areas are mainly the living room and kitchen. I enjoy cooking, so my ideal layout would improve the functionality of an open-plan kitchen. In terms of existing conditions, the lighting is generally good, and the master bedroom faces south. I have the floor plan available, and can share it with you if needed. Please create a detailed design plan, including furniture selection, material recommendations, and budget guidance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Budget-constrained scope and trade-off strategy that includes furniture and appliances
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan recognizes ¥150,000 as tight for an 80㎡ full refresh and responds with prioritized scope (living room and kitchen first), tiered options (good/better/best), a detailed cost breakdown (materials, labor, furniture, appliances), where-to-splurge/save guidance, and contingency (e.g., 8–10%). This matches the user’s cost-effectiveness mindset and ensures acceptance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Feasible open-plan kitchen solution tailored to heavy Chinese cooking and local regulations
goal_alignment.explanation: Check that the plan addresses gas vs induction constraints and code-compliant open solutions (e.g., sliding glass partitions if gas is kept), high-suction hood specs, make-up air/vent routing, grease/odor control, workflow and two-person cooking ergonomics, durable easy-clean finishes, and noise control. This directly serves his cooking habits and desire for an open kitchen without violating rules.

goal_alignment.criterion: Climate- and future-pet-ready material and appliance selection for a warm wood/fabric style
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether wood selections (E0/E1 boards, moisture-resistant substrates, suitable species/finishes) and fabrics (washable, stain-resistant cotton/linen blends) are specified for Hangzhou humidity and potential future pet scratches. Appliances should prioritize energy efficiency and reliability. This keeps the warm aesthetic practical and durable for his context.

goal_alignment.criterion: Data-driven decision support: BOM, quantified comparisons, and time-risk planning
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify that the report provides a structured bill of materials, vendor/brand options with quantified specs and prices, sensitivity analysis (budget/time), and a realistic 3-month Gantt with procurement lead times and risk buffers. This aligns with his analytical decision style and need for predictable execution.

goal_alignment.criterion: Smart-home integration aligned with existing devices and ROI
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the design leverages his current smart speaker/lighting, defines core scenes (cooking, focus, movie, night), plans robust networking (mesh/AP placement, wiring), ensures interoperability and data privacy, and presents a cost-effective upgrade path. This matches his interest in smart products and rational ROI focus.

goal_alignment.criterion: Co-living ergonomics and WFH zoning
goal_alignment.explanation: Check that the plan considers two-person use in living/kitchen, provides a WFH corner with ergonomic desk, cable management, acoustic treatment, and suitable video-call lighting/background, and designs the second bedroom for study/guest flexibility. This fits his lifestyle and remote work habits.

goal_alignment.criterion: Floor plan utilization and MEP-aware, orientation-sensitive design
goal_alignment.explanation: Ensure the proposal explicitly requests and uses the floor plan, sizes furniture to clearances, respects wet-area and structural constraints, and leverages orientation (south-facing master: glare/heat control, blackout). This demonstrates real-world feasibility and personalized spatial reasoning.

goal_alignment.criterion: Maintainability and cleaning efficiency in high-use areas
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate inclusion of easy-clean kitchen surfaces and layouts, dishwasher, pull-out faucet, organized recycling, robot vacuum docking/charging, washable slipcovers, and mold-resistant paints. This matches his convenience/housekeeping usage and ensures long-term livability.

goal_alignment.criterion: Engineer-aligned communication and reasoning style
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the content is structured with clear assumptions, stepwise logic, comparison tables/lists, option trees, and acceptance criteria; uses precise specs and references; and provides clear next actions (e.g., 'share floor plan'). This increases trust and decision efficiency for a data-driven user.

content_alignment.criterion: Kitchen-Centric Functional Specificity for Chinese Open-Plan Cooking
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether the content prioritizes the kitchen with details matched to a cooking enthusiast in a Chinese context: high-suction/pressure hood specs, fume containment strategies for open plans, efficient work triangle and dual-user flow, heat/grease-resistant surfaces, storage for woks/spices, dishwasher/steam-oven fit, sink and lighting specs. This targets the user's core hobby and high-frequency area.

content_alignment.criterion: Budget Fit and Cost-Effectiveness Under ¥150,000 (All-In)
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan tailors a realistic cost breakdown for 80㎡ in Hangzhou and proposes value-tier brand options for furniture/appliances, highlighting where to splurge vs save to keep the total within ¥150k. This aligns with the user's rational, data-driven spending and the explicit budget constraint.

content_alignment.criterion: Natural Wood + Fabric Warm Aesthetic with Material-Safety Specifics
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether recommendations center on wood and fabric choices that achieve warmth while addressing VOC/formaldehyde (E0/E1), finish types (water-based oil/UV), moisture resistance, abrasion ratings, and fabric durability/cleanability. This aligns with the stated aesthetic preference and the need for comfortable, healthy, easy-to-maintain materials.

content_alignment.criterion: Smart Home Integration Tailored to a Tech-Savvy User
content_alignment.explanation: Looks for ecosystem-aware suggestions (e.g., Matter/HomeKit/Zigbee), device interoperability with existing smart speakers/lighting, scene design for cooking/focus/night use, network/power planning, and energy monitoring. This matches the user’s tech background and growing smart home interest.

content_alignment.criterion: Localization to Hangzhou Climate and Living Conditions
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether content accounts for humid climate impacts (moisture-resistant flooring/cabinetry, anti-mold finishes, dehumidification, epoxy grout), laundry/drying strategies, and appropriate shading/ventilation. Adapting to the local environment improves relevance and durability.

content_alignment.criterion: Data- and Spec-Driven Depth for Decision-Making
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluates the inclusion of quantitative benchmarks (e.g., hood m³/min and Pa, countertop hardness, lighting lux/CRI/CCT, fabric rub counts, emissions standards) and trade-off reasoning. This aligns with the user's analytical mindset and preference for evidence-based choices.

content_alignment.criterion: Couple-Oriented Space Programming and Night Productivity Support
content_alignment.explanation: Checks whether the plan considers two occupants’ concurrent use: dual workstation options, acoustic comfort for sleeping vs night work, glare control, and storage allocations for two. This addresses the user's relationship status and night-time efficiency preferences.

content_alignment.criterion: Pet-Readiness and Housekeeping Efficiency
content_alignment.explanation: Assesses whether content pre-empts future pet ownership and routine cleaning: scratch-resistant/washable finishes, performance fabrics, rounded corners, robot vacuum docking, and dirt-trap minimization. This aligns with the user’s service usage and potential pet plans.

presentation_fit.criterion: Logical structure and navigation suited to an engineer (top-down, layered detail, clear sections)
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report starts with an executive summary, then proceeds with numbered headings and a room-by-room and phase-by-phase structure, includes a table of contents, checklists, and section cross-references so the user can skim and deep-dive efficiently.

presentation_fit.criterion: Concise, objective language and precision of wording
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether the tone is professional and neutral (no marketing fluff), uses active voice, quantifies claims (dimensions, costs, lead times), and avoids vague adjectives so the reader gets clear, measurable information.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology appropriateness with brief clarifications
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that interior/material/construction terms (e.g., E0/E1, moisture-resistant MDF, circuit ratings) are used correctly and accompanied by brief definitions or tooltips/footnotes when first introduced to minimize cognitive overhead without oversimplifying.

presentation_fit.criterion: Quantitative presentation with structured tables and charts
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether budgets, bill of materials, item lists, and comparisons are presented in tables; whether there is an itemized cost breakdown by room/category; and whether timelines are shown as a Gantt or milestone chart to enable quick numeric scanning.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use and clarity of visual aids for spatial/function concepts
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether the report includes annotated floor plan overlays, kitchen work-triangle/workflow diagrams, storage layout sketches, and color/mood boards with clear labels and scale references to communicate design intent without dense text.

presentation_fit.criterion: Localization and unit consistency for Mainland readers
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that all figures use RMB and metric (mm/m²), that Mainland-standard terminology and common model naming are used, and that any links or references align with Chinese market conventions to reduce translation/friction costs.

presentation_fit.criterion: Decision traceability: explicit assumptions, constraints, and trade-off rationale formatting
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report surfaces an 'Assumptions & Constraints' section, highlights risks/alternatives in a matrix or callouts, and ties recommendations to criteria (e.g., cost, durability, lead time) so the reasoning is transparent and easy to audit.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Budget realism and itemized cost plan (≤¥150,000 including furniture and appliances) with contingency and trade-offs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report provides an itemized budget covering hard renovation, cabinetry, countertops, flooring/walls, lighting, furniture, and key appliances; includes unit price ranges typical for Hangzhou; sets an 8–12% contingency; and offers substitution options with impact notes so the user can keep the total within ¥150,000.

actionability_practicality.criterion: 3-month schedule feasibility and execution plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report gives a realistic sequence (design–demolition–MEP rough-in–finish–install–acceptance), identifies critical path items (custom cabinets, appliances, quartz lead times), prescribes preordering windows, and includes buffer for inspections/rectification so the project can finish in about three months.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Structural and regulatory feasibility for open-plan kitchen and layout changes
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report addresses building/property-management rules (e.g., gas use in open kitchens, load-bearing wall limits, wet/dry area constraints, noise hours), proposes compliant solutions (induction + open, or sliding glass partitions for gas), and clarifies required approvals to avoid illegal works or redesign.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Kitchen and MEP technical specifications for heavy cooking
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for concrete specs enabling immediate purchasing and construction: hood suction (e.g., ≥20 m³/min) and duct diameter/path, make-up air, grease control, cooktop (gas vs induction power needs), dedicated circuits (e.g., 16A/32A), RCDs, dishwasher/oven water and drainage points, floor drain/waterproofing standards.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Quantified space planning and ergonomics for high-use areas
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for measurable layout guidance that can be drawn and built: kitchen aisle width (e.g., 1000–1200 mm), work triangle distances, island/bar dimensions and overhang, counter heights, living room circulation widths, seating distances, and storage planning sized to the couple’s daily use.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Material performance, indoor air quality, and maintenance practicality
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies that the material recommendations for wood and fabrics include formaldehyde/VOC ratings (E0/E1), prefinished options to reduce curing time, durability metrics (e.g., wear class/Martindale for fabrics), stain/oil resistance for open kitchen adjacency, and cleaning/maintenance instructions suitable for long-term use and possible future pet.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Procurement and vendor strategy with local availability
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines whether the report provides a prioritized shopping list with mainstream domestic brands/channels available in Hangzhou, rough SKUs or spec tiers, and an order schedule (what to buy in which week) to secure on-time delivery and negotiated pricing.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk control and quality acceptance workflow
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for practical safeguards: budget/schedule buffers, alternative options if quotes exceed plan, contract/payment milestones, and trade-specific acceptance checklists (e.g., 24-hour water test, electrical insulation tests, cabinet leveling, countertop joint inspection) to reduce rework and disputes.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Smart home integration feasibility and cost control
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan gives feasible wiring and protocol guidance (neutral-at-switch, Zigbee/Matter compatibility), circuit loads for smart appliances, and a modest budget for essential scenes in kitchen/living so the user can integrate without overcomplicating or overspending.

depth_insight.criterion: Constraint-aware spatial optimization and zoning for 80㎡, 2BR1LR with open-plan living/kitchen
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the plan translates the apartment’s size and usage priorities into high-value spatial decisions—circulation, storage, multi-use zones, and acoustic control—tailored to two adults and high-use living/kitchen areas.

depth_insight.criterion: Kitchen functionality engineering for an avid cook in an open-plan context
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates depth of technical decisions on work triangle/flows, counter length and landing zones, storage systems (e.g., pantry, drawer organization), ventilation performance and noise, grease/smell control, fire safety, electrical/gas loads, and heat/moisture-resistant finishes.

depth_insight.criterion: Material and finish selection depth aligned with 'natural wood + fabric' aesthetics and performance
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether wood species/grade, solid vs veneer strategies, finish systems, and fabric composition are chosen for durability, cleanability, tactile warmth, acoustic benefit, and VOC/IAQ impact, with placement logic (wet vs dry zones).

depth_insight.criterion: Budget realism, cost engineering, and trade-off reasoning within ¥150,000 (incl. furniture & appliances)
depth_insight.explanation: Judges whether the plan provides granular allocations and sourcing strategies, identifies cost drivers (e.g., custom cabinetry, appliances), includes contingency, and articulates clear save/splurge choices that protect core function and aesthetics.

depth_insight.criterion: Timeline and procurement feasibility for ~3 months with risk mitigation
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates recognition of critical path items (cabinetry, countertops, appliances), lead times, sequencing, and permits/utilities coordination, plus mitigation strategies (e.g., modular/pre-fab, in-stock selections, parallel tasks).

depth_insight.criterion: Daylighting, thermal comfort, and environmental responsiveness
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the design leverages good lighting and the south-facing master through reflectance-aware palettes, window treatments, shading/insulation, cross-ventilation, and thermal comfort strategies that enhance livability.

depth_insight.criterion: Lifestyle personalization and functional trade-offs for two adults
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for evidence that daily routines (cooking frequency, entertaining, potential WFH, cleaning workflows) are mapped to concrete decisions on seating, dining, storage, and flexibility, with explicit trade-offs and justifications.

depth_insight.criterion: Specification specificity and market-informed recommendations
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report proposes product/spec ranges or brand tiers (e.g., appliance performance metrics, flooring grades) with price-performance justification suited to the local market and the stated budget.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether each major design choice (layout changes, material selections, furniture/appliances, kitchen plan) is explicitly linked to a stated premise (e.g., budget ¥150k, 80㎡ area, open-plan kitchen need, natural wood/fabric preference, south-facing bedroom, high-use living room/kitchen). Coherence improves when recommendations include clear 'because' statements tied to the client’s requirements and site conditions.

Evaluates whether the proposed scope (demolition, carpentry, finishes, MEP, furniture, appliances) logically matches the ¥150,000 budget and 3-month timeline, with quantitative checks (unit costs, quantities, sums; duration estimates; task dependencies). Coherence requires that numbers add up and the schedule respects critical path (e.g., rough-in precedes finishes) without hidden scope inflation.

Assesses whether the report follows a logical sequence (requirements and constraints → site/plan diagnosis → space program and layout → materials/finishes → furniture/appliances sizing → MEP implications → budget → schedule → risk controls), avoiding circular reasoning or premature conclusions.

Checks that important claims are supported by relevant, appropriately scoped evidence (e.g., cost benchmarks for materials, typical appliance dimensions and clearances, durability/maintenance rationale for wood/fabric choices, code or safety constraints for open-plan kitchens). This ensures decisions aren’t asserted without grounding.

logical_coherence.criterion: Explicit Assumptions, Constraints, and Trade-off Reasoning
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether assumptions (e.g., no structural wall removal, existing plumbing locations), constraints (building codes, ventilation needs for open kitchens), and trade-offs (e.g., engineered wood vs. solid wood to meet budget; island vs. peninsula for circulation) are stated and their impacts traced through to the final plan, budget, and timeline.

Assesses whether transitions between sections make the causal links explicit (e.g., how cooking frequency drives storage and ventilation specs; how material choices affect cost and schedule; how room orientation informs furniture placement), avoiding logical jumps. Focuses on the integrity of reasoning connectors, not stylistic elegance.

clarity_readability.criterion: Information Architecture and Navigability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report has a clear, predictable structure (e.g., executive summary; room-by-room plan; materials; furniture/appliances; budget; timeline; procurement/checklists; appendix with floor plan). Assesses heading hierarchy, section order, table of contents, and anchors so readers can quickly locate what they need.

clarity_readability.criterion: Language Clarity and Instructional Precision
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates use of plain, concise language suitable for non-experts; avoidance of ambiguity; consistent naming of spaces/items; actionable phrasing for steps (e.g., install, place, select) without verbose or vague descriptors; minimal grammar/spelling errors and an appropriate professional tone.

clarity_readability.criterion: Terminology and Units Standardization (with Explanations)
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether technical terms are defined on first use (e.g., E1/E0 formaldehyde grade, veneer vs. solid wood, SPC flooring, CRI/color temperature, soft-close hardware) and whether units are consistent (mm, m², W, L, dB, ¥). Currency formatting, tax notes, and any conversion assumptions are clearly stated.

clarity_readability.criterion: Effectiveness of Tables, Charts, and Visualizations
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether budget tables, timelines (e.g., Gantt-style), and diagrams are legible and self-explanatory with clear titles, labels, legends, units, and totals/subtotals. Visuals (e.g., layout diagrams, material palettes, mood boards) should be high-resolution, uncluttered, and support quick comprehension.

clarity_readability.criterion: Dimensional Specification and Scale Clarity
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that spatial drawings and furniture recommendations include clear dimensions and scale indicators (e.g., scale bars), readable room/furniture measurements, and indicated clearances (aisle widths, door swings). Focus is on visibility and unambiguous presentation, not design correctness.

clarity_readability.criterion: Micro-formatting and Visual Hierarchy
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses typography, spacing, bullet/numbered lists, callouts, and emphasis of key constraints (¥150,000 budget, ~3-month timeline). Ensures adequate white space, consistent styles, and scannability so long sections (e.g., item lists) remain easy to read.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency and Cross-referencing
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates consistent terminology across sections (e.g., “Master Bedroom” vs. “Bedroom A”), item codes/SKUs used across shopping lists and budget tables, and clear figure/table numbering and references so readers can cross-check details without confusion.

task: I would like to renovate my apartment in the coming months to create a home environment that is both comfortable and stylish. My apartment is about 80㎡, with two bedrooms and one living room. I hope to use natural wood and fabric materials in the renovation to create a warm atmosphere. My total renovation budget is about ¥150,000, which I hope will also cover furniture and appliances. I aim to complete the renovation within three months, though I can be flexible if necessary.I live with my partner, and our high-frequency daily activity areas are mainly the living room and kitchen. I enjoy cooking, so my ideal layout would improve the functionality of an open-plan kitchen. In terms of existing conditions, the lighting is generally good, and the master bedroom faces south. I have the floor plan available, and can share it with you if needed. Please create a detailed design plan, including furniture selection, material recommendations, and budget guidance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Open-plan kitchen functionality tailored to cooking habits
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan designs an open kitchen that suits frequent Italian and Southeast Asian cooking: strong but quiet hood for open plan, efficient work triangle, ≥2.2m uninterrupted prep counter, built-in or countertop oven, logical spice/utensil access, dishwasher to save time, coffee/pizza corner, and odor/noise control suitable for a creative live-work home.

goal_alignment.criterion: Shanghai-relevant budget realism with furniture/appliances included
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the budget uses Shanghai market prices and includes all major categories (construction, cabinetry, appliances, furniture, lighting, soft furnishings) within ¥150,000, with clear trade-offs, sourcing strategies (standard modules + selective custom, indie crafts within limits, second-hand where sensible), and contingency for overruns.

goal_alignment.criterion: Work-from-home creative studio integration
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if the plan provides a dedicated studio zone (likely second bedroom) with flat-file/storage for art supplies and photography gear, cable/power planning (including streaming setup), color-accurate task lighting (CRI≥90), acoustic treatment disguised as decor, tidy backdrop, ergonomic desk/chair, and blackout/ambient controls for evening creativity.

goal_alignment.criterion: Pet-friendly design and materials for a lively cat
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine whether finishes and furnishings are cat-suitable: scratch-resistant/washable performance fabrics, water-based matte coatings on wood, closed/ventilated litter niche with odor control, integrated cat shelves/routes that harmonize with the style, pet-safe plants, and easy-clean floor selections.

goal_alignment.criterion: Space planning for couple’s high-use living room + kitchen and wellness
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the layout prioritizes living room and kitchen as activity hubs for two people, with a dining island for 2–4, flexible seating, concealed storage to avoid visual clutter, a defined 2×1.5m yoga/Pilates area, and pathways that accommodate cat movement without congestion.

goal_alignment.criterion: Local regulations and building constraints for open kitchen
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether the plan addresses Shanghai apartment constraints: gas vs induction implications for open kitchens, possible need for fire-rated doors/partitions, electrical load planning (dedicated 16–20A circuits for induction/oven), neighbor noise considerations, and building management approvals.

goal_alignment.criterion: Natural wood and fabric specification with durability and maintenance
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether material recommendations balance the requested warm, natural feel with practicality: engineered/veneered oak/ash where appropriate, FSC/low-VOC products, water/oil resistance near kitchen, performance linens/microfibers for cat and easy upkeep, and clear maintenance guidance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Lighting design for evening creativity and warm atmosphere
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess a layered lighting plan: dimmable warm ambient (2700–3000K), high-CRI task lights for studio and kitchen, accent lights for art display, minimal glare in open plan, and scene presets to support evening peak creativity and cozy daily living.

goal_alignment.criterion: Aesthetic resonance with Jiangnan/Japandi and personal art display
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if the stylistic direction reflects his Jiangnan/boutique guesthouse taste via calm wood/linen/paper textures, restrained palette, and integrates a gallery rail/ledges for rotating his illustrations and travel photos, plus curated handmade/indie pieces within budget.

goal_alignment.criterion: 3-month timeline feasibility and procurement plan
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether the schedule is realistic: front-load measurements and floor plan confirmation, early ordering of long-lead items (cabinets, appliances, sofa), minimal wet works to compress duration, sequencing of trades, and a buffer to handle delays without derailing the move-in.

goal_alignment.criterion: Health and indoor air quality safeguards
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate use of low-VOC paints/adhesives, ventilation plan during/after renovation (air purging schedule), dust control for art equipment, and recommendations for air purification to protect his health and continuous creative work.

goal_alignment.criterion: Decision-support style, optioning, and floor plan utilization
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report requests and uses his specific floor plan, presents 1–2 layout options with pros/cons and cost impact (aligned to his intuitive yet consultative decision style), employs visual language/moodboards, and provides clear, comparable furniture/appliance picks.

content_alignment.criterion: Cooking-Centric Open Kitchen and High-Use Living Area Tailoring
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether content prioritizes an open kitchen optimized for his cooking habits (e.g., work triangle, island/peninsula options, high-suction quiet range hood for open plans, oven suitable for pizza/pasta, durable easy-clean countertop/backsplash) and the living room–kitchen interaction patterns that match his and his partner’s daily high use.

content_alignment.criterion: Creator Workspace and On-Camera Aesthetics Alignment
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan includes a dedicated illustration/studio setup (ergonomic desk/chair, calibrated/task lighting, storage for art/photography gear, cable management) and camera-friendly backdrops, acoustics, and display of artworks/travel photos to support live streaming and content creation.

content_alignment.criterion: Natural Materials, Palette, and Jiangnan-Inspired Style Fit
content_alignment.explanation: Check if material and color selections reflect his preference for warm natural wood and fabrics and subtly reference Jiangnan/Suzhou aesthetics (e.g., oak/ash/walnut tones, linen/cotton/wool textures, ink-wash neutrals, water-green accents, refined handcrafted details) rather than generic ‘modern’ styles.

content_alignment.criterion: Pet-Friendly Integration without Aesthetic Compromise
content_alignment.explanation: Determine whether the content integrates Mo Mo’s needs (scratch-resistant/removable-cover upholstery, cat-safe finishes/plants, concealed litter/feeding stations, perches/shelves as part of millwork) while maintaining the desired warm aesthetic and material consistency.

content_alignment.criterion: Design-Literate Depth and Justification
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether recommendations include the level of specificity suitable for an art-trained user (e.g., engineered wood structure and finish types, fabric rub counts/pilling resistance, lighting CRI/CCT layering, appliance performance criteria) with reasoning that ties back to his aesthetic and use cases.

content_alignment.criterion: Shanghai Climate and Market Context Relevance
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content is localized: moisture management (engineered wood, anti-warp cabinetry, anti-mold strategies), ventilation suited to local cooking, and sourcing from China-available brands/independent designers he prefers to support, plus energy labels and practical local availability.

content_alignment.criterion: Couple Cohabitation and Wellness Routine Consideration
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the plan reflects two-person daily flow and storage, a flexible area for yoga/Pilates with gear storage and appropriate flooring/lighting, a quiet evening ambiance for his creative peak, and dedicated entry/parcel areas given frequent deliveries.

content_alignment.criterion: Budget-Sensitive Selection Matching Spending Preferences
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the content frames ‘splurge vs save’ choices aligned with his quality-focused, rational consumption (e.g., invest in kitchen appliances/hood/oven, workstation ergonomics, mattress; economize on decor or secondary pieces) and explicitly includes furniture and appliances within the scope.

presentation_fit.criterion: Information architecture and scannability tailored to a designer reader
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks whether the report is hierarchically structured (overview → by room → materials/furniture → budget → timeline), uses clear headings, numbered steps, bullet lists, and concise summaries so a visually trained user can scan and jump to sections quickly.

presentation_fit.criterion: Spatial/layout visualization clarity
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates the use of annotated floor plans and before/after overlays, zoning/circulation arrows, and kitchen work-triangle/flow diagrams with a readable legend and scale, enabling effortless comprehension of layout ideas.

presentation_fit.criterion: Material and color palette presentation
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether moodboards, texture close-ups, wood/fabric swatches, and consistent palette references (e.g., hex/Pantone names) are provided and labeled so an illustrator can grasp the tactile and chromatic direction at a glance.

presentation_fit.criterion: Terminology balance and micro-glossary
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks that design/build terms (e.g., veneer vs. solid wood, quartz, E1/E0) are used precisely with brief inline clarifications or a micro-glossary, preventing jargon from blocking understanding.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tone and voice alignment with a creative professional
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates whether the language is calm, precise, and craft-focused (non-salesy), respecting aesthetic judgment and avoiding hype, which aligns with a rational, design-literate reader.

presentation_fit.criterion: Measurement and unit/currency consistency
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks consistent metric notation (mm for furniture, ㎡ for area), dimension callouts on diagrams, and ¥/RMB formatting with clear grouping and rounding—critical for reading drawings and costs without confusion.

presentation_fit.criterion: Budget visualization clarity
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses whether per-room/per-category costs are presented with readable charts or tables, clear legends, color-coding, and labels, enabling fast comprehension of allocation without digging through text.

presentation_fit.criterion: Timeline and dependency visualization
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks for a simple Gantt-style or phased bar chart with legible week markers and dependencies (e.g., procurement before install), so progress is understandable at a glance.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-friendly layout and accessibility
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates short paragraphs, adequate contrast, alt-like captions for images, and tap-friendly section anchors so content remains readable on a phone during evening browsing.

actionability_practicality.criterion: All-in budget realism and itemization (≤¥150,000)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides an itemized, Shanghai-market–based budget covering hard renovation, soft furnishing, and appliances, includes labor and logistics, and reserves 10–15% contingency—so the user can actually purchase and build without overspending.

actionability_practicality.criterion: 3‑month schedule and lead‑time feasibility
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the plan breaks down phases (design/approvals, MEP, finishes, cabinetry, delivery/installation) with realistic durations and dependencies, reflecting custom cabinet/sofa/appliance lead times and on-site sequencing to meet the ~3‑month target.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Buildability and compliance (open kitchen, structure, permits)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the recommendations can be legally and safely executed in a Shanghai apartment, including gas/open‑kitchen rules (e.g., partition solutions), structural/wet‑area constraints, property management approvals, and ventilation/duct routing feasibility.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Functional layout practicality for lifestyle and pets
actionability_practicality.explanation: Judges whether the plan improves real use: a workable kitchen triangle and prep area, dining for two/guests, storage for art supplies/equipment, a streaming‑friendly background, a 2×2 m clear area for yoga/Pilates, and cat‑friendly provisions (litter placement, scratch‑resistant choices).

actionability_practicality.criterion: Material and finish practicality (natural, durable, low‑VOC, pet‑friendly)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if wood and fabric recommendations balance aesthetics with maintenance and longevity (engineered wood, water‑based finishes, quartz in kitchen, washable performance fabrics), specify emission standards (e.g., E0/E1, low‑VOC paints), and consider south‑facing light.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Appliance package and MEP integration for heavy cooking
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether appliance specs (high‑suction hood with adequate static pressure, oven for pizza/pasta, dishwasher, fridge size) and MEP details (duct diameter/path, dedicated circuits/load, outlet counts, make‑up air, gas detector) are specified and coordinated to fit space and budget.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Procurement and contractor execution plan
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for clear sourcing channels (e.g., IKEA/Red Star Macalline/JD/Tmall flagship/custom carpentry), contractor selection criteria, quote comparison methods, payment milestones, warranty terms, and supervision arrangements to turn the plan into procurement actions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Phase checklists and acceptance criteria
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates presence of step‑by‑step checklists (pre‑construction measurement, MEP rough‑in, 24–48 h waterproofing test, tile flatness tolerances, wood moisture, cabinet alignment, appliance fit) and final punchlist to reduce errors and rework.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk and contingency planning (cost/time/material substitutions)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report anticipates price swings, delivery delays, or approval risks and provides fallback materials, alternate brands/specs, and time/budget buffers, enabling the user to react quickly without derailing the project.

depth_insight.criterion: Client- and Site-Specific Diagnosis & Goal Translation
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the plan derives concrete priorities from the apartment’s 80㎡ size, two-bedroom layout, lighting conditions, south-facing master, high-use living/kitchen zones, strong cooking needs, ¥150k all-in budget, and 3-month target—avoiding generic advice.

depth_insight.criterion: Kitchen Functionality Optimization for an Open-plan Layout
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates depth in workflow zoning (prep/cook/clean/store), landing clearances, counter length, storage systems, ventilation/odor control (hood performance, ducting, alternatives), grease and fire safety, heat/moisture materials, and electrical/gas decisions tailored to a serious cook in an open-plan setting.

depth_insight.criterion: Value Engineering and Budget Allocation Realism
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether the budget is itemized and benchmarked (trades, cabinetry, finishes, appliances, furniture, lighting, contingency), with trade-off logic (where to splurge vs save), procurement strategies, and cost-saving tactics that keep the total within ¥150k without undermining core goals.

depth_insight.criterion: Material System Fit (Natural Wood & Fabrics) with Performance and IAQ
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses the insightfulness of selecting wood species/finishes and fabrics that deliver warmth while addressing durability, moisture/grease resistance (especially in kitchen), cleanability, abrasion/stain resistance, and low-VOC/low-formaldehyde standards within budget.

depth_insight.criterion: Space Planning & Functional Zoning for 80㎡, 2BR-1LR
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates how well the plan uses the floor plan to optimize circulation, daylight flow, storage integration, multi-use furniture, dining-island strategies, and living room usability—the primary high-frequency areas—while respecting constraints and adjacencies.

depth_insight.criterion: Buildability, MEP, and Compliance Feasibility
depth_insight.explanation: Checks whether proposals account for structural walls, exhaust routing to façade/shaft, gas vs induction feasibility, electrical load/circuits, plumbing slopes, HOA/policy constraints, and noise isolation—ensuring the design can actually be executed.

depth_insight.criterion: Timeline Feasibility and Risk Mitigation
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the 3-month schedule is supported by sequencing logic (demolition, MEP rough-in, finishes, install), lead-time awareness (custom cabinetry, appliances), and mitigation plans (alternatives, phased deliveries) to reduce delay risk.

depth_insight.criterion: Environmental Comfort Strategy (Lighting, Acoustics, IAQ)
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates layered lighting design (ambient/task/accent), daylight leverage (south-facing master, overall good light), acoustic measures for open plan (soft finishes, partitions), and IAQ strategies (post-renovation airing/filtration) that enhance daily comfort.

depth_insight.criterion: Furniture and Appliance Specification Rigor
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether selections are dimensionally and ergonomically appropriate (clearances, heights), integrate with cabinetry, match cooking habits (hob/oven/hood specs, noise levels), and provide adequate storage within the spatial and budget limits.

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and High-Value, Tailored Solutions
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the plan proposes creative yet feasible ideas (e.g., convertible island-dining, pocket/semi-open partitions, downdraft or dual-vent strategies, modular storage) with clear user-value and evidence-backed justifications beyond common advice.

logical_coherence.criterion: Structured Problem-to-Solution Framework Specific to Renovation
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a logical sequence: requirements and site assessment → constraints and priorities → layout方案 options (if any) and selection rationale → materials/furniture/appliances plan → budget allocation → construction schedule and milestones. A consistent structure prevents logical jumps and ensures each downstream decision rests on earlier premises.

Evaluates whether each major recommendation (layout changes, material choices, furniture/appliance selections) explicitly maps back to stated goals and constraints (e.g., open-plan kitchen functionality, warm natural materials, ¥150k total, 3-month timeline, two-person usage patterns, lighting orientation). Clear traceability demonstrates that conclusions logically derive from premises.
