presentation_fit.criterion: Locality signaling and Shanghai-specific labeling
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks that the report explicitly flags which parts are national baseline versus Shanghai-specific practices, uses RMB formatting, and labels local procedures to enhance immediate relevance.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-friendly, scannable layout
presentation_fit.explanation: Assesses chunking into short paragraphs, bullet lists, meaningful subheadings, and micro-summaries so the report remains readable on a phone, aligning with the user’s social/app consumption habits.

presentation_fit.criterion: Transparent citations, links, and versioning
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluates presence of clear source attributions (official notices/regs) with links, date stamps, and version notes to foster trust and allow quick verification without intrusive footnote clutter.

presentation_fit.criterion: Clean, aesthetic design and accessibility
presentation_fit.explanation: Checks for consistent typography, sufficient white space, simple icons, color cues with adequate contrast, and alt text for visuals, aligning with the user’s design sensibilities and ensuring readability.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Local policy applicability and legal accuracy
actionability_practicality.explanation: The report cites and applies up-to-date Shanghai and national expropriation rules (e.g., local implementing measures, appraisal standards), clarifies which rules govern this case, and links to official sources. Without correct, localized law, downstream actions risk error.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Transparent compensation calculation framework with worked examples
actionability_practicality.explanation: Provides formulas and itemized components (market value appraisal, moving/temporary resettlement subsidies, decoration/fixtures, business interruption, incentives, price-difference for property exchange), plus a filled example and a plug-in template so the user can estimate her own outcome.

actionability_practicality.criterion: End-to-end process roadmap with time limits and submission requirements
actionability_practicality.explanation: Maps each stage (notice, appraisal agency selection/reappraisal, negotiation, hearing, compensation decision, delivery, subsidies, reconsideration/litigation) with statutory deadlines, responsible agencies, and required materials, enabling the user to act on time and avoid forfeiting rights.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Decision framework for choosing monetary vs property exchange tailored to the user
actionability_practicality.explanation: Offers a multi-criteria comparison (liquidity for café relocation, location fit, school access, commute for parents, renovation timelines/costs, appreciation expectations) and simple sensitivity/breakeven checks so the user can pick the most favorable option for her situation.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Evidence preparation checklist covering both residential and business needs
actionability_practicality.explanation: Lists specific documents to gather (title, legality proofs, household registration/residency, tax records, business license, lease, invoices for fit-out/equipment, payroll/contracts for business loss claims) to substantiate compensation and speed processing.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Negotiation tactics and leverage points
actionability_practicality.explanation: Provides actionable steps to improve outcomes: selecting/rotating appraisal agencies, using comparable sales evidence, requesting hearings, documenting décor/fixtures, engaging collectively where appropriate, and timing decisions around incentive policies.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Legal recourse playbook with steps, deadlines, and templates
actionability_practicality.explanation: Explains what can be challenged (e.g., compensation decision), where to file administrative reconsideration, litigation filing venue, limitation periods, required materials, costs, and whether enforcement can be stayed; includes sample wording to save time.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Business continuity and cash-flow planning for café relocation
actionability_practicality.explanation: Translates compensation models into an operational plan: downtime estimates, temporary site options, renovation timelines, deposit/fit-out budgets, marketing to retain customers, and how subsidies/incentives bridge cash needs to minimize revenue loss.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk flags and compliance safeguards
actionability_practicality.explanation: Identifies common pitfalls (missing hearing windows, accepting undocumented side deals, inadequate evidence for business loss, signing away appeal rights) and prescribes safeguards to avoid irreversible mistakes.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Resource and contact readiness (agencies, appraisers, legal aid) with cost/time estimates
actionability_practicality.explanation: Lists practical points of contact and typical service costs/lead times (e.g., expropriation office, appraisal firms roster, local legal aid/bar associations), enabling immediate outreach and scheduling.

depth_insight.criterion: Granular Synthesis of Compensation Policy and Calculation Mechanics
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report goes beyond listing policies to decompose the actual calculation of monetary and property-exchange compensation (market appraisal basis, subsidies, incentives, taxes/fees, area conversion and balancing payments), addresses edge cases (illegal additions, co-ownership, residential vs business use), and distinguishes national baselines from local adjustable parameters.

depth_insight.criterion: Depth on Appraisal/Evaluation Process and Fairness Safeguards
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report details how valuation is conducted (methods, selection of appraisers, re-evaluation and review procedures, hearings, public notice, rights confirmation) and offers concrete fairness tests (quality of comparables, transparency checks, independent reviews) rather than merely stating that appraisals occur.

depth_insight.criterion: Legal Avenues and Strategy by Stage
depth_insight.explanation: Measures whether the report maps negotiation, hearings, information disclosure, administrative reconsideration, and litigation with actionable specifics (deadlines, competent authorities, standards of review, evidence needs, interim relief options) and provides strategic guidance on when and how each path is most effective.

depth_insight.criterion: Decision-analytic Framework for Choosing Cash vs Property Exchange
depth_insight.explanation: Checks for a rigorous, quantitative and qualitative framework (NPV/time value, delivery timing risk, opportunity cost, taxes/fees, relocation costs, future appreciation assumptions, household needs) with formulas or step-by-step methods to compute breakeven points and compare options in a user-specific way.

depth_insight.criterion: Scenario and Sensitivity Analysis
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report explores different homeowner profiles and market scenarios (e.g., urgent liquidity need, long-term holding, business properties, illegal structures) and performs sensitivity tests on key variables (appraised price, resettlement delays, price appreciation, area ratios) to reveal how recommendations change.

depth_insight.criterion: Risk and Uncertainty Identification with Mitigation Tactics
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report identifies material risks (valuation bias, delivery/quality of resettlement units, eligibility disputes, contract enforcement) and provides concrete mitigation strategies (contract clauses, escrow/bonds, parallel appraisals, documentation/evidence practices).

depth_insight.criterion: Original, Actionable Insights and Negotiation Levers
depth_insight.explanation: Rewards unique, practical insights such as data sources for comparables, checklists for verifying measurements/registrations, leverage points in negotiations (e.g., early-sign incentives vs rights preservation), and red flags that are not generic or widely known.

depth_insight.criterion: Localization and Policy Variability Handling
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a method to adapt the analysis to the specific city (where to find local rules, which coefficients/parameters to extract, how to verify public notices and plan disclosures), acknowledging variability while preserving decision usefulness.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report organizes content in a logical order that mirrors the task: (1) policy framework and scope; (2) calculation standards for monetary compensation and property exchange; (3) evaluation process and fairness safeguards; (4) negotiation mechanisms and legal avenues; (5) decision-making framework for choosing the optimal option. A clear sequence prevents logical gaps and ensures later sections build on established premises.

logical_coherence.criterion: Applicability Logic and Normative Hierarchy Consistency
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the report correctly distinguishes and applies national laws, administrative regulations, local rules, and project-level documents, and uses definitions (e.g., market value, evaluation date, administrative reconsideration) consistently. Proper hierarchy and scope avoid contradictions and invalid inferences when deriving compensation standards and procedures.

logical_coherence.criterion: Evidence–Claim Linkage for Policy and Procedural Assertions
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether each key assertion (e.g., valuation basis, fairness safeguards, eligibility for property exchange, reconsideration prerequisites) is explicitly tied to a policy clause, procedural rule, or recognized valuation method. This prevents unsupported leaps and ensures arguments are grounded in verifiable premises.

Examines whether the report lays out the evaluation, negotiation, reconsideration, and litigation processes in the correct sequence with clear dependencies (e.g., appraisal before offer, negotiation before reconsideration, reconsideration before litigation where required) and time limits/trigger points. Accurate sequencing is essential to a coherent legal-process argument.

logical_coherence.criterion: Rigor of Derivation from Premises to Conclusions
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that conclusions about compensation and fairness follow logically from stated premises (formulas, assumptions about appraised value dates, relocation coefficients, and policy constraints). Ensures no non sequiturs, that boundary conditions are addressed, and that intermediate steps are articulated rather than skipped.

logical_coherence.criterion: Decision Framework Logic for Choosing Compensation Model
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a clear, conditional logic for selecting between monetary compensation and property exchange, mapping household constraints and preferences (timing, cash needs, replacement cost, relocation risk) to outcomes. The if–then structure and trade-off rationale must transparently connect inputs to the recommendation.

Evaluates whether transitions explicitly reference what has been established and signal what follows (e.g., moving from valuation standards to fairness safeguards, from negotiation to legal remedies), avoiding abrupt shifts that force readers to infer missing steps.

clarity_readability.criterion: Task-aligned structure and navigability
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether the report is organized with a clear hierarchy (TOC, numbered headings) that maps directly to the reader’s needs: compensation standards, evaluation process, fairness safeguards, legal avenues (reconsideration/litigation), and option selection. This reduces search time and helps readers quickly locate relevant sections.

clarity_readability.criterion: Plain-language clarity and precision
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether sentences are concise, unambiguous, and free of unnecessary jargon; whether jurisdictional qualifiers (national vs local rules) are stated clearly; and whether consistent terminology is used throughout. Clear wording is crucial for non-experts to grasp legal and policy content.

clarity_readability.criterion: Definition and handling of technical/legal terms
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that key terms and acronyms (e.g., market value, appraisal methods, property exchange coefficient, administrative reconsideration, filing deadlines) are defined at first use and/or compiled in a concise glossary with brief context. Proper term handling lowers comprehension barriers.

clarity_readability.criterion: Process visualization (flowcharts and timelines)
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses the presence and clarity of flowcharts/timelines for the evaluation procedure, negotiation steps, administrative reconsideration windows, and litigation sequence. Visualizing steps and deadlines improves recall and reduces errors in following procedures.

clarity_readability.criterion: Comparative tables and calculation walkthroughs
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether monetary vs property exchange options are presented in side-by-side tables (assumptions, pros/cons, cash flows) and whether formulas are explained with worked numerical examples (units, coefficients). This makes complex calculations and trade-offs easy to grasp.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting of key actions and deadlines
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks the use of callouts, checklists, and summary boxes to emphasize required documents, filing deadlines, and decision points. Highlighting time-sensitive items reduces the risk of missed procedural opportunities.

clarity_readability.criterion: Consistency of numbers, units, and formula formatting
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses consistent use of currency, area units (e.g., CNY/sqm), rounding rules, and formula notation, with assumptions footnoted. Consistency prevents misinterpretation and aids quick numeric verification.

clarity_readability.criterion: Layout aesthetics and accessibility
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates readable typography, adequate white space, mobile-friendly layout, color-safe charts, and alt text for visuals. Good aesthetics and accessibility improve comfort and inclusivity over long reading sessions.

task: My city has recently launched an old town redevelopment project, and we own an old property that has been included in the demolition scope. Please summarize the current housing expropriation and demolition compensation policies, including the calculation standards for monetary compensation and property exchange, the evaluation process, and how to ensure fairness in compensation. I would also like to know the legal avenues available to homeowners during the negotiation of compensation plans, and the possible ways to request administrative reconsideration or initiate legal proceedings if dissatisfied with the compensation outcome. Based on the different compensation models, please help me assess how to choose the most favorable option.

goal_alignment.criterion: Localization to Suzhou/Jiangsu and correct project classification
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the article maps national rules to Jiangsu/Suzhou municipal measures, distinguishes urban state-owned land house expropriation versus collective land/homestead settings typical of old-town renewal, and cites current local policy names and effective dates. This matches the user's city-specific context and avoids generic, inapplicable guidance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Engineer-grade calculation standards with worked examples
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if the compensation components are decomposed into explicit formulas and variables (e.g., appraised market value, decoration/attachments, relocation allowance, temporary resettlement fees, incentive windows, price-difference settlement for property exchange) with numerical examples for both monetary compensation and property swap. This fits the user’s technical, formula-oriented cognition and enables reproducible computations.

goal_alignment.criterion: Decision framework with scenario/sensitivity analysis aligned to risk aversion
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the article builds a structured choice model (e.g., NPV/time value for delivery delays, rent subsidy sufficiency, resale liquidity, location premium) and runs sensitivity tests (appraisal variance, delay duration, interest rates). It should reflect the user’s risk-averse profile and long-term asset stability preference to identify the most favorable option under uncertainty.

goal_alignment.criterion: Procedural roadmap with critical timelines and document checklist
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify the presence of a step-by-step process (announcement → survey → appraisal → negotiation/hearing → decision → agreement → relocation → settlement/possession), statutory time limits, required materials, and acceptance checklists for resettlement housing. This supports the user’s process-control mindset and limited time windows (mornings/evenings) for efficient action.

goal_alignment.criterion: Legal avenues specificity (negotiation, reconsideration, litigation)
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the article clearly lays out homeowner rights during negotiation (hearing, information disclosure), and post-decision remedies (where to apply for administrative reconsideration, filing deadlines, jurisdiction for administrative litigation, required evidence, potential outcomes), enabling the user to choose the right path at the right time.

goal_alignment.criterion: Fairness safeguards and enforceability in appraisal and allocation
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for guidance on ensuring fairness: independent third-party appraisal, public disclosure of comparables, objection/review and re-appraisal procedures, lot-drawing rules for property exchange, and how to document and escalate irregularities. This addresses the user’s desire for transparent, data-backed fairness and recourse.

goal_alignment.criterion: Data-driven negotiation toolkit and evidence pack
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the article provides practical negotiation tactics aligned to an engineer’s style: gathering market comparables, documenting finishes/attachments, using standardized evidence templates, recording communications (WeChat/DingTalk), and drafting objective argument structures. This increases bargaining power without confrontation.

goal_alignment.criterion: Financial completeness: taxes, fees, mortgages, and net-outcome clarity
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the article covers tax/fee implications (e.g., deed tax on resettlement, personal income tax exemptions/conditions, handling of existing mortgages), and reconciles all inflows/outflows into a net compensation comparison to prevent surprises and match the user’s cost-effectiveness focus.

goal_alignment.criterion: Transition and delivery risk management
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for coverage of temporary resettlement options, rent subsidy adequacy vs local rents, delivery delay risks and penalties, acceptance standards and defect rectification for resettlement housing, and practical notes (e.g., pet-friendly rentals). This reflects the user’s family lifestyle and operational pragmatism.

goal_alignment.criterion: Clarity, structure, and tooling fit to user habits
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the article uses clear structure, concise language, tables/checklists/flowcharts, and provides calculators or editable templates with references to official sources suitable for quick saving/sharing via Zhihu/WeChat. This matches the user’s consumption and decision-making workflow.

goal_alignment.criterion: Family-inclusive planning and lifestyle alignment
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan recognizes family needs (spouse’s schedule, daughter’s finance insight, pet considerations, preference for quiet communities/garden) and schedules key actions in feasible time slots. This ensures the chosen option fits real-life constraints and preferences.

content_alignment.criterion: Local Policy Anchoring to Suzhou/Jiangsu
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report distinguishes national regulations from Suzhou/Jiangsu implementing measures (e.g., typical local coefficients, subsidy/reward rules, resettlement project practices), and indicates how local variations in different Suzhou districts affect compensation results.

content_alignment.criterion: Quantitative Comparison Depth for Compensation Models
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the content provides explicit calculation frameworks for monetary compensation and property exchange (formulas, variables, sample numbers), plus sensitivity analysis (e.g., valuation benchmarks, swap ratios, area differences) and time-value comparisons to support a rational, engineering-style decision.

content_alignment.criterion: Risk and Uncertainty Scenario Coverage
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the report compares key risks—resettlement delivery delay, valuation date locking, market price volatility, policy changes—using best/base/worst-case scenarios, so a risk-averse reader can gauge downside exposure across options.

content_alignment.criterion: Legal Remedies Mapping with Procedural Specifics
content_alignment.explanation: Assess if the content clarifies which administrative acts are challengeable (expropriation vs compensation decision), negotiation and hearing rights, statutory time limits, competent authorities, and procedural steps relevant to Suzhou, matching the user’s need for precise legal avenues.

content_alignment.criterion: Valuation Process and Fairness Safeguards
content_alignment.explanation: Check whether the report details appraisal institution selection (e.g., random draw/owner choice), evaluation date, comparables disclosure, re-evaluation mechanisms, and measures preventing administrative interference—key for a process-minded, fairness-focused user.

content_alignment.criterion: Financial and Tax Implications of Each Option
content_alignment.explanation: See if the content covers deed tax/taxability of subsidies, fees for new purchases, mortgage/purchase restriction policies in Suzhou, and net-of-tax outcomes, aligning with the user’s investment literacy and cost-effectiveness mindset.

content_alignment.criterion: Lifestyle and Commute Fit Factors in Model Choice
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the analysis integrates personal living needs—pet-friendly policies, access to parks, gardening feasibility (low-rise vs high-rise), workplace commute—to personalize the choice beyond pure numbers.

content_alignment.criterion: Authoritative Policy Citations and Versioning
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether national and Suzhou municipal documents are cited with names/effective dates and applicability to old-town renewal versus other categories, catering to the user’s preference for credible, verifiable sources.

content_alignment.criterion: Property Status Verification and Special Situations
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the content addresses ownership proof, registered vs measured area discrepancies, treatment of illegal additions, shared ownership, household composition eligibility, and land nature, matching the user’s detail-oriented approach to factors that can alter compensation.

presentation_fit.criterion: Language tone and register fit for a senior Chinese technical director
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report uses a neutral, precise, non-promotional tone, with direct and respectful wording typical of Mainland professional/legal communication. Avoids colloquialisms and emotional language, matching the user's rational, pragmatic style.

presentation_fit.criterion: Legal terminology management with plain-language glosses
presentation_fit.explanation: Check if PRC legal terms (e.g., 征收、补偿、行政复议、国有土地上房屋征收与补偿条例) are accurate, consistently used, and immediately clarified in plain language or with a mini-glossary/footnotes so a non-lawyer can follow without ambiguity.

presentation_fit.criterion: Task-aligned structure and navigability
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether the report mirrors the task’s sub-questions with numbered headings (calculation standards, evaluation process, fairness mechanisms, legal avenues, option selection), includes an executive summary and a table of contents/anchors for quick navigation.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-friendly scannability
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess paragraph length (2–4 sentences), use of bullet/numbered lists, clear micro-headings, and highlighting of key figures/dates so the user can skim on WeChat/DingTalk or phone with minimal effort.

presentation_fit.criterion: Procedural visualization clarity (flowcharts/timelines)
presentation_fit.explanation: Check if the expropriation, negotiation, evaluation, administrative reconsideration, and litigation steps are shown in clear flowcharts or timelines with labeled stage gates and statutory time limits to match the user’s process-thinking habit.

presentation_fit.criterion: Quantitative presentation of compensation (formulas, worked examples, tables)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether formulas are clearly formatted, assumptions explicitly stated, units and coefficients labeled, and at least one worked numeric example and a comparison table are provided, enabling engineering-style comprehension.

presentation_fit.criterion: Decision-aid visuals for option selection
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess the presence and clarity of a decision tree or comparison matrix (e.g., monetary vs property exchange vs mixed), with labeled criteria and visual cues (ticks/weights) that make the choice pathway visually obvious.

presentation_fit.criterion: Source attribution and update labeling
presentation_fit.explanation: Check that policy sources are presented in a consistent box or footnote style with document title, issuing authority, and date, plus an 'updated on' note—enhancing trust without cluttering the main text.

presentation_fit.criterion: Localization delineation (national vs Suzhou-specific rules)
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether national regulations and Suzhou municipal/ district rules are visually separated and tagged (e.g., badges or sidebars) to prevent mixing levels of authority, aiding quick orientation.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Local policy operability for Suzhou/Jiangsu
actionability_practicality.explanation: Actionable guidance must reflect current Suzhou/Jiangsu expropriation rules (e.g., temporary resettlement fee standards, appraisal selection rules, incentive deadlines) and point to official sources/contacts so the user can directly act in his locality.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Complete compensation calculation toolkit (formulas + worked examples/worksheet)
actionability_practicality.explanation: The report should provide line-item formulas and filled examples for monetary and property-exchange compensation (market value appraisal, decoration depreciation, moving subsidies, transition fees, early-sign rewards, area differences), plus a worksheet the user can plug figures into to compute totals himself.

actionability_practicality.criterion: End-to-end procedure roadmap with statutory timelines and triggers
actionability_practicality.explanation: A step-by-step flow (notice → inventory → appraisal → disclosure/hearing → agreement → objection/re-evaluation → signing → payment/resettlement) with exact statutory or policy time limits enables the user to sequence actions and avoid missing peremptory deadlines.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Evidence and documentation checklist with acquisition methods
actionability_practicality.explanation: Actionability requires a concrete list (ownership certificates, area/structure proofs, decoration records, photos, invoices, leases, household registration) and how to obtain/verify each item to support valuation, negotiations, and potential legal actions.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Fairness safeguards and intra-process remedies instructions
actionability_practicality.explanation: The report should specify actionable fairness checks (public disclosure review, comparable sales verification, appraiser independence, cross-examination) and how to request re-evaluation/复核评估 or a hearing to correct undervaluation before litigation.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Negotiation playbook grounded in comps and incentives
actionability_practicality.explanation: Practical tactics (setting anchors from comparable transactions, documenting defects/inconveniences, leveraging early-sign rewards and property-exchange options, bundling requests) help the user actively improve terms at the table.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Decision model for choosing compensation mode with scenario/sensitivity analysis
actionability_practicality.explanation: A structured framework comparing cash vs property exchange (and hybrid) using criteria like net proceeds, delivery risk, location/commute, family plans, and investment returns—plus sensitivity to appraisal value, delivery delay, and market prices—lets the user choose the most favorable option.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Total cost, tax, and cashflow impact accounting
actionability_practicality.explanation: Actionability requires net-of-cost calculations: deed tax/fees on exchange, renovation and multiple moves, interim rent, bridge financing, taxes/exemptions, and payment schedules to ensure the plan fits the user’s liquidity and investment needs.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Legal recourse roadmap (reconsideration/litigation) with deadlines and materials
actionability_practicality.explanation: A clear, stepwise guide for hearings, administrative reconsideration, and administrative litigation—covering filing grounds, competent authorities, limitation periods, required documents, and expected timelines—enables the user to protect rights if negotiations fail.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk and contingency planning for delays and uncertainties
actionability_practicality.explanation: Practical alternatives for delayed resettlement/payment, backup housing, property storage, and escalation routes reduce execution risk and align with the user’s risk-averse style.

depth_insight.criterion: Jurisdiction-specific Policy Synthesis and Applicability Judgment
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report goes beyond listing rules to identify the controlling national vs local regulations, policy recency, and their applicability to old-town redevelopment versus other expropriation contexts, highlighting any local implementation nuances that materially affect compensation.

depth_insight.criterion: Rigor and Completeness of Compensation Calculation (Cash and Property Exchange)
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report rigorously derives compensation under both models, covering appraisal methods, valuation date, area standards, deductions for unpermitted parts, add-ons (moving/transition allowances, business interruption), swap ratios/pricing for replacement units, and includes worked examples and sensitivity analysis to key assumptions.

depth_insight.criterion: Depth of Appraisal/Evaluation Process and Fairness Safeguards
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates insight into how the evaluation process ensures fairness in practice—selection and rotation of appraisers, rights to re-appraisal or review, disclosure and hearing mechanisms, comparable evidence standards, and conflict-of-interest controls—and how homeowners can activate these safeguards.

depth_insight.criterion: Legal Remedies Mapping with Procedural Specificity and Practicality
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a procedure-aware pathway from negotiation to administrative reconsideration and litigation, including standing, filing windows, admissibility, standards of review, evidence requirements, interim relief options, and realistic assessment of success likelihood.

depth_insight.criterion: Decision-Oriented Comparative Framework for Choosing Compensation Model
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates the depth of the decision framework comparing cash vs property exchange using quantitative tools (NPV/cash flow), risk assessment (delivery, timing, market), taxes/fees, liquidity needs, and household constraints, with scenario and sensitivity analysis leading to actionable recommendations.

depth_insight.criterion: Insight into Stakeholder Incentives and Negotiation Levers
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report identifies the incentives and constraints of implementing bodies and appraisers, and translates them into concrete negotiation strategies (e.g., timing, data/evidence preparation, collective vs individual bargaining, use of hearings/reviews) to improve terms.

depth_insight.criterion: Coverage of Edge Cases and Heterogeneous Household Situations
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the analysis addresses special conditions—mixed-use or business premises, tenants, co-ownership and liens, unpermitted additions, vulnerable groups—and clarifies their impact on compensation and legal options.

depth_insight.criterion: Originality and Actionable Added Value
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses the presence of unique, decision-enhancing insights such as checklists, common pitfalls, benchmarking against comparable cases, or novel yet practical perspectives that go beyond standard policy restatement.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a logical macro-structure tailored to this task: legal-policy hierarchy → compensation models and standards → appraisal and fairness mechanisms → remedies (negotiation, reconsideration, litigation) → decision framework for choosing compensation. Also checks consistency of definitions (e.g., expropriation vs demolition) and absence of cross-section contradictions.

logical_coherence.criterion: Evidence–Policy Grounding and Norm Hierarchy Clarity
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether each procedural or calculation claim is traceable to a normative source (national law, administrative regulations, local rules) or clearly labeled as general practice; distinguishes general rules from locality-specific provisions, preventing unsupported assertions.

logical_coherence.criterion: Compensation Calculation Logic Consistency and Traceability
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether monetary and property-exchange calculations are derived via explicit, internally consistent rules (e.g., valuation basis, area standards, coefficient application, subsidies, exclusion of double counting) with assumptions stated and, where applicable, example walkthroughs that match the stated formulas.

logical_coherence.criterion: Procedural Causality in Appraisal and Fairness Mechanisms
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that the evaluation process is presented as a causally linked sequence (initiation → appraisal → disclosure → hearing/objections → confirmation), with roles, decision points, and fairness safeguards (third-party appraisal, public notice, re-appraisal) logically tied to specific stages and outcomes.

Assesses whether the logic of dispute resolution is complete and ordered (negotiation and hearings → administrative reconsideration → administrative litigation), with prerequisites, standing, time limits, and effects of each step clearly connected and non-contradictory.

logical_coherence.criterion: Reasoning from Premises to Choice of Compensation Model
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the recommendation on choosing monetary compensation vs property exchange is derived from explicit premises (household needs, budget, timing, risk, market outlook) via a transparent decision logic (criteria weights or trade-offs), avoiding leaps from description to advice.

logical_coherence.criterion: Transitions and Logical Signposting Between Sections
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that section and paragraph transitions explicitly connect what was established to what follows (e.g., ‘Given the appraisal basis, the following calculation applies…’), preventing logical gaps when moving between policy summary, calculations, procedures, remedies, and recommendations.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Layout and Navigation Aligned to Homeowner Tasks
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether sections are clearly organized and labeled to match the reader’s needs (e.g., policy overview, compensation models and calculation standards, valuation process, fairness safeguards, negotiation steps, legal remedies, choice framework). Looks for a clear heading hierarchy, consistent numbering, table of contents, cross-references, adequate white space, and scannable bullet lists so readers can quickly locate what they need.

clarity_readability.criterion: Plain-Language Clarity and Sentence-Level Readability
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether the report uses concise sentences, active voice, and everyday wording instead of legalese; avoids ambiguity and unnecessary jargon; maintains correct grammar and punctuation; and uses consistent units and numerals. This ensures non-experts can understand critical information without misinterpretation.

clarity_readability.criterion: Definition and Consistent Use of Legal/Technical Terms and Jurisdiction Scope Cues
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether specialized terms (e.g., market appraisal, property exchange, administrative reconsideration) are defined at first use or in a glossary; abbreviations are introduced and used consistently; and the report clearly distinguishes national laws from local policies, with dates and jurisdiction indicated to prevent confusion.

clarity_readability.criterion: Transparency of Compensation Calculations and Worked Examples
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks if monetary and property-exchange calculations are presented with clear formulas, variable definitions, and step-by-step numeric examples using realistic figures. Units (m², currency) and rounding rules are explicit, assumptions are labeled as illustrative, and example outcomes are clearly separated from actual policy to help readers self-calculate.

clarity_readability.criterion: Process and Timeline Presentation (Steps, Deadlines, Checklists)
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether procedures (valuation selection, objection handling, negotiation, administrative reconsideration, litigation) are broken into numbered steps with labeled actors, document checklists, and explicit deadlines/limitation periods. Timelines or step lists make the sequence and time sensitivity unambiguous.

clarity_readability.criterion: Design Quality and Clarity of Visualizations and Tables
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether tables, comparison matrices, timelines, and flowcharts are well-labeled, readable, and uncluttered, with clear titles, units, legends, and footnotes. Visuals should use accessible colors, appropriate font sizes, and clear sorting to convey comparisons (e.g., compensation models) at a glance.

clarity_readability.criterion: Emphasis of Key Points and Executive Summary
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks for a concise executive summary and section mini-summaries that surface the main options, key rights, critical deadlines, and takeaways. Use of callouts, highlights, and end-of-report action checklists helps readers remember what matters and act accordingly.

task: My city has recently launched an old town redevelopment project, and we own an old property that has been included in the demolition scope. Please summarize the current housing expropriation and demolition compensation policies, including the calculation standards for monetary compensation and property exchange, the evaluation process, and how to ensure fairness in compensation. I would also like to know the legal avenues available to homeowners during the negotiation of compensation plans, and the possible ways to request administrative reconsideration or initiate legal proceedings if dissatisfied with the compensation outcome. Based on the different compensation models, please help me assess how to choose the most favorable option.

goal_alignment.criterion: Local policy anchoring and applicability to Suzhou/Jiangsu
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report cites and accurately reflects the national framework (e.g., State Council regulations on expropriation), Jiangsu provincial rules, and Suzhou municipal implementation details, and clarifies applicability to 'old town redevelopment' and whether it is state-owned land house expropriation or rural collective land requisition. This matches Mr. Wang’s need for accurate, locally relevant guidance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Transparent compensation calculations with worked Suzhou-relevant examples
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if monetary and property-exchange formulas are explicit (appraised market value, transition rent subsidy, moving fees, decoration compensation, area conversion, price differentials), with clear assumptions, edge cases (illegal additions), and numeric examples reflecting Suzhou market levels. Mr. Wang’s rational, comparative style requires reproducible numbers.

goal_alignment.criterion: Fairness safeguards in appraisal and decision procedures
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content explains agency selection, independence, re-appraisal rights, hearing rights, information disclosure, and prohibition of illegal forced demolition, plus how to trigger review and avoid conflicts of interest. This aligns with his fairness expectations and desire for due process.

goal_alignment.criterion: Rights-protection procedural roadmap (negotiation, reconsideration, litigation)
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether it lays out step-by-step paths during negotiation, how to request a hearing, file administrative reconsideration, and initiate administrative litigation, including typical time limits, evidence requirements, and cost-risk considerations. This suits his risk-averse need to resolve issues lawfully and efficiently.

goal_alignment.criterion: Personalized decision framework for choosing compensation model
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if it provides a criteria matrix reflecting his family priorities: granddaughter’s schooling continuity, proximity to parks/library/hospitals/senior centers, quiet environment, elevator/sunlight, cultural ambience, liquidity needs, and construction quality of resettlement housing. This enables a family-aligned, practical choice.

goal_alignment.criterion: Elder-friendly clarity, structure, and shareability
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate layered organization (executive summary, key numbers, step-by-step guides), plain Chinese definitions for legal terms, bullet lists, and a concise summary suitable for sharing with wife and family on WeChat. Matches his educated but senior-friendly reading preference.

goal_alignment.criterion: Negotiation strategies and documentation checklist
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for practical tactics (questions to ask, how to record meetings) and a checklist of documents (ownership certs, household register, family size proofs, appraisal entrustment, agreements). This reflects his methodical, teacher-like approach to preparing evidence.

goal_alignment.criterion: Transition and resettlement logistics planning
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess coverage of temporary housing options, rent subsidy formulas and duration, moving allowances, transition period extensions, property handover conditions, and schooling arrangements during transition. This reduces disruption to his routines and granddaughter’s studies.

goal_alignment.criterion: Risk and fraud precautions with due process emphasis
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify warnings against scams, unauthorized agents, signing blank documents, and reminders that coercive demolition is prohibited; include complaint channels. This aligns with his risk-averse financial and legal stance.

goal_alignment.criterion: Conservative financial handling of compensation funds and difference payments
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate guidance on escrow, payment schedules, avoiding risky investments, and handling taxes/fees when receiving compensation or paying price differences for property exchange. Fits his low-risk investment profile.

goal_alignment.criterion: Cultural and emotional resonance
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for empathetic tone acknowledging attachment to the old town and preference for quiet, culturally rich environments. This builds trust and aligns recommendations with his lifestyle values.

goal_alignment.criterion: Verifiable resources and templates
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether it provides official portals, local hotlines, and simple templates (hearing request, reconsideration application) to enable independent verification and action in Suzhou. Supports his habit of collecting and sharing reliable sources.

content_alignment.criterion: Localization to Suzhou/Jiangsu Policies and Practice
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the report grounds compensation standards, procedures, and examples in Jiangsu provincial and Suzhou municipal rules and prevailing local practices (e.g., local implementing measures, typical area conversion ratios, local subsidy items), rather than offering only national-level generalities.

content_alignment.criterion: Senior- and Multigenerational-Centric Content Factors
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the content explicitly incorporates household-specific factors relevant to a 65–70-year-old living with a spouse and school-age granddaughter: continuity of schooling, proximity to healthcare/parks, barrier-free/elevator needs, floor/orientation preferences, and transitional housing timing that minimizes family disruption.

content_alignment.criterion: Risk-Averse Financial Comparison of Compensation Models
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the cash vs property exchange analysis is tailored to a pension-based, risk-averse user, covering cash flow stability, management/maintenance fees, future renovation costs, liquidity, rental yield realism, tax/fee impacts, and long-term housing security rather than speculative upside.

content_alignment.criterion: Depth on Valuation and Fairness Mechanisms
content_alignment.explanation: Determine if the report deeply explains valuation bases and fairness safeguards that matter to the user’s sense of justice (e.g., appraiser selection and replacement, comparable selection rules, adjustment coefficients for floor/decoration, public disclosure, objection/reaudit channels), at a level suitable for an educated reader.

content_alignment.criterion: Localized Legal Avenues and Timelines
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the content specifies, in a Suzhou-relevant frame, the negotiation/hearing options, administrative reconsideration authority and time limits, and litigation routes and filing windows, so the user can realistically judge rights protection pathways in his city.

content_alignment.criterion: Concrete Numerical Scenarios with Local Benchmarks
content_alignment.explanation: Check if the report includes worked examples using locally plausible unit prices, area calculations, coefficients, and subsidy/award items to make abstract rules tangible and comparable for the user’s specific market context.

content_alignment.criterion: Cultural Heritage and Community Continuity Consideration
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content recognizes potential intersections with historical street protection, cultural preservation policies, and options that maintain community ties—aligning with the user’s deep cultural attachment and preference for familiar environments.

content_alignment.criterion: Evidence and Documentation Preparedness
content_alignment.explanation: Determine whether the content prompts the user to prepare and preserve key documents and proofs (property certificate, area records, renovation receipts, photos, communications) aligned with his meticulous, scholarly habits, enabling stronger negotiations and rights remedies.

presentation_fit.criterion: Language tone and clarity match a scholarly, respectful senior reader
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether the report uses calm, respectful, and precise language, avoiding slang and sensationalism, with concise sentences and logical transitions—befitting a retired Chinese teacher accustomed to well-crafted prose.

presentation_fit.criterion: Legal terminology handling with plain-language glosses
presentation_fit.explanation: Check whether key legal terms (e.g., monetary compensation, property exchange, appraisal/review appraisal, administrative reconsideration, administrative litigation) are introduced with brief parenthetical explanations to reduce cognitive load for a non-lawyer while keeping formal terms intact.

presentation_fit.criterion: Information architecture and navigation aligned to the task’s sub-questions
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate if the report uses clear sectioning and numbering that mirror the user’s questions (compensation standards, evaluation process, fairness mechanisms, legal avenues, choice framework), includes an executive summary, and ends with a decision checklist for quick orientation.

presentation_fit.criterion: Process visualization and timeline clarity
presentation_fit.explanation: Assess whether procedural content (negotiation steps, appraisal/review, reconsideration and litigation deadlines) is presented as step-by-step sequences or timelines to make complex procedures easy to follow and track.

presentation_fit.criterion: Comparative presentation of compensation models
presentation_fit.explanation: Check if monetary compensation vs property exchange are contrasted side-by-side with consistent headings and bullet points (pros/cons, conditions, timing), enabling quick visual comparison without dense prose.

presentation_fit.criterion: Shareability and accessibility for phone reading
presentation_fit.explanation: Evaluate whether paragraphs are short, key points are bullet-listed, section headers are descriptive, and there are concise summaries that make the content easy to share on WeChat and to discuss with family.

presentation_fit.criterion: Citation formatting and versioning/jurisdiction labeling
presentation_fit.explanation: Check if legal sources are clearly labeled (full names and dates), national vs Suzhou municipal rules are distinguished, and a visible ‘last updated’ note is included to build trust and reduce confusion.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Local legal–procedural applicability to Suzhou/Jiangsu
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report translates national rules into Suzhou/Jiangsu-specific policies, names the responsible local agencies, specifies applicable local measures, and maps them to concrete local steps. Local specificity is essential for actions to be valid and effective in the user’s city.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Action timeline with explicit deadlines and milestones
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report provides a step-by-step schedule from notice to signing, appraisal, objection/hearing, move-out, and the windows for administrative reconsideration/litigation. Clear, dated milestones prevent loss of rights due to missed deadlines and guide timely action.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Quantitative compensation calculation and net-of-tax scenario analysis
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report includes formulas, worked examples, and a calculator template covering market value, structural/location factors, decoration, attachments, relocation/temporary resettlement allowances, taxes/fees (and exemptions), and expected net cash. This enables apples-to-apples comparison of cash vs property exchange.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Decision framework tailored to family priorities and risk profile
actionability_practicality.explanation: Looks for a practical scoring/weighting model that incorporates the family’s needs—granddaughter’s school continuity, elderly-friendly facilities (elevators, hospitals proximity), quiet environment, commute, and risk tolerance—so the report yields a concrete recommendation and a fallback option.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Documentation and evidence readiness checklist
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks if the report provides a clear checklist and how-to for obtaining/organizing ownership certificates, identity and household documents, measurement/appraisal reports, proof of legal area vs additions, photos, and correspondence records. Being evidence-ready is critical for negotiations and disputes.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Operable fairness-protection steps within the expropriation process
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report explains how to request and conduct independent/second appraisal, select or randomize appraisal institutions, challenge comparables, apply for hearings, and seek information disclosure—i.e., concrete steps to secure fair valuation before litigation.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Legal remedies roadmap (administrative reconsideration and litigation)
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report details filing prerequisites, time limits, competent authorities/courts, required materials, admissibility points, timelines, cost estimates, and outcome expectations for reconsideration and administrative litigation, enabling the user to act promptly if dissatisfied.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Negotiation playbook with concrete tactics and materials
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for actionable negotiation strategies: preparing market comparables, commissioning independent valuations, using group negotiation, proposing contract clauses (e.g., delivery date penalties, escrow), and practical phrasing/scripts. This can improve outcomes without immediately escalating to legal action.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Risk and contingency plan for transition and delays
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report provides a plan for temporary housing near the granddaughter’s school, schedules for receiving funds and keys, cash flow buffers, handling delivery delays, and protective measures (escrow, guarantees), reducing real-life disruption for a risk-averse elderly household.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Professional assistance guidance (appraisers/lawyers) and cost–benefit thresholds
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report specifies when to engage professionals, typical fee ranges, selection criteria, and a simple threshold analysis (e.g., expected improvement vs fees) so the user can decide efficiently and avoid unnecessary expense.

depth_insight.criterion: Jurisdiction-aware synthesis of expropriation/compensation policy and key levers
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report maps the governing legal/policy hierarchy (national/basic law vs local implementing rules), identifies current effective rules, and extracts decision-critical levers such as valuation basis and date, comparable selection rules, swap ratios, resettlement area caps, treatment of illegal additions/attachments/fit-outs, business interruption, relocation and transition subsidies, incentive policies, and tax/fee allocation—including edge cases (heritage, non-residential, collective/state land). This transforms generic policy into actionable parameters.

depth_insight.criterion: Quantitative compensation modeling and sensitivity (cash vs property swap)
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report builds explicit formulas and worked examples for monetary compensation and property exchange, incorporating unit prices, area definitions (GFA vs usable), depreciation/condition, attachments/fit-out, temporary resettlement rent and duration, difference payments in swaps, and delivery risk. High-quality insight includes sensitivity analyses to valuation date, comparable selection, swap ratio, delivery delay, and discount rate, making trade-offs transparent and evidence-based.

depth_insight.criterion: Appraisal process insight and fairness safeguards
depth_insight.explanation: Looks for expert-level treatment of how valuations are produced and can be contested: appraisal methods (market, cost, income), appraiser qualifications/selection, transparency requirements (public notices, hearings), right to independent or second appraisal, dispute mechanisms, mass vs individual appraisal limitations, and conflict-of-interest controls. Also identifies typical bias points and concrete mitigation tactics. This is critical to ensuring fair compensation, not just describing the steps.

depth_insight.criterion: Legal remedies roadmap with strategic value for homeowners
depth_insight.explanation: Judges whether the report turns legal pathways into practical leverage: rights during negotiation (information disclosure, hearings, evidence preservation, third-party appraisal), prerequisites and timelines for administrative reconsideration, scope/standard of review, interim relief possibilities, and escalation to litigation (claims, burdens, timing, costs, success considerations). Insight includes how legal options influence negotiation strategy and when to use them.

depth_insight.criterion: Decision framework for choosing the favorable option
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report constructs a multi-criteria choice model—financial NPV and cash flow timing, risk (delivery, legal certainty), liquidity needs, location/quality of resettlement units, family constraints, tax and future appreciation—paired with scenario analysis (e.g., break-even swap ratio, delay thresholds). High depth translates calculations and risks into personalized, threshold-based recommendations.

depth_insight.criterion: Accounting for local variation, policy uncertainty, and data verification
depth_insight.explanation: Evaluates whether the report identifies which elements vary by city/project, prescribes how to verify and source the governing rules (official notices, red-line/planning docs, price references), flags policy-change risk and negotiation windows, and clearly states/justifies assumptions when data are missing. This prevents misapplication of generic rules and enhances reliability.

depth_insight.criterion: Actionable negotiation levers and risk flags
depth_insight.explanation: Checks for original, high-value insights such as leveraging early-move incentives, contesting comparables, documenting improvements/business losses, sequencing relocation, securing delivery/quality guarantees, and identifying contract pitfalls (open-ended delivery dates, waiver clauses, resale restrictions, HOA/fees). The emphasis is on concrete, less-obvious tactics that materially affect outcomes.

depth_insight.criterion: Differentiation by property type and household situation
depth_insight.explanation: Assesses whether the report tailors analysis for residential vs commercial, owner-occupier vs landlord, mortgaged or co-owned properties, tenants’ interests, small business interruption, and vulnerable groups—showing how compensation and legal options change and what to optimize in each case. Such tailoring yields more valuable, context-sensitive conclusions.

logical_coherence.explanation: Checks whether the report follows a logical order tailored to this task: (1) summarize compensation policies, (2) detail monetary vs property exchange calculation standards, (3) explain the valuation/evaluation process, (4) lay out fairness mechanisms, (5) present legal avenues (negotiation, reconsideration, litigation), and (6) build a decision framework to choose the favorable option. Prevents back-and-forth and ensures later reasoning rests on established premises.

logical_coherence.criterion: Normative traceability and relevance of supporting evidence
logical_coherence.explanation: Assesses whether every policy/procedure claim is tied to appropriate legal/administrative sources, with applicability and hierarchy made explicit (e.g., national law vs local implementing rules), and whether the cited sources actually support the stated rule/process.

logical_coherence.criterion: Causal reasoning from premises to homeowner-specific recommendations
logical_coherence.explanation: Evaluates whether the report explicitly links premises (household needs, time horizon, cash constraints, tax/fees, market conditions) and policy parameters (compensation rates, exchange ratios, settlement of price differences) to conclusions via if-then or decision-tree logic, stating assumptions and boundary conditions.

logical_coherence.criterion: Procedural logic integrity of the evaluation and remedy process
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that the process is sequenced correctly (e.g., survey → valuation → offer → consultation/hearing → agreement/decision → payment/relocation → objection → review/revaluation → administrative reconsideration → litigation) with dependencies and prerequisites clearly stated.

logical_coherence.criterion: Consistency of definitions, scope, and measurement bases
logical_coherence.explanation: Verifies consistent use of key terms and bases (e.g., expropriation vs demolition, legal housing area vs illegal extensions, gross vs net area, valuation date, inclusion/exclusion of subsidies), and that no contradictions appear across sections.

Assesses whether transitions make explicit how each section depends on the previous one (e.g., using valuation standards to explain fairness, then using both to ground legal remedies, then using all to inform the choice model), with cross-references to earlier definitions when used later.

Evaluates whether the report distinguishes general (nationwide) rules from city-specific policies, flags where local deviations affect calculations or procedures, and avoids blending rules from different jurisdictions without clarification.

logical_coherence.criterion: Quantification and settlement logic coherence for compensation models
logical_coherence.explanation: Checks that any formulas/examples for monetary compensation and property exchange are internally consistent: all components are included once (market value, subsidies, relocation/temporary housing, price-difference settlement), units and valuation dates align, and comparisons use the same base so conclusions follow numerically.

clarity_readability.criterion: Structural Layout and Navigability
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks whether the report has a clear section hierarchy aligned to the user’s questions (e.g., compensation standards, evaluation process, fairness mechanisms, legal remedies, option selection), a table of contents, consistent heading levels and numbering, readable paragraph spacing, and internal cross-references/hyperlinks so readers can jump to procedures or formulas quickly.

clarity_readability.criterion: Plain-Language Clarity and Wording Precision
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether sentences are concise and unambiguous, jargon is minimized, and modal verbs (e.g., must/should/may) are used consistently to avoid misinterpretation; checks grammar, punctuation, and consistent terminology so non-experts can understand requirements and conditions without legalese.

clarity_readability.criterion: Definition and Explanation of Technical and Legal Terms
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether key terms (e.g., housing expropriation, market value at expropriation date, appraisal/valuation agency, property exchange, transitional compensation, incentives, administrative reconsideration, administrative litigation) are explicitly defined via a glossary or inline callouts, with brief plain-language explanations and, where helpful, short examples.

clarity_readability.criterion: Clarity of Formulas, Calculations, and Worked Examples
clarity_readability.explanation: Checks that compensation formulas are clearly formatted (variables defined, units/currency specified, coefficients explained), assumptions are stated, and at least one worked example shows step-by-step computation so readers can replicate calculations and compare options numerically.

clarity_readability.criterion: Process Visualization and Timeline Readability
clarity_readability.explanation: Evaluates whether the expropriation and appeal/reconsideration processes are shown with flowcharts or timelines that label each step, responsible party, required documents, and statutory time limits; includes step-by-step checklists to reduce cognitive load and prevent missed deadlines.

clarity_readability.criterion: Highlighting Key Choices and Comparison Matrices
clarity_readability.explanation: Assesses whether the report presents side-by-side tables or matrices comparing compensation options (e.g., monetary vs property exchange) with clearly labeled criteria (total value, timing, cash flow, risk, rehousing availability), and uses callout boxes to highlight critical reminders and takeaways.

task: I wish to promote my child’s creativity development and emotional expression through artistic activities within the next six months. Please provide a detailed art education plan covering painting, crafts, music, dance, and other fields, including specific activity arrangements, a list of required materials, introductions to artists and cultural backgrounds, and how to enhance my child’s interest and creativity in art through family interaction. Additionally, please evaluate how different art forms impact children’s emotional expression, concentration, and self-confidence, and offer personalized activity recommendations based on my child’s interests and abilities.

goal_alignment.criterion: Dual-child, interest-and-ability–matched personalization
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan distinctly tailors activities to the 8-year-old daughter (painting/dance, longer attention span, symbolic expression) and the 5-year-old son (building toys/basketball, sensory-motor needs), while also including joint sibling activities. It should show scaffolding, skill progression, and cross-pollination (e.g., dance-informed drawing for daughter; building-informed sculpture for son). This is critical to resonance and usability for this specific family.

goal_alignment.criterion: Schedule-fit 6‑month roadmap with feasible sessions and contingencies
goal_alignment.explanation: Check if the plan maps to weekday evening short sessions and richer weekend blocks, with monthly milestones and buffer weeks. It should include low-prep options, travel-friendly tasks for business trips, and weather alternatives, reflecting Liu Qiang’s time discipline and weekend family focus.

goal_alignment.criterion: Family interaction design with role clarity and routines
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the plan leverages the wife’s primary school teaching skills (instructional routines, reflection prompts) and the father’s organizational strengths (planning, checklists), defines sibling collaboration roles, and suggests pet-involved prompts. This aligns with the family’s cooperative decision-making and education emphasis.

goal_alignment.criterion: Evidence-based mapping from art forms to outcomes with observable indicators
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if each art form (painting, crafts, music, dance) is explicitly linked to emotional expression, concentration, and self-confidence with simple rubrics/KPIs (e.g., time-on-task, emotional vocabulary used, willingness to perform). This fits the user’s data-driven, case-oriented style and supports progress tracking.

goal_alignment.criterion: East–West cultural integration and local resource leverage
goal_alignment.explanation: Check for balanced introductions to Jiangnan and Chinese artists/traditions (e.g., Suzhou gardens aesthetics, Kunqu, Su embroidery, Wu Guanzhong) and Western artists (e.g., Matisse, Kandinsky), with cultural context and suggested visits (Shanghai Museum, Power Station of Art, Suzhou classical gardens). This aligns with the user’s bi-cultural mindset and weekend field trip habit.

goal_alignment.criterion: Practical materials list with cost–quality balance and procurement guidance
goal_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether materials are non-toxic, reusable, easy to source locally/online in Shanghai, and come with quantities, estimated costs, and storage tips. This matches the user’s quality–cost focus and preference for efficient, reliable preparation.

goal_alignment.criterion: Executive, structured presentation (roadmaps, tables, checklists)
goal_alignment.explanation: Assess if the content is organized into timelines, weekly plans, tables, and concise rationales akin to case-study format, enabling quick comprehension and co-decision with the spouse. This suits an MBA manager’s cognitive preferences.

goal_alignment.criterion: STEAM/project-based integration leveraging building and sports interests
goal_alignment.explanation: Check whether crafts/music/dance are integrated with engineering/building (e.g., kinetic sculptures, sound experiments) and rhythm from basketball for dance/music, turning the son’s interests into creative entry points and broadening both children’s creativity.

goal_alignment.criterion: Motivation and creativity-boost mechanisms (gamification, portfolio, exhibitions)
goal_alignment.explanation: Determine if the plan includes home exhibitions, a family portfolio, simple badges/challenges, and reflective storytelling, plus field trips and sharing with grandparents. These mechanisms nurture intrinsic motivation and self-confidence for both children.

goal_alignment.criterion: Age-appropriate safety and risk management
goal_alignment.explanation: Verify guidance on non-toxic materials, tool supervision, dance warm-ups to prevent injury, sound volume control for music, pet safety around materials, and weather-proof alternatives. This reflects the family’s risk-aware, pragmatic orientation.

content_alignment.criterion: Dual-child, age-ability differentiated pathways across art forms
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether the plan provides separate and developmentally appropriate activity tracks for the 8-year-old (painting, dance) and the 5-year-old (building/crafts, movement), with clear progression and scaffolding that match each child’s current interests and abilities.

content_alignment.criterion: Cultural and local resource alignment (Jiangnan–Shanghai, East–West balance)
content_alignment.explanation: Check if artist/cultural introductions and examples reflect Jiangnan aesthetics (e.g., Suzhou gardens, Kunqu), Chinese masters and moderns (e.g., Wu Guanzhong, Qi Baishi), plus Western references, and if Shanghai/Suzhou-specific institutions or public art are incorporated to ground the content in the family’s cultural–geographical context.

content_alignment.criterion: Family-interaction design tailored to parental roles and family assets
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether the content proposes specific parent–child interaction formats that leverage the mother’s primary school teaching strengths (e.g., literacy-integrated art games) and the father’s planning/analytical strengths (e.g., home exhibitions with feedback rubrics), and sensitively uses the family Labrador as a theme to foster empathy and emotional expression.

content_alignment.criterion: Evidence-backed comparative evaluation of art forms’ impacts
content_alignment.explanation: Determine whether the report includes a structured, comparative analysis (e.g., matrix/framework) of painting, crafts, music, and dance on emotional expression, attention, and self-confidence for ages 5–8, referencing mechanisms or credible evidence summaries rather than generic claims to match the user’s preference for in-depth analysis.

content_alignment.criterion: Schedule and context fit to evening/weekend rhythms and six-month horizon
content_alignment.explanation: Assess whether content selection is scoped to short evening sessions and richer weekend modules, aligns to a clear six-month timeline, and reflects frequent park visits and occasional trips (e.g., portable nature sketching, travel-inspired art prompts), matching the family’s time-use patterns.

content_alignment.criterion: Materials and resource curation aligned with quality–cost preferences and local availability
content_alignment.explanation: Check if material lists prioritize safe, durable, high-quality items with cost-effective tiers and mention availability via Shanghai retailers or common platforms, reflecting the family’s willingness to invest rationally in education.

content_alignment.criterion: Integration of specific interests into cross-disciplinary activity design
content_alignment.explanation: Evaluate whether painting/dance (daughter) and building toys/basketball (son) are explicitly woven into activities across art domains (e.g., rhythm from dribbling into music/dance, architectural crafts from building toys), to amplify engagement and intrinsic motivation.

presentation_fit.criterion: Executive summary and upfront objectives (TL;DR) are provided and scannable
presentation_fit.explanation: For a time-efficient MBA manager, a concise TL;DR with goals, key outcomes, and the 6-month structure enables rapid grasp and aligns expectations before deep reading.

presentation_fit.criterion: Structured roadmap and navigation (6-month timeline, modular sections by art form and by time period)
presentation_fit.explanation: A clear top-down architecture with a month-by-month (or phase-based) roadmap and consistent section headers reduces cognitive load and supports planning and quick reference.

presentation_fit.criterion: Tone and terminology fit (professional yet parent-friendly, with brief definitions for educational/development terms)
presentation_fit.explanation: A concise, evidence-aware tone builds trust for a data-driven reader while brief term explanations keep the plan accessible for family members executing activities.

presentation_fit.criterion: Use of lists, checklists, and step-by-step formatting for activities and materials
presentation_fit.explanation: Numbered steps and material checklists improve readability and execution readiness without requiring deep narrative parsing, matching the user’s need for clarity and speed.

presentation_fit.criterion: Comparative visuals and frameworks (tables/matrices for art form vs. impact; simple calendars/timelines)
presentation_fit.explanation: Framework-style visuals (e.g., matrices) align with the user’s case-study mindset and make complex comparisons (emotional expression, concentration, self-confidence) immediately legible.

presentation_fit.criterion: Distinct labeling and separation for each child’s track (8-year-old daughter vs. 5-year-old son)
presentation_fit.explanation: Clearly labeled subsections or tracks allow quick scanning and prevent confusion between age-appropriate activities, suiting a family with two children and shared planning.

presentation_fit.criterion: Mobile-friendly formatting (short paragraphs, meaningful subheadings, bullet density, screenshot-ready sections)
presentation_fit.explanation: Reading on WeChat/phone during fragmented time benefits from compact sections, consistent headings, and bullet points that are easy to capture and revisit.

presentation_fit.criterion: Family interaction callouts and role-oriented prompts are visually distinguished (e.g., ‘Parent prompts’, ‘Weekend family module’)
presentation_fit.explanation: Dedicated callouts for family interaction and role cues support the couple’s collaborative style and make interaction segments easy to locate and act upon.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Calendarized 6-month routine that fits the family’s time constraints
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether the report provides a week-by-week schedule with session frequency, duration, and workload that align with school nights and weekend family time, plus notes for peak work periods and school holidays.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Step-by-step activity protocols across painting, crafts, music, and dance
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates if each activity includes clear objectives, time needed, setup, instructions, and simple variations so parents can execute without expert training.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Materials and equipment plan with costs and easy sourcing in China
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses whether the report lists all required materials per activity, provides estimated costs with tiered options (basic vs. premium), and names common purchase/rental channels (e.g., Taobao/JD/offline stores) plus storage/cleanup tips.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Safety, space, and mess management guidance
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks for child-safe tool recommendations, hazard notes (e.g., scissors, glue, small parts), noise control for music/dance, and apartment-friendly setup to ensure the plan is practical and low-risk at home.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Personalization and differentiation for two children
actionability_practicality.explanation: Verifies that the plan includes parallel tracks and joint activities tailored to an 8-year-old who likes painting/dance and a 5-year-old who prefers building, with progression steps and difficulty scaling to maintain engagement.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Outcome mapping and measurement with decision rules
actionability_practicality.explanation: Determines whether each activity is linked to emotional expression, concentration, and self-confidence with observable indicators, simple rubrics/logs, monthly reviews, and rules to reallocate time across art forms based on results.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Family interaction playbook to boost interest and creativity
actionability_practicality.explanation: Assesses the presence of concrete parent-child interaction methods (creative prompts, growth-mindset praise, turn-taking roles, reflection questions) and integration into existing routines (e.g., park walks with the dog).

actionability_practicality.criterion: Local resource integration with practical access details
actionability_practicality.explanation: Checks whether Shanghai/Suzhou museums, concerts, workshops, and classes are suggested with estimated time, cost, booking channels, and age suitability as well as online alternatives when offline options are unavailable.

actionability_practicality.criterion: Contingency plans for schedule disruptions
actionability_practicality.explanation: Evaluates whether the report provides quick-setup, minimal-viable activities, rainy-day/illness substitutions, and travel-week adaptations so the plan remains consistent despite disruptions.

depth_insight.criterion: Mechanistic Linkage Between Activities and Targeted Outcomes
